Title: FIX · Production Error Boundaries for Operator Routes
Description:
## Problem

Zero `error.tsx` files exist in the `(operator)/` route group. Any unhandled error in an operator page (data fetch failure, Supabase RPC timeout, agent crash) renders a white screen with no recovery path. 17 routes affected.

## Context

The root `not-found.tsx` catches 404s but there is no error boundary at any level — not at the group level (`(operator)/error.tsx`), not at individual route level. The Next.js `error.tsx` convention wraps the page in an Error Boundary that receives `{error, reset}` props.

## Scope

Add a single `(operator)/error.tsx` that:

* Renders within the OperatorPanel shell (sidebar + CopilotKit still functional)
* Shows a clear error message
* Provides a "Try again" button (`reset()`)
* Provides a "Return to dashboard" link (`/app`)
* Logs the error to console for debugging
* Respects dark mode

## Out of scope

* Individual per-route error.tsx files (group-level catch-all is sufficient for MVP)
* Custom error reporting service (Sentry, etc.)
* Recovery from CopilotKit provider-level crashes

## Acceptance

- [ ] Navigating to any broken `/app/*` route shows error page, not white screen
- [ ] "Try again" button calls `reset()` to re-render
- [ ] "Return to dashboard" link navigates to `/app`
- [ ] Error is logged to console
- [ ] `npm run build` passes (no type errors)

## Files

**NEW:** `app/src/app/(operator)/error.tsx` — client component error boundary (~30 lines)
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: Planner schema & reusable engine core
Description:
**Audit score: 84/100** — Strong foundation, tightened via this revision.

## Goal

Introduce a single `planner` schema and pure TypeScript engine that can model any time-bound, multi-stakeholder workflow across shoots, campaigns, and CRM deals.

## Key changes per audit

* **Entity type safety:** `entity_type IN ('shoot', 'campaign', 'crm_deal')` CHECK constraint
* **No duplicate instances:** `UNIQUE (org_id, entity_type, entity_id, workflow_id)`
* **RLS role matrix:** four-tier (owner / manager / contributor / viewer) with explicit permissions
* **Status enums:** instance (`draft→planned→active→blocked→completed→archived→cancelled`), task (`todo→in_progress→blocked→done→cancelled`), dependency types
* **Cycle detection:** engine rejects circular deps (A→B→A)
* **Indexes:** 6 B-tree indexes on entity lookup, status, assignee, events, dependencies
* **Two-PR split:** PR1 = schema + RLS + seed; PR2 = engine + types + tests (prevents oversized ~250+ LOC migration)
* **Pure engine:** v1 engine is read-only schedule calc + permission resolution — no DB writes

## Acceptance criteria

* A — `planner` schema with 10 tables, enums, CHECK constraints, indexes.
* B — Polymorphic linking with entity type constraint + uniqueness.
* C — RLS with four-tier role matrix (owner/manager/contributor/viewer).
* D — Status enums for instances, tasks, dependencies.
* E — Pure TypeScript engine with `createInstance`, `buildSchedule`, `shiftTask`, `resolveDependencies` (with cycle detection), `checkGate`, `getEffectivePermissions`.
* F — Idempotent seed for default workflow template.
* G — Realtime publication for instances, tasks, events, assignments.

## Out of scope (v1 engine only — no writes)

Cloudflare DO/Queue, UI, AI, auto-shift, approval v2, DB-side business logic in engine.

## Wiring — two PRs

**PR 1:** `supabase/migrations/·sql` + `seed-planner-workflows.sql` + RLS verify probe
**PR 2:** `app/src/lib/planner/engine.ts` + `types.ts` + `engine.test.ts`

## Verify

* `npm run supabase:verify-rls` — all 4 role tiers
* `cd app && npx vitest run src/lib/planner/engine.test.ts` — ≥90% coverage incl cycle detection + constraint validation

Title: AGENT-002 — Shared AI Tool Registry
Description:
**Priority:** P0 · **Type:** Architecture · **Estimate:** M

Create one shared tool registry so Mastra agents, Cloudflare Workers, and future Cloudflare Agents do not duplicate tool definitions.

**Tools to include:** Firecrawl/crawl, Cloudinary, Supabase, Browser, Search, Email, Stripe/payments, MCP tools, CRM tools, Booking tools, Shoot tools, Brand tools, Notification tools

**Deliverables:**

* Tool registry design (interface, registration, lookup, invocation)
* Tool permission model (read vs write vs admin)
* Read/write classification per tool
* HITL requirements per tool
* Input/output schemas per tool
* Error handling patterns per tool
* Audit logging requirements

**Acceptance criteria:**

* Tools are declared once, used everywhere
* Dangerous tools (write, delete, pay, publish) require human approval
* Tools have typed input/output schemas (Zod)
* Tools are provider-independent — they call the provider adapter, not AI SDKs
* Every tool call is logged to Supabase `ai_agent_logs`
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

## IPI-127 · AIOR-011 — CopilotKit license + production runtime

**Linear:** [IPI-127](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-127) · **Mirror:** [IPI2-176](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI2-176)

**In plain terms:** After you sign in on [www.ipix.co](<https://www.ipix.co>), the AI sidebar should load quietly — no red console errors, no `401` on `/api/copilotkit/info`. This ticket is the **ops checklist** to put the CopilotKit license and auth flags in Vercel so threads and chat work in production.

**Who does what**

| Role | Job |
| -- | -- |
| **Engineer** | Add `COPILOTKIT_LICENSE_TOKEN` (+ related vars) to the Vercel `ipix-operator` project via Infisical |
| **Operator (you)** | Sign in → open any `/app/*` page → confirm zero CopilotKit errors in DevTools |

**Blocked by:** [IPI-125](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-125) — Google login + Supabase redirect URLs must work first

**Unblocks:** Saved chat threads, production agent smoke, operator demos

**Skills:** `copilotkit` → `references/ipix-production.md` · `infisical`

**MVP priority:** P0 · **Estimate:** 1 point

---

### What “done” looks like (no jargon)

1. You can sign in with Google on production.
2. You land on `/app` (or any child route like `/app/shoots`).
3. Browser console shows **no** `401` / `Failed to fetch` on `/api/copilotkit/info`.
4. The right-hand AI panel opens; you can send one test message and get a streamed reply.
5. (Optional, license on) Left “Threads” drawer is unlocked — not the “licensed feature” placeholder.

**Prod smoke URL (2026-06-25):** [https://www.ipix.co/app/shoots](<https://www.ipix.co/app/shoots>) — signed-in visit loads the Shoots placeholder with **no CopilotKit console errors** ✅

---

### Flow

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  subgraph Vercel["Vercel ipix-operator"]
    L[COPILOTKIT_LICENSE_TOKEN]
    A[OPERATOR_AUTH_ENABLED]
    S[Supabase public keys]
  end
  subgraph Browser
    U[Signed-in operator]
    P["/app/shoots"]
    API["/api/copilotkit/info"]
  end
  L --> API
  A --> P
  S --> U
  U --> P --> API
```

---

### Completion steps

#### A. Vercel env (Infisical → `ipix-operator`)

- [ ] **A1** `COPILOTKIT_LICENSE_TOKEN` — from CopilotKit Cloud / Clerk subscription
- [ ] **A2** `INTELLIGENCE_API_KEY` — required when license token is set (see `app/src/app/api/copilotkit/[[...slug]]/route.ts`)
- [ ] **A3** `OPERATOR_AUTH_ENABLED=true` in production (middleware gates `/app/*`)
- [ ] **A4** `GEMINI_API_KEY`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` already set

#### B. Smoke (production)

- [X] **B1** [https://www.ipix.co/app/shoots](<https://www.ipix.co/app/shoots>) — page loads, no CopilotKit console errors when signed in
- [ ] **B2** `GET /api/copilotkit/info` → `200` (Network tab, while signed in)
- [ ] **B3** Send one chat message → SSE stream completes
- [ ] **B4** Threads drawer unlocked (only if license token present)

#### C. Docs

- [ ] **C1** `app/.env.example` comments match production checklist
- [ ] **C2** `copilotkit` skill `references/ipix-production.md` kept in sync

---

### Quick debug

| Symptom | Likely cause | Fix |
| -- | -- | -- |
| `401` on `/api/copilotkit/info` | Not signed in, or session cookie missing | IPI-125 OAuth + Supabase Site URL |
| “Licensed feature” on Threads | No `COPILOTKIT_LICENSE_TOKEN` on Vercel | A1 |
| Runtime throws on boot | License set but no `INTELLIGENCE_API_KEY` | A2 |
| CopilotKit mounts before redirect | Unauthenticated hit on `/app/*` | Expected fix in `(operator)/layout.tsx` — panel skipped until authed |

## Purpose

Extend dev/QA seed data so the Booking Wizard can be tested end-to-end with real talent profiles, valid org memberships, availability, and booking request writes.

## Current state

[IPI-451](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-451/be-sd1-database-seed-data-for-developmenttesting) (BE-SD1) shipped `supabase/seed.sql` with deterministic UUIDs for orgs, brands, CRM — but it contains **0 talent profiles, 0 availability rows, 0 bookings**. The Booking Wizard has **22 Vitest unit tests** (component level) but **zero Playwright E2E tests**.

## Deliverables

- [ ] talent profile seed data (2+ profiles, mixed rate tiers, verified/different verification_status)

Booking approval triggers Planner ([IPI-476](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-476/planner-schema-and-reusable-engine-core)+):

This E2E seed data should include enough entities (talent → booking → planner path) so the Planner integration E2E can reuse these seeds without duplication. Coordinate seed UUIDs with [IPI-476](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-476/planner-schema-and-reusable-engine-core).


Title: BE-DB · Fix migration ordering bug blocking `supabase db reset`
Description:
## Forensic Findings

### Root Cause

A migration-ordering conflict between two PRs merged on the same day:

| PR | Issue | Merged | Migration | Timestamp |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| [#72](<https://github.com/amo100/ipix/pull/72>) | [IPI-26](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-26/ipi-bi-003-brand-intelligence-supabase-schema-v2) / IPI-BI-003 | 2026-06-25T09:26Z | `20260626000000_brand_intelligence_v2_foundation.sql` | `20260626` |
| [#83](<https://github.com/amo100/ipix/pull/83>) | [IPI-123](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-123/dash-003-d1-brand-intelligence-report-view) DASH-003 AC5 | 2026-06-25T19:15Z | `20260625000001_add_ai_profile_draft.sql` | `20260625000001` |

PR #72 was merged **first** (10h earlier) and creates `CREATE TYPE public.brand_intake_status AS ENUM(...)` with timestamp `20260626`.

PR #83 was merged **second** and contains `ALTER TYPE public.brand_intake_status ADD VALUE IF NOT EXISTS 'draft_ready'` with timestamp `20260625000001`.

Since `20260625000001` < `20260626000000`, **chronological timestamp order is the reverse of merge order**. When Supabase replays migrations in timestamp order (`supabase db reset`), the ALTER TYPE runs before the CREATE TYPE, producing:

```
ERROR: type "public.brand_intake_status" does not exist (SQLSTATE 42704)
```

### Why Remote Works

On the remote database, migrations are applied in merge order (each PR's migrations in sequence). By the time PR #83 ran, the type already existed from PR #72. Remote deployment is unaffected.

### Why Local Breaks

`supabase db reset` wipes the database and replays all migrations sorted by filename/timestamp. Since `20260625000001` < `20260626000000`, the ALTER runs against a database where the type has not yet been created.

### Migration Audit

* **2 files** reference `brand_intake_status` / `intake_status`:
  * `20260625000001_add_ai_profile_draft.sql` — `ALTER TYPE ... ADD VALUE 'draft_ready'` (4 lines)
  * `20260626000000_brand_intelligence_v2_foundation.sql` — `CREATE TYPE ... AS ENUM` + `alter column intake_status type ... USING` (94 lines)
* **No other migrations** create, rename, or alter the type or column.
* **89 references** in app code to `intake_status` / `brand_intake_status` (types, server actions, workflows, tests) — all treat it as a string.
* **GitHub**: no open PR or issue mentions this bug. Verified #265 ([IPI-451](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-451/be-sd1-database-seed-data-for-developmenttesting) seed data) is unrelated.
* **Linear**: no existing issue for this bug.

### Discoverability

This was found while investigating [IPI-451](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-451/be-sd1-database-seed-data-for-developmenttesting) / PR #265 (seed/test users), which depends on `supabase db reset` to work reliably.

---

## Recommended Fix: Option A (In-place)

Merge the `CREATE TYPE IF NOT EXISTS` block into `20260625000001_add_ai_profile_draft.sql` **before** the `ALTER TYPE`.

**File:** `supabase/migrations/20260625000001_add_ai_profile_draft.sql`

```sql
-- IPI-123 DASH-003 AC5: draft column + enum value for HITL re-analyze workflow
-- Stores pending AI profile until operator confirms; NULL = no pending draft

-- Ensure enum type exists before ALTER (ordering fix: PR #72's CREATE TYPE
-- has timestamp 20260626 but this migration runs first on db reset)
do $$
begin
  if not exists (select 1 from pg_type where typname = 'brand_intake_status') then
    create type public.brand_intake_status as enum (
      'brand_created',
      'crawl_running',
      'crawl_complete',
      'analysis_running',
      'scores_complete',
      'ready',
      'failed'
    );
  end if;
end $$;

ALTER TYPE public.brand_intake_status ADD VALUE IF NOT EXISTS 'draft_ready';
ALTER TABLE public.brands ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS ai_profile_draft JSONB;
```

**Why Option A is safer than Option B (renumbering):**

| Factor | Option A (in-place CREATE) | Option B (rename timestamp) |
| -- | -- | -- |
| **Remote safety** | No-op (type already exists, IF NOT EXISTS guard) | Migration hash changes — Supabase CLI sees a new migration |
| **DB reset fix** | ✅ Fixes the ordering | ✅ Fixes the ordering |
| **Migration repair needed** | None | Likely needed (`supabase migration repair --status applied <old>`) |
| **Downstream migration impact** | None (type already exists for v2 foundation) | Would need to ensure all downstream migrations still reference correct timestamp |
| **Risk of merge conflict** | Very low (single file, additive change) | High (renaming files breaks git history tracking) |

### Option A — No Risk to Existing Deployments

* The modified migration is already applied on remote — Supabase will not re-execute it.
* `CREATE TYPE IF NOT EXISTS` is a no-op on databases where the type already exists.
* All existing tests, workflows, and app code are unaffected.
* The `draft_ready` enum value continues to be added by the `ALTER TYPE` on the next line.

---

## Risk Assessment

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
| -- | -- | -- | -- |
| Re-creating type with missing values | Low | Medium | Enum values in CREATE match v2 foundation exactly |
| Migration hash mismatch on remote | None | N/A | File renamed? No. Timestamp changed? No. |
| CI / pre-push hook failure | Low | Low | Only affects `supabase db reset`, not `cd app && npm test` |
| Other developers blocked by this bug | **Already happening** | High | Any dev running `supabase db reset` hits this |

## Production Impact

**None.** Remote database is not affected. PR #72 was applied before PR #83 on remote. This bug only manifests in local development environments during `supabase db reset`.

## Confidence Score

**98%** — Root cause is fully confirmed by migration file contents, timestamps, merge order, git history, and PR metadata. The fix is a 10-line additive change with zero side effects.

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [X] AC1: Root cause identified and documented
- [ ] AC2: Fix implemented (in-place CREATE TYPE in `20260625000001_add_ai_profile_draft.sql`)
- [ ] AC3: `npx supabase db reset --linked` completes without SQLSTATE 42704
- [ ] AC4: All migrations execute in timestamp order without error
- [ ] AC5: Remote database unaffected (confirm type exists: `\dT public.brand_intake_status`)
- [ ] AC6: No partially applied schema state
- [ ] AC7: Test suite still passes: `cd app && npm test`

## Verification Commands

```bash
# After fix:
npx supabase db reset --linked

# Verify type exists and has all 8 values:
psql "$SUPABASE_DB_URL" -c "\dT+ public.brand_intake_status"
psql "$SUPABASE_DB_URL" -c "SELECT unnest(enum_range(NULL::public.brand_intake_status));"

# Run app tests to confirm no regression:
cd app && npm test
```

## Rollback Plan

1. Revert the edit to `20260625000001_add_ai_profile_draft.sql`
2. No migration repair needed (file was not renamed)
3. No remote impact (migration was already applied)
4. Alternative: if deeper issues emerge, use Option B (rename to `20260626000001`)

## Dependencies

* [IPI-26](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-26/ipi-bi-003-brand-intelligence-supabase-schema-v2) / IPI-BI-003 (PR #72) — already merged, enum schema
* [IPI-123](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-123/dash-003-d1-brand-intelligence-report-view) DASH-003 AC5 (PR #83) — already merged, draft feature

## Blocking Tasks

* Blocks clean `supabase db reset` for [IPI-451](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-451/be-sd1-database-seed-data-for-developmenttesting) (PR #265) seed data verification
* Blocks any developer needing a fresh local DB

## Related PRs/Issues

* PR #72: `[IPI-26] IPI-BI-003 — Brand intelligence v2 schema` (merged 2026-06-25)
* PR #83: `feat(brand-hub): IPI-123 DASH-003 AC5 — re-analyze draft + HITL hook` (merged 2026-06-25)
* [IPI-451](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-451/be-sd1-database-seed-data-for-developmenttesting): Seed/Test Users (depends on clean `db reset`)
* PR #265: [IPI-451](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-451/be-sd1-database-seed-data-for-developmenttesting) fix (discovered during work on this)

## Estimated Complexity

**XS** — One file, one additive change, 10 lines, no renaming, no migration repair.

## Priority

**High** — Blocks local development for anyone running `supabase db reset`.

Title: SCR-31 · CRM Deal Detail — React Parity
Description:
## SCR-31 — CRM Deal Detail React Parity

**Priority:** P1 · **Dependencies:** [IPI-395](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-395/scr-30-crm-pipeline-react-parity) (SCR-30 Pipeline) — pipeline should be functional first; [IPI-244](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-244/intel-002-approvalqueue-hitl-write-actions-in-intelligencepanel) (ApprovalCard component)

**SSOT:** `Universal-design-prompt-new/tasks/screens/SCR-31-crm-deal-detail.md`

**Route:** `/app/crm/pipeline/[id]`

**Design source:** `Pages/SCR-31-CRM-Deal-Detail.dc.html`

**Current state:** 30-line stub with id/org validation + CrmScreenGate

---

## Overview

Deal detail with stage selector, activity timeline, health score, approval gate for won/lost conversion, and brand-convert handoff path. Won/lost transition is gated behind an **ApprovalCard** — this is the only path that can set terminal stages.

## Purpose

Operator views and advances a deal through stages, sees activity history and health, and triggers won/lost conversion with HITL approval.

## User journey

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  A[Pipeline kanban] -->|click deal card| B[Deal detail /app/crm/pipeline/[id]]
  B --> C[Header: deal name, value, company, stage chip]
  B --> D[Stage selector: 6 buttons, Lead→Lost]
  D --> E[Lead→Negotiation: immediate stage change]
  D --> F[Won/Lost: → ApprovalCard gate]
  F --> G[ApprovalCard: confirm with reason]
  G --> H[Won banner + brand convert link]
  G --> I[Lost: closed-lost state]
  B --> J[Activity timeline: deal events, AI summary, calls, emails]
  B --> K[IntelligencePanel: health score factors]
  B --> L[Chat dock: crm-assistant context]
```

## Component reuse verification

| Component | Usage | Reuse from |
| -- | -- | -- |
| StatusChip | Deal stage chip | RF-01 |
| ApprovalCard | Won/Lost gate confirmation | Existing component ([IPI-244](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-244/intel-002-approvalqueue-hitl-write-actions-in-intelligencepanel)) |
| EmptyState | No activity | RF-A7b |
| ErrorState | Error with retry | RF-A7b |
| IntelligencePanel | Health factors, NBA, drafted follow-up | Existing shell |
| N/A | NO new stage engine | Shared with pipeline (SCR-30) |
| N/A | NO custom timeline | Unified `crm_activities` pattern |

## Data source table

| Section | Source | Status |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Deal header | `crm_deals` by id | 🟢 live |
| Company name | `crm_companies` join | 🟢 live |
| Primary contact | `crm_contacts` join via deal | 🟢 verify deal→contact FK |
| Value | `deals.value` | 🟢 live |
| Linked shoot | `deals.shoot_id` → `shoot.shoots` | 🟢 verify FK exists |
| Stage | `deals.stage` | 🟢 live |
| Activity timeline | `crm_activities` by deal_id | 🟢 live |
| Health factors | crm-assistant or derived | 🟡 verify agent |
| Brand conversion | Won deal → `brands` create/link | 🟡 verify brand pathway |
| Intel panel | crm-assistant | 🟢 wired |

## DC grid spec

| Property | DC value |
| -- | -- |
| Shell | `56px | minmax(0,1fr) | 320px` |
| Breadcrumb | Pipeline > {company} — {deal name} |
| Header | h1 22px/700 for deal name, mono 18px/600 for value, stage chip |
| Stage buttons | 6 pill buttons, colored by stage, won/lost=gated style |
| Overview grid | 2×2, 4 fields: Company, Contact, Value, Linked shoot |
| Activity timeline | Same unified pattern: icon circle + line + content |
| Won banner | green bg, check-circle icon, "Converted to brand" + brand link |
| Lost banner | red bg, alert |
| Intel panel | Health score bar chart, ApprovalCard for drafted follow-up |

## Acceptance criteria

| # | Criterion | Proof |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 1 | Deal loads by id — header shows name, value, company, stage chip | browser |
| 2 | Breadcrumb: Pipeline > {company} — {name} | click → list |
| 3 | Stage selector: 6 buttons, clicking Lead→Negotiation changes stage immediately | click updates |
| 4 | Won/Lost buttons trigger ApprovalCard overlay | click → confirmation |
| 5 | ApprovalCard: Cancel reverts, Approve transitions stage | both paths tested |
| 6 | Won state: green banner with "Converted to brand" + link | visual match to DC |
| 7 | Lost state: closed-lost indication | visual match |
| 8 | Overview grid: Company (↗ link), Contact (↗ link), Value (£), Linked shoot (↗ link) | all navigable |
| 9 | Activity timeline: AI summary, proposal sent, scoping call, deal created | visual match to DC |
| 10 | Health factors in IntelligencePanel: 3-bar chart (recency, velocity, engagement) | panel updates |
| 11 | ApprovalCard in Intel panel: AI-drafted follow-up with Edit/Approve | matches DC |
| 12 | Loading: skeleton | visible during fetch |
| 13 | Empty activity: honest empty state | no fake data |
| 14 | Error: ErrorState with retry | mock failure |
| 15 | Not found: invalid id → 404 | notFound() |
| 16 | tokens.css only — no legacy hex | grep check |
| 17 | CrmScreenGate removed | component replaced |

## Mobile & a11y

| # | Requirement |
| -- | -- |
| 1 | Stage selector wraps on mobile, buttons >=44px |
| 2 | Timeline keyboard-navigable |
| 3 | ApprovalCard focus-traps on open (Esc closes) |
| 4 | Stage/risk never color-only — text label present |
| 5 | 390px: stacked layout, no overflow |

## AI boundaries

| Area | Status |
| -- | -- |
| crm-assistant | 🟢 route context |
| Health score | 🟡 may use crm-assistant or derived |
| ApprovalCard | 🟢 existing component, reads agent decision log |
| Chat dock | 🟡 placeholder |
| AI writes | 🔴 none — won/lost gated, draft message is HITL |
| Stage transitions | 🟢 immediate for non-terminal, ApprovalCard for terminal |

## Approval gate rules (from DC, CRM-HANDOFF.md)

1. Clicking Won/Lost opens an ApprovalCard, does NOT immediately transition
2. ApprovalCard shows: current stage → target stage, deal summary, reason
3. "Cancel" closes the card. "Approve" transitions and (for Won) triggers brand-convert
4. Won deals show conversion banner + link to Brand Detail
5. No drag, button, or agent may bypass the approval gate (hard rule from CRM-HANDOFF.md §3)

## Test plan

```bash
cd app && npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm test && CI=true npm run build
```

Browser: deal renders · stage selector works · won/lost approval gate · conversion banner · activity timeline · 390px pass · no console errors

## Out of scope

* Creating/editing deals inline
* Won→brand conversion automation (API exists but full flow is follow-up)
* Reports/analytics
* Email/calendar sync

### Scope — screens in this pass

**Out of scope:** Tasks hub, Communications, Calendar, Documents, Analytics, Relationship Graph — see `00-design-audit.md`.

Title: CRM-UX-001 · Companies list + detail screens
Description:
**In plain terms:** Operator can list, filter, search, create, and open companies (prospects and already-converted brands together) — no AI yet.

**Blocked by:** CRM-DATA-001 (IPI-362) · **Unblocks:** CRM-AI-002

## User story

As a **brand operator**, I want to see every company I'm building a relationship with — prospect or already a brand — in one searchable list, so I don't lose track of who I'm talking to.

## User journey

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  A[Operator opens /app/crm/companies] --> B[Filter/search list]
  B --> C[Click a company card]
  C --> D[Company Detail: Overview/Contacts/Deals/Activity tabs]
  D --> E{Already a brand?}
  E -->|yes| F[Link to existing Brand Detail]
  E -->|no| G["Prospect — no convert button here, that's Deal Detail's job"]
```

## Wireframe (ASCII — superseded by the real Claude Design prototypes)

Real DC HTML now exists: `Universal design prompt/crm/SCR-26-CRM-Companies-List.dc.html` (list) and `SCR-27-CRM-Company-Detail.dc.html` (detail). Use those as the visual source of truth, not this ASCII sketch.

```
┌────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────┐
│ NavSidebar │ Companies                     [+New] │ IntelligencePanel│
│  · CRM     │ [Status ▾][Owner ▾][Industry ▾] 🔍   │  (selected co.)  │
│    Compan. │ ┌─────────┐ ┌─────────┐ ┌─────────┐  │  quick facts     │
│    Contact │ │ Acme Co.│ │ Nova Inc│ │ Zed LLC │  │  EvidenceBlock   │
│    Pipeline│ │ prospect│ │ active  │ │ inactive│  │  [Open][Log act.]│
│            │ └─────────┘ └─────────┘ └─────────┘  │                  │
│            │  ── PersistentChatDock ────────────▼ │                  │
└────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────┘
```

## Phase 0 — production state (verified 2026-07-05 against `origin/main`)

| Area | Exists today? | This issue changes? |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Route `/app/crm/companies` | ✅ merged, renders `<CrmScreenGate screen="Companies" />` | Replace gate with real workspace |
| Route `/app/crm/companies/[id]` | ✅ merged, same gate | Replace gate with real workspace |
| Shell (`CrmLayout` → `CrmRecordContext`) | ✅ merged | No change |
| `listCompanies` | ✅ merged (`app/src/lib/crm/queries.ts`, PR #215) | Reuse as-is |
| `getCompany(id)` | 🔴 does not exist | **Build this issue** — join contacts/deals/activities by `company_id` |
| CopilotKit context | ✅ `CrmRecordContext` already injects `companyId` on detail routes | No change needed |

## AI Integration Matrix (per `tasks/design-docs/copilotkit-mastra.md` §12)

```text
CopilotKit
- [x] Headless UI hooks used: CrmRecordContext (shared, already wired)
- [ ] Frontend tools / Display components: none page-specific

Mastra
- [x] Agent: crm-assistant (existing, wave 1) — searchCompanies already covers "find a company" from chat
- [ ] Workflow / Tools / Memory: none new for this issue
```

No new AI wiring in this issue — this is screen visuals only.

## Implementation Steps

**Full plan:** `docs/plan/tasks/2026-07-04-crm-companies-screens.md` (data-layer tasks already shipped) · `tasks/crm/tasks/02-crm-design-to-react-conversion-plan.md` (screen conversion plan, current).

### Task 0 — `getCompany(id)`

* Add to `app/src/lib/crm/queries.ts` — joins contacts/deals/activities by `company_id`
* Test: `app/src/lib/crm/queries.test.ts` — mocked Supabase client
* TDD loop: write failing test → confirm fail → implement → confirm pass → `npm run typecheck`

### Task 1 — Companies list, replacing `<CrmScreenGate>`

* `listCompanies` already merged — wire it into `app/src/app/(operator)/app/crm/companies/page.tsx`
* Filter (status/owner/industry) + search (name/domain) UI matching `SCR-26-CRM-Companies-List.dc.html`

### Task 2 — `CompanyCard` component

* Create `app/src/components/crm/company-card.tsx` — **no** `brand-hub/brand-list-card.tsx` **file exists in this codebase to copy** (Brand's list renders rows inline in `brand/page.tsx`, not via an extracted card component) — build a small local card matching the DC file directly, use shadcn `Badge` (not `StatusChip` — doesn't exist)
* Test: `app/src/components/crm/company-card.test.tsx` — renders name + status badge, fires `onOpen`

### Task 3 — Company Detail, replacing `<CrmScreenGate>`

* `app/src/app/(operator)/app/crm/companies/[id]/page.tsx` + `app/src/components/crm/company-detail-tabs.tsx` — shadcn `Tabs` (Overview/Contacts/Deals/Activity)
* `brand_id` renders as a Brand Detail link only when non-null (test both states)
* **Activity tab imports the shared** `ActivityTimeline` **component built in IPI-366 — don't rebuild it here**

### Task 4 — Nav entry

* Modify `app/src/components/operator-panel/nav-sidebar.tsx` — one new CRM item, following the existing item shape. Do not rebuild the nav component.

## Description

`/app/crm/companies` (list) and `/app/crm/companies/:id` (detail, tabbed: Overview/Contacts/Deals/Activity). **Routes already exist on** `main`**, gated behind** `<CrmScreenGate>` — this issue replaces the gate, not a greenfield build. Design source of truth: `Universal design prompt/crm/SCR-26-CRM-Companies-List.dc.html`, `SCR-27-CRM-Company-Detail.dc.html` (the old `tasks/crm/design/02a`/`02b` prompt docs are superseded). Reuses `OperatorShell`/`IntelligencePanel`/`PersistentChatDock` as-is. No `PageHeader`/`FilterBar`/`SearchBar`/`StatusChip` exist — confirmed by grep — use shadcn `Badge`/inline `Select`/`Input` per IPI-373 AC A3.

Company Detail's Activity tab **is** the communications timeline (`crm_activities`, no separate Communications screen). No "convert to brand" button here — conversion only happens from Deal Detail (CRM-AI-001).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Companies list: filter by status/owner/industry, search by name/domain, `[New Company]` inline form
- [ ] Company Detail: Overview/Contacts/Deals/Activity tabs render real data
- [ ] `brand_id` shown as a link to existing Brand Detail only when populated
- [ ] Empty/loading/error states match `SCR-26`/`SCR-27` DC files
- [ ] No `PageHeader`/`FilterBar`/`SearchBar`/`StatusChip` imports (IPI-373 AC A3)

## Implementation notes

* New nav entry: one `NavSidebar` item for CRM
* Non-goals: CRM Dashboard landing page, Documents tab (stub only, "Coming later")
* **Branch naming:** use `ipi/363-crm-companies-screens`, not Linear's auto-suggested `ai/` prefix

## Verify

- [ ] `cd app && npm run typecheck && npm test`
- [ ] `npm run build` — confirm the gate is replaced, no dead route
- [ ] Manual: create a company, verify RLS blocks a second org from seeing it

Title: BE-CRM-OPT · CRM convenience RPCs (optional P3)
Description:
## BE-CRM-OPT — CRM convenience RPCs (optional P3)

**Track:** Backend · **Priority:** P3 · **Complexity:** M · **SSOT:** `tasks/backend/BE-CRM-opt-convenience-rpcs.md`
**Branch:** `ipi/crm-opt-convenience-rpcs`

## Objective

Optional RPCs where direct table access or Mastra tools are insufficient. **CRM MVP does not require these** — `crm-assistant` tools + RLS table access cover CRM1–5.

## Gap analysis

| Expected RPC | Live | Alternative today |
| -- | -- | -- |
| `quick_add_crm` | 🔴 | Mastra `log-activity` + direct insert |
| `search_crm` | 🔴 | `search-companies` / `search-contacts` tools |
| `crm_timeline` | 🔴 | Query `crm_activities` with RLS |
| `convert_deal` | 🔴 | Trigger fn `crm_deals_verify_convert_stage` exists; no public RPC |

## Proposed RPCs (pick subset per PR)

* **quick_add_crm** — atomic multi-table insert (company/contact/deal stub)
* **search_crm** — unified full-text search across companies + contacts + deals
* **crm_timeline** — paginated `crm_activities` with actor enrichment
* **convert_deal** — terminal-stage + brand creation with HITL audit trail

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Each RPC org-scoped via `is_org_member`/editor checks
- [ ] `convert_deal` respects terminal-stage trigger — no bypass
- [ ] Revoke public execute where service-role-only
- [ ] `supabase:verify-rls` green
- [ ] One RPC group per PR if >200 LOC

## Skills

`ipix-supabase` · `mastra` · `task-verifier`

## Note

**Defer until CRM1–4 UI ships** unless Quick-Add UX requires atomic multi-table insert.

Title: INFRA-001 — Cloudflare Worker Deployment Pipeline
Description:
# [IPI-472](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-472/infra-001-cloudflare-worker-deployment-pipeline) · INFRA-001 — Cloudflare Worker Deployment Pipeline

## Purpose

Create a safe, repeatable deployment path for the iPix Cloudflare platform so engineers can preview changes, promote them to production, observe failures, and roll back without risking the live app.

This task covers the deployment pipeline for both:

1. `app/` — Next.js 16 operator + marketing app deployed with OpenNext on Cloudflare Workers.
2. `services/cloudflare-worker/` — AI Gateway Worker used by [IPI-454](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-454/ai-gateway-cloudflare-provider-routing) · CF-AI-001 — AI Gateway — Cloudflare provider routing.

The old statement that this work must not affect a Vercel-hosted Next.js app is superseded by the approved Vercel → Workers migration plan. Vercel remains production only until the Cloudflare preview smoke gate passes.

## Plain-English user story

> As an iPix engineer, when I merge a change, I want Cloudflare to build the correct project, create a safe preview, run verification, and only promote a tested version to production so a bad deployment can be detected and rolled back quickly.

## Real-world journeys

### Journey A — Next.js application change

1. Engineer opens a PR changing `app/`.
2. CI runs lint, typecheck, tests, normal Next build, and OpenNext build.
3. Cloudflare builds from root directory `app/`.
4. A preview URL is created for smoke testing.
5. CF-MIG-220 verifies login, operator shell, CopilotKit streaming, Mastra workflows, Cloudinary, and critical APIs.
6. Only a green version is promoted to production.
7. If production fails, operations roll back to the last known-good Worker version.

### Journey B — AI Gateway change

1. Engineer changes `services/cloudflare-worker/`.
2. Worker unit/integration tests run.
3. `wrangler deploy --dry-run` or equivalent upload verification passes.
4. The Worker is deployed with runtime secrets stored in Cloudflare, not committed to Git.
5. Health and routing checks verify the gateway before Mastra traffic is switched.
6. If the release fails, the previous Worker version is restored.

## Deployment architecture

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  GH[GitHub PR / merge] --> CI[CI verification]
  CI --> APP{Changed path}

  APP -->|app/**| ON[OpenNext build]
  ON --> PW[Preview Worker version]
  PW --> SM[CF-MIG-220 smoke gate]
  SM -->|green| PROD1[ipix-operator production deployment]

  APP -->|services/cloudflare-worker/**| WT[Worker tests + Wrangler verification]
  WT --> GWPRE[AI Gateway preview/version]
  GWPRE --> HEALTH[Health + routing checks]
  HEALTH -->|green| PROD2[AI Gateway production deployment]

  PROD1 --> OBS[Logs / metrics / traces]
  PROD2 --> OBS
  OBS -->|failure| RB[Rollback to known-good version]
```

## Scope

### A. Next.js / OpenNext deployment

* Cloudflare Workers Builds or approved CI path for `app/`.
* Build root set to `app/` for the monorepo.
* `opennextjs-cloudflare build` verification on relevant PRs.
* Preview/version deployment before production promotion.
* Build variables and secrets separated from runtime variables and secrets.
* Custom domain and DNS cutover only after CF-MIG-220 is green.

### B. AI Gateway Worker deployment

* Test and deploy `services/cloudflare-worker/` independently.
* Keep gateway deployment separate from the Next.js Worker.
* Configure runtime secrets for provider credentials and Cloudflare account configuration.
* Document health checks and routing verification.

### C. Environments and secrets

* Separate preview/staging/prod behavior using Cloudflare versions, preview URLs, bindings, and secrets.
* Do not commit secrets.
* Document which variables are required at build time versus runtime.
* Use Cloudflare runtime Secrets for production credentials.

### D. Rollback and observability

* Document Worker rollback procedure.
* Record the known-good production version before promotion.
* Enable production observability for errors, latency, and deployment failures.
* Note that Worker rollback does not roll back external data or database schema changes; database migrations need their own compatibility/rollback plan.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `app/` CI runs lint, typecheck, tests, `next build`, and `opennextjs-cloudflare build`.
- [ ] `services/cloudflare-worker/` tests pass.
- [ ] AI Gateway Worker deploy verification passes before promotion.
- [ ] Cloudflare project/build root is explicitly configured for `app/` in the monorepo.
- [ ] Preview URLs or uploaded Worker versions are used to test changes before production promotion.
- [ ] Preview access policy is documented: public preview exceptions are explicit; sensitive previews can be protected with Cloudflare Access.
- [ ] Build variables/secrets and runtime variables/secrets are documented separately.
- [ ] Production credentials are stored as Cloudflare Secrets and are never committed.
- [ ] Worker names in Cloudflare match the `name` values in the relevant Wrangler configuration.
- [ ] Custom domain and route ownership are documented.
- [ ] Rollback command/runbook is tested and documented.
- [ ] Observability is enabled for production Workers.
- [ ] Deployment pipeline supports the Vercel → Workers migration and no longer assumes Vercel is the permanent Next.js host.

## Verification checklist

```bash
# Next.js Worker
cd app
npm run lint
npm run typecheck
npm test
CI=true npm run build
CI=true npx opennextjs-cloudflare build

# AI Gateway Worker
cd ../services/cloudflare-worker
npm test
npx wrangler deploy --dry-run
```

## Dependencies / relations

* [IPI-454](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-454/ai-gateway-cloudflare-provider-routing) · CF-AI-001 — AI Gateway — Cloudflare provider routing
* [IPI-468](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-468/sec-001-cloudflare-ai-security-architecture) · SEC-001 — Cloudflare AI Security Architecture
* CF-MIG-110 · OpenNext foundation
* CF-MIG-111 · CI OpenNext build
* CF-MIG-220 · Preview smoke gate
* CF-MIG-810 · Production cutover and rollback

* [https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/ci-cd/builds/configuration/](<https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/ci-cd/builds/configuration/>)
* [https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/versions-and-deployments/preview-urls/](<https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/versions-and-deployments/preview-urls/>)
* [https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/versions-and-deployments/rollbacks/](<https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/versions-and-deployments/rollbacks/>)
* [https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/configuration/secrets/](<https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/configuration/secrets/>)

Title: SEC-001 — Cloudflare AI Security Architecture
Description:
# [IPI-468](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-468/sec-001-cloudflare-ai-security-architecture) · SEC-001 — Cloudflare AI Security Architecture

## Purpose

Define the security model for the iPix Cloudflare AI platform so public users can safely access intended features, operators can authenticate normally, internal Workers can communicate privately, model credentials remain protected, and agent actions stay inside approved permissions.

The goal is **not** to put Cloudflare Access in front of every endpoint. The goal is to apply the right control at each boundary:

* Public marketing/API routes → explicit public exceptions + rate limiting/WAF where appropriate.
* Operator routes → Supabase Auth + org-scoped authorization/RLS.
* Worker-to-Worker calls on the same Cloudflare account → prefer Service Bindings where practical.
* Protected HTTP services / automation crossing service boundaries → Cloudflare Access service tokens or equivalent authenticated service credentials.
* AI Gateway → authenticated gateway/token or binding-based access.
* Secrets → Cloudflare runtime Secrets; build-only secrets separated from runtime credentials.

## Plain-English user story

> As an iPix operator or customer, I want the AI platform to protect my account, brand data, provider keys, and sensitive workflows while still allowing public marketing experiences to work without unnecessary login friction.

## Real-world journeys

### Journey A — Public visitor uses marketing chat

1. Visitor opens an iPix service page.
2. Public marketing chat endpoint accepts the request without operator login.
3. WAF/rate-limit controls protect the endpoint from abuse.
4. The request is routed to the approved AI path.
5. Logs exclude unnecessary PII and secrets.
6. The visitor receives a streamed response.

### Journey B — Operator uses CopilotKit

1. Operator authenticates through Supabase Auth.
2. Next.js Worker resolves the authenticated user and organization.
3. Supabase RLS limits database access to allowed tenant/org rows.
4. Mastra invokes only tools permitted for that agent and user context.
5. High-risk write/admin actions require human approval before execution.
6. Audit metadata records the action without leaking credentials or sensitive payloads.

### Journey C — Next Worker calls AI Gateway Worker

1. `ipix-operator` needs model inference.
2. Prefer a Cloudflare Service Binding for same-account internal Worker communication where the architecture supports it; otherwise use an authenticated HTTP boundary.
3. AI Gateway validates the request boundary.
4. Provider credentials remain server-side and are read from Cloudflare Secrets.
5. Gateway routes to Workers AI or approved fallback provider.
6. Logs/analytics avoid storing sensitive prompt content beyond approved policy.

## Security architecture

```mermaid
flowchart TB
  PUB[Public visitor] --> EDGE[Cloudflare edge]
  OP[Authenticated operator] --> EDGE

  EDGE --> WAF[WAF + rate limiting]
  WAF --> NEXT[Next.js OpenNext Worker]

  NEXT --> AUTH[Supabase Auth context]
  AUTH --> RLS[Supabase RLS / org scope]

  NEXT --> MAS[Mastra agents + tool permissions]
  MAS --> HITL{Risky write/admin action?}
  HITL -->|yes| APPROVAL[Human approval]
  HITL -->|no| TOOL[Approved tool call]
  APPROVAL --> TOOL

  MAS --> GW[AI Gateway Worker]
  GW --> WAI[Workers AI]
  GW --> GEM[Gemini fallback]

  SEC[Cloudflare Secrets] --> NEXT
  SEC --> GW
  GW --> LOG[AI Gateway / Worker observability]
  NEXT --> LOG
  LOG --> REDACT[PII + secret logging policy]
```

## Control matrix

| Boundary | Recommended control | Notes |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Public marketing pages | Public | No Access login wall |
| Public marketing AI/API routes | WAF + endpoint rate limits | Document explicit public exceptions |
| `/app/*` operator app | Supabase Auth + app authorization | Preserve current product login flow |
| Supabase data | RLS + org-scoped policies | Service role is server-only and exceptional |
| Worker → Worker, same CF account | Service Binding preferred | Avoid unnecessary public URL hop where practical |
| External automation → protected service | Access service token / scoped service auth | Use least privilege and rotation |
| AI Gateway | Authenticated Gateway or pre-authenticated binding | Never expose provider secrets to clients |
| Provider API keys | Workers Secrets | Not `vars`, not source control |
| Build secrets | Workers Builds build secrets | Not automatically available at runtime |
| Agent tools | Read / write / admin permissions + HITL | Align with [IPI-465](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-465/agent-002-shared-ai-tool-registry) · AGENT-002 — Shared AI Tool Registry |

## Scope

### A. Endpoint classification

Create and maintain a route classification:

* public
* authenticated user
* operator/admin
* internal service
* webhook

Every exposed Worker/API route must have an explicit classification and corresponding auth/rate-limit policy.

### B. Worker-to-Worker authentication

* Prefer Service Bindings for same-account internal Worker calls when suitable.
* Use Access service tokens for protected HTTP applications or automation that cannot use bindings.
* Do not expose the AI Gateway Worker anonymously unless there is a documented public use case.

### C. Secrets and environment separation

* No provider API key in client bundles.
* No service-role key in client code.
* Runtime secrets stored as Cloudflare Secrets.
* Build variables/secrets documented separately from runtime variables/secrets.
* Dev/staging/prod credentials separated.
* Rotation procedure documented for Cloudflare, provider, Supabase, and integration secrets.

### D. Public endpoint abuse protection

* Apply WAF and/or rate limiting to public AI, auth-sensitive, webhook, and expensive routes as appropriate.
* Rate limits should be route-specific; do not use one global threshold blindly.
* Document expected 429/error behavior and client retry behavior.

### E. AI Gateway security

* Enable authenticated access for AI Gateway where used through HTTP.
* Use least-privilege tokens and document their account scope.
* Treat AI Gateway tokens as sensitive because gateway permissions can be account-scoped.
* If using a Worker-side AI Gateway binding, document that calls are pre-authenticated within the Cloudflare account.

### F. Data and tenant isolation

* Supabase RLS remains the primary database tenant boundary.
* Queries and tools must carry validated org/user context.
* Service-role usage requires explicit justification and server-only containment.
* Agent memory and retrieval must preserve org/tenant filters.

### G. Agent permissions and HITL

* Tools classified as `read`, `write`, or `admin`.
* Write/admin tools declare approval rules.
* Destructive, financial, publication, booking-confirmation, and irreversible actions require explicit human approval unless separately approved by policy.
* Permission checks occur server-side, not only in UI controls.

### H. Logging and privacy

* Never log API keys, service tokens, access tokens, cookies, or Authorization headers.
* Define what prompt/response content may be retained.
* Redact or omit sensitive PII from Worker and AI Gateway logs according to policy.
* Keep enough structured metadata for debugging: request ID, org ID where safe, route, provider/model tier, latency, status, retry/fallback outcome.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Endpoint inventory classifies each route as public, authenticated, admin, internal service, or webhook.
- [ ] No API key or service credential is exposed in client bundles.
- [ ] Runtime credentials are stored as Cloudflare Secrets; build-only secrets are documented separately.
- [ ] Public AI/API endpoints have documented WAF/rate-limit policies or explicit rationale for omission.
- [ ] Same-account Worker-to-Worker path is evaluated for Service Bindings before adding public HTTP service-token plumbing.
- [ ] Protected HTTP service boundaries use authenticated service credentials.
- [ ] AI Gateway HTTP access is authenticated, or binding-based pre-authentication is documented.
- [ ] Supabase RLS/org scope is verified for AI tools that access tenant data.
- [ ] Service-role usage is server-only, justified, and documented.
- [ ] Agent tools are classified read/write/admin and risky actions require HITL approval.
- [ ] Logging policy prohibits secrets and defines PII/prompt retention rules.
- [ ] Dev/staging/prod credentials and environment boundaries are documented.
- [ ] Secret rotation and incident-response steps are documented.

## Verification checklist

* Inspect client bundle/env exposure for provider and Supabase privileged keys.
* Verify route/auth matrix against actual `app/src/app/api/**` routes.
* Verify public endpoints return expected behavior without auth and protected endpoints reject unauthenticated access.
* Verify rate-limit rules on selected expensive/public endpoints.
* Verify Supabase RLS tests for org-scoped tables used by AI tools.
* Verify AI Gateway/Worker auth configuration and secret storage.
* Verify HITL paths for risky write/admin tools.
* Verify logs do not contain Authorization headers, API keys, service-role values, or unapproved PII.

## Related tasks

* [IPI-454](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-454/ai-gateway-cloudflare-provider-routing) · CF-AI-001 — AI Gateway — Cloudflare provider routing
* [IPI-465](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-465/agent-002-shared-ai-tool-registry) · AGENT-002 — Shared AI Tool Registry
* [IPI-472](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-472/infra-001-cloudflare-worker-deployment-pipeline) · INFRA-001 — Cloudflare Worker Deployment Pipeline
* [IPI-463](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-463/cf-ai-008-ai-provider-failover-and-rollback) · CF-AI-008 — AI Provider Failover & Rollback

## Official references

* [https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/runtime-apis/bindings/service-bindings/](<https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/runtime-apis/bindings/service-bindings/>)
* [https://developers.cloudflare.com/cloudflare-one/access-controls/service-credentials/service-tokens/](<https://developers.cloudflare.com/cloudflare-one/access-controls/service-credentials/service-tokens/>)
* [https://developers.cloudflare.com/waf/](<https://developers.cloudflare.com/waf/>)
* [https://developers.cloudflare.com/waf/rate-limiting-rules/](<https://developers.cloudflare.com/waf/rate-limiting-rules/>)
* [https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/configuration/secrets/](<https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/configuration/secrets/>)
* [https://developers.cloudflare.com/ai-gateway/configuration/authentication/](<https://developers.cloudflare.com/ai-gateway/configuration/authentication/>)
* [https://developers.cloudflare.com/ai-gateway/](<https://developers.cloudflare.com/ai-gateway/>)
* [https://supabase.com/docs/guides/database/postgres/row-level-security](<https://supabase.com/docs/guides/database/postgres/row-level-security>)

Title: CRM-AI-001 · Won/Lost HITL gate + brand conversion route
Description:
**In plain terms:** The single most safety-critical piece of the CRM module — the only path that may ever mark a deal `won`/`lost`, and the only path that may create/link a `brands` row from a CRM company.

**Blocked by:** CRM-UX-004 (IPI-366) · **Unblocks:** CRM-QA-001 (this issue never actually blocked CRM-AI-002/IPI-368 in Linear — wave-1 agent work is intentionally independent, see the ordering note in IPI-368)

## User story

As a **brand operator**, I want to be the one who says "yes, this deal is won" — and have that turn into a real brand automatically — so nothing important happens without my say-so, and I never have to re-enter data I already captured.

## User journey

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  A[Operator selects Won or Lost on Deal Detail] --> B["ApprovalCard: Before X → After won/lost"]
  B -->|Cancel| C[Revert, no write]
  B -->|Approve| D["POST /api/crm/deals/:id/convert"]
  D --> E[Create or link a brands row]
  E --> F[Link to existing Brand Detail — Brand Intelligence takes over]
```

## Wireframe (ASCII — final fidelity via Claude Design using tasks/crm/design/02f prompt)

```
┌────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────┬──────────────┐
│            │ Deal: Acme Co. — Spring Order               │              │
│            │ ┌─ ApprovalCard ─────────────────────────┐  │  (same panel│
│            │ │ Mark this deal as Won?                 │  │   as 02f)   │
│            │ │ Before: negotiation → After: won        │  │              │
│            │ │ Creates/links a brands record for Acme  │  │              │
│            │ │              [Cancel]     [Approve]     │  │              │
│            │ └─────────────────────────────────────────┘  │              │
└────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────┘
```

## Implementation Steps

**Full plan with complete code:** `docs/plan/tasks/2026-07-04-crm-won-lost-gate.md`.

**⚠️ Depends on IPI-362 Task 2's DB trigger being live** — this route is the only legitimate caller of `set_config('app.crm_convert','1',true)`.

### Task 1 — `api/crm/deals/[id]/convert` route

* Create `app/src/app/api/crm/deals/[id]/convert/route.ts` (POST) — reuses `withOperatorAuth`/`createSupabaseServerClient`
* On `decision: "won"`: create a `brands` row if `crm_companies.brand_id` is null, else link the existing one
* On `decision: "lost"`: set stage, never touch `brands`
* Sets `app.crm_convert` session flag + the stage `UPDATE` in one transaction (ideally via a `SECURITY DEFINER` RPC wrapping both)
* Test: 3 cases — no existing brand / existing brand / lost-never-touches-brands (assert `insert` spy not called)
* Regression test: a direct `PATCH` to the deal (IPI-365's route) still cannot set won/lost

### Task 2 — `ApprovalCard` wiring on Deal Detail

* Modify `app/src/components/crm/deal-stage-control.tsx` (from IPI-366 Task 3) — replace the inert won/lost handler with the real gate
* Reuse the existing `intel-approval-card.tsx`/`applyDraft` pattern — not a new confirm modal
* Test: failed response reverts to prior stage + shows an error — **never** an optimistic success state
* On success: link to `/app/brand/${brandId}`

### Task 3 — Code-search regression proof

* Create a source-grep test confirming exactly one file in `app/src/app/api/crm/**` + `app/src/mastra/tools/**` contains `won`/`lost` write logic

## Description

`api/crm/deals/[id]/convert` — a POST route, reusing the existing force-dynamic + auth pattern (`api/marketing-chat/[[...slug]]/route.ts`). On the Deal Detail screen, selecting `won`/`lost` opens an inline `ApprovalCard` ("Mark this deal as Won? Before: X → After: won. This will create or link a brands record for {company}.") — only clicking Approve calls this route. Cancel reverts the UI with no write.

**This is the single highest-leverage architectural decision in the whole CRM module** (`tasks/crm/02-crm-architecture-brief.md` Executive Summary): a CRM company is not a parallel identity system — `won` converts/links `crm_companies.brand_id` to an existing (or newly created) `brands` row, handing off to existing Brand Intelligence/Shoot/Campaign flows.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] **No other button, menu item, keyboard shortcut, or the Pipeline board's drag interaction (CRM-UX-003) may set** `stage = won/lost` **without going through this route** — verify by code search, not just manual testing
- [ ] `won` creates a `brands` row if none exists, or links the existing one if `crm_companies.brand_id` is already set
- [ ] `lost` sets the stage with no brand-side effect
- [ ] If the route fails, the UI does not show optimistic success — it reverts and surfaces the error explicitly
- [ ] Approving links to the existing Brand Detail screen (`/app/brand/:id`), not a new CRM-owned page
- [ ] Add a regression test asserting a direct `PATCH` to the deal cannot set `won`/`lost`

## Implementation notes

* Never FK into legacy `fashion_brands` — only the active, org-scoped `brands` table
* This route is the one place `rls-policy-auditor` + `migration-reviewer` review is mandatory before merge, given the irreversibility
* Auth: use the existing operator API auth pattern (`withOperatorAuth` + `createSupabaseServerClient`), same as other `api/crm/*` routes
* **Branch naming:** use `ipi/367-crm-won-lost-gate`, not Linear's auto-suggested `ai/` prefix

## Verify

- [ ] `cd app && npm run typecheck && npm test`
- [ ] Manual: attempt every non-approved path to set won/lost and confirm each is blocked
- [ ] `infisical run -- npm run supabase:verify-rls`

Title: CRM-QA-001 · MVP acceptance verification
Description:
**In plain terms:** Prove the CRM module's hard safety guarantees hold end-to-end before this project moves to Done — not just per-issue testing, but the cross-cutting invariants that only show up when every piece is wired together.

**Blocked by:** CRM-AI-001 (IPI-367), CRM-AI-003 (IPI-369) · **Unblocks:** none (terminal issue)

## User story

As a **product owner**, I want proof — not just a status flag — that a deal can never silently become "won" and that no org can see another org's data, before I tell anyone the CRM MVP is done.

## User journey

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  A[All 8 prior issues merged] --> B[Run 4 invariant tests]
  B --> C{All pass?}
  C -->|yes| D[Mark CRM MVP Done — todo.md + Linear]
  C -->|no| E[Back to the owning issue, not a new patch here]
```

## Implementation Steps

**Full plan with complete code:** `docs/plan/tasks/2026-07-04-crm-verification.md`.

### Task 1 — No silent won/lost

* Create `app/src/app/api/crm/__tests__/no-silent-won-lost.test.ts` (source-grep, exactly 1 write site)
* Create a direct-SQL negative test: raw `UPDATE ... SET stage='won'` without the session flag **must raise** in Postgres directly

### Task 2 — Cross-org data blocked

* Seed 2 test orgs, 1 row each in all 4 `crm_*` tables
* 16 negative assertions (4 tables × 4 verbs) — all must return zero rows / permission denied for the other org

### Task 3 — Won deal creates/links a brand

* Create `.../convert/__tests__/no-orphaned-won-deals.test.ts` — 3 fixtures (no brand / existing brand / already-won idempotency) — every one ends with non-null `crm_companies.brand_id`
* **This task may surface real bugs in IPI-367's route — fix them in this PR, don't reopen IPI-367 silently**

### Task 4 — Agent cannot send messages directly

* Static grep: zero email/messaging client imports under `app/src/mastra/tools/crm/**`
* Runtime trace: `draftFollowUp` → approve → `logActivity`, mocked send function never called

### Task 5 — Full verify matrix + evidence + sign-off

* Run full matrix, manual browser smoke through all 6 CRM routes
* Update `tasks/crm/todo.md` + `tasks/plan/todo.md` CRM row to 🟢
* Evidence under `tasks/crm/audit/` (this repo's real convention — not a fabricated `ecommerce/` path)
* Move all 9 Linear issues to Done

## Description

Four concrete acceptance tests, per `tasks/crm/05-crm-prd.md` §15/§17 and `tasks/crm/notes-1.md`'s review:

1. **No silent** `won`**/**`lost` — code-search + manual test confirming the only write path to `crm_deals.stage = won/lost` is `api/crm/deals/[id]/convert`, across the Pipeline board drag, Deal Detail stage control, and every `crm-assistant` tool. **Also assert the IPI-362 DB trigger itself rejects a raw SQL update** — app-level testing alone is not sufficient.
2. **Cross-org data blocked** — a second test org cannot read or write another org's `crm_companies`/`crm_contacts`/`crm_deals`/`crm_activities` rows.
3. **Won deal creates/links a brand** — the convert route produces a real `brands` row (new or linked) every time, with no orphaned `won` deals.
4. **Agent cannot send messages directly** — `draftFollowUp` output can be traced to always terminate at `ApprovalCard`/`logActivity`, never an external send call.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All 4 tests above pass and are captured as automated tests where feasible (RLS pen test, unit test on the convert route, direct-SQL trigger test) — not manual-only where automation is possible
- [ ] `todo.md` updated to reflect CRM MVP status
- [ ] Full verify matrix green (see below)
- [ ] Manual browser smoke confirms all 6 CRM screens are reachable

## Implementation notes

* **Evidence:** capture test output/screenshots under `tasks/crm/audit/` — not a bespoke `ecommerce/` path
* **Branch naming:** use `ipi/370-crm-verification`, not Linear's auto-suggested `ai/` prefix

## Verify

- [ ] `cd app && npm run lint && npm run build && npm run test`
- [ ] `infisical run -- npm run supabase:verify`
- [ ] `infisical run -- npm run supabase:verify-rls`
- [ ] Manual: browser smoke through all 6 CRM screens

Title: DNA-005 · Asset approval flow
Description:
## DNA-005 — Asset Approval Flow

**In plain terms:** HITL approval for DNA overrides and product link commits.

**Business purpose:** Operators can approve or reject AI-suggested DNA score changes or product links, with full audit trail. Reject preserves prior scores.
**Technical purpose:** Shared `ApprovalCard` component with brand intake, wired via `ai_agent_logs`.

**Blocked by:** [IPI-154](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-154) (DNA-004) · AIOR-008 (HITL pattern)

Description:
**Priority:** P0 — Foundation
**Status:** ⚪ Backlog (create after [IPI-457](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-457/ipi-457-cf-ai-005-unified-ai-provider-types-and-registry) merge + [IPI-454](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-454/ipi-454-cf-ai-001-ai-gateway-cloudflare-provider-routing) AC-F)
**Tags:** MASTRA, CLOUDFLARE, AI
**SSOT:** `tasks/cloudflare/mastra/mastra-audit.md`

## Purpose

Cut over **all Mastra agents and tools** from direct Gemini/Groq assumptions to the shared provider adapter and Cloudflare AI Gateway.

## Blocked by

* [IPI-457](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-457/ipi-457-cf-ai-005-unified-ai-provider-types-and-registry) — unified types/registry on `main`
* [IPI-454](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-454/ipi-454-cf-ai-001-ai-gateway-cloudflare-provider-routing) — AC-F Mastra → gateway wire

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] No agent/tool file imports `@google/generative-ai`, `@ai-sdk/google`, or `@ai-sdk/groq` directly
- [ ] All model calls go through `resolveModel(tier)` → gateway OpenAI-compat (`AI_GATEWAY_URL`)
- [ ] `AI_GATEWAY_URL` path tested on OpenNext preview (`:8787`) — marketing + operator smoke
- [ ] Gemini remains **vision + fallback** only (env-controlled)
- [ ] Workers AI as default tier flip **blocked** until [IPI-462](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-462/ipi-462-cf-ai-006-ai-provider-evaluation-suite) eval sign-off
- [ ] `cd app && npm run lint && npm test && npm run build` green
- [ ] Update `.claude/skills/mastra/SKILL.md` — gateway-first model section

## Out of scope

* Standalone `CloudflareDeployer` / second Mastra Worker
* RAG / pgvector ([IPI-141](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-141/aior-026-rag-foundation-brandassetcampaign-kb) defer)

## Verification

```bash
cd app && npm test
rg '@ai-sdk/(google|groq)' app/src/mastra/agents app/src/mastra/tools  # expect 0 after cutover
# Preview: wrangler tail shows gateway requests when chatting
```

### AI Gateway verification checklist (after cutover)

- [ ] `AI_GATEWAY_URL` configured in env / wrangler vars
- [ ] Gateway worker receives incoming requests from Mastra (confirm via wrangler tail or logs)
- [ ] Gateway routes requests to the correct provider (Workers AI default, Gemini fallback)
- [ ] Streaming works end-to-end: CopilotKit UI receives SSE tokens
- [ ] Structured output (JSON mode / `response_format`) works through gateway
- [ ] Tool calling works through gateway (function definitions forwarded, results returned)
- [ ] Fallback provider works when primary provider returns 5xx / timeout
- [ ] Metrics visible inside Gateway dashboard (request count, latency, error rate per provider)

*Source:* `linear/issues/IPI-MASTRA-CF-001-provider-gateway-cutover.md`

Title: UX P0: AI explainability — show why scores changed with confidence + evidence
Description:
## Problem

Scores appear as numbers with no explanation. A brand can go from DNA 45 → 72 and the operator has no idea why. There is no confidence signal, no supporting evidence, and no way to judge whether to trust the AI's output.

This violates UX principle #9: *Every AI recommendation is explainable — show the why + a confidence signal.*

## Expected behaviour

For each score on the Scores tab:

* **Why it changed** — one sentence: "Visual identity score improved because 3 brand images were found with consistent colour palette"
* **Confidence** — percentage: "AI confidence: 84%"
* **Evidence** — 1–3 bullet sources: page URLs, image URLs, or quotes the AI used to reach this score
* **Score history** — sparkline showing last 3 scores for this dimension so trends are visible

On the ApprovalCard diff:

* Hovering a score delta shows the evidence tooltip inline

## Implementation

* `brand_scores.details` JSONB already exists and stores per-score detail — populate it from the Gemini response
* `brand_scores.source` column already exists — use it for the evidence URL
* `ScoresTab` renders scores as pills — expand each into an accordion with rationale + evidence
* `ai_profile.evidenceSources` already parsed in `parseAiProfile` — wire to the scores display
* Confidence: add `confidence` field to each score row in the Gemini prompt output; store in `brand_scores.details`

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Each score shows a one-line "why" explanation
- [ ] Each score shows AI confidence %
- [ ] Each score links to 1–3 evidence sources (URLs or quoted text)
- [ ] Evidence shown in expandable accordion, collapsed by default
- [ ] ApprovalCard score deltas show evidence on hover/expand
- [ ] No extra DB query — use existing `brand_scores.details` and `ai_profile.evidenceSources`
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

### EvidenceBlock payload contract (required fields)

Every persisted grounding payload MUST include:

- [ ] `evidenceSchemaVersion` — semver string (e.g. `1.0.0`) for forward-compatible parsing
- [ ] `workflowId` — Mastra workflow id or edge fn name when applicable
- [ ] `agentId` — Mastra agent registry key (matches route-agent-map)
- [ ] `modelId` — resolved registry constant (never hardcoded in payload builder)
- [ ] `confidence` — 0–1 float or tier enum mapped to EvidenceBlock badge
- [ ] `citations[]` — `{ url, title?, snippet? }` from groundingMetadata / urlContextMetadata
- [ ] `timestamp` — ISO8601 UTC of inference
- [ ] `evidence[]` — pillar breakdown compatible with IPI-246 props
- [ ] **Backward compatibility** — readers accept rows missing new fields (graceful degrade)

---

### Acceptance criteria

- [ ] JSONB metadata shape documented for `groundingMetadata` + `urlContextMetadata`
- [ ] `insertAgentLog` accepts full EvidenceBlock payload contract above
- [ ] Brand Detail / Assets / Campaigns can map log payload → EvidenceBlock `evidence[]` props
- [ ] **Mastra:** workflow suspend payloads include typed `evidence[]` / confidence fields
- [ ] Source URLs render in operator UI (not lost after edge response)
- [ ] `npm run supabase:verify-brand-intelligence` still green

### Verification checklist

- [ ] Unit tests — payload mapper + backward-compat fixtures
- [ ] Integration — sample `ai_agent_logs` row after BI run contains full contract
- [ ] `infisical run -- npm run supabase:verify-brand-intelligence` green
- [ ] `infisical run -- npm run supabase:verify-rls` green (if schema touched)
- [ ] `cd app && npm test src/mastra` green
- [ ] Browser MCP — Brand Detail smoke: EvidenceBlock renders `evidence[]` from log
- [ ] Documentation updated — AI-EXPLAINABILITY.md payload contract
- [ ] Types regenerated if JSONB columns extended

Title: Workflow engine v2: dependencies & approvals
Description:
**Spec:** `linear/issues/IPI-483-PLN-008-workflow-engine-v2-dependencies-approvals.md`

## What this builds

Advanced workflow engine features on top of the core planner: dependency-based auto-shift, gate approval workflows, and multi-instance dependency tracking. Enables the "live schedule" that reacts to changes.

### Instance Lifecycle (State Diagram)

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Draft: create_instance
    Draft --> Planned: generate_schedule
    Planned --> Active: start_first_task
    Active --> Blocked: gate_blocks
    Blocked --> Active: gate_approved
    Active --> AtRisk: risk_detected
    AtRisk --> Active: risk_resolved
    Active --> Completed: final_task_done
    Completed --> Archived: archive
    Draft --> Cancelled: cancel
    Planned --> Cancelled: cancel
    Active --> Cancelled: cancel
```

### Dependency Auto-Shift

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Task "Item delivery" end_date moved +2 days] --> B[Engine queries planner.dependencies]
    B --> C{Successor exists?}
    C -->|Yes| D[Calculate forward shift + lag]
    D --> E[Update successor start/end dates]
    E --> F{Successor has successors?}
    F -->|Yes| D
    F -->|No| G[Mark plan as AtRisk if deadline missed]
    C -->|No| H[No propagation]
    G --> I[Notify affected assignees]
```

### Engine Types (Class Diagram)

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class PlannerEngine {
        +buildSchedule(phases, params)
        +shiftTask(taskId, deltaDays, tasks, deps)
        +detectCycles(deps)
        +checkGate(instance, phase, tasks, assignments, userId)
        +resolveDependencies(taskId, deps)
        +getEffectivePermissions(userId, assignments, instanceId)
    }
    class Task { +id, +instanceId, +phaseId, +title, +startDate, +endDate, +status, +priority }
    class Dependency { +id, +fromTaskId, +toTaskId, +depType, +lagDays }
    class Instance { +id, +orgId, +workflowId, +entityType, +entityId, +status }
    PlannerEngine --> Task : manages
    PlannerEngine --> Dependency : resolves
    PlannerEngine --> Instance : gates
    Task "1" --> "*" Dependency : has
```

### Acceptance criteria

* A — Dependency auto-shift: changing a task's dates propagates to successors with lag/type awareness.
* B — Gate approval flow: phase gates check task completion + role hierarchy before advancing.
* C — Cycle detection at shift time prevents infinite loops.
* D — Permission matrix enforces 4-tier role model (owner/manager/contributor/viewer) on all mutations.

Title: Mastra planner AI tools + CopilotKit HITL
Description:
**Spec:** `linear/issues/IPI-482-PLN-007-mastra-planner-ai-tools-copilotkit-hitl.md`

## What this builds

AI-powered planning inside the CopilotKit chat sidebar. The Mastra `production-planner` agent provides tools for schedule generation, shift analysis, and gate checks — all requiring explicit human approval (HITL) before writing to the DB.

### AI Schedule Generation with HITL

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[User asks: "Build a 5-week schedule for Summer Lookbook"] --> B[Mastra production-planner agent]
    B --> C[Tool: buildSchedule]
    C --> D[Read workflow template + deliverables]
    D --> E[Generate proposed tasks + dependencies]
    E --> F[Return draft to chat]
    F --> G{User approves?}
    G -->|Yes| H[Tool: commitSchedule]
    H --> I[Edge function: schedule-shoot-plan]
    I --> J[INSERT planner.tasks + dependencies]
    J --> K[Notify subscribers]
    G -->|No| L[User edits / cancels]
    L --> M[Agent revises draft]
    M --> F
```

### User Journey

```mermaid
journey
    title Producer schedules a 5-week shoot with AI
    section Create plan
      Open shoot schedule: 5: Producer
      Generate AI schedule: 4: Producer, AI Agent
      Review proposed timeline: 4: Producer
      Approve schedule: 5: Producer
    section Execute
      Invite team members: 4: Producer
      Monitor phase transitions: 4: Producer
      Resolve blocker: 3: Producer, Photographer
      Approve final delivery: 5: Client approver
    section Wrap
      Archive instance: 5: Producer
```

### Acceptance criteria

* A — Mastra `production-planner` agent has tools: `buildSchedule`, `shiftTask`, `resolveDependencies`, `checkGate`, `getEffectivePermissions`.
* B — All WRITE tools require CopilotKit HITL (human-in-the-loop) confirmation before executing.
* C — CopilotKit sidepanel shows draft timeline as an A2UI card before commit.
* D — Agent reads current schedule state via RLS (READ tools).
* E — Agent responds in natural language with date summaries, warnings, and explanations.

Title: Notification rules + Cloudflare Queue fan-out
Description:
**Spec:** `linear/issues/IPI-481-PLN-006-notification-rules-cloudflare-queue-fan-out.md`

## What this builds

Declarative notification rules engine that triggers email / SMS / push / in-app alerts on planner events (phase transitions, gate approvals, task assignments). Uses Cloudflare Queues for reliable fan-out delivery.

### Notification Fan-Out

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Task as planner.tasks UPDATE
    participant Edge as schedule-shoot-plan edge function
    participant PG as Postgres
    participant Queue as planner-notify Queue
    participant Worker as planner-notify Worker
    participant SES as Email provider
    participant FCM as Push provider

    Task->>Edge: phase transition detected
    Edge->>PG: INSERT public.notifications
    Edge->>PG: SELECT planner.notification_rules
    Edge->>Queue: enqueue notification job
    Queue->>Worker: deliver job
    Worker->>PG: fetch notification + user prefs
    Worker->>SES: send email
    Worker->>FCM: send push
    Worker->>PG: update delivery_status
```

### Acceptance criteria

* A — `planner.notification_rules` table stores declarative rules: event_type, target_role, channel, template_ref, delay_minutes.
* B — On planner event INSERT, an edge function evaluates matching rules and enqueues notification jobs.
* C — Cloudflare Queue buffers and retries delivery (max 3 retries, backoff).
* D — Channels: in_app (via existing `public.notifications`), email, push.
* E — Users can opt out per channel via user preferences.
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: Real-time sync via Supabase + Cloudflare Durable Objects
Description:
**Spec:** `linear/issues/IPI-480-PLN-005-real-time-sync.md`

## What this builds

Real-time synchronization for planner instances via **Supabase Realtime** (DB change streaming) + **Cloudflare Durable Objects** (presence/cursor sync). Multiple users see task updates and each other's cursor positions in under one second.

### Real-Time Update Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    actor U1 as User A (Producer)
    actor U2 as User B (Photographer)
    participant SPA as Next.js SPA
    participant RT as Supabase Realtime
    participant PG as Postgres
    participant DO as planner-coordinator DO
    participant GW as planner-gateway Worker

    U1->>SPA: Drag task to new date
    SPA->>PG: UPDATE planner.tasks (RLS)
    PG-->>RT: change event
    RT-->>SPA: push updated task
    SPA-->>U1: render new position
    RT-->>SPA: push updated task (User B)
    SPA-->>U2: render new position
    SPA->>GW: send cursor position
    GW->>DO: broadcast presence
    DO-->>GW: presence list
    GW-->>SPA: show "Maya is viewing Production"
```

### Cloudflare Edge Components

```mermaid
block-beta
    columns 3
    spa["Next.js SPA"]:3
    gateway["planner-gateway Worker"]
    do["planner-coordinator DO"]
    kv["planner-cache KV"]
    queue["planner-notify Queue"]
    notify["planner-notify Worker"]
    ai["planner-ai Worker"]
    spa --> gateway
    gateway --> do
    gateway --> queue
    queue --> notify
    spa --> kv
    spa --> ai
```

### Acceptance criteria

* A — Supabase Realtime broadcasts planner.tasks/events/assignments changes on channel `planner:<instance_id>`
* B — Cloudflare Durable Objects track per-instance presence (who is viewing which task)
* C — WebSocket upgrade via planner-gateway Worker
* D — Sub-1-second latency for task updates
* E — Presence indicators in the planner workspace header

Title: Role-based views + assignments
Description:
**Spec:** `linear/issues/IPI-479-PLN-004-role-based-views-assignments.md`

## Goal

Add instance-level role assignments and permission-aware views so each user sees only what matters to them.

## Problem

* Everyone sees the same schedule tab, which leaks details and creates noise.
* There is no way to assign owners to phases or restrict gate approvals.

## User story

> As a retoucher, when I open a production plan, I see only retouching tasks and approvals relevant to my role so I can focus without being overwhelmed.

## Acceptance criteria

* A — `planner.assignments` links user to instance with role and permissions JSONB.
* B — Default permission sets per role; owners can customize.
* C — Invite flow by email/role from planner settings.
* D — Timeline/kanban/calendar hide tasks the user cannot see; "My tasks" filter available.
* E — `/app/planner/dashboard` shows personalized stats and upcoming strip.
* F — Approval gates visible only to matching `phase.required_role`.

## Out of scope

Real-time presence, notifications, AI assignment suggestions, workflow gate logic.

## Wiring plan

* `app/src/lib/planner/permissions.ts`
* `app/src/components/planner/RoleDashboard.tsx`
* `app/src/components/planner/InviteMemberDialog.tsx`
* `app/src/components/planner/PlannerSettings.tsx`
* `app/src/app/(operator)/app/planner/dashboard/page.tsx`
* `supabase/functions/planner-invite-member/index.ts`

## Verify

* `cd app && npx vitest run src/lib/planner/permissions.test.ts`
* Browser login as retoucher: only retouching tasks visible
* `npm run supabase:verify-rls`
