Title: AI-CHAT-001 · Global Context-Aware AI Chat Context Engine
Description:
## AI-CHAT-001 — Global Context-Aware AI Chat Context Engine **Priority:** P2 · **Type:** Shared AI Infrastructure · **Complexity:** XL (cross-cutting refactor, not greenfield) **This is not CRM-specific.** Today's chat dock heading logic already spans Brand/Shoot/CRM, but not uniformly — see Current State below. The goal is ONE reusable engine every entity/module calls into, replacing three overlapping, disconnected systems that exist today. **⚠️ DC design files unavailable for this pass:** `Universal-design-prompt-new/` (all `.dc.html` sources) is no longer present in the repo checkout at spec-writing time — confirmed absent from both the main checkout and the authoring worktree. This ticket is grounded entirely in **live code** (cited file:line below), which is the substantive part for an infrastructure ticket. **Before implementation starts:** locate/restore the DC files and spot-check the chat-dock visual pattern on Brand Detail, Shoot Detail, and one Contact/Company Detail screen against what's built here. --- ## Current State (verified 2026-07-08 — read before estimating, this is not a greenfield build) Three separate, non-communicating systems already exist: | # | System | File | What it does | Gap | | -- | -- | -- | -- | -- | | 1 | Dock **heading** text | `src/lib/intelligence/use-route-welcome.ts` | Static `if/else` waterfall keyed on `normalizeRoutePath(pathname)`. Brand/shoot branches *can* interpolate a name via an optional `context` param; CRM branches (lines ~189-206) are 100% hardcoded literals (e.g. `"Contact record — log touchpoints and link to companies"`) and ignore `context` entirely. | Route-**type** aware, never entity-**instance** aware for CRM. Only `brandName` is actually wired through today. | | 2 | Agent **context** (backend) | `src/components/crm/crm-record-context.tsx` | Resolves `companyId`/`contactId`/`dealId` from the pathname, pushes them into the Mastra agent via CopilotKit's `useAgentContext` (4 calls). Mounted once in `src/app/(operator)/app/crm/layout.tsx:6`. `crm-assistant-agent.ts` instructions explicitly rely on this. | Works today, but CRM-only, id-only (no name/status), and invisible to the dock UI — doesn't touch the heading or composer placeholder. | | 3 | Panel **context** (UI) | `src/context/active-brand-context.tsx` + `src/context/intelligence-detail-context.tsx` | `ActiveBrandContext` holds an id only; `IntelligencePanel` re-resolves the name/status by looking the id up in an already-fetched list. `IntelligenceDetailContext` is a generic `ReactNode` override escape hatch, unused by any CRM page today. | Brand-only pattern; not entity-generic; doesn't touch the dock. | | 4 | Composer **placeholder** | `operator-chat-dock.tsx` → CopilotKit's `` | Never configured — `"Type a message..."` is CopilotKit's own library default (`chatInputPlaceholder` prop is never set). | 100% unpersonalized everywhere, on every route, today. | **Net effect:** an operator gets a route-*type*-aware heading (sometimes), a totally generic input placeholder (always), and the agent silently already knows the CRM entity id (but not weaved into anything the user sees). This ticket's job is to collapse 1–4 into one call site per page. --- ## Objective Replace the unpersonalized chat composer + fragmented heading logic with one hook a page calls once: ```ts useChatContext({ entityType, // registry key, e.g. 'crm-contact' | 'brand' | 'shoot' entityId, entityName, entityStatus, breadcrumbs, }) ``` The engine derives, from a per-`entityType` registry entry: * Composer placeholder ("Ask about {entityName}…") * Dock heading / conversation title * Suggested prompts (2–4 per entity kind) * Context chips (for `IntelligencePanel`, optional consumer) * Breadcrumb rendering * Agent context registration — **wraps** the existing `useAgentContext` mechanism, doesn't replace CopilotKit's own hook Pages own zero placeholder/prompt/heading logic. One registry entry per entity kind; the engine itself never changes when a new entity kind is added. --- ## MVP scope vs. Phase 2 (grounded in the pages that actually exist today) The request's full scope list includes several pages that **do not exist yet** in `app/src/app/(operator)/app/` (verified inventory below). Building registry entries for pages with no route is speculative — same discipline already applied when Profile360 was scoped down in [IPI-392](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-392/rf-04b-contact-detail-profile360-extract) (see that PR's Linear comment). Split accordingly: ### MVP — real pages today (build + migrate these) | Module | Pages | entityType | | -- | -- | -- | | CRM | Companies List, **Company Detail**, Contacts List, **Contact Detail** | `crm-company`, `crm-contact` | | Brand | Brand List, **Brand Detail** | `brand` | | Shoot | Shoots List, **Shoot Detail**, Shoot Wizard (new) | `shoot` | | Command Center | `app/page.tsx` | generic/no-entity fallback (route-type heading only, same as today) | ### Phase 2 — pages don't exist yet, or exist as stubs with no real entity data | Module | Status today | | -- | -- | | CRM Pipeline / Deal Detail | `crm/pipeline/page.tsx` + `[id]/page.tsx` exist as **files** but render `CrmScreenGate` stubs ([IPI-395](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-395/scr-30-crm-pipeline-react-parity)/396, in progress separately) — no entity to pass yet | | Campaigns Detail | Only `campaigns/page.tsx` (list) exists | | Assets Detail / Channel Preview | Only `assets/page.tsx` (list) exists | | Booking Detail/Wizard, Talent Profile/Availability/Role Dashboard | No pages exist — separate "Model Booking MVP" Linear project, in progress | | Analytics Dashboard/Campaign Analytics | No pages exist | | Profile360 (full `EntityConfig` engine), Event Management, Notifications | Aspirational — wire in as each ships, per this ticket's own registry pattern (zero core-engine changes needed) | **Acceptance criteria below are written against the MVP set only.** Phase 2 rows get a registry entry the day their page ships — that's the whole point of the registry pattern (see diagram 7). --- ## Proposed architecture ### 1 — Overall architecture ```mermaid flowchart TB subgraph shell["OperatorShell (operator-panel.tsx)"] PAGE["Page component (Server Component: fetch data)"] WORKSPACE["Client Workspace component
e.g. ContactDetailWorkspace"] PAGE --> WORKSPACE WORKSPACE -->|"useChatContext({entityType, entityId,
entityName, entityStatus, breadcrumbs})"| ENGINE ENGINE["ChatContextProvider (new)
replaces use-route-welcome.ts's static waterfall"] REGISTRY[("Entity Registry
placeholder templates · suggested prompts · icons")] ENGINE <--> REGISTRY ENGINE --> DOCK["PersistentChatDock (operator-chat-dock.tsx)
heading + placeholder + suggested prompts"] ENGINE --> AGENTCTX["useAgentContext() calls
(CopilotKit — same mechanism CrmRecordContext already uses)"] ENGINE -.->|optional| INTEL["IntelligencePanel context chips"] AGENTCTX --> MASTRA["Mastra agent (crm-assistant, brand-intelligence, ...)"] end ``` ### 2 — Context resolution flow ```mermaid flowchart LR A["Page mounts, e.g. /app/crm/contacts/[id]"] --> B["Workspace calls useChatContext(
{entityType:'crm-contact', entityId, entityName:'Maria Lopez', ...})"] B --> C{"entityType registered?"} C -- No --> D["Fallback: generic route-type heading
(today's use-route-welcome.ts behavior)"] C -- Yes --> E["Look up placeholder template + suggested prompts + icon"] E --> F["Interpolate entityName/Status →
'Ask about Maria Lopez…'"] F --> G["Merge with breadcrumbs → ChatContextValue"] G --> H["Provider re-renders dock + registers agent context"] ``` ### 3 — AI request lifecycle ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant U as User participant Dock as PersistentChatDock participant CK as CopilotKit runtime participant Agent as Mastra agent U->>Dock: Types "What's the deal status?" Dock->>CK: submit message Note over CK,Agent: Entity context already registered
via useAgentContext on page mount CK->>Agent: message + injected context (entityType, entityId, entityName) Agent->>Agent: use injected context FIRST (existing crm-assistant-agent.ts pattern) Agent-->>CK: streamed response CK-->>Dock: render response Dock-->>U: scoped answer ``` ### 4 — User journey ```mermaid journey title Operator asks about a specific contact section Browse Open Contacts List: 5: Operator See generic "Type a message...": 2: Operator section Navigate Click Maria Lopez: 5: Operator Contact Detail page loads: 5: Operator section Ask (after this ticket) Dock instantly shows "Ask about Maria Lopez…": 5: Operator Type a question, get a scoped answer: 5: Operator ``` ### 5 — Component hierarchy ```mermaid flowchart TD SHELL["OperatorShell"] --> PROVIDER["ChatContextProvider (new)"] PROVIDER --> ROUTES["MVP route pages: Brand List/Detail,
Shoot List/Detail/Wizard, CRM Companies/Contacts + 2 detail pages,
Command Center (fallback)"] PROVIDER --> DOCK2["PersistentChatDock"] PROVIDER -.-> INTEL2["IntelligencePanel (optional consumer)"] ROUTES --> HOOK["calls useChatContext(...) once"] ``` ### 6 — Data flow ```mermaid flowchart LR DB[("Supabase")] --> SERVER["Server Component, e.g. contacts/[id]/page.tsx"] SERVER -->|props: data| CLIENT["Client Workspace, e.g. ContactDetailWorkspace"] CLIENT -->|"derives entityName/Status from
already-fetched data — no extra fetch"| HOOK2["useChatContext(...)"] HOOK2 --> DOCKSTATE["Dock UI state"] HOOK2 --> AGENTSTATE["Agent context state"] ``` ### 7 — Entity registration flow (onboarding a future entity kind) ```mermaid flowchart TD A["New entity type needed, e.g. future 'Event'"] --> B["Add entityType to the registry (one config object)"] B --> C["Add placeholder template"] C --> D["Add suggested-prompt set (2-4 prompts)"] D --> E["Add icon mapping"] E --> F["Call useChatContext(...) once in the new page"] F --> G["Done — zero changes to ChatContextProvider itself"] ``` --- ## Deliverables - [ ] `ChatContextProvider` + `useChatContext` hook (`src/context/chat-context.tsx` or `src/lib/intelligence/chat-context/`) - [ ] Entity registry (placeholder templates, suggested prompts, icon mapping) — one config object per MVP entityType - [ ] Migration: `use-route-welcome.ts`'s CRM branches replaced by registry lookups (brand/shoot branches migrate too, once-only interpolation logic subsumed) - [ ] Migration: `CrmRecordContext` generalized into the new engine's agent-context registration (CRM keeps working identically — same ids reach the same agent) - Title: SEARCH-001 — AI Search & Vector Architecture
Description:
**Priority:** P1 · **Type:** Architecture · **Estimate:** M

Define search and vector strategy for iPix. Compare Supabase pgvector, Cloudflare Vectorize, Cloudflare AI Search, and Workers AI embeddings.

**Compare:**

* Supabase pgvector — existing, integrated with @mastra/pg, 2000-dim support, HNSW/IVFFlat indexes
* Cloudflare Vectorize — Worker-native, 1536-dim max, eventually consistent
* Cloudflare AI Search — managed search, no vector dimension limits
* Workers AI embeddings — BGE base/small/large, M3, Qwen3
* Hybrid keyword + vector search — combining full-text with embeddings

**Search use cases to map:**

* CRM — search companies, contacts, deals by name + attributes
* Brand — search brand profiles, DNA scores, guidelines
* Shoot — search shoot metadata, shot lists, notes
* Asset — search asset metadata, tags, DNA pillars
* Knowledge base — brand guidelines, SOPs, playbooks

**Acceptance criteria:**

* Supabase pgvector remains default unless Vectorize/AI Search clearly wins on cost + quality
* Workers AI embeddings evaluated for cost and quality against Gemini embeddings
* CRM, brand, shoot, and asset search use cases are mapped to specific search strategies
* Define MVP search architecture (what ships first)
* Define advanced search architecture (hybrid, multi-modal, cross-entity)

Title: AGENT-004 — Cloudflare Workflows & Orchestration
Description:
## Scope clarification (2026-07-09)

* **Cloudflare Workflows** = durable **cross-system** orchestration (webhooks, approvals, >30s external waits, retries) — [CF Workflows docs](<https://developers.cloudflare.com/workflows/>).
* **Mastra workflows** (`app/src/mastra/workflows/`) = in-process agent steps inside OpenNext — keep for brand/shoot HITL.
* Do **not** replace Mastra with CF Workflows; use CF Workflows when leaving the Next Worker or needing long wall-clock waits.

---

**Priority:** P1 · **Type:** Architecture · **Estimate:** M

Decide how to orchestrate long-running iPix workflows. Compare Mastra workflows, Cloudflare Workflows, Queues, Durable Objects, and Supabase jobs.

**Workflows to design:**

* Brand Analysis (crawl → analyze → score → approve)
* CRM enrichment (import → match → enrich → store)
* Shoot workflow (brief → plan → shoot → deliver → review)
* Booking workflow (request → quote → approve → confirm)
* Notifications (event → route → deliver → read)
* Publishing (content → review → schedule → publish)
* Asset review (upload → analyze → score → approve)

**Acceptance criteria:**

* Choose MVP orchestration approach
* Identify which workflows need queues (async fan-out)
* Identify which workflows need Durable Objects (stateful coordination)
* Identify which workflows can stay in Supabase/Next.js
* Avoid Cloudflare Workflows unless they reduce complexity over Mastra

Title: IPI-463 · CF-AI-008 — AI Provider Failover & Rollback
Description:
**Priority:** P2
**Depends on:** [IPI-454](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-454/ai-gateway-cloudflare-provider-routing) (AI Gateway), [IPI-461](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-461/ai-provider-adapter-common-agent-interface) (Provider Adapter)
**Merged from:** [IPI-182](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-182/ai-priority-inference-tier-cloudflare-gateway-routing) (Priority Inference Tier)

Define retry and fallback order across providers. Graceful failure if providers are down. Env/feature-flag rollback for rapid provider switching. Includes priority routing / inference tier design.

**Scope:**

* Failover chain: Primary → Fallback → Error. Example: Workers AI 429/5xx → Gemini → Workers AI (retry) → 503
* Graceful degradation: per-tier fallback policy (structured output fails → retry with flash model; vision fails → return error with clear message; streaming fails → fall back to non-streaming)
* Circuit breaker: track consecutive failures per provider per tier; after N failures, skip provider for M minutes
* Priority routing: inference tiers map to provider routing priorities via Cloudflare Gateway
* Feature-flag rollback: `AI_PROVIDER={provider}` env var switches all traffic instantly; per-request override via `x-ai-provider` header
* Rollback playbook: documented in `docs/operations/ai-failover.md`

**See:** tasks/cloudflare/plan.md
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: IPI-462 · CF-AI-006 — AI Provider Evaluation Suite
Description:
**Priority:** P1
**Depends on:** [IPI-454](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-454/ai-gateway-cloudflare-provider-routing) (AI Gateway), [IPI-461](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-461/ai-provider-adapter-common-agent-interface) (Provider Adapter)
**Merged from:** [IPI-464](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-464/ai-provider-feature-comparison-living-matrix) (Provider Feature Matrix)

Compare Gemini, Workers AI, NVIDIA NIM, and future providers across quality, latency, cost, structured output, streaming, tool calling, and JSON reliability.

**Scope:**

* Build eval harness that exercises each provider through the AI Gateway
* Measure per-provider: quality score, p50/p95/p99 latency, cost per call, structured output success rate, streaming throughput, tool call accuracy, JSON schema adherence
* Run against a fixed eval set (20-50 prompts covering shoot planning, brand analysis, DNA scoring, CRM, booking)
* Deliverable: Provider Feature Matrix (`docs/ai/provider-feature-matrix.md`) comparing streaming, tool calling, JSON mode, embeddings, vision, long context, cost, rate limits per provider
* Update matrix after each new provider evaluation
* Output a comparison report at `docs/ai/provider-eval-report.md`
* Schedule: run weekly in CI, archive results to R2

**Do NOT:**

* Hard-code provider-specific eval logic — the harness is provider-agnostic
* Ship this as a production endpoint — it's an offline/CI tool

**See:** tasks/cloudflare/plan.md
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: CRM-POST-012 · Relationship Journey Replay (AI-generated story)
Description:
## CRM-POST-012 · Relationship Journey Replay (AI-generated story)

**In plain terms:** Every entity has an AI-generated **story** — a coherent milestone journey instead of dozens of raw records. Example for Nike:

```
2024
  ↓ First outreach
  ↓ Discovery meeting
  ↓ Brand audit
  ↓ Spring campaign
  ↓ Three shoots · Forty-two assets
  ↓ Commerce launch
  ↓ Fashion Week sponsorship
  ↓ Revenue +$240k
```

**Blocked by:** IPI-370 · **Related:** [IPI-379](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-379) (raw history) · IPI-367 · IPI-380 · IPI-376

**Skills:** `mastra` · `gemini` · `copilotkit` · `ipix-supabase` · `frontend-design` · `mermaid-diagrams`

**Labels:** CRM · AI · FRONTEND · DESIGN

**Milestone:** CRM-M5 · Post-MVP Hub
**Spec:** `tasks/crm/07-relationship-hub-ai-roadmap.md` · `tasks/crm/05-crm-prd.md` §5.3

---

## vs CRM-POST-010 (IPI-379)

| IPI-379 Timeline & Memory | **This issue — Journey Replay** |
| -- | -- |
| Chronological activity feed + "summarize since 2024" | **Curated story arc** with named milestone nodes |
| CRM-centric rows | **Cross-hub narrative:** CRM → Brand → Shoot → Asset → Commerce |
| EvidenceBlock prose | **Vertical journey UI** (stepper) + one-paragraph story |
| Operator reads bullets | Operator **scans the relationship movie poster** |

Both share fetch layer; Journey Replay adds milestone taxonomy + cross-entity linking + presentation component.

---

## Design Reference

**Primary:** `Universal design prompt/Brand Detail.v2.image-first.dc.html` · lifecycle / proof sections
**CRM:** `app/design/CRM/02b-crm-company-detail.md` · new **Journey** tab
**Pattern:** `recommendation` card + vertical stepper (`design-plan.md` · confidence/evidence on each step)
**Related:** `Command Center.v2.image-first.dc.html` · portfolio story strip (optional widget)

---

## Dependencies

**Required:**

* IPI-370 — CRM MVP verified
* IPI-379 or shared fetch helpers — activity + deal history (can ship same PR stack if coordinated)
* IPI-367 — won deal → brand conversion (brand milestones need `brands` link)
* Org-scoped FK graph: `crm_companies` ↔ `brands` ↔ shoots ↔ assets (existing platform tables)

**Optional (enhances milestones):**

* IPI-376 / IPI-377 — graph traversal for missing links
* Commerce aggregates — read-only Mercur product link / order summary when wired (no invented revenue)
* IPI-380 Digital Twin — "prefers editorial" as epilogue chip, not a milestone

**Setup:** Milestone detector runs server-side; revenue/outcome metrics **only** when sourced from linked commerce rows or operator-entered won deal value — never LLM-hallucinated dollars

---

## Scope

* Mastra tool `replayRelationshipJourney({ entityType: company|brand|contact, entityId, since?, until? })` →

  ```typescript
  {
    entityName: string
    storyParagraph: string      // Gemini — facts from milestones only
    milestones: Array<{
      id: string
      label: string              // e.g. "First outreach", "Three shoots"
      occurredAt: string | null
      category: crm|brand|production|commerce|outcome
      href?: string              // deep link to source record
      evidence: Array<{ type, id, label }>
      metric?: { label: string; value: string }  // e.g. assets: 42, revenue: +$240k
    }>
    confidence: number
  }
  ```
* **Deterministic milestone builder** (no LLM): map events → canonical steps (first activity, discovery deal stage, won/lost, brand created, shoot count, asset count, commerce link count, deal value sum)
* UI: **Journey** tab on Company Detail + Brand Hub — `RelationshipJourneyReplay` vertical stepper; click step → navigate; expand → `EvidenceBlock`
* Chat: *"Tell me Nike's story since 2024"* → tool + render in dock
* **Read-only** — no stage changes from journey view

**Not in V1:** Video export, public share links, auto-generated case-study PDF, fictional milestones, revenue without data source

---

## Sequence Diagram

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as Operator
    participant UI as Journey Tab / Chat
    participant T as replayRelationshipJourney
    participant D as Milestone detector (deterministic)
    participant S as Supabase RLS
    participant G as Gemini

    U->>UI: Open Journey · Nike
    UI->>T: replayRelationshipJourney(companyId)
    T->>S: CRM activities, deals, brand, shoots, assets, commerce links
    S-->>T: rows
    T->>D: classify → ordered milestones
    D-->>T: milestone[] with evidence refs
    T->>G: narrate storyParagraph from milestones only
    G-->>T: paragraph
    T-->>UI: journey payload
    UI-->>U: vertical stepper + story
```

---

## State Diagram

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Loading
    Loading --> Sparse: fewer than 2 milestones
    Loading --> Ready: milestone chain built
    Loading --> Error: fetch failed
    Sparse --> Ready: more history added later
    Ready --> StepSelected: click milestone
    StepSelected --> Ready: navigate back
    Error --> Loading: retry
```

---

## Component Tree

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    CompanyDetail[Company / Brand Detail] --> JourneyTab[Journey Tab]
    JourneyTab --> StoryHeader[Entity name + year range + story paragraph]
    JourneyTab --> Stepper[RelationshipJourneyReplay]
    Stepper --> Step[MilestoneStep × N]
    Step --> Connector[↓ connector]
    Step --> MetricChip[metric badge optional]
    Step --> Evidence[EvidenceBlock expand]
    ChatDock[crm-assistant] --> Tool[replayRelationshipJourney]
    Tool --> Stepper
```

---

## User Journey

```mermaid
journey
    title Operator replays Nike relationship story
    section Orient
      Open Company Nike Journey tab: 5: Operator
      Scan vertical milestones in 10 seconds: 5: Operator
    section Trust
      Expand Three shoots see shoot ids: 5: Operator
      See revenue tied to won deal not invented: 5: Operator
    section Act
      Click Commerce launch go to product links: 5: Operator
      Ask agent summarize story in chat: 5: Operator
```

---

## Wireframes

```
Desktop — Company Detail · Journey tab
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Nike · Relationship Journey                    Since 2024         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ "From first outreach in Q1 2024 through Fashion Week, Nike      │
│  became a full-stack partner — 3 shoots, 42 assets, commerce live."│
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  2024                                                           │
│    ● First outreach          Mar 12   [CRM activity]              │
│    ↓                                                            │
│    ● Discovery meeting       Apr 03   [Meeting logged]          │
│    ↓                                                            │
│    ● Brand audit             May 18   [Brand Hub →]             │
│    ↓                                                            │
│    ● Spring campaign         Jun 01   [Campaign →]              │
│    ↓                                                            │
│    ● Three shoots            Jun–Aug  [3 shoots]                │
│    ↓                                                            │
│    ● Forty-two assets        —        [42 assets]                │
│    ↓                                                            │
│    ● Commerce launch         Sep 10   [Products →]              │
│    ↓                                                            │
│    ● Fashion Week sponsorship Oct 22  [Activity]                │
│    ↓                                                            │
│    ● Revenue +$240k          Won deal  [Deal →]  ⚠ sourced       │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Mobile — collapsible steps, full-width ↓ connectors
```

---

## API Wiring

| Route / Tool | Status | Auth | Returns | RLS |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| `replayRelationshipJourney` Mastra tool | 🔴 create | server agent | journey payload (above) | ✅ |
| GET `/api/crm/companies/[id]/journey` | 🔴 optional | `withOperatorAuth` | cached journey JSON | ✅ |
| Cross-entity reads | 🟡 extend | server | brands, shoots, assets, commerce_product_links | ✅ existing policies |

**Revenue rule:** `outcome` milestone uses `crm_deals.value` on won deals and/or linked commerce metrics when present; omit step if no numeric source.

---

## User Stories

### Story 1: Operator grasps full relationship in one glance

**As an** Operator  
**I want** a vertical milestone story on a company or brand profile  
**So that** I onboard to an account without opening ten screens.

**Acceptance:** Journey tab shows ≥2 milestones when data exists; each step links to source record.

### Story 2: Operator trusts the narrative

**As a** Brand Manager  
**I want** every milestone backed by evidence ids  
**So that** the AI story never invents shoots or revenue.

**Acceptance:** Expand step shows evidence list; revenue step absent when no won deal value; unit test catches hallucination guard.

### Story 3: Operator shares context in chat

**As an** Operator  
**I want** to ask "what's Nike's story?" in the copilot  
**So that** I get the same journey in conversation before a exec call.

**Acceptance:** Tool invocation renders stepper or compact list in chat; matches Journey tab for same entity.

---

## Acceptance

- [ ] **A1** Milestone detector unit tests — fixture company with CRM + brand + shoots — proof: vitest, deterministic order
- [ ] **A2** No revenue milestone without numeric source — proof: test with/without won deal value
- [ ] **A3** `RelationshipJourneyReplay` UI on Company Detail (+ Brand when linked) — proof: screenshot/storybook
- [ ] **A4** Story paragraph cites only milestone labels present in payload — proof: schema validation test
- [ ] **A5** RLS cross-org isolation — proof: second org empty journey
- [ ] **A6** `cd app && npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm test` green

### Verify

```bash
cd app && npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm test
infisical run -- npm run supabase:verify-rls
```

Title: CRM-POST-011 · Market & Trend Intelligence (outreach signals)
Description:
## CRM-POST-011 · Market & Trend Intelligence (outreach signals)

**In plain terms:** Monitors competitors, fashion trends, hiring, launches, and news to recommend outreach — surfaces *why now* for a relationship touch.

**Blocked by:** IPI-370 · **Related:** IPI-130 · CRM-POST-004 · `brand-intelligence` edge · `social-discovery`

**Skills:** `mastra` · `gemini` · `firecrawl` · `ipix-supabase` · `mermaid-diagrams`

**Labels:** CRM · AI · BRAND · EDGE

**Milestone:** CRM-M5 · Post-MVP Hub
**Spec:** `tasks/crm/07-relationship-hub-ai-roadmap.md` · `tasks/crm/05-crm-prd.md` §5.3

---

## Design Reference

**Primary:** `Universal design prompt/Command Center.v2.image-first.dc.html` · signals / alerts strip
**Related:** Brand Intelligence panels · `EvidenceBlock` for external source citations
**No duplicate crawl stack** — reuse `brand-intelligence` + Firecrawl webhook patterns

---

## Dependencies

**Required:**

* IPI-370 — CRM MVP gate
* Existing `brand-intelligence` edge fn + crawl pipeline (IPI-130 area)
* Company/brand linkage (`crm_companies` ↔ `brands` when converted)

**Optional:**

* CRM-POST-004 — scheduled enrichment jobs (same cron infrastructure)
* IPI-378 — rank outreach in daily NBA queue

**Setup:**

* Store signals in `crm_signals` or extend `brand_intelligence_snapshots` — migration in dedicated PR
* Edge cron or webhook-triggered refresh — no client-side Firecrawl keys

---

## Scope

* Ingest **read-only** external signals per company/brand: competitor moves, trend tags, hiring posts, product launches, news mentions (sources: existing crawl + optional RSS/News API behind edge)
* Mastra tool `getMarketSignals({ companyId?, brandId?, horizonDays?: number })` → ranked signals with `{ title, source, url, relevanceScore, suggestedOutreach?, evidence }`
* UI: "Signals" panel on Company Detail + optional Command Center widget; suggestion chip *"Draft outreach re: \[signal\]"* → `draftFollowUp` (IPI-369) — **never auto-send**
* CRM agent narrates: *"Competitor X launched sustainable line — good moment to pitch your SS27 lookbook"*

**Not in V1:** Autonomous outreach, paid news APIs without budget approval, scraping behind login walls, n8n orchestration (per roadmap — edge-first)

---

## Sequence Diagram

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Cron as Scheduled job / webhook
    participant E as brand-intelligence edge
    participant FC as Firecrawl / sources
    participant DB as Supabase
    participant A as crm-assistant
    participant U as Operator

    Cron->>E: refresh signals(brandId)
    E->>FC: crawl / fetch public pages
    FC-->>E: markdown + metadata
    E->>E: extract trends, launches, hiring
    E->>DB: upsert signals (RLS org)
    U->>A: "Any outreach angles for Adidas?"
    A->>DB: getMarketSignals
    DB-->>A: ranked signals
    A-->>U: EvidenceBlock + draft chip
```

---

## State Diagram

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> NoSignals
    NoSignals --> Fetching: cron / manual refresh
    Fetching --> Ready: signals stored
    Fetching --> Error: source failed
    Error --> Fetching: retry backoff
    Ready --> Stale: TTL exceeded
    Stale --> Fetching
    Ready --> Displayed: operator views
    Displayed --> ActionDraft: draft outreach chip
    ActionDraft --> Displayed: dismissed
```

---

## Component Tree

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    CompanyDetail[Company Detail] --> SignalsTab[Signals Tab / Panel]
    SignalsTab --> SignalList[SignalCard list]
    SignalList --> Evidence[Source link + snippet]
    SignalList --> DraftChip[Draft outreach chip]
    DraftChip --> Approval[ApprovalCard via draftFollowUp]
    CommandCenter[Command Center] --> Widget[Market signals widget — optional]
```

---

## User Journey

```mermaid
journey
    title Operator acts on market signal
    section Monitor
      Open company see 2 new signals: 5: Operator
      Read competitor launch citation: 5: Operator
    section Decide
      Click draft outreach chip: 5: Operator
      Review ApprovalCard draft: 5: Operator
    section Act
      Approve and log activity: 4: Operator
      Skip irrelevant signal dismiss: 5: Operator
```

---

## Wireframes

```
Desktop — Company Detail · Signals
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Adidas AG · Signals                    Last refreshed 2h   │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ 🔴 Competitor · Nike SS27 pre-launch (Vogue, link)  Score 91│
│    Suggested: reference your outdoor editorial track record │
│    [Draft outreach] [Dismiss]                               │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ 🟡 Trend · Quiet luxury momentum (WGSN summary)      Score 74│
│    [View evidence]                                          │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Command Center widget (optional)
┌─ Market pulse ─────────────────────┐
│ 3 companies with fresh signals     │
│ [Review Adidas] [Review Acme]      │
└────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## API Wiring

| Route | Status | Auth | Returns | RLS |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| `getMarketSignals` Mastra tool | 🔴 create | server agent | `{ signals[], refreshedAt }` | ✅ |
| POST `/api/crm/companies/[id]/signals/refresh` | 🔴 create | `withOperatorAuth` | triggers edge job id | ✅ |
| `brand-intelligence` edge (extend) | 🟡 extend | service role internal | normalized signals | ✅ org_id on write |
| Migration `crm_signals` or snapshot extend | 🔴 create | — | signal rows | ✅ policies |

---

## User Stories

### Story 1: Operator sees timely outreach angles

**As an** Operator  
**I want** external news and trend signals on a company profile  
**So that** I reach out when context is hot not random.

**Acceptance:** ≥1 signal with source URL after refresh; relevance score visible; stale TTL shown.

### Story 2: Operator drafts outreach from a signal

**As an** Operator  
**I want** a one-click draft that references the signal  
**So that** I don't copy-paste headlines manually.

**Acceptance:** Draft chip invokes `draftFollowUp`; ApprovalCard required; activity logs signal id.

### Story 3: Operator dismisses irrelevant noise

**As an** Operator  
**I want** to dismiss bad signals  
**So that** the feed stays trustworthy.

**Acceptance:** Dismiss hides signal for org; does not affect other companies; refresh can surface new items only.

---

## Acceptance

- [ ] **A1** Signal ingest unit test — fixture HTML → structured signal — proof: edge or tool test
- [ ] **A2** `getMarketSignals` returns ranked list with sources — proof: vitest
- [ ] **A3** UI Signals panel + draft chip → ApprovalCard path — proof: manual / Playwright
- [ ] **A4** RLS + no secrets in client bundle — proof: grep + cross-org test
- [ ] **A5** Reuses brand-intelligence / Firecrawl — proof: no second crawl stack in diff
- [ ] **A6** `infisical run -- npm run supabase:verify` + verify-rls if migration
- [ ] **A7** `cd app && npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm test` green

### Verify

```bash
cd app && npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm test
infisical run -- npm run supabase:verify
infisical run -- npm run supabase:verify-rls
infisical run -- npm run supabase:verify-brand-intelligence
```

Title: CRM-POST-010 · AI Timeline & Memory (cross-entity history)
Description:
## CRM-POST-010 · AI Timeline & Memory (cross-entity history)

**In plain terms:** Creates a chronological history of every interaction — instantly understand customer history. Operator asks: *"Summarize everything we've done with Adidas since 2024."*

**Blocked by:** IPI-370 · IPI-366 (deal activity timeline MVP) · **Related:** IPI-369 · IPI-375 · CRM-MCP-001

**Skills:** `mastra` · `gemini` · `copilotkit` · `ipix-supabase` · `mermaid-diagrams`

**Labels:** CRM · AI · SUPA · FRONTEND

**Milestone:** CRM-M5 · Post-MVP Hub
**Spec:** `tasks/crm/07-relationship-hub-ai-roadmap.md` · `tasks/crm/05-crm-prd.md` §5.3

---

## Design Reference

**Primary:** `app/design/CRM/02f-crm-deal-detail.md` · Activities tab (chronological feed)
**Related:** `Universal design prompt/Command Center.v2.image-first.dc.html` · activity strip
**Component:** `EvidenceBlock` + existing IntelligencePanel activity section (`panel-contract.ts`)

---

## Dependencies

**Required:**

* IPI-366 ✅ pattern — `crm_activities` unified timeline on Deal Detail
* IPI-369 — `summarizeRelationship` (Gemini narrates fetched rows)
* IPI-370 — CRM MVP verification gate

**Optional (enhances):**

* CRM-MCP-001 — Chatwoot/email threads merged into timeline
* IPI-376 — graph navigation from timeline entities

**Setup:** No new tables in V1 — extend queries across `crm_activities` + linked `crm_deals`/`crm_contacts`/`crm_companies`; optional `since` filter param

---

## Scope

**MVP slice (already planned):** Single-entity timeline on Deal/Company/Contact detail + `summarizeRelationship` for current record.

**This issue (Post-MVP):**

* Mastra tool `summarizeRelationshipHistory({ companyId?, contactId?, brandId?, since?: ISO date, until?: ISO date })` — fetches **all** org-visible activities + deal stage changes + won/lost events across linked entities; deterministic sort; Gemini produces narrative + bullet timeline
* UI: "History" tab on Company Detail + global search chip in chat dock — temporal queries via natural language routed to tool
* Export: read-only markdown block in `EvidenceBlock` — no writes

**Not in V1:** Cross-platform memory (Linear, email bodies full-text), vector recall of old notes, auto-ingest from MCP without CRM-MCP-001

---

## Sequence Diagram

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as Operator / Chat Dock
    participant CK as CopilotKit / crm-assistant
    participant T as summarizeRelationshipHistory
    participant S as Supabase (RLS)
    participant G as Gemini via resolveModel()

    U->>CK: "Summarize Adidas since 2024"
    CK->>T: tool call (company name resolved)
    T->>S: SELECT activities + deals + stage history
    S-->>T: rows (org-scoped)
    T->>T: sort chronologically, cap token budget
    T->>G: narrate structured facts only
    G-->>T: summary + milestones
    T-->>CK: { timeline[], summary, evidence[] }
    CK-->>U: EvidenceBlock + suggestion chips
```

---

## State Diagram

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Idle
    Idle --> ResolvingEntity: NL query / tab open
    ResolvingEntity --> Fetching: entity id known
    ResolvingEntity --> Ambiguous: multiple matches
    Ambiguous --> Fetching: operator picks company
    Fetching --> Summarizing: rows loaded
    Summarizing --> Ready: Gemini OK
    Summarizing --> Fallback: LLM error
```

## API Wiring

| Route / Tool | Status | Auth | Returns | RLS |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| `summarizeRelationshipHistory` Mastra tool | 🔴 create | server-only via agent | `{ summary, events[], evidence[] }` | ✅ org via user JWT in tool context |
| GET `/api/crm/companies/[id]/activities` | 🟡 extend | `withOperatorAuth` | paginated activities + deal events | ✅ existing policies |
| CopilotKit `/api/copilotkit` | 🟢 exists | session | tool invocation | ✅ |

**Auth pattern (if new route):**

```typescript
export async function GET(request: Request, { params }: { params: { id: string } }) {
  try {
    await withOperatorAuth(request);
  } catch (e) {
    if (e instanceof OperatorAuthError) {
      return NextResponse.json({ error: "Unauthorized" }, { status: 401 });
    }
    throw e;
  }
  const svc = await createSupabaseServerClient();
  // since/until query params; RLS on crm_activities
}
```

## User Stories

### Story 1: Operator reviews full relationship history

**As an** Operator  
**I want** a chronological feed of every CRM touchpoint for a company  
**So that** I never ask a colleague "what happened with this client?"

**Acceptance:** History tab shows ≥ all `crm_activities` + stage changes for linked deals, newest first.

### Story 2: Operator asks temporal summary in chat

**As an** Operator  
**I want** to ask "summarize everything since 2024" in natural language  
**So that** I get a narrative without scrolling 50 timeline rows.

**Acceptance:** Tool returns summary grounded in fetched rows; no invented meetings; `since` filter honored in test fixture.

### Story 3: Operator handles ambiguous company name

**As an** Operator  
**I want** the agent to clarify when two companies match "Adidas"  
**So that** summaries never mix orgs or duplicate records.

**Acceptance:** Ambiguous query returns disambiguation chips before summary; RLS test proves cross-org isolation.

## CRM-POST-009 · Predictive Next Best Action (daily prioritization)

**In plain terms:** Helps operators prioritize the highest-impact work every day — a ranked action queue across deals, contacts, and follow-ups (not just the one action on the current screen).

**Blocked by:** IPI-370 (CRM MVP verification) · **Related:** IPI-369 · IPI-374 · IPI-375 · CRM-POST-001

### Scope

**MVP delivers contextual NBA** (one next step on the active Company/Contact/Deal — IPI-369 IntelligencePanel). **This issue** adds **portfolio-level prioritization**:

* Mastra tool `rankDailyActions({ limit?: number })` — deterministic scoring first (`scoreDealHealth`, due `crm_activities`, stage velocity, deal value), LLM narrates *why* each row ranks where it does

Create Postgres functions (RPCs) for semantic brand search and graph traversal.

**Blocked by:** GRAPH-001 (tables) · GRAPH-003 (embeddings) — see [`GRAPH-004-graph-search-rpcs.md`](<./GRAPH-004-graph-search-rpcs.md>)

### RPCs

#### 1. `search_brands(p_embedding, p_limit, p_exclude_brand_id)`

Cosine similarity on `brands.embedding` + shared nodes output.

#### 2. `traverse_brand_graph(p_start_node_id, p_max_hops, p_edge_types)`

Recursive CTE n-hop traversal with edge type filter.

- [ ] `traverse_brand_graph()` RPC: recursive CTE, edge type filtering
- [ ] RLS: service_role execute (Edge Functions, not client)

Description:

## Context

Lets a brand send a booking request in under 60 seconds. No shared `WizardStep` component exists (verified — Shoot Wizard itself inlines its own step-progress in an 824-line page.tsx) — this wizard gets a booking-local step-progress bar, not an import.

## Scope

* Booking Wizard route (standalone, no 3-panel shell — same convention as Shoot Wizard): dates + shoot link → rate → message → review → send
* Booking Agent (`booking`) registered — drafts only, **never confirms** (confirm is human-only, Phase 5)
* `checkTalentAvailability`/`draftBookingQuote`/`createBookingDraft` tools
* Quote/counter flow (API PATCH — only allows `requested→quoted` / `requested→declined`, confirm is a separate route entirely)
* Booking notification events wired on create/quote

## Out of scope

Any path that sets `status='confirmed'` from this phase's routes (reserved for the approve route only, Phase 5). Payment/deposit fields.

## Implementation steps

Title: DESIGN-081 · De-fork ApprovalCard — Consolidate 4 Implementations Into 1
Description:
## Context

Component-spec diff (2026-07-01, comparing `Universal design prompt/components/ApprovalCard.dc.html` against production) found that `ApprovalCard` — which the design system explicitly documents as "the only gate for an AI write action... do not fork" — has been **forked into 4 separate React implementations**:

* `app/src/components/brand-hub/approval-card.tsx`
* `app/src/components/shoot/hitl/BudgetApprovalCard.tsx`
* `app/src/components/shoot/hitl/DeliverableApprovalCard.tsx`
* `app/src/components/shoot/hitl/ShotListApprovalCard.tsx`

This is a rule violation already shipped to production, not just an unbuilt design gap — flagged as the single highest-priority finding from the component audit.

## Problem

Four independent ApprovalCard implementations mean HITL (human-in-the-loop) approval behavior can drift out of sync across Brand Hub vs the three Shoot HITL surfaces — a bug fix or design change to "how approvals work" now has to be applied 4 times, and already may have already drifted (prop names differ: source's `state`/`beforeText`/`afterText` vs production's `variant`/`before`/`after`).

## Proposal

Consolidate into one shared `ApprovalCard` component (props matching or reconciled with the design source: `title`, `confidence`, `evidence`, `before`, `after`, `variant`) that `brand-hub`, `BudgetApprovalCard`, `DeliverableApprovalCard`, and `ShotListApprovalCard` all render through — each surface passes its own data/copy into the shared component rather than maintaining its own markup/logic.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] One `ApprovalCard` component exists under a shared location (e.g. `app/src/components/approval-card/`)
- [ ] Brand Hub's approval card, and all 3 shoot HITL cards (Budget/Deliverable/ShotList), render through the shared component
- [ ] No visual regression on any of the 4 surfaces
- [ ] Prop names reconciled with the design source (`variant`, `before`, `after`, `confidence`, `evidence`) — document the final agreed prop names in `tasks/design-docs/handoff/03-component-map.md`
- [ ] Existing tests for all 4 surfaces still pass; add one shared component test

## Skills

`vercel-react-best-practices` — `rerender-memo` (the shared component will render on every list of approvals; memoize it), `bundle-analyzable-paths` · `react-patterns` (Server Component vs Client Component boundary — approval actions likely need client interactivity, keep the boundary tight)

## Dependencies

* Design spec: `Universal design prompt/components/ApprovalCard.dc.html` + `components/COMPONENTS.md`
* Component-map doc: `tasks/design-docs/handoff/03-component-map.md` (update after reconciling prop names)

## Out of scope

Changing HITL approval *behavior* — this is a structural consolidation, not a UX change. Any of the 4 surfaces' current visual variants should be preservable as props/variants on the shared component.

Title: Intelligence Panel: AI Suggestion Rail
Description:
# IPI-285: Intelligence Panel — AI Suggestion Rail

**Linear:** [IPI-285](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-285)  
**Design Ref:** `Universal design prompt/Brand Detail.v2.image-first.dc.html` § Intelligence Panel  
**Parent:** IPI-255 (DESIGN-071 deferred items)  
**Priority:** High (P2)

---

## Context

IPI-255 shipped DNA scores and pending approvals in the Intelligence Panel. AI-powered suggestions were deferred as part of the original DESIGN-071 scope.

---

## Problem

Users must ask the AI assistant for recommendations explicitly. The Intelligence Panel could proactively surface AI-generated suggestions based on brand context, reducing cognitive load and surfacing non-obvious actions.

---

## User Stories

### Story 1: Brand Manager seeing low commerce score

**As a** Brand Manager  
**I want** to see AI suggestions when DNA scores reveal gaps  
**So that** I know the next action to improve the score without asking

**Acceptance:** Suggestion "Add product shots to improve commerce readiness" appears when score < 70

### Story 2: Operator onboarding new brand

**As an** Operator  
**I want** proactive AI recommendations during brand analysis  
**So that** I can guide the brand owner on priority improvements

**Acceptance:** 3-5 suggestions render below DNA scores with action buttons

### Story 3: Creative Director with high consistency

**As a** Creative Director  
**I want** positive reinforcement when brand scores are strong  
**So that** I know to reference this brand for others

**Acceptance:** Suggestion "High consistency score — use as reference" appears when consistency > 85

---

## User Journey

```mermaid
journey
    title Operator reviews Beta Brand with low commerce score
    section Intelligence Panel Loads
      Navigate to /app/brand/beta: 5: Operator
      DNA scores load: Visual 80, Commerce 55: 3: Operator
    section AI Suggestion Appears
      See suggestion "Commerce readiness low": 4: Operator
      Read description "Add product shots": 5: Operator
      See confidence 85%: 5: Operator
    section Take Action
      Click "Plan shoot →" action button: 5: Operator
      Navigate to shoot wizard: 5: Operator
      Create product shoot with deliverables: 5: Operator
```

---

## Proposal

Add a SuggestionRail component to the Intelligence Panel that shows 3-5 AI-generated suggestions based on:

* Brand DNA scores (e.g., "Visual inconsistency detected → audit logo usage")
* Pending approvals status
* Current route context (brand detail vs shoot planning)
* Historical patterns

Implement `GET /api/brands/[id]/suggestions` endpoint backed by Mastra `brand-suggestions` tool.

---

## API Wiring

| Route | Status | Auth | Returns | Agent |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |

```typescript
    // Generate 3-5 suggestions via Gemini

  },
```

Title: UX P1: Workspace progress bar — Brand → DNA → Competitors → Shoot → Commerce → Campaign
Description:
## Problem

Operators have no sense of where a brand is in the overall workflow. Is it 20% done? 80%? What's blocking it from being ready to sell? There is no high-level progress view.

## Expected behaviour

A horizontal progress track near the top of the brand detail page showing the 6-stage workflow:

```
Brand Setup → DNA Analysis → Competitors → Shoot → Commerce → Campaign
    ✓              ✓             ○            ○         ○          ○
```

* Completed stages: filled circle + label in dark colour
* Current stage: filled orange circle + label bold
* Future stages: empty circle + label grey

Clicking a stage navigates directly to that section/page.

Below the track: **"Stage 3 of 6 — Competitor Analysis not started. Run it now →"**

## Implementation

* Compute stage completion from existing data:
  * Brand Setup: always done once brand exists
  * DNA Analysis: `intake_status === 'ready'`
  * Competitors: query competitor analysis runs for this brand
  * Shoot: query `shoots` where `brand_id = id`
  * Commerce: query `commerce_product_links` for this brand
  * Campaign: query campaigns for this brand
* Add `WorkspaceProgressBar` component to `BrandHubClient` header area
* Stage data passed as props from page query (add counts to existing page query)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Progress track visible on brand detail page
- [ ] 6 stages shown with correct completion state
- [ ] Current stage highlighted in orange
- [ ] "Next stage" call-to-action shown below track
- [ ] Clicking a stage navigates to that section
- [ ] Updates in real time when status changes (via `router.refresh()` on action completion)
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: UX P1: Contextual next steps after brand approval (Shoot, Competitors, Commerce, Campaigns)
Description:
## Problem

After a brand is approved and status becomes `ready`, the page shows the profile and scores — then stops. There is no suggestion of what to do next. The user has to navigate away and figure it out themselves.

This violates UX principle #5: *Always show the next step — no surface dead-ends.*

## Expected behaviour

When `intake_status = 'ready'`, show a **"What's next?"** section below the tabs with 4 action cards:

| Action | Description | Link |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 🎯 Competitor Analysis | See how this brand compares to competitors | `/app/brand/[id]/competitors` |
| 📸 Plan a Shoot | Schedule a content shoot for this brand | `/app/shoots?brand=[id]` |
| 🛒 Commerce Setup | Connect products and set up commerce | `/app/brand/[id]/commerce` |
| 📢 Create Campaign | Build a campaign brief using this brand | `/app/campaigns?brand=[id]` |

Cards should show completion state: if a shoot already exists for this brand, show "1 shoot planned ✓" instead of "Plan a Shoot".

## Implementation

* Add `NextStepsSection` component rendered in `BrandHubClient` when `intakeStatus === 'ready'`
* Query counts: shoots for this brand, campaigns, competitor analysis runs — add to the page query
* Cards link to downstream workflows with `?brand=[id]` pre-filled so context isn't lost
* Mark completed steps visually (green check, "Done" label) so the operator can see their overall progress

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] "What's next?" section appears when brand is `ready`
- [ ] 4 cards shown: Competitors, Shoot, Commerce, Campaigns
- [ ] Each card shows completion state if work already exists
- [ ] Links pre-fill brand context in destination pages
- [ ] Section hidden for any other `intakeStatus`
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: UX: Every brand status shows one clear next action (no dead ends)
Description:
## Problem

Several brand states leave the user on a page with data but no obvious next step. This creates dead ends where users don't know what to do.

Affected states:

* `brand_created` — page shows empty profile with generic "Gaps to address" list. No prominent CTA to run analysis.
* `failed` — page shows nothing. No error explanation, no retry button.
* `ready` — shows profile and scores, but no suggestion of what to do next (plan a shoot? run competitor check?).

## Expected behaviour

Each status maps to one primary action:

| Status | Primary CTA | Secondary |
| -- | -- | -- |
| `brand_created` | **"Analyze this brand"** (big, centre) | — |
| `crawl_running` | Progress banner (already exists) | Cancel |
| `analysis_running` | Progress banner (already exists) | — |
| `draft_ready` | **"Review draft"** (orange, prominent) | — |
| `ready` | **"Plan a shoot →"** | "Run competitor check" |
| `failed` | **"Retry analysis"** + error reason | Contact support |

## Implementation

* Add a `StatusCallToAction` component that renders based on `intakeStatus`
* Place it in `BrandHubClient` below the header, above the tabs
* `failed` state: query `brands.ai_profile._error` for the failure reason and display it in plain English
* `ready` state: CTA links to `/app/shoots` with brand pre-selected (pass `?brand=<id>`)
* `brand_created` state: large "Analyze" button that calls `reanalyzeBrand()` — same action as Re-analyze button but more prominent

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `brand_created`: large "Analyze this brand" CTA shown (Re-analyze button can remain as secondary)
- [ ] `failed`: error reason shown in plain English + Retry button
- [ ] `ready`: "Plan a shoot" link shown as next step
- [ ] `draft_ready`: "Review draft" is the visual primary action (not just the banner)
- [ ] No dead-end state — every status page has at least one actionable button
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: UX: Show before/after diff in draft approval card
Description:
## Problem

When a brand analysis draft is ready, the `ApprovalCard` and `DraftBanner` show the new draft values (tagline, category, confidence) but not the current live values. Operators are asked to approve changes they cannot compare.

This violates UX principle #9: *Every AI recommendation is explainable — show the why + a confidence signal.*

## Expected behaviour

Show a two-column comparison for every field that changed:

| Field | Before | After |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Tagline | *(none)* | Coastal swimwear for confident women |
| Category | Fashion | Swimwear |
| Confidence | 60% | 78% → **+18%** |

Fields that did not change should be collapsed or hidden to reduce noise.

## Implementation

* `ApprovalCard` already receives `draft: AiProfile` and `liveScores`. Add `liveProfile: AiProfile` prop (already available in `page.tsx` as `profile`)
* Pass `profile` → `liveProfile` from `BrandHubClient` into `ApprovalCard` and `DraftBanner`
* Render a diff table: for each field present in either profile, show old → new. Highlight changed fields.
* Unchanged fields: collapse behind a "Show unchanged (N)" toggle

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] ApprovalCard shows old vs new for: tagline, category, confidenceScore, productionReadiness
- [ ] Changed fields are visually highlighted (e.g. orange border or background)
- [ ] Unchanged fields hidden by default with expand option
- [ ] Score deltas (already shown) remain correct
- [ ] DraftBanner gets the same treatment
- [ ] No extra DB query — use data already on the page
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: MI-03a · Preview redesign — pick a platform first, then its tabs
Description:
Upgrade the Channel Preview studio (`/app/preview`, shipped in MI-03 / IPI-188) from a fixed 4-frame wall to a **platform-first** chooser.

## Goal

User picks **platforms**, not individual placements. Each selected platform auto-renders all its placements, grouped under a tab. Fewer clicks, cleaner page.

## Scope (UI only — no new tables, no edge functions)

* **Platform multi-select** — checkboxes: Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, Pinterest, YouTube, Amazon, Shopify (the 9 channels already seeded in `recommendation_rules`).
* **Auto-expand placements** — selecting Instagram shows Feed + Story + Reel; Facebook shows Feed + Story; etc.
* **Group by platform tabs** — `Instagram (3)`, `Facebook (2)`… instead of one long scroll.
* **Expand channel set 4 → 9** — extend `PREVIEW_CHANNELS`, `CHANNEL_LABELS`, `CHANNEL_LAYOUT`, `CHANNEL_FALLBACK_RATIO` in `app/src/lib/media/channel-specs.ts`; extend `getAllChannelSpecs` to resolve all 9.
* **Status badge** — add an "Approved / Needs review" chip next to the existing spec caption (spec line `1080×1350 · 4:5 · JPEG` already renders — keep it).
* **Safe-zone toggle split** — current single "Show safe zones" checkbox → Safe Area / Crop / Text Area (cosmetic; reuses existing `safeZone` data).

## Files

* `app/src/lib/media/channel-specs.ts` — extend constants to 9 channels
* `app/src/lib/media/channel-specs.server.ts` — `getAllChannelSpecs` over 9
* `app/src/components/media/channel-preview-studio.tsx` — selector + tabs
* `app/src/components/media/device-frame-preview.tsx` — Reel layout (reuse fullscreen), Pinterest/YouTube frames per fidelity decision (see MI-03r note: generic frame first)

## Out of scope (separate tasks)

AI review (MI-03b), approval persistence (MI-03c), campaign view (MI-03d), publishing (MI-03e), smart cropping (MI-03r). Bespoke YouTube/Pinterest chrome can start as a generic ratio+safe-zone frame.

## Acceptance

* Select platforms → only those render, grouped in tabs
* Instagram selection shows Feed + Story + Reel
* All 9 seeded channels resolvable; unseeded → graceful "no spec" fallback
* One concern per PR (UI only), passes `npm run lint` + `tsc --noEmit` + `build`
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: SHOOT-DATA-002 · Library of shot types the AI can reference
Description:
# Purpose

Ground `generateShotListDraft` in a curated visual taxonomy instead of hallucinated text descriptions. A `shot_type_references` table stores 80–100 categorized shot types with Cloudinary reference images, metadata, and channel-fit scores. Every AI-suggested shot becomes a concrete, photographed example the operator can see and swap — not an invented description.

**Research source:** Squareshot DISCOVER library (527 images) — `docs/screenshots/squareshot/8G-shotlist-reference.png` · `docs/screenshots/squareshot/squareshot.md`

# Business Context

Squareshot's 527-image reference library is static and generic. iPix can build a living reference library that grows with every completed shoot — approved assets (DNA score ≥80) feed back as new reference rows. Over time, `lookupShotReferences` returns real brand photography from past iPix shoots, not stock examples. That's the moat.

# User Story

As a **Brand Manager**
I want to see a real photo example for each AI-suggested shot
So that I can approve or swap shots confidently without guessing what the description means

As a **Creative Director**
I want to browse a reference library by category and angle
So that I can add specific shot types to a shoot plan with visual precision

# Squareshot Taxonomy (source of truth for seeding)

Categories and subcategories to cover at launch:

| Category tab | Subcategory | Key angles |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Clothing | Ghost | Front, Back, Detail, Flat lay |
| Clothing | Model | Full body, Half body, Close-up, Lifestyle |
| Beauty | Product | Overhead white, 45°, Detail texture, Group |
| Beauty | Swatch | Macro, Spread, Color range |
| Beauty | Model | Hands in-use, Face close-up, Full body |
| Accessories | Product | Hero, Detail, Lifestyle styled |
| Home Goods | Product | Overhead styled, Lifestyle room, Detail |

**Launch target: 80–100 rows** covering all 7 category tabs. 527 is not needed to unlock AI value.

# Data Model

## New table: `shot_type_references`

```sql
CREATE TABLE shot_type_references (
  id            uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  category      text NOT NULL,
  subcategory   text,
  angle         text NOT NULL,
  description   text NOT NULL,
  reference_url text NOT NULL,
  model_type    text,
  background    text,
  channel_fit   jsonb DEFAULT '[]',
  tags          text[] DEFAULT '{}',
  created_at    timestamptz DEFAULT now()
);

-- category values: 'clothing', 'beauty', 'accessories', 'home_goods'
-- subcategory: 'ghost', 'model', 'product', 'swatch'
-- model_type: 'full-body', 'half-body', 'hands-only', null
-- background: 'white', 'custom-backdrop', 'lifestyle', 'studio-gradient'
-- channel_fit: ["instagram_feed", "shopify_pdp", "amazon", "tiktok"]

CREATE INDEX idx_shot_type_refs_category ON shot_type_references(category);
CREATE INDEX idx_shot_type_refs_channel ON shot_type_references USING gin(channel_fit);
CREATE INDEX idx_shot_type_refs_tags ON shot_type_references USING gin(tags);
```

**RLS:** SELECT is public (no auth required — reference data, not brand data). INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE restricted to `service_role`.

## Cloudinary folder structure

```
ipix/shot-references/
  clothing/ghost/front.jpg
  clothing/ghost/back.jpg
  clothing/ghost/detail.jpg
  clothing/model/full-body-lifestyle.jpg
  clothing/model/close-up.jpg
  beauty/product/overhead-white.jpg
  beauty/product/45-degree-gradient.jpg
  beauty/product/detail-texture.jpg
  beauty/model/hands-in-use.jpg
  beauty/model/face-close-up.jpg
  accessories/hero.jpg
  accessories/detail.jpg
  accessories/lifestyle-styled.jpg
  home-goods/overhead-styled.jpg
  home-goods/lifestyle-room.jpg
```

Upload via Cloudinary MCP (`/cloudinary`). Store resulting URLs in `shot_type_references.reference_url`.

# AI Integration

## New Mastra tool: `lookupShotReferences`

```typescript
// tools/lookupShotReferences.ts
// Queries shot_type_references filtered by category + channel_fit
// Called by generateShotListDraft BEFORE generating shot descriptions

// Input
interface LookupShotRefsInput {
  product_type: 'clothing' | 'beauty' | 'accessories' | 'home_goods'
  subcategory?: string           // 'ghost' | 'model' | 'product' | 'swatch'
  channels: string[]             // ['instagram_feed', 'shopify_pdp', ...]
  style_profile?: string         // from brand_scores — 'minimal' | 'editorial' | 'luxury'
  shot_count: number             // how many to return
}

// Output
interface ShotTypeReference {
  id: string
  category: string
  subcategory: string | null
  angle: string
  description: string
  reference_url: string          // Cloudinary URL — render as thumbnail
  model_type: string | null
  background: string
  channel_fit: string[]
  tags: string[]
}
```

## Updated `generateShotListDraft` flow

**Before (current):** Generate shot descriptions → hallucinated text
**After:** `lookupShotReferences()` → returns anchored references → each shot in output carries `reference_id` + `reference_url`

```typescript
// generateShotListDraft now returns:
interface ShotListItem {
  description: string
  channel: string
  deliverable_ids: string[]
  reference_id: string           // NEW — FK to shot_type_references.id
  reference_url: string          // NEW — Cloudinary thumbnail URL
  angle: string                  // NEW — from reference taxonomy
}
```

The `reference_id` is stored in `shot_list.reference_id` — add this column to the migration ([IPI-183](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-183/shoot-data-001-ai-native-shoot-schema-rls-draft-table)).

# UI Changes

## Step 5 Wizard ([IPI-87](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-87/shoot-ux-004-shoot-wizard-6-step-ai-native-shoot-creation)) — visual shot picker

Each shot list row gains a thumbnail:

```
Shot # │ [thumbnail] │ Description          │ Angle      │ Channel
  1    │ [📷]        │ Model hands in-use   │ Close-up   │ IG ✓
  2    │ [📷]        │ Product hero 45°     │ 45°        │ SHP ✓
  3    │ [📷]        │ Lifestyle warm scene │ Full scene │ TT ✓

[Browse reference library →]
```

**"Browse reference library" modal** — DISCOVER-style UX:

* Category tabs: CLOTHING (GHOST) / CLOTHING (MODEL) / BEAUTY (PRODUCT) / BEAUTY (SWATCHES) / ACCESSORIES / HOME GOODS
* Image grid (3-column, hover shows angle + channel_fit)
* Click thumbnail → metadata panel slides in (like Squareshot's right panel)
* "SELECT A SHOT" button → replaces that row's reference in the shot list

## Shot List tab right panel ([IPI-86](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-86/shoot-ux-003-shoot-detail-page-ai-native-production-workspace))

When operator selects a shot row, right panel shows:

```
Selected: Shot #3 — Lifestyle warm
────────────────────────────────────
[reference thumbnail — 200px]

Angle:       Full scene
Background:  Lifestyle warm
Channel fit: TikTok, Instagram
Model type:  Full body

[Swap reference →]  ← opens DISCOVER modal
```

## New component: `ShotReferenceModal.tsx`

```
src/components/shoot/reference/
  ├── ShotReferenceModal.tsx     # full-screen DISCOVER browser
  ├── ReferenceCategoryTabs.tsx  # category filter tabs
  ├── ReferenceGrid.tsx          # image grid with hover state
  └── ReferenceDetailPanel.tsx   # metadata side panel + SELECT button
```

# Seeding Strategy

## Phase A — scaffold immediately (unblocks AI system)

Use Gemini to generate structured text descriptions for all 80–100 shot types. Upload placeholder/stock images to Cloudinary. `lookupShotReferences` works immediately with text-grounded suggestions even before real brand photography exists.

Seed script: `supabase/seed/shot-type-references.ts` — runs once, idempotent (`ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`).

## Phase B — enrich over time (automatic flywheel)

Every completed shoot where assets reach DNA score ≥80 can feed back:

```sql
-- Operator marks an approved asset as "reference quality"
-- Edge fn: promote-to-reference
INSERT INTO shot_type_references (category, subcategory, angle, description, reference_url, ...)
SELECT ... FROM shoot_assets WHERE id = $asset_id AND dna_score >= 80;
```

After 10–20 shoots, `lookupShotReferences` returns real iPix-brand photography instead of stock. This is the flywheel Squareshot cannot replicate — their 527 images are static.

# Edge Functions

| Function | Purpose |
| -- | -- |
| `lookup-shot-references` | Query `shot_type_references` by category + channel_fit. Called by Mastra `lookupShotReferences` tool. |
| `promote-to-reference` | (Phase B) Promote an approved shoot asset to the reference library. Requires `dna_score >= 80` check. |

# Mastra Integration

`lookupShotReferences` is a new tool registered on `production-planner`. It must be called inside `generateShotListDraft` before any shot descriptions are generated.

```typescript
// In production-planner tool registration:
tools: {
  lookupShotReferences,    // NEW
  planDeliverables,
  generateShotListDraft,   // UPDATED — calls lookupShotReferences internally
  estimateShootBudget,
  explainShootDnaAlerts,
  explainProductLinkingGaps,
}
```

# Dependencies

* [IPI-183](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-183/shoot-data-001-ai-native-shoot-schema-rls-draft-table) **SHOOT-DATA-001** — core shoot schema migration. `shot_list` table must exist before adding `reference_id` column. Run this migration in the same PR or after [IPI-183](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-183/shoot-data-001-ai-native-shoot-schema-rls-draft-table) lands.
* [IPI-87](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-87/shoot-ux-004-shoot-wizard-6-step-ai-native-shoot-creation) **SHOOT-UX-004** — Wizard Step 5 visual picker requires `reference_url` in shot list items.
* [IPI-86](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-86/shoot-ux-003-shoot-detail-page-ai-native-production-workspace) **SHOOT-UX-003** — Shot List tab right panel reference panel.
* [IPI-148](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-148/shoot-ai-001-shoot-planner-agent) **SHOOT-AI-001** — `production-planner` tool registration.

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `shot_type_references` table created with correct indexes and RLS
- [ ] 80+ rows seeded covering all 7 category tabs
- [ ] Reference images uploaded to `ipix/shot-references/` in Cloudinary
- [ ] `lookup-shot-references` edge fn deployed and queryable
- [ ] `lookupShotReferences` Mastra tool registered on `production-planner`
- [ ] `generateShotListDraft` output includes `reference_id` + `reference_url` per shot
- [ ] `shot_list` table has `reference_id` FK column
- [ ] Step 5 Wizard: each shot row shows reference thumbnail
- [ ] "Browse reference library" modal opens with category tabs + image grid
- [ ] Shot List detail tab right panel shows reference image for selected shot row
- [ ] Phase B: `promote-to-reference` edge fn validates `dna_score >= 80` before inserting

# Testing Checklist

- [ ] `lookupShotReferences({ product_type: 'beauty', channels: ['instagram_feed', 'shopify_pdp'] })` → returns ≥5 references with valid Cloudinary URLs
- [ ] `lookupShotReferences({ product_type: 'clothing', subcategory: 'ghost' })` → returns front/back/detail angles
- [ ] `generateShotListDraft` with beauty product → all shots have non-null `reference_url`

Title: IPI-BI-034 — Brand Intelligence: CopilotKit Actions
Description:
Wire the core Brand Intelligence capabilities as CopilotKit frontend actions so operators can trigger analysis, refresh profiles, compare competitors, and plan campaigns from the chat interface.

**Actions to register:**

1. `analyzeBrand(url, brandName?)` — triggers full brand intake workflow (IPI-BI-009 Mastra workflow) from chat. Shows progress via streaming.
2. `refreshProfile(brandId)` — re-runs brand-intelligence edge function for existing brand, invalidates TanStack Query cache, shows diff of changed fields.
3. `compareCompetitors(brandId)` — triggers competitor-intelligence agent for the active brand, streams competitor discovery progress.
4. `planCampaign(brandId, season, objective?)` — triggers campaign-planner agent, returns 3-5 campaign concepts in chat as selectable cards.
5. `navigateToBrand(brandId)` — navigates operator to `/app/brand/{brandId}` (extends existing `navigateTo` frontend tool).

**Implementation:**

* Register all 5 as CopilotKit `useFrontendTool` actions in `OperatorPanel`
* Wire `analyzeBrand` to Mastra workflow trigger via `callEdgeFunction`
* Wire `refreshProfile` to direct edge function call with cache invalidation
* Wire `planCampaign` to `campaign-planner` Mastra agent via `useAgent({ agentId: 'campaign-planner' })`
* SharedBrandState: `activeBrandId` propagated to all agents via CopilotKit shared state

**Acceptance criteria:**

* All 5 actions callable from CopilotKit chat sidebar
* `analyzeBrand` streams progress messages during analysis
* `planCampaign` returns structured campaign cards (not raw text) in chat
* `refreshProfile` shows before/after diff of changed Brand Profile fields
* `activeBrandId` persists across agent switches (production-planner → creative-director)

**Reuse:** Existing `useFrontendTool('navigateTo')` in OperatorPanel as the pattern. CopilotKit v2 conventions in `.claude/skills/copilotkit/references/upgrade/ipix-v2-conventions.md`.
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: IPI-BI-014 — Customer Persona Agent
Description:
Mastra agent that generates 2-3 psychographic customer personas from brand content, audience signals, and product positioning data.

**Scope:**

* New Mastra agent `customer-persona`
* Tool: `generatePersonas(brandId)` — reads `brands.ai_profile` + `brand_social_channels` + `brand_competitors` as context
* Gemini structured output: 3 personas, each with: `name`, `ageRange`, `gender`, `incomeRange`, `lifestyle`, `values[]`, `painPoints[]`, `motivations[]`, `channels[]`, `purchaseTrigger`, `avatarDescription`
* Writes to `brand_personas` table (new migration needed — slot 1/2/3, approved bool, UNIQUE(brand_id, slot))
* Working memory: `{ activeBrandId, generatedPersonas[], approvalStatus }`

**Schema migration required:**

* `brand_personas`: brand_id FK, slot (1/2/3), name, age_range, gender, income_range, lifestyle, values JSONB, pain_points JSONB, motivations JSONB, channels JSONB, purchase_trigger, avatar_description, approved bool default false. UNIQUE(brand_id, slot). RLS: SELECT via is_org_member.

**Acceptance criteria:**

* 3 distinct personas generated per brand
* Each persona covers all 11 fields
* Personas written to brand_personas with approved=false (HITL)
* Operator can approve individual personas via Brand Hub UI

**Blocked by:** IPI-BI-002 (brand-intelligence-v2 for full Brand Profile as context input)
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: IPI-BI-013 — Competitor Intelligence Agent
Description:
Mastra agent that identifies and analyzes 3-5 brand competitors via Gemini Google Search grounding + Firecrawl mini-crawl (20 pages per competitor).

**Scope:**

* New Mastra agent `competitor-intelligence`
* Step 1: Gemini Search grounding discovers 3-5 competitors from brand category + positioning
* Step 2: Firecrawl mini-crawl (20 pages max) per competitor
* Step 3: Gemini analysis produces `CompetitorProfile` per competitor: name, url, category, price_point, scores JSONB, strengths[], weaknesses[], social_presence[], unique_angles[], threat_level (low/medium/high)
* Writes to `brand_competitors` table (from IPI-BI-003 schema)
* Working memory: `{ activeBrandId, competitorUrls[], analyzedCount }`

**Acceptance criteria:**

* 3-5 competitors discovered and analyzed per brand
* Each competitor has threat_level assigned (low/medium/high)
* `brand_competitors` rows written with full CompetitorProfile
* Agent respects 20-page Firecrawl limit per competitor
* Agent run logged in `brand_agent_results`

**Blocked by:** IPI-BI-001 (Firecrawl), IPI-BI-003 (brand_competitors schema), IPI-BI-002 (brand-intelligence-v2 baseline)

**Reuse:** Google Search grounding already active in brand-intelligence edge function. Firecrawl mini-crawl reuses same SDK as full crawl with `limit: 20` param.
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: IPI-BI-012 — Collection Intelligence Agent
Description:
Mastra agent that analyzes brand collections for seasonal themes, styling direction, editorial positioning, and collection naming patterns.

**Scope:**

* New Mastra agent `collection-intelligence`
* Tool: `analyzeCollections(brandId, siteUrl)` — Firecrawl crawl of collection landing pages + lookbook pages
* Structured output: `collections[]` (name, season, year, theme, productCount, editorialTone), `namingConvention`, `seasonalCadence`, `stylingDirection`, `editorialPositioning`
* Feed collection intelligence into `brand-intelligence-v2` edge function as additional context
* Write to `brands.ai_profile.collectionIntelligence` JSONB

**Acceptance criteria:**

* Agent identifies all named collections from crawled pages
* Seasonal cadence detected (SS/AW/Resort/Pre-fall/drops)
* Editorial tone extracted per collection
* Naming convention pattern identified

**Blocked by:** IPI-BI-001 (Firecrawl), IPI-BI-011 (Product Intelligence — shared crawl data)

**Reuse:** Firecrawl crawl output from IPI-BI-011 (share raw_data, don't re-crawl). Gemini structured output schema pattern from existing edge function.

Title: IPI-BI-011 — Product Intelligence Agent
Description:
Mastra agent that crawls and analyzes a brand's product catalog: categories, pricing tiers, SKU count, materials, seasonality, size range, sustainability labels.

**Scope:**

* New Mastra agent `product-intelligence`
* Tool: `analyzeProductCatalog(brandId, collectionsUrl)` — Firecrawl crawl of `/collections/*` pages

Title: IPI2-184 — AI Agent Control Center: /app/agents workspace
Description:
## Goal

Build `/app/agents` — a visible AI workforce control panel so operators can see, configure, and run their AI agents directly.

## Why

Currently AI agents are invisible infrastructure. Operators should be able to:

* See what agents are available and what each does
* View recent agent runs and outputs
* Trigger agents manually
* Configure agent behaviour (tone, priorities)
* Monitor token usage / cost

## Agent Roster (8 agents)

| Agent | Purpose | Status |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Brand Strategist | URL → brand profile, DNA scoring | Partially built (brand-intelligence edge fn) |
| Creative Director | Campaign briefs, content strategy | Mastra agent (no tools) |
| Production Planner | Shoot planning, crew, scheduling | Mastra agent (no tools) |
| Marketplace Manager | Shopify/Amazon product content | Not built |
| Content Manager | Social content calendar | Not built |
| Asset DNA Agent | Image scoring via audit-asset-dna | Edge fn built, not exposed |
| Matching Agent | Brand ↔ creator/vendor matching | Not built |
| Analytics Agent | KPI insights, trend reports | Not built |

## Page Layout

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  AI Workforce                          [+ Add Agent]    │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  ● Brand Strategist          Last run: 2h ago  [Run]   │
│    Analyzes brand websites, generates DNA scores        │
│                                                         │
│  ● Creative Director         Last run: 5h ago  [Run]   │
│    Generates campaign briefs and content strategy       │
│                                                         │
│  ○ Marketplace Manager       Not configured    [Setup] │
│    Manages Shopify/Amazon product listings              │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Recent Runs                                            │
│  Brand Strategist · MAZ · 86/100 · 2h ago             │
│  Creative Director · FW26 Brief · 5h ago               │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Data

* Agent config: stored in new `agent_configs` table or Mastra registry
* Run history: `ai_agent_logs` (already exists)
* Token usage: `ai_agent_logs.tokens_used`

## Routes

* `/app/agents` — agent list + status
* `/app/agents/[id]` — agent detail, config, run history

## Supabase tables

Reads `ai_agent_logs` for run history. May add `agent_configs` for per-agent settings.

## Definition of Done

- [ ] `/app/agents` lists all 8 agents with status (active/not configured)
- [ ] Each card shows last run time + output summary from `ai_agent_logs`
- [ ] "Run" button triggers agent via CopilotKit action
- [ ] Run history table (agent, brand, score/output, timestamp, tokens)
- [ ] Empty state for agents with no runs

## Blocked by

* IPI-13 (Brand Hub — agents need brand context)
* IPI-14 (CopilotKit brand-aware actions)

## Related

* IPI-12 — Brand Intelligence tools
* IPI-17 — Executive Dashboard (surfaces agent recommendations)

Title: IPI2-179 — CopilotKit: brand-aware agent actions + workflow suggestions

## Problem

The `production-planner` agent has a one-line instruction ("Help operators plan shoots") and no brand context. It behaves as a generic assistant. `useConfigureSuggestions` shows static workspace chips unrelated to the user's brand.

## Goal

Make the agent brand-aware: update system prompts, inject brand context via `useAgentContext`, and make suggestion chips dynamic based on brand scores.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `production-planner` system prompt updated per spec §8:
  - Detects brand name/URL mentions → always triggers `analyzeBrandUrl` tool (never prose-only)
  - Routes to correct next workflow based on score thresholds (visual <70 → asset audit, etc.)
  - Greets returning users with brand name + score
- [ ] `creative-director` system prompt updated per spec §8:
  - Checks for brand context before generating creative content
  - Prompts for brand URL if no brand is loaded
- [ ] `useAgentContext` in `OperatorPanel` injects brand data when on `/app/brand/*`:

  ```ts
  { brand_id, brand_name, production_readiness, scores }
  ```
- [ ] `useConfigureSuggestions` dynamically updates:
  - No brand: "Analyze My Brand", "What can iPix do for me?"
  - brand.production_readiness ≥ 80: "Plan FW26 shoot for [[brand.name](<http://brand.name>)]", "Generate moodboard"
  - commerce_readiness < 70: "Improve marketplace presence", "Audit Instagram"
- [ ] `navigateTo` tool extended: accepts `{ brandId?: string, path?: string, section?: string }`

## Files to edit

* `app/src/mastra/agents/index.ts` — update both agent instructions
* `app/src/components/operator-panel/operator-panel.tsx` — dynamic suggestions + brand context
* `app/src/mastra/prompts/` — extract prompts to files for maintainability

`docs/vercel/10-onboarding.md` §3 Flow 5 + Flow 6, §6 tasks #10-12, §8 full agent prompt text
