Title: SCR-21b · Booking Wizard draft persistence
Description:
## IPI-410D — Booking Wizard Draft Persistence

**Priority:** P3 · **Track:** Frontend UX
**Depends on:** [IPI-410](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-410/scr-21-booking-wizard) (Booking Wizard — Done)

## Purpose

Preserve Booking Wizard progress across page refresh and accidental tab close. Currently all wizard state is in React useState — any navigation loss resets to step 0.

## Current state

`booking-wizard-workspace.tsx` stores all state in React `useState` hooks (step, dateStart, dateEnd, rateQuotedInput, messageDraft, draftFetched, etc.). There is no localStorage/sessionStorage backup, no Supabase draft table, no recovery mechanism.

## Deliverables

- [ ] sessionStorage MVP: persist { step, dateStart, dateEnd, rate, message, draftFetched } on field changes
- [ ] Recovery banner on reload: "You have a draft booking request. Resume where you left off?"
- [ ] "Start over" clears saved draft
- [ ] Successful send clears saved draft
- [ ] Reject/Cancel clears saved draft
- [ ] Expiration strategy: discard saved drafts older than 24h
- [ ] Multi-tab safety: last-write-wins is acceptable for MVP (no conflict resolution)
- [ ] No sensitive data stored (only wizard fields — no auth tokens, no booking IDs from prior requests)
- [ ] Tests: reload recovery, draft clear on send, draft clear on start-over, stale draft discard

## Future integration: Planner

Once the Planner ([IPI-476](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-476/planner-schema-and-reusable-engine-core)+) is live, a persisted draft that was recovered and sent would feed into:

```
Booking Approved → Planner Instance → Tasks → Notifications → Calendar → Shoot Timeline
```

Draft persistence ensures the operator doesn't lose context between wizard completion and Planner task creation. If the draft survives to send, the planner can pick up the booking event via notification/trigger.

## Out of scope

* Supabase draft storage table (not needed for MVP — sessionStorage suffices)
* Cross-device draft sync
* Conflict resolution for multi-tab edits
* Booking agent draft recovery (agent memory is ephemeral per process)

## Proof

```bash
cd app && npx vitest run src/components/booking/
```
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: BE-RT2: Extend get_shoot_detail RPC to include resource_type
Description:
## Purpose

PR #251 ([IPI-371](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-371/design-054c-shoot-detail-remaining-tab-parity-restart-v2-image-first) Assets tab) added an Assets tab that renders `shoot.shoot_assets` via `next/image`. The `get_shoot_detail` RPC doesn't select the table's own `resource_type` column (missing from the `json_build_object` in the assets aggregate at line 84). The frontend uses a stopgap heuristic `isVideoFormat()` that checks file extensions (mp4/mov/webm/m4v/avi) — works for common cases but isn't as reliable as the real `resource_type` column from the database.

This task replaces the format heuristic with the database's `resource_type` field.

## Migration

Extend `get_shoot_detail` RPC's assets aggregate (currently lines 79-93 of `20260703025133_extend_get_shoot_detail_rpc.sql`) to add `'resource_type', a.resource_type` to the `json_build_object`:

```sql
'assets', coalesce((
    select json_agg(json_build_object(
        'id', a.id,
        'url', a.url,
        'cloudinary_id', a.cloudinary_id,
        'format', a.format,
        'width', a.width,
        'height', a.height,
        'dna_score', a.dna_score,
        'status', a.status::text,
        'resource_type', a.resource_type,   -- ← ADD
        'created_at', a.created_at
    ) order by a.created_at desc)
    from shoot.shoot_assets a
    where a.shoot_id = p_shoot_id
), '[]'::json),
```

## Data sources

| Source | Status | Notes |
| -- | -- | -- |
| `shoot.shoot_assets.resource_type` | 🟢 Live | Column exists, enum: image/video/raw/auto |

## TypeScript changes

Add `resource_type: string | null` to `ShootDetailAsset` in `app/src/lib/shoot/get-shoot-detail.ts`.

## UI changes

* `app/src/components/shoot/shoot-detail-tabs/assets-tab.tsx`: change `const isVideo = isVideoFormat(a.format)` → `const isVideo = a.resource_type === 'video'`
* `app/src/components/shoot/shoot-detail-format.ts`: remove `isVideoFormat()` function and `VIDEO_FORMATS` constant

## AC

- [ ] **Migration:** `get_shoot_detail` RPC updated to select `a.resource_type` in the assets JSON aggregate
- [ ] **Types:** `ShootDetailAsset` includes `resource_type: string | null`
- [ ] **UI:** Assets tab uses `resource_type === 'video'` instead of format heuristic
- [ ] **Cleanup:** `isVideoFormat()` and `VIDEO_FORMATS` removed from `shoot-detail-format.ts`
- [ ] `npm run typecheck` passes
- [ ] `npm test` passes
- [ ] `npm run build` passes

## Out of scope

* Video player UI (thumbnail + play button) — deferred to separate task
* Adding `resource_type` to other RPCs — only `get_shoot_detail` here
* Other RPCs that query `shoot.shoot_assets` — not changed

## Skills

`ipix-supabase` · `task-verifier`
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: SCR-25 · Role Dashboards
Description:
**Plain English:** Build two role-specific dashboards from one DC design. `/app/model` = talent's self-view (my bookings, my shoots). `/app/roster` = agency/agent view (all talent in roster, upcoming bookings). Two routes, one visual layout.

## Route Mapping

| Route | Audience | Content |
| -- | -- | -- |
| `/app/model` | Talent/model | My upcoming bookings, KPI bar, quick actions |
| `/app/roster` | Agency/agent | All talent in roster, upcoming shoots, add talent |

## User Journey

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  A[Navigate to /app/model or /app/roster] --> B[Role gate check]
  B -->|Talent role| C[Model dashboard]
  B -->|Agency role| D[Roster dashboard]
  C --> C1[KPI: shoot count, pending approvals]
  C --> C2[My upcoming bookings list]
  C --> C3[Quick actions]
  D --> D1[KPI: talent count, upcoming shoots]
  D --> D2[Roster talent grid]
  D --> D3[Add talent CTA]
```

## Dashboard Sections

| Section | Model | Roster |
| -- | -- | -- |
| KPI bar | Upcoming shoots, pending approvals, next booking | Total talent, upcoming shoots, pending availability |
| Upcoming bookings | My bookings list | All talent bookings |
| Quick actions | View profile, check availability | Add talent, manage roster |

## Route & Grid

* **Routes:** `/app/model` + `/app/roster` (both greenfield — create)
* **Shell:** fixed-3col, grid `56px | minmax(0,1fr) | 340px`

## API / RPC Verification

| Endpoint | Status |
| -- | -- |
| list_bookings | 🟢 live |
| talent_profiles table | 🟡 verify |
| get_talent_profile | 🟢 live |
| KPI aggregation | 🟡 needs RPC or client-side count |
| Upcoming shoots query | 🟡 needs or join bookings + shoots |

## States

| State | AC |
| -- | -- |
| Populated (model) | KPI bar 3-col, upcoming bookings, quick actions |
| Populated (roster) | Talent list, upcoming shoots, add talent CTA |
| Loading | 3 shimmer KPI cards |
| Empty (no bookings) | "No upcoming bookings" + CTA |
| Empty (no talent in roster) | "No talent in roster" + add CTA |
| Error | ErrorState with retry |

## Component Reuse

EmptyState, ErrorState, Skeleton, StatusChip from ui/. KPI kit inline (RF-A6 deferred).

## Files

* `app/src/app/(operator)/app/model/page.tsx` (new)
* `app/src/app/(operator)/app/roster/page.tsx` (new)
* `app/src/components/dashboards/model-dashboard-workspace.tsx` (new)
* `app/src/components/dashboards/roster-dashboard-workspace.tsx` (new)
* `app/src/components/dashboards/dashboards.module.css` (new)

## Playwright Matrix

| State | Route | 1280px | 390px |
| -- | -- | -- | -- |
| Populated | /app/model | KPI 3-col, bookings list | KPI 2x2 stack |
| Populated | /app/roster | Talent grid, shoots list | Single column |
| Empty (no bookings) | /app/model | "No upcoming bookings" + CTA | Same |
| Error | Both | ErrorState + retry | Same |

## Out of Scope

Shell rebuild, backend migrations, mobile shell, RF-A6 KPI kit extraction, real-time updates, booking management (create/cancel)

## Risk

| Risk | Mitigation |
| -- | -- |
| KPI aggregation needs new RPC | Client-side count from list_bookings MVP |
| Role detection not implemented | Use profiles table |
| Two routes = double pages | Shared KPIBar/BookingList/QuickActions components |

Title: SCR-24 · Talent Onboarding
Description:
**Plain English:** Build the Talent Onboarding wizard at /app/talent/profile. 3-step wizard (Profile → Portfolio → Rates) with Mastra agent drafting bio/rates behind HITL. Agent never writes to DB directly — operator approves first. Shell: split-2col (400px form | preview/guidance).

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  A[Navigate to /app/talent/profile] --> B{New profile?}
  B -->|Yes| C[Wizard step 1: Profile form]

  E --> F[Approve or edit bio]
  F --> G[Step 2: Portfolio images]
  G --> H[Step 3: Rate card]
  H --> I[Agent drafts rates → FieldReview]
  I --> J[Approve → Commit insert]
  J --> K[Redirect to talent profile]
```

* **Route:** `/app/talent/profile` (greenfield — create)
* **Shell:** split-2col, grid `400px | minmax(0,1fr)`

| Endpoint | Status |
| -- | -- |

## States

| Step 1 Profile | Name, bio, headshot upload form |

| Step 3 Rates | Rate card per shoot type |
| Loading | Shimmer form fields |
| AI drafting | "Drafting bio..." spinner |

| Error | ErrorState with retry |

Title: BE-ACT1 · Org activity log + list_org_activity RPC
Description:
## BE-ACT1 — Org activity log + list_org_activity RPC

**Track:** Backend · **Priority:** P3 · **Complexity:** M · **SSOT:** `tasks/backend/BE-ACT1-org-activity-log.md`
**Branch:** `ipi/act1-org-activity-log` · **Unblocks:** SCR-18 ([IPI-408](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-408/scr-18-collaboration-activity-audit))

## Objective

Org-scoped append-only activity feed for `/app/activity` (SCR-18). **New** `org_activity_events` **table** + `list_org_activity` **RPC** federating `crm_activities` + `agent_decision_log` into one cursor-paginated feed.

## Gap analysis

| Source | Live | Use |
| -- | -- | -- |
| `org_activity_events` | 🔴 | Native comments, mentions, edits, approvals, system |
| `crm_activities` | 🟢 | Map to comment/edit category |
| `agent_decision_log` | 🟢 | Map to approval category |
| `list_org_activity` RPC | 🔴 | Single query for React |

## Design decision

| Option | Verdict |
| -- | -- |
| Extend `crm_activities.type` | ❌ Breaks CRM anchor + semantics |
| Federation-only view (no new table) | ❌ Can't store open/resolved comments or @mentions |
| `org_activity_events` **+ federation RPC** | ✅ Matches SCR-18 DC filters + immutable audit |

## Proposed schema

```sql
create table public.org_activity_events (
  id            uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  org_id        uuid not null references public.organizations(id) on delete cascade,
  actor_id      uuid references public.profiles(id) on delete set null,
  event_type    text not null check (event_type in ('comment','mention','approval','edit','upload','system')),
  entity_type   text not null,      -- brand, shoot, campaign, asset, booking, deal
  entity_id     uuid,
  summary       text not null,      -- "edited the brief for Adidas Q2 campaign"
  body          text,               -- comment text / diff snippet
  metadata      jsonb not null default '{}',
  status        text not null default 'open' check (status in ('open','resolved','na')),
  created_at    timestamptz not null default now()
);
```

Append-only. No `updated_at`. `na` for non-actionable rows. `resolve_org_activity_event` RPC for open→resolved.

Indexes: `(org_id, created_at desc)`, `(org_id, event_type)`, `(org_id, status)` where `status='open'`.

## RLS

| Policy | Rule |
| -- | -- |
| SELECT | `is_org_member(org_id)` |
| INSERT | `is_org_member(org_id)` and `actor_id = auth.uid()` |
| UPDATE | none (use RPC) |
| DELETE | none (append-only) |

### `list_org_activity(p_org_id, p_filter, p_scope, p_cursor, p_limit)`

UNION ALL across:

1. `org_activity_events` — primary
2. `crm_activities` — map type→category
3. `agent_decision_log` — map→approval

Returns stable ActivityFeedRow shape.

### `resolve_org_activity_event(p_id, p_org_id)`

Sets status=resolved. Org-member gated.

## Files affected

* `supabase/migrations/*_org_activity_events.sql` (migration-only PR)
* App types regenerate

## Do NOT

* Ship triggers on every table in ACT1
* Ship React for SCR-18 in same PR
* Consulted: ipix-supabase, task-verifier

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Migration applied on remote
- [ ] RLS per `is_org_member` pattern
- [ ] `list_org_activity` returns federated rows
- [ ] `resolve_org_activity_event` works
- [ ] Indexes on filter columns
- [ ] `infisical run -- npm run supabase:verify-rls` green
- [ ] Types regenerated

## Verification

```bash
infisical run -- npm run supabase:verify && npm run supabase:verify-rls
```

```sql
select to_regclass('public.org_activity_events');
select proname from pg_proc where proname in ('list_org_activity','resolve_org_activity_event');
```

Title: CRM-ADV-007 · AI Digital Twin (entity preference profiles)
Description:
## CRM-ADV-007 · AI Digital Twin (entity preference profiles)

**In plain terms:** Maintains a continuously updated profile for every brand, person, and organization — personalized recommendations and planning. Example: *"This brand prefers editorial shoots, outdoor locations, and quarterly campaigns."*

**Blocked by:** IPI-370 · IPI-367 (won → brand conversion) · **Related:** IPI-19 (Brand DNA) · IPI-376 · CRM-ADV-003

**Skills:** `mastra` · `gemini` · `ipix-supabase` · `frontend-design` · `mermaid-diagrams`

**Labels:** CRM · AI · BRAND · DESIGN

**Milestone:** CRM-M6 · Advanced Hub
**Spec:** `tasks/crm/07-relationship-hub-ai-roadmap.md` · `tasks/crm/05-crm-prd.md` §5.5

---

## Design Reference

**Primary:** `Universal design prompt/Brand Detail.v2.image-first.dc.html` · brand profile + DNA sections
**CRM:** `app/design/CRM/02b-crm-company-detail.md` · Overview tab preference chips
**Component:** `EvidenceBlock` with confidence badge (`recommendation` pattern from `design-plan.md`)

---

## Dependencies

**Required:**

* IPI-370 — CRM MVP stable
* Brand record exists post-conversion (IPI-367) or company profile for prospects
* Structured facts source: `crm_activities`, deal history, Brand DNA fields (when present)

**Optional:**

* IPI-376 — graph edges enrich "prefers agency X"
* CRM-POST-010 — timeline feeds preference extraction

**Setup:**

* V1: JSONB `preference_profile` on `crm_companies` + `brands.metadata.twin` (migration) OR derive-on-read without persist (document choice in PR)
* Eval harness stub — no ML training in V1; rules + LLM synthesis from evidence only

---

## Scope

**Not a separate chatbot** — structured profile object consumed by `crm-assistant`, `production-planner`, and IntelligencePanel.

* Mastra tool `getEntityTwin({ entityType, entityId })` → `{ preferences[], constraints[], cadence?, confidence, evidence[] }`
* Background job (optional V1.1): recompute twin on new activity / DNA audit — Post-MVP cron, not blocking AC
* UI: "Preferences" section on Company Detail + Brand Hub — read-only chips + "why" expander (`EvidenceBlock`)
* **Consent:** org-scoped only; no PII export; human can dismiss incorrect preference (soft-delete flag)

**Not in V1:** Full autonomous planning, synthetic persona chat, cross-tenant learning, replacing Brand DNA audit

---

## Sequence Diagram

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as Operator
    participant P as IntelligencePanel
    participant T as getEntityTwin
    participant S as Supabase
    participant G as Gemini

    U->>P: Open Company / Brand detail
    P->>T: getEntityTwin(companyId)
    T->>S: activities, deals, brand DNA, shoots
    S-->>T: structured rows
    T->>T: rule extract (cadence, locations, style tags)
    T->>G: synthesize preferences from facts only
    G-->>T: preferences + evidence refs
    T-->>P: twin object
    P-->>U: chips + confidence + evidence
```

---

## State Diagram

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Loading
    Loading --> Empty: no history / new company
    Loading --> Computed: enough signals
    Loading --> Partial: sparse data
    Empty --> Computed: after activities accumulate
    Partial --> Computed: more evidence
    Computed --> Stale: new activity since cached_at
    Stale --> Computed: refresh
    Computed --> Dismissed: operator hides preference
    Dismissed --> Computed: re-enable
```

---

## Component Tree

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    Detail[Company / Brand Detail] --> Overview[Overview Tab]
    Overview --> TwinSection[PreferenceProfileSection]
    TwinSection --> Chips[Preference chips + confidence]
    TwinSection --> Evidence[EvidenceBlock — why]
    TwinSection --> Dismiss[Dismiss incorrect — HITL]
    Agent[crm-assistant] --> Tool[getEntityTwin]
    Tool --> TwinSection
```

---

## User Journey

```mermaid
journey
    title Brand Manager plans shoot using Digital Twin
    section Discover
      Open brand after won deal: 5: Brand Manager
      See preference chips editorial outdoor quarterly: 5: Brand Manager
    section Trust
      Expand evidence see 3 past shoots cited: 5: Brand Manager
      Dismiss wrong preference: 4: Brand Manager
    section Act
      Ask agent plan Q2 campaign: 5: Brand Manager
      Agent respects twin constraints in draft: 5: Brand Manager
```

---

## Wireframes

```
Desktop — Company Detail · Preferences (Digital Twin)
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Adidas AG · Overview                                        │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Preference profile (AI)          Confidence: 82%            │
│ ┌────────────┐ ┌────────────┐ ┌──────────────┐              │
│ │ Editorial  │ │ Outdoor loc│ │ Quarterly    │              │
│ │ shoots     │ │ preferred  │ │ campaigns    │              │
│ └────────────┘ └────────────┘ └──────────────┘              │
│ Evidence ▾ — 3 shoots, 2 deal notes (2024–2026)           │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ [Dismiss incorrect]  [Refresh from latest activity]         │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## API Wiring

| Route / Tool | Status | Auth | Returns | RLS |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| `getEntityTwin` Mastra tool | 🔴 create | server agent | `{ preferences[], evidence[], confidence }` | ✅ |
| Migration `preference_profile jsonb` | 🔴 optional | — | column on `crm_companies` / brands metadata | ✅ org policies |
| GET `/api/crm/companies/[id]/twin` | 🔴 optional | `withOperatorAuth` | cached twin | ✅ |

---

## User Stories

### Story 1: Operator sees preference summary at a glance

**As a** Brand Manager  
**I want** preference chips on the company/brand profile  
**So that** I plan shoots without re-reading two years of notes.

**Acceptance:** ≥3 preference chips when ≥5 activities exist; each chip links to evidence ids.

### Story 2: Operator corrects a wrong inference

**As an** Operator  
**I want** to dismiss an incorrect preference  
**So that** future recommendations don't repeat the mistake.

**Acceptance:** Dismiss persists; twin regen excludes dismissed key; audit log entry.

### Story 3: Agent uses twin in planning conversation

**As a** Creative Director  
**I want** the copilot to cite twin preferences when suggesting campaign timing  
**So that** recommendations feel personalized not generic.

**Acceptance:** `crm-assistant` tool call includes twin; response mentions cited preference with evidence.

---

## Acceptance

- [ ] **A1** `getEntityTwin` unit test — golden company fixture — proof: vitest, deterministic rules layer
- [ ] **A2** UI renders preferences + confidence + evidence expander — proof: screenshot
- [ ] **A3** Dismiss preference persists and excludes from next tool response — proof: integration test
- [ ] **A4** No twin data leaks across orgs — proof: RLS test
- [ ] **A5** Migration reviewed + `infisical run -- npm run supabase:verify-rls` if schema change
- [ ] **A6** `cd app && npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm test` green

### Verify

```bash
cd app && npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm test
infisical run -- npm run supabase:verify-rls
```

## AIOR-017b — Postgres Durable Stream Cache

**Audit sync 2026-06-30** · Mastra Plan v1.2

### Problem

**Not the same as** [IPI-129](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-129) **✅ Done.**

| Layer | Status | Issue |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Workflow + memory PostgresStore | ✅ Done | IPI-129 / IPI-134 |
| Durable agent **stream** event cache | 🟡 In-memory | This issue |

`app/src/mastra/durable.ts` uses `createDurableAgent` with default `InMemoryServerCache`. Stream replay (`observe(runId, offset)`) is lost on deploy/restart.

Code comment still says "Upgrade when IPI-129 lands" — **misleading**; IPI-129 shipped workflow PG only.

### Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Wire Postgres-backed cache (or Mastra-supported persistent cache) for durable agent stream events
- [ ] `observe(runId, offset)` survives app restart in staging with `DATABASE_URL`
- [ ] Update `durable.ts` comment — remove stale IPI-129 reference
- [ ] `durable.test.ts` + integration note in PR

### Dependencies

**Blocked by:** [IPI-133](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-133) durable agents ✅

**Requires:** `DATABASE_URL` in prod/staging (same pooler as IPI-129)

**Phase:** Post-MVP / P2

Snapshot total: **225** lints (36 INFO · 187 WARN · 2 ERROR). Re-run advisors after remediation.

---

Title: FIX · Provider options alignment across Mastra tools (gateway-era)
Description:
## FIX · Provider options alignment across Mastra tools (gateway-era)

**Was:** Gemini `thinkingLevel` only — **renamed** per Mastra × Cloudflare audit (2026-07-09).

**Audit SSOT:** `tasks/cloudflare/mastra/mastra-audit.md`

### Goal

Single helper for provider-specific generation options — **not** Gemini-only `thinkingBudget` / `thinkingLevel` scattered in tools.

### Scope

| Surface | Today | Action |
| -- | -- | -- |
| `resolveProviderOptions()` in `models.ts` | Gemini-only branch | Extend for gateway / provider-aware opts |
| `suggestShootBrief.ts` | `thinkingBudget: 0` | Use `resolveProviderOptions()` |
| `visual-identity.ts`, `social-discovery.ts` | partial | Align via helper |
| Edge `supabase/functions/_shared/gemini.ts` | `thinkingLevel` | **Out of scope** (edge) |

### Provider rules

* **Gemini:** `thinkingLevel` via `resolveProviderOptions()` when `AI_PROVIDER=gemini`
* **Groq / Workers AI / Gateway:** omit Gemini-shaped options (no-op or tier-specific later)
* Never mix `thinkingLevel` + `thinkingBudget` on same Gemini 3 request (400)

### Steps

1. Centralize in `app/src/mastra/models.ts` → `resolveProviderOptions(tier?, task?)`
2. Migrate Mastra tools/agents to use helper only
3. `cd app && npm test` — `models.test.ts`, tool tests green

### Blocked by

* IPI-223 model registry stable (done) · IPI-457 merge (for full provider enum)

*Source:* `linear/issues/IPI-240-provider-options-alignment.md`

Title: IPI-218C: Approval queue count + Supabase Realtime badge
Description:
## Context

Follow-up to [IPI-218](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-218/3-panel-operator-layout-wire-right-panel-to-copilotkit-context) (PR #127). Adds a live pending-approval badge to `BrandContextPanel` using Supabase Realtime as a **refresh trigger only** — API is always source of truth.

---

## Schema (verified against migrations)

```
brand_intake_drafts.status values (confirmed in approve/route.ts):
  pending           — initial draft state
  pending_approval  — AI draft awaiting HITL review  ← count THIS
  approved          — HITL approved
  rejected          — HITL rejected

brands.intake_status (separate column, different concern):
  draft_ready | analysis_running | crawl_running | ready

Approval UI: /app/brand/[id]  (brand detail page)
  — NOT /app/brand?status=pending_review  (that param does not exist)
  — NOT /app/brand  (brand list, no filter support)

RLS: brand_intake_drafts row access scoped via brands.user_id = auth.uid()
```

---

## Wireframe

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│ BRAND CONTEXT PANEL  (320px)        │
│ ┌───────────────────────────────┐   │
│ │ Acme Corp  ●3      [active]   │   │  ← orange dot + count when > 0
│ └───────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                     │
│  ⚠  3 pending approvals             │
│                    [Review →]       │  ← links to /app/brand/{brandId}
│                                     │
│  ─────────────────────────────────  │
│  DNA SCORES                         │
│  ████████░░  Visual    82           │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘

Badge states
  count = 0       → badge hidden, no approval row
  count > 0       → orange dot + count; approval row visible
  RT event fires  → debounced refetch → new count from API
  RT fails        → static count from initial fetch still renders
```

---

## Data flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant P as BrandContextPanel
    participant A as GET /api/brands/[id]
    participant DB as Supabase
    participant RT as Supabase Realtime

    P->>A: fetch on mount (brandId)
    A->>DB: brands row + brand_scores + COUNT(pending_approval drafts, head:true)
    DB-->>A: {brand, scores, pendingCount}
    A-->>P: initial render

    P->>RT: subscribe(brand-approvals-{brandId}, filter: brand_id=eq.{brandId})
    Note over RT: INSERT/UPDATE on brand_intake_drafts for this brand
    RT-->>P: event (payload ignored — notification only)
    P->>A: debounced refetch (300ms) → update pendingCount from API
    A-->>P: fresh count

    P->>RT: unsubscribe + remove channel on brandId change or unmount
```

## Badge state machine

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Hidden   : initial fetch → pendingCount = 0
    Hidden  --> Visible : pendingCount > 0 (fetch result)
    Visible --> Hidden  : refetch → pendingCount = 0
    Visible --> Visible : refetch → new count
    Visible --> [*]     : unmount / brandId change → unsubscribe
    Hidden  --> [*]     : unmount / brandId change → unsubscribe
```

---

## User stories

* As an operator, I see a live badge showing how many brand drafts need approval so I never miss a pending HITL review.
* As an operator, the badge updates automatically when a draft moves to `pending_approval` so I do not need to refresh.
* As an operator, clicking the badge takes me to the brand's detail page where I can approve or reject the draft.

---

## Acceptance Criteria

**API —** `GET /api/brands/[id]`

- [ ] Response includes `pendingCount: number` — count of `brand_intake_drafts` where `status = 'pending_approval'` and `brand_id = id`
- [ ] Count query uses `{ count: 'exact', head: true }` — no row data loaded
- [ ] `pendingCount` respects RLS (operator only sees their own brand's count)
- [ ] `pendingCount = 0` when brand exists but has no pending_approval drafts

**UI — badge**

- [ ] Badge hidden when `pendingCount = 0`
- [ ] Orange dot + numeric count shown in panel header when `pendingCount > 0`
- [ ] Approval row ("N pending approvals · Review →") shown when count > 0
- [ ] "Review →" navigates to `/app/brand/{brandId}` (brand detail page with approval UI)

**Realtime**

- [ ] Subscription uses `filter: brand_id=eq.{brandId}` to scope events to this brand
- [ ] Channel name: `brand-approvals-{brandId}`
- [ ] RT event triggers a **debounced refetch** (≥300ms) — payload is ignored, API is source of truth
- [ ] Moving a draft to `pending_approval` increments badge (via refetch) within ~2s
- [ ] Approving a draft (`status → approved`) decrements badge (via refetch) within ~2s
- [ ] Switching brand: old channel removed **before** new channel is created (no duplicate subscriptions)
- [ ] Unmounting `BrandContextPanel`: channel is removed (`supabase.removeChannel()`)
- [ ] After 10 brand switches: no accumulated zombie subscriptions

**Failure handling**

- [ ] If Realtime subscription fails, badge still shows from initial API fetch (no crash, log warning)
- [ ] If refetch fails after RT event, existing count is preserved (no reset to 0)

**Implementation notes**

* `createSupabaseBrowserClient()` for Realtime (client-side only)
* `createSupabaseServerClient()` for API count (server-side, RLS-enforced)
* Status to count: `pending_approval` — NOT `pending_review` (that value does not exist in schema)
* Review link: `/app/brand/${brandId}` — NOT `/app/brand?status=pending_review`
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: UX P2: AI activity timeline — live history of crawl, analysis, approval, and edits
Description:
## Problem

The Activity tab exists but shows a limited, static event list built from `buildActivityTimeline()` using only `brands.created_at` and `brands.intake_status`. It does not show:

* When crawls ran and how many pages were found
* When analysis completed and what model was used
* Who approved/rejected a draft and when
* What manual edits were made to the profile

## Expected behaviour

A rich, live timeline in the Activity tab:

```
Today
  ✓  Draft approved by S.K.               11:04 AM
  ⚙  AI analysis completed (Gemini 2.5)   10:58 AM  →  View draft
  ⚙  Website crawled — 14 pages found     10:51 AM
  ✏  Tagline edited manually              09:32 AM  →  Undo

Yesterday
  ✓  Brand created                        3:14 PM
```

Each event is a row with: icon, description, time, and optional action link.

## Implementation

* Extend `buildActivityTimeline()` to pull from:
  * `brand_crawls` — crawl start/end, pages found
  * `ai_agent_logs` — analysis runs, model used, duration
  * `brand_intake_drafts` — approval/rejection events with `approved_at`, `rejected_at`
  * `brand_profile_history` (IPI-P2 undo issue) — manual edits
* Add these to the page query (already queries `brand_crawls`)
* Group events by date, render as timeline with icons
* Realtime: subscribe to `brand_crawls` + `brand_intake_drafts` so timeline updates without page refresh during active analysis

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Crawl events shown with page count
- [ ] Analysis completion events shown with model name and duration
- [ ] Draft approve/reject events shown with actor name and timestamp
- [ ] Manual profile edits shown (requires IPI P2 version history)
- [ ] Events grouped by date
- [ ] Live updates during active crawl/analysis via Realtime subscription
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: UX P2: Undo and version history for all AI and operator changes
Description:
## Problem

Once a draft is approved or a profile field is edited, there is no way to go back. If an operator approves the wrong draft or the AI overwrites a manually corrected field, the change is permanent.

This violates UX principle #10: *Everything is undoable — no destructive AI action without a way back.*

## Expected behaviour

* **Undo toast** — after any write action (approve, apply draft, edit field), show a 5-second undo toast: *"Draft applied. Undo →"*
* **Version history** — the Activity tab shows a list of all profile versions with timestamps. Clicking a version shows what it contained. An "Restore this version" button reinstates it.
* **Soft deletes** — before overwriting `ai_profile`, copy the current value to `brand_profile_history` table with `created_at` and `changed_by`

## Implementation

### Schema

```sql
CREATE TABLE brand_profile_history (
  id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  brand_id uuid REFERENCES brands(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  profile jsonb NOT NULL,
  changed_by uuid REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  change_type text, -- 'ai_draft_applied' | 'manual_edit' | 'rejected_revert'
  created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now()
);
```

* Before every `UPDATE brands SET ai_profile = ...`, INSERT current `ai_profile` into `brand_profile_history`
* `applyDraft` and `updateProfileField` server actions both need this guard
* Undo toast: pass the previous version ID in the action response; undo restores from history
* Activity tab: query `brand_profile_history` ordered by `created_at DESC` and render as timeline entries

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Undo toast shown for 5s after: approve draft, apply draft, edit field
- [ ] Undo restores the previous profile version
- [ ] Activity tab shows all profile version history with timestamps and change type
- [ ] "Restore this version" reinstates any past profile
- [ ] `brand_profile_history` has RLS (user can only see their own brand history)
- [ ] Migration includes RLS policy
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: UX: Profile tab — inline edit with revert-to-AI option
Description:
## Problem

The Profile tab shows the AI-generated profile fields (tagline, mission, category, etc.) as read-only text. If the AI got something wrong, the operator has no way to fix it short of re-running the entire analysis pipeline.

This violates UX principle #7: *AI drafts, humans decide — every AI write is a reversible draft behind a gate.*
And principle #10: *Everything is undoable — no destructive AI action without a way back.*

## Expected behaviour

* Each profile field has an edit icon on hover
* Clicking puts that field into an inline text input (or textarea for longer fields)
* Save button commits to `brands.ai_profile` (JSONB patch, not full replace)
* "Revert to AI" button restores the original AI value for that field (stored in `ai_profile._ai_original` or retrieved from the most recent approved draft)
* Auto-save after 1 second of inactivity (with a subtle "Saved" indicator)

## Implementation

* `ProfileTab` currently renders `AiProfile` fields as static text
* Add edit mode per-field: `useState<string | null>(editingField)`
* Save via a new server action `updateProfileField(brandId, field, value)` — does a JSONB patch: `UPDATE brands SET ai_profile = ai_profile || '{"tagline": "new value"}'`
* Revert: store original AI values in `ai_profile._ai_values` at approval time (set in `applyDraft`)
* RLS: user's session client can UPDATE their own brand (already allowed)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Each field in ProfileTab shows edit icon on hover
- [ ] Clicking field → inline input, Save / Cancel buttons appear
- [ ] Save patches only that field (not full profile replace)
- [ ] "Revert to AI" restores original AI value for that field
- [ ] Visual "Saved" confirmation (1–2 seconds)
- [ ] Optimistic update — UI updates immediately, rolls back on error
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: UX: Re-analyze shows step-by-step progress with time estimate
Description:
## Problem

When a user clicks "Re-analyze", the button disables and the page goes silent. The `AnalysisProgressBanner` polls `intake_status` and shows a status string, but:

1. No time estimate is given before clicking — users don't know if this takes 10 seconds or 5 minutes
2. The status strings (`crawl_running`, `analysis_running`) are raw enum values, not human-friendly
3. There is no step indicator showing where in the pipeline the brand currently is

This violates UX principle #1: *Remove waiting — stream progress, never a blank spinner.*

## Expected behaviour

**Before clicking:**
Button label: "Re-analyze (~2 min)"

**After clicking — progress banner shows:**

```
Step 1 of 3 — Crawling website…       ████░░░░░░
Step 2 of 3 — Reading content…        (pending)
Step 3 of 3 — Scoring with AI…        (pending)
```

Each step lights up as `intake_status` transitions through `crawl_running → analysis_running → draft_ready`.

## Implementation

* `AnalysisProgressBanner` already polls `intake_status` via Supabase Realtime — extend it to map status → step number
* Add step labels: `crawl_running` → "Crawling website", `analysis_running` → "Scoring with AI", `draft_ready` → "Draft ready for review"
* Add estimated time to the Re-analyze button tooltip or label
* Progress bar: step N of 3 with fill

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Button shows time estimate before click ("~2 min" or similar)
- [ ] After click, banner shows current step name in plain English (not the enum value)
- [ ] Step indicator shows which of 3 steps is active
- [ ] When draft is ready, banner transitions to the DraftBanner/ApprovalCard immediately
- [ ] No polling regression — still uses Realtime subscription
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: UX: Brand list shows DNA score, status badge, and last-analyzed date
Description:
## Problem

The brand list page (`/app/brand`) shows brand names and URLs only. With more than a handful of brands there is no way to see which need attention, which are healthy, or when they were last analyzed — without clicking into each one.

## Expected behaviour

Each brand row shows:

* **DNA score** — coloured pill (green/amber/red matching `scoreColor()`)
* **Status badge** — coloured chip matching `intakeStatusLabel()` / `intakeStatusColor()` (already used in brand detail)
* **Last analyzed** — relative date ("3 days ago") from `brand_scores.updated_at` or `brands.updated_at`
* **Quick action** — if `draft_ready`, show a small "Review draft →" link inline

## Implementation

* Brand list page already queries `brands`. Add `intake_status`, `updated_at` to the select
* Join `brand_scores` to get the DNA score (or compute from scores already fetched — see `computeDnaScore()`)
* Reuse `intakeStatusColor()`, `intakeStatusLabel()`, `scoreColor()` from `lib/brand-hub.ts` and `lib/brand-utils.ts`
* No new dependencies needed

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Each brand row shows DNA score pill (coloured)
- [ ] Each brand row shows status badge (coloured, matching brand detail page)
- [ ] Last analyzed date shown (relative format)
- [ ] Brands with `draft_ready` show "Review draft →" shortcut link
- [ ] List remains fast (single query with join, no N+1)
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: MI-03d · See one photo across every platform at once
Description:
Flip the model: instead of previewing one placement at a time, upload one asset and see it across a whole campaign set at once — IG Feed/Story/Reel, Facebook, TikTok, Pinterest, Amazon, Shopify, (Email later).

## Scope

* A "Campaign" mode that takes one uploaded asset and lays out every target placement in a single board.
* Per-card status rolls up to a campaign-level readiness summary (uses MI-03b checks).

## Depends on

* MI-03a (selection + multi-placement rendering)
* Real value depends on MI-03r (smart per-placement cropping) — without it, every card shows the same CSS-cropped image rather than a properly recomposed one.

Blocked by MI-03a.
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: MI-03c · Approve, edit, or schedule each image from the preview
Description:
Each preview card gets actions: ✅ Approve · ✏️ Edit · 📅 Schedule.

## Reuse, don't rebuild

A HITL approval-card pattern is already in flight (`feat/ipi-52-approval-cards`, `app/src/components/brand-hub/approval-card.tsx`) and the roadmap's MI-08 covers "recommendation HITL approval card → persist via edge fn". Adopt that pattern: nothing persists until Approve; write via a Supabase Edge Function (never write tables directly from the client).

## Scope

* Approve → persist an approval record (which asset, which placement, by whom) via edge fn.
* Edit → adjust caption/asset URL/crop and re-preview.
* Schedule → set a publish date (feeds MI-03e).

## Out of scope

🔄 Regenerate (generative image editing — capability doesn't exist yet).

Blocked by MI-03a. Coordinate with MI-08 / the approval-cards branch to share the HITL component.
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: MI-03b · AI checks each image's quality for its platform
Description:
Auto-flag problems on each preview frame: face/subject cropped, text outside safe zone, low contrast, wrong aspect, etc. Show "⚠ Face cropped → move up 15%" or "✅ Ready · Brand Score 96/100".

## Needs (real work — not just UI)

* A vision-capable check. Either a Mastra tool calling a vision model (Gemini) on the rendered asset, or a deterministic geometry check against `image_specs` safe zones for the cheap wins (text-in-safe-zone, aspect mismatch) before adding ML face detection.
* Tie the score to the existing `brand_scores` (brand intelligence) where relevant.

## Suggested phasing

1. Deterministic checks first (aspect/safe-zone/format vs `image_specs`) — no ML, ships value fast.
2. Vision model for subject/face crop detection — separate follow-up.

## Out of scope

"Regenerate" (generative image editing) — no such capability exists yet; track separately if wanted.

Blocked by MI-03a (needs the per-placement frame + selection model).
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: WEB-015.11 · Testing (unit/integration/e2e) + security review

Title: DEVX-004 · Operator app (app/) deploy target — Vercel project
Description:
## DEVX-004 — Decide + set up the operator app deploy target

The `app/` Next.js operator app deploys **nowhere** (local + CI only). The existing `Vercel – lumina-studio` check fails with **"Account is blocked"** (account/billing, not code).

### Tasks

- [ ] Resolve the Vercel "Account is blocked" state (account owner) — or pick another host.
- [ ] Decide app/ deploy shape: **separate Vercel project** (recommended — Next needs a Node server for the CopilotKit runtime) vs subpath vs other.
- [ ] Configure project root = `app/`, build = `npm run build`, output standalone; runtime env (`GEMINI_API_KEY`, optional CopilotKit license) as **server** env, never `NEXT_PUBLIC_`.
- [ ] Confirm `/api/copilotkit/info` 200 with all 3 agents on the deployed URL.

### Acceptance

- [ ] Operator app reachable at a deployed URL; runtime + sidebar work; secrets server-only.
- [ ] Vercel check green (or replaced).

Source: efficiency review + PR24/25 post-merge verify. Gate IPI2-127 (real auth) before any multi-user/public deploy.
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: DEVX-002 · Fix root Vite ESLint — full scan hangs / 28k errors (useless gate)
Description:
## DEVX-002 — Root ESLint is unusable as a gate

From the 2026-06-22 efficiency review. `npm run lint` at the repo root reports **\~28k problems** and/or times out on a full scan; only `npx eslint 'src/**/*.{ts,tsx}'` completes. "lint passes" is meaningless for the Vite app and real issues hide behind the noise.

### Likely cause

ESLint scans generated/vendored/non-`src` files (`dist/`, `app/`, `github/`, docs) rather than just `src/`.

### Tasks

- [ ] Scope the root ESLint config to `src/` (or add proper `ignores` for `dist/`, `app/`, `github/`, generated files).
- [ ] Fix/disable the 2 pre-existing shadcn/ui empty-interface errors (`command.tsx`, `textarea.tsx`).
- [ ] Make `npm run lint` complete in seconds with a meaningful count.

### Acceptance

- [ ] `npm run lint` (root) finishes quickly with 0 (or a small, real, tracked) errors.
- [ ] CI `build` job lint signal trustworthy again.

Note: **root Vite only** — `app/` already lints clean. Source: efficiency review (item #6).
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: DEVX-001 · Operator app dev loop — `dev:clean` + local fixtures/seed (no live Gemini)
Description:
## DEVX-001 — Operator app dev loop hardening

From the 2026-06-22 efficiency review. Two recurring frictions slow `app/` iteration:

1. **Stale** `.next` **corruption.** Turbopack panics / `tsc` sees leftover `.next/dev/types` after a killed dev server or concurrent build, producing phantom "Cannot find module 'next'" / build failures that clean rebuilds don't have.
2. **Live-Gemini dependency for UI work.** The chat/agents need `GEMINI_API_KEY` to do anything, so UI iteration can't happen offline.

### Tasks

- [ ] Add `app/package.json` script `dev:clean` (`rm -rf .next && npm run dev`).
- [ ] Add **fixtures/seed** so the operator UI renders with mock agent responses when no `GEMINI_API_KEY` is set.
- [ ] Add a graceful "agent unavailable" UI state when agent init fails.
- [ ] Document the dev loop in `app/AGENTS.md`.

### Acceptance

- [ ] `npm run dev:clean` works from a polluted state.
- [ ] UI renders meaningfully with no `GEMINI_API_KEY`, no crash.
- [ ] Verification per TEST-001 (IPI2-129).

Source: efficiency review (item #4).
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: IPI-BI-033 — Content Strategy Agent
Description:
Mastra agent that generates a platform-specific content strategy and 30-day content calendar skeleton from brand persona + social channel data + content pillars.

**Scope:**

* New Mastra agent `content-strategy`
* Tool: `generateContentStrategy(brandId, month, year)` — reads contentPillars, brand_social_channels, brand_personas, seoIntelligence as context
* Strategy output: per-platform (Instagram, TikTok, Pinterest, LinkedIn) with: `postingFrequency`, `contentMix` (% editorial/product/UGC/educational), `toneOfVoice`, `hashtagStrategy[]`, `bestPostingTimes[]`
* Calendar output: 30-day skeleton with `ContentWeek[]` → `ContentPost[]` (channel, format, topic, caption_seed, hashtags[], image_direction, publish_day, call_to_action)
* Schema required: `brand_content_calendars` (brand_id, month, year, status) + `brand_content_posts` tables
* Write calendar to new tables

**Schema migrations:**

* `brand_content_calendars`: brand_id FK, month int, year int, status (draft/approved/active), created_at. UNIQUE(brand_id, month, year).
* `brand_content_posts`: calendar_id FK, week_number int, channel text, format text, topic text, caption_seed text, hashtags JSONB, image_direction text, publish_day text, call_to_action text, approved bool default false.

**Acceptance criteria:**

* 30-day content plan generated with posts for each active social channel
* Posts distributed according to platform content mix strategy
* Calendar written to `brand_content_calendars` + `brand_content_posts`
* Operator can approve/edit individual posts via Brand Hub content tab

**Blocked by:** IPI-BI-004 (Social Discovery), IPI-BI-014 (Customer Persona), IPI-BI-015 (SEO Intelligence)
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: IPI-BI-032 — Creative Director Agent
Description:
Upgrade the existing `creative-director` Mastra agent with full tools and working memory to generate art direction briefs from Brand DNA + visual identity profile.

**Scope:**

* Extend existing `creative-director` agent (already registered in `app/src/mastra/index.ts`)
* Add tools: `generateCreativeBrief(brandId, campaignId)`, `getContentPillars(brandId)`, `getBrandMood(brandId)`, `getMoodboardDirection(brandId)`
* Working memory schema: `{ activeBrandId, activeBriefId, approvedMoodboard, styleReferences[] }`
* Structured brief output: `shootTitle`, `concept`, `moodDescription`, `colorPalette[]`, `lightingStyle`, `locationSuggestions[]`, `wardrobeDirection`, `propSuggestions[]`, `heroShotDescription`, `supportingShotList[]`, `referenceImages[]`
* Brief written to `brand_intake_drafts` (status=pending) for production-planner consumption
* Handoff: approved brief → production-planner `createShootBrief` tool

**Acceptance criteria:**

* creative-director produces a full creative brief for any brandId + campaignId input
* Brief includes all 11 structured fields
* `getBrandMood` reads `brands.ai_profile.visualIdentity` for color and style context
* Working memory persists across chat turns (activeBrandId, activeBriefId)
* HITL: brief draft requires operator approval before production-planner picks it up

**Blocked by:** IPI-BI-005 (Visual Identity Agent — provides moodboard and color context), IPI-BI-031 (Campaign Planner — provides campaignId input)

**Reuse:** Existing `creative-director` agent registration — extend in place, do not replace. `callEdgeFunction` bridge pattern.
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: IPI-BI-031 — Campaign Planner Agent
Description:
Mastra agent that generates campaign ideas from Brand Profile + product catalog + trend signals, producing shoot-ready campaign briefs.

**Scope:**

* New Mastra agent `campaign-planner`
* Tool: `generateCampaigns(brandId, seasonContext)` — reads full Brand Profile, product catalog, content pillars, personas as context
* Structured output per campaign: `campaignName`, `theme`, `objective`, `targetPersona`, `keyProducts[]`, `visualDirection`, `channels[]`, `budget_tier` (minimal/standard/premium), `shootBriefSeed` (handed to creative-director)
* Generates 3-5 campaign concepts per run
* Writes campaign drafts to `brand_intake_drafts` (status=pending) for HITL approval
* Working memory: `{ activeBrandId, season, generatedCampaigns[], approvedCount }`

**Acceptance criteria:**

* 3-5 distinct campaign concepts generated with all required fields
* Each campaign has a `shootBriefSeed` that creative-director can consume
* Drafts saved to `brand_intake_drafts` for operator approval
* CopilotKit action `planCampaign(brandId, season)` triggers this agent from chat

**Blocked by:** IPI-BI-014 (Customer Persona — campaigns must reference personas), IPI-BI-013 (Competitor — avoids competitor campaign overlap)

**Reuse:** `brand_intake_drafts` HITL pattern (status=pending → operator approves). `callEdgeFunction` for Mastra→edge bridge. CopilotKit `useFrontendTool` pattern from OperatorPanel.

Title: IPI-BI-023 — Competitor Dashboard
Description:

Title: IPI-BI-022 — pgvector: Brand Embeddings
Description:
Generate and store vector embeddings for brand profiles to enable semantic similarity search, brand matching, and RAG over past analyses.

**Scope:**

* Add `brands.embedding vector(768)` column with HNSW index (migration following pattern from `20260621000000` agent_context_snapshots)
* Generate embeddings via Gemini Embedding API (`@google/genai` — already in edge function SDK) in brand-intelligence edge function after profile extraction
* Embedding input: concatenated brand profile text (name + tagline + mission + values + contentPillars + targetAudience)
* Add `search_similar_brands(query_embedding vector, match_count int)` SECURITY DEFINER RPC (mirrors `search_context_snapshots()` pattern)
* Wire semantic search in Brand Hub: "Brands similar to this one" section

**Acceptance criteria:**

* Every analyzed brand has a non-null `embedding` vector(768) in brands table
* HNSW index built on brands.embedding
* `search_similar_brands()` RPC returns top-N brands by cosine similarity
* Brand Hub shows 3 similar brands by embedding distance

**Blocked by:** IPI-BI-002 (brand-intelligence-v2 edge function — embedding generated at end of analysis run)

**Reuse:** `agent_context_snapshots` HNSW migration pattern from `20260621000000`. `search_context_snapshots()` RPC as the template. Gemini Embedding API via same `@google/genai` SDK already loaded.
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: IPI-BI-021 — Knowledge Graph: Brand Nodes
Description:
Use Graphify to index Brand Profile data as knowledge graph nodes and edges, enabling relationship discovery across brands, products, competitors, and personas.

**Scope:**

* New Mastra agent `knowledge-graph-manager`
* Tool: `upsertBrandGraph(brandId)` — reads full brand data (ai_profile + brand_social_channels + brand_competitors + brand_personas) and upserts Graphify nodes + edges
* Node types: Brand, Competitor, Persona, SocialChannel, Collection, ContentPillar, Keyword
* Edge types: COMPETES_WITH, HAS_PERSONA, ACTIVE_ON (social), SELLS_IN (collection), TARGETS (keyword)
* Graphify CLI invoked via subprocess from Mastra agent: `graphify update <brand-data-path>`

**Graph queries to enable:**

* `graphify query "Brand X competitors"` — returns competitor nodes
* `graphify path "Brand X" "Persona Y"` — shows relationship path
* `graphify affected "content-pillar"` — shows all brands using that pillar

**Acceptance criteria:**

* Brand nodes created in Graphify for every analyzed brand
* Competitor edges created linking brand → competitor nodes
* Persona nodes linked to brand node
* Graph queryable via `graphify query` CLI

**Blocked by:** IPI-BI-013 (Competitor Intelligence), IPI-BI-014 (Customer Persona Agent)

**Reuse:** Graphify CLI already installed and indexing repo at `docs/graphify/graphify-out/graph.json`. Same `graphify update` command pattern. Do NOT run `graphify update docs/graphify` — always update over the correct path.
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: IPI-BI-015 — SEO Intelligence Agent
Description:
Mastra agent that extracts keyword strategy, meta content quality, content gaps, and SEO positioning from a brand's website crawl.

**Scope:**

* New Mastra agent `seo-intelligence`
* Tool: `analyzeSEO(brandId, crawlResultId)` — reads `brand_crawl_results.raw_data` (page titles, meta descriptions, headers, alt text)
* Gemini analysis produces: `primaryKeywords[]`, `longTailKeywords[]`, `metaQuality` (0-100), `contentGaps[]`, `topPerformingPages[]`, `seoPositioning`, `schemaMarkupPresent` bool, `internalLinkingScore` (0-100)
* Write to `brands.ai_profile.seoIntelligence` JSONB
* Feed keywords into content-strategy agent (Epic 4) as input

**Acceptance criteria:**

* Primary and long-tail keywords extracted from crawled page content
* Content gaps identified (topics competitors cover that brand doesn't)
* Meta quality score derived from title length, description completeness, alt text coverage
* No external SEO API required — analysis from crawl content only

**Blocked by:** IPI-BI-001 (Firecrawl — needs crawl output), IPI-BI-013 (Competitor Intelligence — for gap analysis)

**Reuse:** `brand_crawl_results.raw_data` from Firecrawl (already crawled — no re-crawl needed). Gemini structured output schema pattern.
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: PLAT-ICONS · NavSidebar Emoji → Lucide Icon Migration
Description:
## Context

`Universal design prompt/design-audit-2026-06-28-rev2.md` (finding #1, 🟡) flagged that the design system's own Rule 9 mandates Lucide icons, but production `app/src/components/operator-panel/nav-sidebar.tsx` renders raw emoji glyphs (⌂ 📷 ◈ 🖼 📣 🤝 💬) for Home/Shoots/Brand/Assets/Campaigns/Matching/Threads. Re-verified live on disk (2026-07-01): **still emoji, not fixed** — this is a real, currently-open gap, not a stale finding.

## Problem

Emoji render inconsistently across OS/browser font stacks (different weight, color rendering on some platforms since these are pictographic emoji, not outline glyphs) and violate the project's own icon-system rule.

## Proposal

Replace the 7 emoji glyphs in `nav-sidebar.tsx` with the equivalent `lucide-react` icon components (e.g. Home, Camera, Gem/Layers for Brand, Image, Megaphone, Handshake/Users for Matching, MessageSquare for Threads — pick the closest semantic match, confirm with design if ambiguous).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 7 nav icons use `lucide-react` components, no emoji glyphs remain in `nav-sidebar.tsx`
- [ ] Visual weight/size consistent with the rest of the nav (match existing icon sizing token)
- [ ] No layout shift — icon bounding box matches current emoji footprint
- [ ] Import icons directly (`import { Home } from "lucide-react"`), not a barrel/wildcard import

## Skills

`vercel-react-best-practices` — `bundle-barrel-imports`: import each Lucide icon directly rather than a barrel import, to avoid pulling the full icon set into the bundle

## Dependencies

None — isolated, self-contained change to one file

## Out of scope

Icon changes anywhere else in the app (scope this to NavSidebar only; expand in a follow-up if other emoji usage is found elsewhere)
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: Codacy applies T-SQL/SQL Server linter to Postgres migrations — false positives on every supabase/migrations/*.sql
Description:
## Context

Discovered while reviewing [amo-tech-ai/lumina-studio#167](https://linear.app/amo100/review/fixsupabase-remove-security-definer-from-shoot-views-854c085bd3d0) (`ipi/security-definer-views-fix`) — Codacy's check reported 6 "issues" (4 medium, 2 minor) against `supabase/migrations/20260701070100_fix_security_definer_views.sql`.

## Root cause

No `.codacy.yml` exists in the repo, so Codacy runs its default analyzer set against `.sql` files. The findings are all **T-SQL / SQL Server** conventions, not PostgreSQL:

* "Expected `SET ANSI_NULLS ON` near top of file"
* "Expected `SET NOCOUNT ON` near top of file"
* "Expected `SET QUOTED_IDENTIFIER ON` near top of file"
* "Expected `SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL READ UNCOMMITTED` near top of file"
* 2x "Expected TSQL Keyword to be capitalized" — flagging `security_invoker`, a fixed lowercase Postgres view option name, as if it were a stylable T-SQL keyword

Adding SQL Server pragmas to a Postgres migration would be actively wrong (some of that syntax doesn't exist / isn't valid in Postgres). Spot-checked an existing merged migration (`20260628105303_add_commit_shoot_draft_rpc.sql`) — same pattern would trigger identical false positives, confirming this is repo-wide, not specific to one PR.

## Impact

* Every future PR touching `supabase/migrations/**` will show a spurious Codacy "fail"/"action_required" status, training reviewers to ignore it (alert fatigue) — or worse, someone "fixes" it by adding nonsense SQL Server directives to a Postgres file.
* Not currently merge-blocking: `main` has no branch protection rules, so this isn't a required check today. Will matter if/when branch protection is added.

## Fix

Add `.codacy.yml` at repo root with a language/pattern override so `supabase/migrations/**/*.sql` (and any other `.sql` under `supabase/`) is excluded from the T-SQL analyzer, or scanned with a Postgres-aware ruleset if Codacy offers one. See [https://docs.codacy.com/repositories-configure/codacy-configuration-file/](<https://docs.codacy.com/repositories-configure/codacy-configuration-file/>) for the `exclude_paths` / per-tool config syntax.

## Verify

* Open a throwaway PR touching a `supabase/migrations/*.sql` file and confirm Codacy no longer flags T-SQL pragmas.
* Confirm existing/legit SQL style rules (if any are wanted) still apply.
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: SHOOT-DETAIL-REGENERATE — AI-regenerate brief / shots / budget via Mastra HITL gate
Description:
## Purpose

Trigger Mastra to rewrite a specific section (brief, shots, or budget) using brand context. Always draft-only — operator must confirm before any DB write.

## User story

As an operator I can regenerate any shoot section via AI so that I get an improved draft I can accept or discard without touching the original.

## HITL gate — mandatory

The API route returns a draft ONLY. No DB write until the operator clicks Accept. On Accept, the client calls `PATCH /api/shoots/[shootId]` (IPI-210). Reject = no action.

## API route

```
POST /api/shoots/[shootId]/regenerate
Auth: withOperatorAuth → 401
Body: { section: "brief" | "shots" | "budget" }
Response: 200 { draft: { brief? | shots? | budget_breakdown? } }
export const maxDuration = 60
```

* Calls Mastra tool via `mastra.getAgent("shoot-wizard").getTool("<name>").execute!(...)` — never raw `generateText` in route
* No DB write in this route

## Supabase

Read: `get_shoot_detail` RPC for context. Write: only via `PATCH /api/shoots/[shootId]` on operator confirm.

## Audit

`ai_agent_logs` row on every call (before confirm): `agent_name: "shoot-wizard"`, `input: { section, shoot_id }`. Non-blocking.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A. Regenerate brief → non-empty draft text returned
- [ ] B. Regenerate shots → array with shot_number + description
- [ ] C. Regenerate budget → object with category keys + numeric values
- [ ] D. Draft shown inline; original unchanged until Accept clicked
- [ ] E. Accept → PATCH called, DB updated; Reject → no change
- [ ] F. Spinner during generation
- [ ] G. 403 if not owner; 400 if section invalid
- [ ] H. Audit log on every call
- [ ] I. No generateText in route handler

## Implementation prompt

```
Implement SHOOT-DETAIL-REGENERATE for the iPix operator app.

Key constraint: this is a HITL gate. The route returns a draft only.
DB is written only when the operator clicks Accept (calls PATCH /api/shoots/[shootId]).

1. Verify shoot-wizard agent tool names:
   cat app/src/mastra/agents/shoot-wizard.ts | grep "name:"

2. Create app/src/app/api/shoots/[shootId]/regenerate/route.ts

export const dynamic = "force-dynamic"
export const maxDuration = 60
export async function POST(req, { params }) {
  const operator = await withOperatorAuth(req) // → 401
  const { shootId } = await params
  const { section } = await req.json()
  if (!["brief","shots","budget"].includes(section)) return 400

  // brand check → 403
  const { data: context } = await svc.rpc("get_shoot_detail", { p_shoot_id: shootId, p_user_id: operator.id })
  if (!context) return 404

  const mastra = getMastra() // inside handler body only
  const toolName = { brief: "generateBrief", shots: "generateShotList", budget: "generateBudget" }[section]
  const tool = mastra.getAgent("shoot-wizard").getTool(toolName)
  const draft = await tool.execute!({ context: { shoot: context.shoot, brand: context.brand } })

  // Audit (non-blocking)
  try { await svc.from("ai_agent_logs").insert({ agent_name: "shoot-wizard", input: { section, shoot_id: shootId } }) } catch {}

  return NextResponse.json({ draft })
}

3. Add Regenerate button inside edit mode
   - Shows draft in preview panel
   - Accept → calls PATCH /api/shoots/[shootId]
   - Reject → clears draft, original unchanged

Branch: ipi/215-shoot-detail-regenerate
```

**Spec:** `docs/plan/tasks/03-shoot-detail-actions.md`
**Depends on:** IPI-210 merged

Title: IPI-218D: Mobile drawer collapse for brand context panel
Description:
## Context

Follow-up to [IPI-218](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-218/3-panel-operator-layout-wire-right-panel-to-copilotkit-context) (PR #127). On ≤1024px the `BrandContextPanel` is CSS-hidden but still mounted. This replaces that with a floating pill + slide-up drawer. No new dependency — reuse existing overlay/sheet pattern.

---

## Key implementation rule (verified against codebase)

```
The shell is already:  height: 100dvh; overflow: hidden;
→ Body scroll lock is NOT needed — background never scrolls.

No matchMedia/useMediaQuery hook exists in the codebase.
→ Use CSS media queries for visibility.
→ Use JS (useEffect + matchMedia listener) only for:
     - closing drawer on resize to desktop
     - rendering pill conditionally

BrandContextPanel must render ONLY ONCE per breakpoint:
→ Desktop (≥1024px): rendered in 4th grid column (existing IPI-218 behaviour)
→ Mobile (<1024px):  rendered ONLY inside the drawer (not in grid column)
→ This avoids double-fetch: one hidden in grid + one in drawer.
```

---

## Wireframe

```
MOBILE (<1024px) — no active brand
┌──────────────────────────┐
│ [≡]  CENTER CONTENT      │
│                          │
└──────────────────────────┘   ← no pill

MOBILE — brand active, drawer closed
┌──────────────────────────┐
│ [≡]  CENTER CONTENT      │
│                          │
│              ┌──────────┐│
│              │ ◈ Brand  ││  ← pill, above chat/safe-area
│              └──────────┘│
└──────────────────────────┘

MOBILE — drawer open
┌──────────────────────────┐
│ [≡]  CENTER CONTENT      │
│░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░│  ← overlay (tap to close, stopPropagation inside)
│░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░│
├──────────────────────────┤
│ Acme Corp      [active]  │
│ DNA SCORES               │  ← BrandContextPanel (only render here on mobile)
│ ████████░░  Visual   82  │
│ ASSETS                   │  ← drawer content scrolls internally
│ ┌──┐ ┌──┐ ┌──┐          │
│ └──┘ └──┘ └──┘          │
│                   [× ×]  │  ← close btn  aria-label="Close brand context"
└──────────────────────────┘

DESKTOP (≥1024px) — unchanged from IPI-218
┌───────┬────────────────┬───────┬───────────┐
│ Nav   │ CENTER         │ Chat  │ BrandPanel│
└───────┴────────────────┴───────┴───────────┘
  No pill, no drawer — 4th column panel only
```

---

## Interaction flow

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> PillVisible   : mobile AND activeBrandId set
    [*] --> HiddenDesktop : desktop (≥1024px)
    [*] --> HiddenNoBrand : mobile AND no activeBrandId

    PillVisible   --> DrawerOpen   : tap pill
    DrawerOpen    --> PillVisible  : tap overlay OR Escape OR close btn
    DrawerOpen    --> HiddenNoBrand : activeBrandId cleared (focus → pill safely)
    DrawerOpen    --> HiddenDesktop : viewport resizes to ≥1024px (drawer auto-closes)
    HiddenNoBrand --> PillVisible   : activeBrandId set (mobile)
    HiddenDesktop --> PillVisible   : viewport resizes to <1024px AND activeBrandId set
```

## Component structure

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    OS[OperatorShell]
    OS -->|desktop only: activeBrandId set| GP[4th grid column → BrandContextPanel]
    OS -->|mobile only: activeBrandId set| Pill[MobileBrandPill floating]
    OS -->|mobile only: drawerOpen| Drawer[MobileBrandDrawer]
    Drawer --> BCP[BrandContextPanel — rendered here ONLY on mobile]
    Drawer --> Overlay[dim overlay — stopPropagation inside sheet]
    Drawer --> Close[× close btn aria-label Close brand context]
    Pill -->|tap| Drawer
```

---

## User stories

* As a mobile operator, I tap a floating pill to reveal the brand context panel so I can check DNA scores without leaving the main content.
* As a mobile operator, pressing Escape or tapping the overlay closes the drawer and returns focus to the pill.
* As a desktop operator, I see no change — the 4th column panel works as before and no drawer or pill appears.

---

## Acceptance Criteria

**Desktop (≥1024px)**

- [ ] Floating pill is NOT rendered
- [ ] Drawer is NOT rendered
- [ ] 4th grid column `BrandContextPanel` behaviour unchanged from [IPI-218](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-218/3-panel-operator-layout-wire-right-panel-to-copilotkit-context)
- [ ] `BrandContextPanel` is rendered exactly once (in the grid column)

**Mobile (<1024px) — pill**

- [ ] Pill appears at `bottom:4.5rem; right:1rem` (above chat input/safe-area) when `activeBrandId` is set
- [ ] Pill is hidden when no brand is active
- [ ] Pill shows ◈ icon and "Brand" label

**Mobile — drawer**

- [ ] Tapping pill opens slide-up drawer containing `BrandContextPanel`
- [ ] Drawer uses `slideIn` + `fadeIn` keyframes from `operator-shell.module.css`
- [ ] Tapping dim overlay closes drawer (stopPropagation inside drawer sheet prevents accidental close)
- [ ] Close button (×) inside drawer closes drawer
- [ ] `BrandContextPanel` rendered ONLY inside drawer on mobile (not also in hidden grid column) — prevents double-fetch
- [ ] Drawer content scrolls internally (`overflow-y: auto`) if taller than viewport
- [ ] Switching brands while drawer is open: panel content updates to new brand (drawer stays open)
- [ ] Clearing active brand: drawer closes; focus returned safely

**Accessibility**

- [ ] Drawer has `role="dialog"` and `aria-modal="true"`
- [ ] Drawer has `aria-labelledby` pointing to the brand name heading inside
- [ ] Close button has `aria-label="Close brand context"`
- [ ] Opening drawer moves focus into drawer (first focusable element)
- [ ] Escape key closes drawer and returns focus to pill
- [ ] Tab / Shift+Tab cycles within drawer only (focus trap)
- [ ] Closing drawer (any method) returns focus to pill button

**Viewport / resize**

- [ ] Resize from mobile → desktop (≥1024px) while drawer open: drawer closes, desktop panel renders
- [ ] Resize from desktop → mobile (<1024px) while brand active: pill appears, drawer starts closed
- [ ] `isMobile` state managed via `useEffect + matchMedia` listener (not inline — avoids SSR hydration mismatch)

**Quality**

- [ ] No new CSS dependency introduced
- [ ] No new npm package added
- [ ] No body scroll lock code (shell is already `height:100dvh; overflow:hidden`)
- [ ] `BrandContextPanel` fetches exactly once regardless of breakpoint

Title: MI-03r · Research auto-cropping images per platform (Cloudinary)
Description:
**Later / research — do not build yet.**

Today the preview shows the *same* asset in every frame, cropped only by CSS `object-fit`. To truly turn one upload into correct outputs per placement (the "move image up 15%" / auto-recompose idea), we need real server-side cropping.

## Research questions

* Use Cloudinary transforms (`c_fill`, `g_face`/`g_auto`, per target aspect ratio + safe zone) to generate a correctly-cropped derivative per placement.
* Where do transforms run — on render (URL params) vs. baked derivatives stored in `cloudinary_assets` / `asset_variants`?
* How to respect each placement's safe zones from `image_specs` when choosing the crop.
* Cost/perf of generating N derivatives per asset per campaign.

## Why deferred

This is the advanced engine under Campaign view (MI-03d) and the "fix: move image up 15%" suggestions (MI-03b). Valuable but not needed for the platform-first UX win (MI-03a). Spike it, write findings, then size implementation.

Output of this task = a short findings doc + a sizing estimate, not production code.

Title: Cloudinary Config Cleanup
Description:
Fix broken Cloudinary environment/config values before any SDK or upload work (IPI-257 074a) can safely proceed.

**Real-world example:** it's like a delivery address with an extra apartment number typo'd onto the end — the postal code alone still "looks" almost right, but the truck goes to the wrong building every time. That's what `CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME` is doing today.

## Tasks

- [ ] Fix corrupted `CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME` in `.env.local` — currently `dzqy2ixl0/nvdlhrodvevgwdsneplk` (has a stray `/nvdlhrodvevgwdsneplk` suffix appended), should be `dzqy2ixl0` (matches `CLOUDINARY_URL` and `NEXT_CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME` already in the same file)
- [ ] Fix the hardcoded wrong cloud name in `dev-panel-fixture.ts` (currently `dza2bjwwp`, does not match the real project cloud name)
- [ ] Remove stale `VITE_CLOUDINARY_*` vars (Vite is retired) and any unused duplicate `NEXT_CLOUDINARY_*` vars
- [ ] Add missing `f_auto,q_auto` to any named transform preset that doesn't already end with it (see preset table in `docs/linear/issues/IPI-257-cloudinary-pipeline.md`)

## Why this is priority #1

Confirmed live bug as of 2026-07-03 — this breaks any code path reading `process.env.CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME` directly before any SDK install or upload-signing work is even attempted.

Related: full detail in `tasks/design-docs/cloudinary.md` Phase 5 audit table and roadmap.
