Title: BE-B0b · Booking Agent Verification & Finalization
Description:
## BE-B0b — Booking Agent Verification & Finalization

**Track:** Backend · **Priority:** P2 (agent shipped, UI unblocks via [IPI-410](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-410/scr-21-booking-wizard)/411) · **Complexity:** S
**SSOT:** `tasks/backend/BE-B0b-booking-mastra-agent.md`
**Branch (agent code, shipped):** `ai/ipi-397-be-b0b-booking-mastra-agent-draft-only`
**Unblocks:** SCR-21 ([IPI-410](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-410/scr-21-booking-wizard)), SCR-22 ([IPI-411](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-411/scr-22-booking-detail))

This issue verifies and finalizes the booking Mastra agent. The backend implementation (agent + tools + registry + route mapping) is **95% shipped** on `main`. What remains is verification: draft-only guarantees, HITL gate, CopilotKit resolution, and snapshot tests.

---

## User story

> As an operator drafting a talent booking, I want the AI to draft messages and rate quotes based on the shoot brief and talent profile — without committing anything to the database — so I can review and approve before the booking is created.

---

## Data flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant O as Operator
    participant CK as CopilotKit Dock
    participant BA as booking Agent
    participant BT as booking-tools.ts
    participant DB as Supabase RPCs

    O->>CK: "Draft booking for Maria (Zara)"
    CK->>BA: getLocalAgents() → booking
    BA->>BT: checkTalentAvailability(talentId, dates)
    BT->>DB: search_talent + get_booking
    DB-->>BT: availability + rate card
    BT-->>BA: slots data
    BA->>BT: draftBookingQuote(talentId, brief, dates)
    BT-->>BA: rate quote + message draft (read-only, no RPC)
    BA-->>CK: HITL approval card
    CK->>O: Review draft quote
    Note over O,DB: Operator approves → createBookingDraft with operatorConfirmed: true
    O->>CK: Approve
    CK->>BA: createBookingDraft(operatorConfirmed: true)
    BA->>BT: createBookingDraft with operatorConfirmed: true
    BT->>DB: create_booking_request
```

---

## Current state (all verified on `main`)

### Agent infra — shipped

| Component | Path | Proof |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Agent definition | `app/src/mastra/agents/booking-agent.ts` | ✅ Exists on disk |
| Agent export | `app/src/mastra/agents/index.ts:58` | ✅ Exported |
| Registry | `app/src/mastra/index.ts:23` — `booking: bookingAgent` | ✅ Registered |
| Tools | `app/src/mastra/tools/booking-tools.ts` | ✅ 3 tools (checkTalentAvailability, draftBookingQuote, createBookingDraft) |
| Tool tests | `app/src/mastra/tools/booking-tools.test.ts` | ✅ 10 tests passing |
| Route mapping | `app/src/lib/route-agent-map.ts:16-18` | ✅ Maps /app/bookings, /app/model, /app/roster → "booking" |
| CopilotKit runtime | `api/copilotkit/[[...slug]]/route.ts` | ✅ getLocalAgents includes booking |

### Booking RPCs — shipped (do not recreate)

All verified on Supabase remote:

* `talent.bookings` + `version` optimistic lock
* `create_booking_request`, `transition_booking`, `confirm_booking`, `get_booking`, `list_bookings`
* `search_talent`, `toggle_shortlist_item`

---

## Draft-only guarantees (already shipped)

The agent is **hard-gated** against accidental writes:

| Guard | Location | What it does |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Agent instructions line 28 | `booking-agent.ts:28` | "You NEVER confirm or approve a booking — no confirm_booking tool exists" |
| Agent instructions line 29 | `booking-agent.ts:29` | "Never call createBookingDraft without explicit operator approval" |
| Tool schema guard | `booking-tools.ts:155-157` | `operatorConfirmed` must be `true` — documented in schema |
| Tool runtime guard | `booking-tools.ts:175-178` | Throws `Error` if `operatorConfirmed` is false — blocks write before RPC call |
| Test coverage | `booking-tools.test.ts:176-189` | Test asserts throw + no RPC call when `operatorConfirmed: false` |
| No confirm tool | `booking-tools.ts:3` | "Never expose confirm_booking — confirmation is human-only via POST .../approve" |
| Read-only tool | `booking-tools.ts:126` | `draftBookingQuote` described as "Read-only — does not write to the database" |
| Read-only impl | `booking-tools.ts:139` | `execute: async (input) => buildQuoteDraft(input)` — pure function, no DB call |

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A. Agent instructions explicitly enforce draft-only behavior (verified: ✅ already shipped, lines 28-31)
- [ ] B. Agent never invokes persistence RPCs without `operatorConfirmed: true` (verified: ✅ blocked at tool level, lines 175-178)
- [ ] C. HITL approval required before booking creation (verified: ✅ agent instructions + tool guard)
- [ ] D. Booking quote generation works: `draftBookingQuote` returns local draft, zero RPC calls (verified: ✅ pure function, test asserts no RPC)
- [ ] E. Availability lookup works: `checkTalentAvailability` calls RPC and returns candidate result (verified: ✅ tool + test)
- [ ] F. CopilotKit resolves `agentId="booking"` correctly from route-agent-map → Mastra registry → runtime (verify with `grep -rn '"booking"' app/src/mastra/`)
- [ ] G. Snapshot test added for agent instructions (`booking-agent.snapshot.test.ts`) to prevent prompt drift
- [ ] H. Integration test added: user → booking agent → availability → quote → draft returned → NO booking RPC
- [ ] I. `cd app && npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm test` green
- [ ] J. Booking Wizard (SCR-21, [IPI-410](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-410/scr-21-booking-wizard)) and Booking Detail (SCR-22, [IPI-411](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-411/scr-22-booking-detail)) remain explicitly out of scope

---

## Out of scope

* Booking Wizard UI (SCR-21, [IPI-410](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-410/scr-21-booking-wizard)) — separate frontend task
* Booking Detail UI (SCR-22, [IPI-411](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-411/scr-22-booking-detail)) — separate frontend task
* Calendar sync or external booking integrations
* `/app/bookings`, `/app/model`, `/app/roster` page creation (route mapping already shipped)

---

## Suggested test additions

### Snapshot test: `booking-agent.snapshot.test.ts`

```typescript
// Verify agent instructions contain draft-only wording
// When instructions change, snapshot update requires explicit review
import { bookingAgent } from "./booking-agent";

it("instructions enforce draft-only behavior", () => {
  expect(bookingAgent.instructions).toContain("NEVER confirm or approve");
  expect(bookingAgent.instructions).toContain("operatorConfirmed");
  expect(bookingAgent.instructions).not.toContain("confirm_booking");
});
```

### Integration test

```typescript
// Verify end-to-end: user asks for draft → no RPC write
it("draft-only flow does not call persistence RPCs", async () => {
  const result = await bookingAgent.execute("Draft booking for Maria for Aug 1-3");
  // Assert: availability was checked
  // Assert: quote was drafted locally
  // Assert: NO create_booking_request was called (no operatorConfirmed)
});
```

### Regression test

```typescript
it('agent responds with draft, not confirmation, to "Book Maria"', async () => {
  const result = await bookingAgent.execute("Book Maria for Aug 1-3");
  expect(result.text).not.toMatch(/Booking created|Confirmed|Booked/i);
  expect(result.text).toMatch(/draft|prepared|ready|review/i);
});
```

---

## Verification

```bash
# Agent exists and is registered
grep -rn '"booking"' app/src/mastra/
# Should show: index.ts:23, agents/index.ts:58, route-agent-map.ts:16-18

# Existing tests pass
cd app && npx vitest run src/mastra/tools/booking-tools.test.ts
cd app && npx vitest run src/mastra/agents/index.test.ts

# Route agent map correct
grep -A1 "booking" app/src/lib/route-agent-map.ts

# Full app gate
cd app && npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm test
```

## Skills

`mastra` · `copilotkit` · `gemini` · `task-verifier` · `ipix-task-lifecycle`

## Failure points (pre-mortem)

| Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Agent accidentally writes bookings | 🔴 High | operatorConfirmed guard + instructions ban auto-commit |
| createBookingDraft tool performs persistence | 🟡 Medium | Intentional — only after operatorConfirmed: true |
| Route map drift (registry ↔ runtime ↔ agent map) | 🟡 Medium | grep verify in AC-F |
| RPC drift (agent calls wrong RPC) | 🟡 Medium | No booking RPC exists as tool without operatorConfirmed guard |
| Agent prompt drift removes draft-only wording | 🟢 Low | Snapshot test AC-G catches this |

Title: SCR-30 · CRM Pipeline — React Parity
Description:
## SCR-30 — CRM Pipeline React Parity

**Priority:** P1 · **Dependencies:** [IPI-385](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-385/rf-01-extract-statuschip-crm-status-tokens)/386/387 · [IPI-388](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-388/rf-03-crm-companies-contacts-list-screens) (CRM lists) — list screens should be functional first

**SSOT:** `Universal-design-prompt-new/tasks/screens/SCR-30-crm-pipeline.md`

**Route:** `/app/crm/pipeline`

**Design source:** `Pages/SCR-30-CRM-Pipeline.dc.html`

**Current state:** 7-line CrmScreenGate stub

---

## Overview

6-stage kanban pipeline board (Lead → Qualified → Proposal → Negotiation → Won → Lost) with horizontal scroll, deal cards, stage-category health score in IntelligencePanel. Won/Lost stages are locked behind an **approval gate** — no drag, button, or agent may bypass it.

## Purpose

Operator visualizes all deals by stage, moves deals between non-terminal stages, and views deal health without leaving the board.

## User journey

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  A[Pipeline /app/crm/pipeline] --> B[6 horizontal columns: Lead → Lost]
  B --> C[Deal cards: company, value, risk dot, days in stage]
  C --> D[IntelligencePanel: health score + NBA on selection]
  C --> E[Click deal → Deal Detail /app/crm/pipeline/[id]]
  B --> F[Lead→Negotiation: real-time move]
  B --> G[Won/Lost: approval gate only]
```

## Component reuse verification

| Component | Usage | Reuse from |
| -- | -- | -- |
| StatusChip | Deal stage chip in cards | RF-01 |
| EmptyState | No deals in pipeline | RF-A7b |
| ErrorState | Error with retry | RF-A7b |
| IntelligencePanel | Health score per selected deal | Existing shell |
| ApprovalCard | Won/Lost gate confirmation | Existing component |
| N/A | NO kanban library | CSS horizontal scroll + columns |
| N/A | NO copy of existing deal layout | Deal card inline, detail = SCR-31 |

## Data source table

| Section | Source | Status |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Deals by stage | `crm_deals` filtered by org + stage | 🟢 live |
| Deal value | `deals.value` | 🟢 live |
| Stage | `deals.stage` (lead/qualified/proposal/negotiation/won/lost) | 🟢 live |
| Risk | `deals.risk_score` or derived from activity recency | 🟡 verify |
| Days in stage | `deals.stage_entered_at` computed diff | 🟢 live |
| Health factors | crm-assistant or derived from activity data | 🟡 verify agent |
| Pipeline total | Aggregate of deal values | 🟢 computed |
| Intel panel | crm-assistant route context | 🟢 wired |

## DC grid spec

| Property | DC value |
| -- | -- |
| Shell | `56px | minmax(0,1fr) | 320px` (fixed-3col) |
| Column width | 210px fixed |
| Column header | padding 11px 13px, colored dot, label, count, value |
| Deal card | padding 10px 11px, border-radius var(--r-md), company/value/risk/days |
| Risk chip | inline-flex, gap 4px, font 10px/600 |

| "At risk" filter toggle | Toggle button, active=red border+bg |
| Scroll | `overflow-x:auto` on pipeline container |
| Pipeline total | h1 £52,000 mono next to "Pipeline" title |

## Acceptance criteria

| # | Criterion | Proof |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 1 | 6 kanban columns render (Lead → Lost) with correct colors | browser: matches DC colors |
| 2 | Deal cards show company name, value, risk dot+label, days in stage | visual match to DC |
| 3 | Pipeline total value renders in header | aggregate from deals |
| 4 | Horizontal scroll when columns overflow | window resize |
| 5 | Won/Lost columns show lock icon + approval gate | click → ApprovalCard |
| 6 | "At risk only" filter toggle narrows to at-risk deals | toggle works |
| 7 | Selecting a deal updates IntelligencePanel with health breakdown | panel shows factors |
| 8 | Clicking a deal card routes to `/app/crm/pipeline/[id]` | navigates |
| 9 | Empty state: no deals → EmptyState | truncate data |
| 10 | Loading: skeleton columns | visible during fetch |
| 11 | Error: ErrorState with retry | mock failure |
| 12 | tokens.css only — no legacy hex | grep check |
| 13 | CrmScreenGate removed | component replaced |

## Won/Lost approval gate (must match SCR-31 design)

When a user clicks Won or Lost stage button:

1. An ApprovalCard overlay appears with deal summary + reason
2. "Cancel" and "Approve" buttons
3. Approving transitions the deal stage
4. Won deals show a conversion banner ("Converted to brand")
5. This is the ONLY path to set won/lost — no drag, no button, no agent bypass

## Mobile & a11y

| # | Requirement |
| -- | -- |
| 1 | Columns wrap to stage accordion at <1024px (per CRM-HANDOFF.md) |
| 2 | Keyboard navigation between deal cards (arrow keys, Enter to open) |
| 3 | Risk indicator has text label, not color-only |
| 4 | Touch targets >=44px on deal cards and filter |
| 5 | Horizontal scroll indication (fade edges or scroll hint) |

## AI boundaries

| Area | Status |
| -- | -- |
| crm-assistant | 🟢 route context for panel |
| IntelligencePanel | 🟢 health factors + NBA per deal |
| Chat dock | 🟡 placeholder |
| AI writes | 🔴 none (moves are user-initiated, approval gate) |
| Stage transitions | 🟢 direct write for Lead→Negotiation, ApprovalCard for Won/Lost |

## Test plan

```bash
cd app && npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm test && CI=true npm run build
```

Browser: 6 columns render · deal cards show data · won/lost approval gate works · at-risk filter works · 390px accordion works · no console errors

## Out of scope

* Drag-and-drop (keyboard/card click move is MVP pattern per CRM-HANDOFF.md)
* Deal detail page (SCR-31 — separate issue)
* Creating/editing deals
* Pipeline analytics widgets

Title: CRM-DATA-001 · Schema + RLS — companies, contacts, deals, activities
Description:
**In plain terms:** Create the four CRM tables and their RLS policies so every later CRM screen and the agent have something real to read/write.

**Blocked by:** none · **Unblocks:** CRM-UX-001, CRM-UX-002, CRM-UX-003, CRM-UX-004

## User story

As an **operator**, I want my company/contact/deal data stored securely and scoped to my organization, so that I never see (or accidentally leak) another org's relationship data.

## User journey

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  A[Engineer opens IPI-362] --> B[Write migration: 4 tables + RLS + trigger]
  B --> C[rls-policy-auditor review]
  C --> D[Regenerate supabase.ts types]
  D --> E[Merge — unblocks Companies/Contacts/Pipeline/Deal Detail]
```

## Architecture diagram

```mermaid
erDiagram
  organizations ||--o{ crm_companies : org_scoped
  organizations ||--o{ crm_contacts : org_scoped
  organizations ||--o{ crm_deals : org_scoped
  organizations ||--o{ crm_activities : org_scoped
  brands ||--o| crm_companies : "brand_id nullable"
  crm_companies ||--o{ crm_contacts : "company_id nullable"
  crm_companies ||--o{ crm_deals : company_id
  crm_deals ||--o{ crm_activities : "deal_id nullable"
  crm_deals ||--o{ notifications : "crm_deal_id nullable"
```

## Implementation Steps

**Full plan with complete DDL:** `docs/plan/tasks/2026-07-04-crm-schema-rls.md`. Condensed here so the steps are visible without leaving Linear.

**✅ Task 4's blocker is now resolved (2026-07-04)** — decision made, ready to implement in the same migration as Tasks 1-3.

### Task 1 — Migration: 4 tables

* Create `supabase/migrations/<today>_crm_core_schema.sql`: `crm_companies`, `crm_contacts` (jsonb `email`/`phone` arrays), `crm_deals`, `crm_activities` (CHECK ≥1 of company/contact/deal anchors). Reuse existing `handle_updated_at()` trigger on all 4 (confirmed live, no-arg, safe to reuse as-is).
* Apply via `apply_migration` (MCP) — remote only, never `supabase start`.
* Verify: `infisical run -- npm run supabase:verify`

### Task 2 — `won`/`lost` DB-level guard trigger

* Create `crm_deals_guard_terminal_stage()` — `BEFORE UPDATE` trigger raising unless `current_setting('app.crm_convert', true) = '1'` is set in the same transaction.
* Verify: direct SQL `UPDATE ... SET stage='won'` without the flag **must raise**; the same update **must succeed** when preceded by `select set_config('app.crm_convert','1',true);`

### Task 3 — RLS policies (all 4 tables)

* 4 policies per table (`select`/`insert`/`update` via `is_org_member(org_id)`, `delete` via `is_org_owner(org_id)`) — write out all 4 tables explicitly, not just one as a template.
* Route through `rls-policy-auditor` before merge.
* Verify: `infisical run -- npm run supabase:verify-rls`, plus a manual cross-org negative test (second test org sees 0 rows).

### Task 4 — Extend `public.notifications` — ✅ RESOLVED

**Decision (2026-07-04):** add a nullable `crm_deal_id uuid references crm_deals(id)` column to `public.notifications`, and extend the CHECK:

```sql
alter table public.notifications add column crm_deal_id uuid references public.crm_deals(id);
alter table public.notifications drop constraint notifications_at_least_one_recipient;
alter table public.notifications add constraint notifications_at_least_one_recipient
  check (num_nonnulls(brand_org_id, talent_profile_id, agency_org_id, crm_deal_id) >= 1);
```

Chosen over routing through `brand_org_id` (broken for prospects — most of the CRM lifecycle predates a `brands` link) and over deferring to Phase 2 (drops a real, already-spec'd feature). Purely additive — existing booking/talent notification rows are unaffected. `kind` itself needs no migration (unconstrained text) — `deal_stage_changed`/`follow_up_due` are just new string values.

* Verify: insert a test row with `crm_deal_id` set and `kind: 'deal_stage_changed'`, confirm it's readable under RLS by the owning org.

### Task 5 — Regenerate types

* `npm run supabase:types && cd app && npm run typecheck` — 0 errors, 4 new tables + the `notifications.crm_deal_id` column present in generated types.

## Description

Add `crm_companies`, `crm_contacts`, `crm_deals`, `crm_activities` — all org-scoped, RLS gated through the existing `is_org_member(org_id)`/`is_org_owner(org_id)` `SECURITY DEFINER` helpers (never a new tenancy pattern). Full column spec: `tasks/crm/02-crm-architecture-brief.md` §Database.

* `crm_companies`: `org_id, brand_id (→ brands, nullable — null until a deal converts it), name, domain, industry, status, source, owner (→ profiles)`.
* `crm_contacts`: `org_id, company_id (nullable), profile_id (nullable), name, email (jsonb array of {value,type}), phone (jsonb array) — not single columns`.
* `crm_deals`: `org_id, company_id, stage (lead/qualified/proposal/negotiation/won/lost), value, currency, shoot_id (nullable), campaign_id (nullable — FK inactive until IPI-268 campaigns table lands), owner, expected_close_date, closed_at`.
* `crm_activities`: unified `note/call/email/meeting/task/ai_summary` timeline — `company_id/contact_id/deal_id` all nullable with a check constraint requiring ≥1. One table, not five.
* All tables use the existing `handle_updated_at()` trigger convention; no soft-delete column (this schema has none).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All 4 tables exist with RLS enabled, reviewed by `rls-policy-auditor`
- [ ] `crm_deals_guard_terminal_stage()` trigger installed and blocks any `UPDATE ... SET stage = 'won'/'lost'` that doesn't set `app.crm_convert = '1'` in the same transaction
- [ ] `crm_contacts.email`/`.phone` are jsonb arrays, not text columns
- [ ] `public.notifications.crm_deal_id` column + updated recipient CHECK live
- [ ] `app/src/types/supabase.ts` regenerated after migration
- [ ] Cross-org read of another org's `crm_*` rows returns empty

## Implementation notes

* Reuse: `organizations`/`org_members`, `is_org_member`/`is_org_owner` (`20260624000000_ipi16_org_layer.sql`)
* Never FK `crm_companies.brand_id` into legacy `fashion_brands` — only the active `brands` table
* `campaign_id` stays nullable/unenforced until IPI-268 ships — do not block on it
* Non-goals: soft-delete, per-type activity tables, contact merge/dedup
* **Branch naming:** use `ipi/362-crm-schema-rls`, not Linear's auto-suggested `ai/` prefix — worktree at `wt-ipi-362-crm-schema`

## Verify

- [ ] `infisical run -- npm run supabase:verify`
- [ ] `infisical run -- npm run supabase:verify-rls`
- [ ] `npm run supabase:types`
- [ ] `cd app && npm test`

Title: MODEL-GATE-10 — AI booking helper (copilot drafts, human confirms)
Description:
## Purpose

Title: MODEL-GATE-07 — Notification inbox API routes
Description:
## Simple purpose

Create the notification inbox API routes that the app will use after per-user notification read state exists.

Think of this as: notification RPCs are safe, Next.js routes validate requests, and future inbox/bell UI can list notifications and update read state.

## Branch

Use `ipi/ipi-345-model-gate-07-notifications-api-routes` at git checkout.

Linear may suggest `ai/ipi-345-model-gate-07-notifications-api-routes`; always use `ipi/`, not `ai/`.

## User stories

* As a brand, talent, or agency user, I can list only notifications meant for me.
* As a user, I can update read state for notifications I am allowed to see.
* As the app, I can request unread-only notifications and paginate safely.
* As the system, another user's notification IDs cannot be updated by me.
* As a reviewer, I can verify the API uses per-user read state, not shared notification flags.

## Scope

* `GET /api/notifications`
* `POST /api/notifications/read`
* Validation for cursor, limit, unread filters, and notification ID batches.
* Standard error envelope from `tasks/models/engineering/api-contracts.md`.
* Route wrappers for `list_notifications` and `mark_notifications_read` RPCs.

## Out of scope

* No notification bell UI.
* No email/push delivery.
* No booking lifecycle trigger changes unless required by notification RPC correctness.
* No booking API routes.
* No Mastra agent.

## Source docs

* `tasks/models/engineering/api-contracts.md`
* `tasks/models/engineering/rpc-contracts.md`
* `tasks/models/engineering/testing-spec.md`

## Mermaid notification API flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant UI as Future Notification UI
  participant API as /api/notifications routes
  participant Auth as withOperatorAuth
  participant RPC as Notification RPCs
  participant DB as Supabase

  UI->>API: list or update read state
  API->>Auth: verify authenticated user
  API->>API: validate cursor/filter/id batch
  API->>RPC: list_notifications / mark_notifications_read
  RPC->>DB: enforce recipient-scoped access
  DB-->>RPC: visible notifications only
  RPC-->>API: result
  API-->>UI: standard response envelope
```

## Route contract

| Route | Purpose | Underlying RPC |
| -- | -- | -- |
| `GET /api/notifications` | List caller-visible notifications | `list_notifications` |
| `POST /api/notifications/read` | Update read state for caller-visible notifications | `mark_notifications_read` |

## Security requirements

* Authenticated only.
* Recipient-scoped reads only.
* ID batches affect only caller-visible notifications.
* Do not use shared `notifications.read` for UX state.
* Standard error envelope only; no stack traces.
* Invalid cursor/limit/id batch returns stable validation errors.

## Files likely touched

* `app/src/app/api/notifications/route.ts`
* `app/src/app/api/notifications/read/route.ts`
* `app/src/app/api/notifications/**/*.test.ts`
* `app/src/lib/booking/validation.ts` or a shared notification validation file

## Tests

* `cd app && npm test -- notifications`
* `cd app && npm run lint`
* `cd app && npm run build`
* `infisical run -- npm run supabase:verify-rls`

## Required test cases

* List route returns only caller-visible notifications.
* Unread-only filter works.
* Cursor and limit validation work.
* Updating one visible notification succeeds.
* Updating a batch of visible notifications succeeds.
* Invisible IDs are rejected or ignored according to `api-contracts.md`.
* Bulk read-state update only affects caller-visible notifications.
* Route never writes shared `notifications.read` state.

## Acceptance criteria

* Notification routes exist and compile.
* Routes call notification RPCs; they do not mutate notification tables directly.
* Read state remains per user.
* Tests cover visible, invisible, batch, unread, and validation cases.
* Future notification panel UI can be built without direct table access.

## Definition of Done

* Notification API routes implemented.
* Notification route tests passing.
* Lint, build, and RLS verification green.
* CI green.
* Linear updated with PR link and verification evidence.

## Risk

🟡 Medium — security-sensitive but narrower than booking lifecycle routes.

## Dependencies

Blocked by [IPI-343](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-343/model-gate-05-notification-read-receipts-per-user) and [IPI-346](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-346/model-gate-08-app-typescript-types-for-booking-apis).

Blocks [IPI-347](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-347/model-gate-09-booking-backend-qa-gate-tests-rls).

## Audit corrections retained

* Use `ipi/ipi-XXX-short-name`, not `ai/ipi-XXX-...`.

Title: MODEL-GATE-06 — Booking API routes for the operator app
Description:
## Simple purpose

Create the backend API routes that the future Booking Wizard, Booking List, and Booking Detail screens will call.

Think of this as: RPCs are safe, but the app needs clean Next.js routes with validation, auth, and predictable errors.

## Branch

Use `ipi/ipi-344-model-gate-06-booking-api-routes-and-validation` at git checkout.

Linear may suggest `ai/ipi-344-model-gate-06-booking-api-routes-and-validation`; always use `ipi/`, not `ai/`.

## User stories

* As a brand operator, I can send a booking request through a stable API route.
* As a brand, talent, or agency user, I can list and view bookings through API routes without direct table access.
* As an allowed actor, I can request valid booking status changes through the API.
* As a brand operator, I can approve/confirm when the workflow allows it.
* As the app, I receive consistent success and error responses.
* As the system, server-only credentials stay server-only and RPC security is preserved.

## Scope

* `POST /api/bookings`
* `GET /api/bookings`
* `GET /api/bookings/[id]`
* `PATCH /api/bookings/[id]`
* `POST /api/bookings/[id]/approve`
* Shared booking validation.
* Standard error envelope from `tasks/models/engineering/api-contracts.md`.
* Booking service wrappers around the public RPCs.

## Out of scope

* No UI.
* No notification API routes.
* No Mastra agent.
* No direct table mutation from routes.
* No new booking lifecycle rules; use existing RPCs.

## Source docs

* `tasks/models/engineering/api-contracts.md`
* `tasks/models/engineering/rpc-contracts.md`
* `tasks/models/engineering/implementation-plan.md`

## Mermaid route flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant UI as Future Booking UI
  participant API as /api/bookings routes
  participant Auth as withOperatorAuth
  participant RPC as Public booking RPCs
  participant DB as Supabase

  UI->>API: create/list/get/patch/approve
  API->>Auth: verify user + operator context
  API->>API: validate input
  API->>RPC: call user-scoped RPCs
  RPC->>DB: enforce party checks + lifecycle rules
  DB-->>RPC: booking result
  RPC-->>API: result or deterministic error
  API-->>UI: standard response envelope
```

## Route contract

| Route | Purpose | Underlying RPC/path |
| -- | -- | -- |
| `POST /api/bookings` | Create request | `create_booking_request` |
| `GET /api/bookings` | List role-visible bookings | `list_bookings` |
| `GET /api/bookings/[id]` | Get one booking | `get_booking` |
| `PATCH /api/bookings/[id]` | Status/date transitions | `transition_booking` |
| `POST /api/bookings/[id]/approve` | Confirm approved booking | existing `confirm_booking` path only |

## Security requirements

* Use `withOperatorAuth`.
* User-scoped client for user RPCs.
* Server-only privileged client is allowed only for `confirm_booking` inside the approve route after operator gate.
* No privileged credential may be returned to the browser.
* No raw direct table updates from routes.
* Standard error envelope only; no stack traces.
* Map stale/invalid/unauthorized/conflict errors to stable HTTP codes.

## Files likely touched

* `app/src/lib/booking/validation.ts`
* `app/src/lib/booking/booking-service.ts`
* `app/src/app/api/bookings/route.ts`
* `app/src/app/api/bookings/[id]/route.ts`
* `app/src/app/api/bookings/[id]/approve/route.ts`
* `app/src/app/api/bookings/**/*.test.ts`

## Tests

* `cd app && npm run lint`
* `cd app && npm test -- booking`
* `cd app && npm run build`
* `infisical run -- npm run supabase:verify-rls`

## Required test cases

* Create route validates required fields.
* List route returns only caller-visible bookings.
* Detail route rejects non-party access.
* Patch route maps stale version to conflict behavior.
* Patch route rejects invalid transitions.
* Approve route is idempotent where expected.
* Approve route maps overlap/double-booking to 409.
* Standard error envelope is used everywhere.
* Server-only credentials are never sent to the client.

## Acceptance criteria

* Booking API routes exist and compile.
* Routes call RPCs; they do not mutate tables directly.
* Input validation exists.
* Error mapping matches `api-contracts.md`.
* Tests cover auth, validation, stale/conflict, and unauthorized access.
* Booking UI can be built against these contracts.

## Definition of Done

* API routes implemented.
* Booking route tests passing.
* Lint, build, typecheck, and RLS verification green.
* CI green.
* Linear updated with PR link and verification evidence.

## Risk

🔴 High — this is the main app-facing booking API layer and must be secure before UI implementation.

## Dependencies

Blocked by [IPI-340](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-340/model-gate-02-send-booking-request-brand-asks-talent), [IPI-341](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-341/model-gate-03-booking-status-changes-accept-decline-confirm), [IPI-342](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-342/model-gate-04-list-and-view-bookings-by-role), and [IPI-346](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-346/model-gate-08-app-typescript-types-for-booking-apis).

Blocks [IPI-347](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-347/model-gate-09-booking-backend-qa-gate-tests-rls) and [IPI-348](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-348/model-gate-10-ai-booking-helper-copilot-drafts-human-confirms).

Title: MODEL-GATE-04 — List and view bookings (by role)
Description:
## Simple purpose

This task creates safe read-only booking views for the app.

Different users need to see different bookings. A brand should see its bookings. A talent should see bookings for their profile. An agency should see bookings for talent it manages. Nobody should see another org's private booking data.

Think of this as: **who are you → what bookings are you allowed to see → return only that data**.

## Branch

Use `ipi/ipi-342-model-gate-04-get-and-list-booking-rpcs` at git checkout.

Linear may suggest `ai/ipi-342-model-gate-04-get-and-list-booking-rpcs` — always use `ipi/`, not `ai/`.

## User stories

* As a brand operator, I can list and open bookings for my organization.
* As independent talent, I can list and open bookings for my own talent profile.
* As an agency operator, I can list and open bookings for roster talent I manage.
* As the system, I prevent users from seeing bookings they do not participate in.
* As the app, I can paginate booking inboxes safely and consistently.

## Scope

Title: MODEL-GATE-03 — Booking status changes (accept, decline, confirm)
Description:
## Simple purpose

This task creates the safe backend state machine for booking status changes.

After a booking request exists, different people need to respond: talent/agency can quote, approve, decline, or cancel depending on role; brand can approve some changes; confirmation remains protected by the existing `confirm_booking` path.

Think of this as: **requested booking → controlled status changes → history + notifications → no stale or unauthorized edits**.

## Branch

Use `ipi/ipi-341-model-gate-03-transition-booking-rpc-and-fsm-actor-rules` at git checkout.

Linear may suggest `ai/ipi-341-model-gate-03-transition-booking-rpc-and-fsm-actor-rules` — always use `ipi/`, not `ai/`.

## User stories

* As talent, I can accept, decline, quote, or cancel requests that belong to me.
* As an agency, I can respond for roster talent I manage.
* As a brand operator, I can approve valid booking changes when the workflow allows it.
* As the system, I can reject invalid status transitions.
* As the system, I can reject stale edits using `expected_version`.
* As the system, I can write an audit/history row for every valid lifecycle change.

## Scope

* Add `public.transition_booking(...)` RPC.
* Enforce the FSM actor matrix from `rpc-contracts.md`.
* Require `expected_version` and reject stale writes.
* Increment `talent.bookings.version` only inside valid transition RPC updates.
* Set `approved_by` when brand approval is required.
* Require `cancellation_reason` for cancel transitions.
* Add `event_type = reschedule` history rows for date-only changes.
* Create `scripts/test-booking-transition-fsm.sql`.
* Create `scripts/test-booking-transition-concurrency.sql`.
* Tighten direct `bookings_update_party` RLS/WITH CHECK so clients cannot bypass the RPC.

## Out of scope

* No new create request RPC; that is `IPI-340 · MODEL-GATE-02 — Send booking request (brand asks talent)`.
* No direct confirm implementation; existing `confirm_booking` remains the only confirm path.
* No API routes.
* No UI.
* No notification read receipts.

## Source docs

* `tasks/models/engineering/rpc-contracts.md`
* `tasks/models/engineering/backend-spec.md`
* `tasks/models/audit/03-model-gate-audit.md`

## Mermaid state machine

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
  [*] --> requested

  requested --> quoted: talent/agency sends quote
  requested --> approved: talent/agency accepts request
  requested --> declined: talent/agency declines
  requested --> cancelled: brand/talent/agency cancels
  requested --> expired: expire_stale_bookings cron only

  quoted --> approved: brand approves quote
  quoted --> declined: brand/talent/agency declines
  quoted --> requested: reschedule reset
  quoted --> cancelled: brand/talent/agency cancels
  quoted --> expired: expire_stale_bookings cron only

  approved --> confirmed: confirm_booking only
  approved --> cancelled: brand/talent/agency cancels

  confirmed --> cancelled: allowed cancellation path only

  declined --> [*]
  expired --> [*]
  cancelled --> [*]
```

## Mermaid request flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant Actor as Brand/Talent/Agency
  participant RPC as public.transition_booking
  participant DB as talent.bookings
  participant H as booking_status_history
  participant N as notifications

  Actor->>RPC: transition request + expected_version
  RPC->>RPC: Verify actor permission + valid FSM move
  RPC->>DB: Update booking + increment version
  RPC->>H: Insert status/reschedule history row
  RPC->>N: Create lifecycle notification
  RPC-->>Actor: updated booking status + version
```

## Business rules

* All status changes must go through `transition_booking`, except `confirmed` and `expired`.
* `confirmed` remains controlled by existing `confirm_booking` only.
* `expired` remains controlled by existing `expire_stale_bookings` cron only.
* Every valid transition must check actor permission.
* Every valid transition must check `expected_version`.
* If the version is stale, return deterministic stale/conflict behavior.
* Date-only reschedules must create `event_type = reschedule` history.
* Direct client table updates must not bypass lifecycle rules.

## Supabase/RLS requirements

* SECURITY DEFINER with explicit actor checks.
* Explicit `search_path`.
* Preserve `(select auth.uid())` pattern.
* Reject or prevent direct authenticated status updates.
* Tighten `bookings_update_party` WITH CHECK as layer 2 of the RLS lockdown.
* Never allow direct `confirmed` transition through this RPC.

## Files likely touched

* `supabase/migrations/*_transition_booking_rpc.sql`
* `scripts/test-booking-transition-fsm.sql`
* `scripts/test-booking-transition-concurrency.sql`
* `scripts/verify-rls.mjs` if needed for new lifecycle probes
* `app/src/types/supabase.ts` only if types are regenerated in this PR

## Tests

```bash
infisical run -- npm run supabase:verify-rls
psql "$DATABASE_URL" -f scripts/test-booking-transition-fsm.sql
psql "$DATABASE_URL" -f scripts/test-booking-transition-concurrency.sql
npm run supabase:types
cd app && npm run typecheck
```

## Required test cases

* Valid requested → quoted succeeds for allowed actor.
* Valid requested → approved succeeds for allowed actor.
* Valid requested → declined succeeds for allowed actor.
* Valid quoted → approved succeeds for allowed actor.
* Valid cancel succeeds only for allowed actors and requires reason.
* Invalid transition fails.
* Unauthorized actor transition fails.
* Stale `expected_version` fails.
* Parallel transition test produces exactly one success and one stale failure.
* Date-only reschedule writes `event_type = reschedule` history.
* Direct status UPDATE is blocked.
* `confirmed` cannot be created through `transition_booking`.
* `expired` is not created through `transition_booking`.

## Acceptance criteria

* `public.transition_booking(...)` exists on remote.
* FSM actor matrix is enforced.
* `expected_version` is required and effective.
* Valid transition increments `version`.
* Invalid/stale/unauthorized transitions fail deterministically.
* Lifecycle history rows are written.
* Lifecycle notifications still work.
* Direct status update bypass is blocked.
* SQL FSM and concurrency tests exist and pass.

## Definition of Done

* Migration merged and applied.
* FSM SQL test committed and passing.
* Concurrency SQL test committed and passing.
* RLS verification green.
* Types regenerated if needed.
* Typecheck green.
* CI green.
* Linear updated with PR link and verification evidence.

## Risk

🔴 High — this is the core correctness gate for booking lifecycle, concurrency, RLS, and UI unblock.

## Dependencies

Blocked by:

* `IPI-339 · MODEL-GATE-01 — Booking edit safety (revision numbers)`
* `IPI-340 · MODEL-GATE-02 — Send booking request (brand asks talent)`

Blocks:

* `IPI-342 · MODEL-GATE-04 — List and view bookings (by role)`
* `IPI-344 · MODEL-GATE-06 — Booking API routes for the operator app`
* `IPI-346 · MODEL-GATE-08 — App TypeScript types for booking APIs`
* `IPI-347 · MODEL-GATE-09 — Booking backend QA gate (tests + RLS)`
* `IPI-348 · MODEL-GATE-10 — AI booking helper (copilot drafts, human confirms)`

## Audit corrections retained

* Use `ipi/ipi-XXX-short-name`, not `ai/ipi-XXX-...`.
* Tighten `bookings_update_party` WITH CHECK.
* Create both FSM and concurrency SQL scripts.
* `expired` status transitions via cron only.
* Add explicit D5 acceptance for date-only reschedule history.

Title: MODEL-GATE-02 — Send booking request (brand asks talent)
Description:
## Simple purpose

This task creates the safe backend path for sending a booking request.

A brand operator wants to book a model/talent. This RPC creates one new booking in `requested` status. It does **not** approve, confirm, cancel, or complete the booking. Those lifecycle changes belong to `IPI-341 · MODEL-GATE-03 — Booking status changes (accept, decline, confirm)`.

Think of this as: **brand asks → talent/agency receives request → booking waits for response**.

## Branch

Use `ipi/ipi-340-model-gate-02-create-booking-request-rpc` at git checkout.

Linear may suggest `ai/ipi-340-model-gate-02-create-booking-request-rpc` — always use `ipi/`, not `ai/`.

## User stories

* As a brand operator, I can send a booking request to talent so the conversation starts in the system.
* As a talent or agency, I can receive a request without the booking being automatically confirmed.
* As the system, I can prove who requested the booking using `(select auth.uid())`.
* As the system, I can reject invalid or cross-organization booking attempts.

## Scope

* Add `public.create_booking_request(...)` RPC.
* Force new bookings to `status =` `'``requested``'`.
* Set `requested_by = (select auth.uid())`.
* Validate brand org membership.
* Validate talent/profile existence.
* Validate date range.
* Validate optional `shoot_id` ownership when provided.
* Return `booking_id`, `status: 'requested'`, `version`, and `expires_at`.
* Create `scripts/test-create-booking-request.sql`.

## Out of scope

* No status transition FSM.
* No approve/decline/confirm logic.
* No API route.
* No UI.
* No Mastra tool.
* No notification read receipts.

## Source docs

* `tasks/models/engineering/rpc-contracts.md`
* `tasks/models/engineering/api-contracts.md`
* `tasks/models/engineering/backend-spec.md`
* `tasks/models/audit/03-model-gate-audit.md`

## Mermaid flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant Brand as Brand Operator
  participant RPC as public.create_booking_request
  participant DB as talent.bookings
  participant Trigger as Booking notification trigger
  participant Inbox as Talent/Agency notification

  Brand->>RPC: Request talent booking
  RPC->>RPC: Validate org, talent, dates, optional shoot
  RPC->>DB: Insert booking with status=requested
  DB->>Trigger: booking_requested event
  Trigger->>Inbox: Create recipient notification
  RPC-->>Brand: booking_id, requested, version, expires_at
```

## Business rules

* New bookings always start as `requested`.
* Caller must be an authenticated brand org member.
* `requested_by` must come from `(select auth.uid())`, not a client-provided field.
* If `shoot_id` is passed, the brand must own or be allowed to use that shoot.
* Invalid date ranges must fail.
* Missing or invisible talent profiles must fail.
* This RPC does not confirm availability; confirmed double-booking protection stays in the existing `confirm_booking` path.

## Supabase/RLS requirements

* Use SECURITY DEFINER with explicit `search_path`.
* Revoke public/anon grants.
* Grant only `authenticated` if user context is required.
* Preserve `auth.uid()` semantics.
* Follow existing `ipix-supabase` RLS conventions.
* No broad `FOR ALL` policy.

```bash
cd app && npm run typecheck
```

Title: MODEL-FIX — Tighten Notifications RLS Update Policy
Description:
**Fix-forward from** [IPI-307](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-307/model-p1-core-schema-rls-notifications-and-confirm-rpc)**'s post-push security advisor scan.** Real gap, not deferred work — surfaced by Supabase's own linter immediately after the MODEL-P1 push.

## Context

`notifications_update_read_state` (from `20260701125400_notifications_table.sql`) has `with check (true)` — the `using` clause correctly restricts *which rows* an owner can touch, but the permissive `with check` means once a row is touched, the owner can rewrite *any* column on it, including reassigning `brand_org_id`/`talent_profile_id`/`agency_org_id` to someone else's, or altering `kind`/`payload` after the fact. Confirmed via `mcp__plugin_supabase_supabase__get_advisors` (security lint) immediately after the [IPI-307](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-307/model-p1-core-schema-rls-notifications-and-confirm-rpc) push.

## Scope

* New migration (does not edit the already-pushed `20260701125400_notifications_table.sql` — this repo's own convention: never rewrite applied remote history)
* Replace the permissive `with check (true)` on `notifications_update_read_state`
* Mirror the ownership `using` clause in `with check` (same 3-way OR: brand/agency/talent-owner)
* Additionally lock `with check` so `brand_org_id`, `talent_profile_id`, `agency_org_id`, `kind`, and `payload` cannot change from their `old` values — only `read` (and `read_at` if added later) may be updated
* Add a `notifications` RLS probe section to `scripts/verify-rls.mjs` (closes the gap flagged after [IPI-307](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-307/model-p1-core-schema-rls-notifications-and-confirm-rpc): the existing smoke suite doesn't cover any Model Booking tables yet)

## Out of scope

Any other Model Booking table's RLS — this issue is scoped to the one confirmed advisor finding on `notifications`.

## Implementation steps

1. Migration: `drop policy notifications_update_read_state`, recreate with tightened `with check` (mirrors `using` + `old.*` column-equality guards via a comparison against `old` in the check expression, or a trigger if the column-lock can't be expressed in a pure RLS `with check`).
2. Extend `scripts/verify-rls.mjs`: create 2 test users, assert user A can mark their own notification read, cannot reassign `brand_org_id` to a different org, cannot read/mark another user's notification.

## Files likely touched

`supabase/migrations/<ts>_notifications_rls_tighten.sql`, `scripts/verify-rls.mjs`.

## Tests

```bash
infisical run -- npm run supabase:verify-rls
npm run supabase:types
cd app && npm run typecheck
```

Plus the new probe itself is the real test for this fix.

## Success criteria

* `with check` on the update policy no longer allows `true` unconditionally.
* An owner can mark their own notification read.
* An owner cannot reassign ownership columns or rewrite `kind`/`payload` via the same update path.
* `verify-rls.mjs` has a notifications-specific probe section (previously missing for all Model Booking tables).

## Risk level

Low — narrow, single-policy fix with a clear before/after; the only complexity is expressing column-level immutability inside a `with check` clause (Postgres RLS doesn't support per-column locks directly, so this may need `old.col is not distinct from new.col` comparisons chained in the check expression, which does work as a valid pattern).

## Related docs

`06-model-booking-implementation-plan.md` (notifications task), [IPI-307](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-307/model-p1-core-schema-rls-notifications-and-confirm-rpc) (source of the finding).

Title: MODEL-P1 — Core Schema, RLS, Notifications, and Confirm RPC
Description:
**Bundles implementation tasks:** `MODEL-001`–`MODEL-008` (incl. `006a`/`006b`/`006c`) from `tasks/models/06-model-booking-implementation-plan.md` Phase 1. Note: this Linear issue is `MODEL-P1` (Phase 1), a different granularity than the plan doc's own `MODEL-001..008` task IDs — don't confuse the two when cross-referencing.

## Context

Foundation schema for the Model Booking MVP — talent profiles, agency rosters, availability, bookings, and the notification system every later phase depends on.

## Scope

* `talent_profiles` + `talent_profiles_public` view (marketplace-safe read)
* `talent_profile_sources` (AI-field provenance — source URL + confidence per field)
* `agency_talent` (agency roster join)
* `talent_availability`
* `bookings` (full state machine incl. `expired`, `cancelled_by`, `cancellation_reason`, `expires_at`)
* `booking_status_history` (append-only audit trail, also carries booking messages)
* `notifications` — **genuinely new work**, not a reuse: documented in `docs/architecture/05-supabase-data-architecture.md:215` but never actually migrated (verified — no existing migration creates it)
* `confirm_booking` RPC — transactional + idempotent (conditional `UPDATE ... WHERE status='approved'`, then crew-attach, in one function)
* `EXCLUDE` constraint (`btree_gist` + `daterange`) on `confirmed` bookings per talent — DB-level double-booking prevention, not app-level check
* `pg_cron` scheduled `expire_stale_bookings()` job (expiry is deterministic, never an agent tool)
* Supabase types regeneration + RLS policy audit

## Out of scope

Payments, contracts, full reviews system, enterprise RBAC (3-actor model only: brand org member / talent profile owner / agency org member). pgvector similarity matching (schema reserves an `ai_embedding` column; querying it is gated on [IPI2-123](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI2-123/match-001-brand-matching-engine-pgvector)).

## Implementation steps

1. Migration: `talent_profiles` + public view + RLS.
2. Migration: `agency_talent`, `talent_availability`, `talent_profile_sources`.
3. Migration: `bookings` + `EXCLUDE` constraint + `booking_status_history`.
4. Migration: `notifications` table.
5. Migration: `confirm_booking` RPC + `pg_cron` expiry job (confirm `pg_cron`/`btree_gist` are enabled first — `list_extensions`, same caution already applied to `pgvector`).
6. `npm run supabase:types` (root) + `rls-policy-auditor` review on every new policy.

## Files likely touched

`supabase/migrations/*_talent_profiles.sql`, `*_agency_talent.sql`, `*_bookings_schema.sql`, `*_notifications_table.sql`, `*_booking_confirm_rpc.sql`, `*_expire_stale_bookings_cron.sql`; `app/src/types/supabase.ts` (regenerated).

## Required skills

`ipix-supabase`, `mermaid-diagrams` (ERD if useful).

## Dependencies

None — this is the foundation phase. Blocks all others.

## User journey impact

Enables every later journey (discovery, profile creation, booking) by providing the data layer.

## Tests

```bash
infisical run -- npm run supabase:verify-rls
npm run supabase:types
cd app && npm run typecheck
```

Plus: a manual SQL test inserting two overlapping `confirmed` bookings for the same talent (assert `exclusion_violation`), and calling `confirm_booking` twice on the same booking (assert idempotent no-op on the second call).

## Success criteria

* No cross-tenant RLS leakage (brand can't read another brand's booking rate; agency can't read another agency's roster).
* Double-booking blocked at the DB level, not just the app.
* `confirm_booking` is transactional and idempotent (verified via the two-call test above).
* Expired bookings transition automatically via the scheduled job, not a client-triggered action.

## Risk level

**High** — this phase carries the two hardest correctness guarantees in the whole feature (no double-booking, no duplicate confirmation). Test the negative/concurrent cases explicitly, not just the happy path.

## Related docs

`tasks/models/02-model-booking-architecture-brief.md` (schema rationale) · `06-model-booking-implementation-plan.md` Phase 1, Blockers #4/#8/#9.

## The problem this solves

Approval state shows thinking dots + text fallback or brand-hub DNA `ApprovalCard` — not DC L256–301 large preview (236×4:5 AI result + ref thumb). Empty state uses grey block instead of DC L196 faded 200×150 editorial preview.

**Fix:** Dashboard approval stub with preview image; empty state uses EmptyState DC pattern. Full Approve/Edit/Discard → IPI-244.

## Scope guard

**Out of scope:** Full HITL workflow (IPI-244) · OperatorShell · IntelligencePanel rewrite · schema

## Image loading

* Empty preview: 200×150 decorative · `alt=""` if purely decorative

> As an **operator** with pending drafts, I see a visual approval card with a fashion preview image — not just a text count — so I know what needs my review.

## Skills to run

Title: CC-IMG-001 · Cloudinary sample-images module
Description:
# IPI-291 · CC-IMG-001 — Cloudinary sample-images module

**Linear:** [IPI-291](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-291)  
**Parent:** [IPI-290](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-290)  
**Plan:** `tasks/design-docs/implementation/command-center.md` § Cloudinary rules · Image priority  
**Visual target:** `tasks/design-docs/implementation/command.png`  
**Estimate:** 2 points

---

## Skills to run

| Order | Skill | Purpose |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 1 | `worktrees` | Branch `ipi/17-command-center-dc-polish` |
| 2 | `ipix-task-lifecycle` | Phase 3 implement · verify matrix |
| 3 | `cloudinary` | URL transforms · **Cloudinary MCP** `list-images` for 8-id pool |
| 4 | `vercel-react-best-practices` | Hoist static URL pool · cache URL builder |
| 5 | `gen-test` | `sample-images.test.ts` |
| 5 | `gen-test` | `sample-images.test.ts` |
| 6 | `lean` | No new abstractions beyond URL helper |
| 7 | `graphify` | Optional — blast radius if touching queries |

**Before Done:** `@task-verifier` probes in `verifier-probes-ipix.md` § IPI-290–295.

---

## The problem this solves

Hero and recent-work tiles have no `coverUrl` / `imageUrl` types or URL builder. DC prototype uses `ph()` map → `Universal design prompt/images/{n}-fashionos.jpeg` and Cloudinary `services/` folder on `dzqy2ixl0`.

**Fix:** Central `sample-images.ts` with curated DC-aligned public_ids + deterministic fallbacks per image priority chain (plan § Image priority rules).

---

## Scope guard

**In scope:** `lib/command-center/sample-images.ts` · type extensions · `.env.local` cloud name  
**Out of scope:** React component rewrites · OperatorShell · NavSidebar · IntelligencePanel · mobile · schema

---

## Cloudinary rules

* Cloud: `dzqy2ixl0`
* Transforms: `f_auto,q_auto,c_fill,g_auto`
* Hero: `w_208,h_208` · Recent: `w_276,h_345`
* No API secrets in client bundle

---

## Image priority (implement in helper)

Brand cover → approved asset → shoot image → Cloudinary sample → local design JPG → neutral placeholder

---

## User stories

### Story 1: Dev preview shows real photos

**As a** developer, **I want** `?skip=1` to use Cloudinary JPGs, **so that** layout QA matches DC without live brand assets.

**Acceptance:** Dev fixture URLs resolve to 200 OK images.

### Story 2: Live users get graceful fallbacks

**As an** operator, **I want** fashion imagery even when DB has no cover, **so that** the dashboard never shows grey-only tiles.

**Acceptance:** `heroFallbackForBrand(id)` returns stable URL per brand.

### Story 3: Env hygiene

**As an** engineer, **I want** `CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME=dzqy2ixl0`, **so that** transforms don't 404.

---

## Design reference

| DC | `Command Center.v2.image-first.dc.html` L497–498 (`IMG` + `ph()`) |
| Local | `Universal design prompt/images/5-fashionos.jpeg` … `24-fashionos.jpeg` |
| Cloudinary | `5-fashionos_wc2p1c`, `9-fashionos_ddj5jx`, … (`asset_folder: services`) |

---

## Technical notes

**New file:** `app/src/lib/command-center/sample-images.ts`

* `cloudinaryImageUrl(publicId, { w, h })` — mirror `app/src/app/api/brands/[id]/assets/route.ts`
* `heroFallbackForBrand(brandId)` — hash → 8-id pool (DC keys: char, clay, denim, …)
* `recentFallbackForShoot(shootId, index)` — same pool, no adjacent duplicates
* Default cloud: `process.env.CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME ?? "dzqy2ixl0"`

**Do NOT:** Commit `.env.local` · expose API secret in client · add migration · rewrite components (IPI-292+).

**Env fix (local only):** `CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME=dzqy2ixl0` — current value has invalid suffix `/nvdlhrodvevgwdsneplk`.

**Extend types (prep for IPI-292/293):**

* `HeroBrand.coverUrl?: string | null`
* `RecentShoot.imageUrl?: string | null`

---

## API wiring

| Route | Status | Auth | Returns |
| -- | -- | -- | -- |
| Server `page.tsx` KPI fetch | ✅ exists | Supabase session | `CommandCenterData` |
| New API route | ⚪ not needed | — | fallbacks client-safe URLs only |

No migration. Optional later join `cloudinary_assets` (IPI-271).

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] **A** `sample-images.ts` exports URL builder + 8 curated public_ids aligned to DC `ph()` map
- [ ] **B** Unit test: URL shape + deterministic fallback for same brandId
- [ ] **C** `DEV_PREVIEW_COMMAND_CENTER_DATA` includes sample `coverUrl` + tile `imageUrl`s
- [ ] **D** `.env.local` documents `CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME=dzqy2ixl0` (local fix only)
- [ ] **E** No secrets in client bundle · no new env vars beyond existing Cloudinary name

## Out of scope

* React component image wiring (IPI-292/293)
* OperatorShell · NavSidebar · IntelligencePanel
* Schema migration · new API routes

---

## Completion steps

#### A. Setup

- [ ] **A1** Worktree + branch — proof: `git branch --show-current` = `ipi/17-command-center-dc-polish`
- [ ] **A2** Fix `CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME=dzqy2ixl0` in `.env.local` — proof: grep exact line

#### B. Implement

- [ ] **B1** `sample-images.ts` + 8 public_ids — proof: vitest green
- [ ] **B2** Extend `HeroBrand.coverUrl` · `RecentShoot.imageUrl` in types.ts
- [ ] **B3** Wire URLs into `DEV_PREVIEW_COMMAND_CENTER_DATA` (coverUrl + tile imageUrls)

#### C. Verify

- [ ] **C1** `cd app && npx vitest run src/lib/command-center/sample-images.test.ts`
- [ ] **C2** `curl -I` one hero URL → HTTP 200
- [ ] **C3** `cd app && npx tsc --noEmit`
- [ ] **C4** Linear → Done

---

## Test


# 🛠 Implementation Prompt Pack (2026-06-30)

**Worktree:** `ipi/270-design-010-tokens` · `../wt-ipi-270-design-010-tokens`
**Skills:** frontend-design · shadcn · design-md · task-verifier · mermaid-diagrams
**Source:** `Universal design prompt/tokens.css` (Zeely v3) · `Universal design prompt/Component Library.dc.html`

## User stories

* As a **developer**, I reference one semantic token set (`--color-action`, `--card-radius`, …) so every operator screen matches DC without hardcoded hex.
* As a **designer**, operator and marketing share a reconciled palette/type scale, so the brand reads consistent across surfaces.
* As a **reviewer**, a grep gate proves no new component ships raw hex.

## Token flow (DC → app)

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    DC["Zeely v3 tokens.css (DC)"] --> MAP["Semantic map: --color-action, --card-radius, --space-*"]
    MAP --> APP["app/src/styles/tokens.css"]
    APP --> TW["tailwind.config.ts utilities"]
    TW --> COMP["Operator components"]
    MKT["iPix marketing tokens (Cormorant + Outfit)"] -.reconcile.-> APP
```

## Reconcile decision

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    T{Token exists in app?} -->|no| ADD[Add semantic var]
    T -->|yes, same value| KEEP[Keep]
    T -->|yes, diverges| R{Operator vs marketing?}
    R -->|operator shell| USE_DC[Adopt DC value]
    R -->|marketing route| SCOPE[Scope under marketing selector]
```

## Implementation steps (Test block each)

| Step | Prompt | Test / proof |
| -- | -- | -- |
| **A** | Diff `Universal design prompt/tokens.css` vs `app/src/styles/tokens.css` → produce mapping table (DC name → app name → value) | mapping table in PR |
| **B** | Add/rename semantic tokens in `app/src/styles/tokens.css`; wire into `tailwind.config.ts` | tokens resolve in a sample component |
| **C** | Reconcile marketing font tokens (Cormorant/Outfit) — no regression on `(marketing)` routes | marketing route visual unchanged |
| **D** | `rg -n '#[0-9a-fA-F]{3,6}' app/src/components` on new operator components → replace with vars | grep returns 0 in touched files |
| **E** | Visual diff vs DC Component Library at 1440 + 375; run **task-verifier** | screenshots + verifier pass |

## Out of scope

Component redesign · new screens · marketing palette overhaul. **Token layer only.**

## Verification

```bash
cd app && npm run lint && npx tsc --noEmit && npm run build
rg -n '#[0-9a-fA-F]{3,6}' app/src/components   # expect 0 in changed files
```

## Evidence required (Done gate)

- [ ] Token mapping table · grep-clean output
- [ ] 1440 + 375 screenshots vs DC
- [ ] task-verifier report · PR link · Linear → Done

# IPI-274 · DESIGN-056 — Shoot Wizard React Parity

## Scope contract (read first)

**IPI-274 is a visual reskin of the existing 6-step shoot wizard only.** Preserve all existing behavior. Do **not** add new steps, backend logic, AI tools, API routes, schema/migrations, or the booking flow. "Done" means visual parity with the DC chrome — **not** feature expansion.

## Core rule

**Don't code from Linear text alone.** Prove disk + Supabase + browser first. **HTML design wins for layout.**

**Shell:** `OperatorPanel` already wraps `/app/shoots/new` — work is **center workspace chrome only** (same as brand PR #181). Do **not** rebuild NavSidebar or IntelligencePanel.

## Production state (2026-07-05)

| -- | -- |
| Route | ✅ `shoots/new/page.tsx` ~824 LOC (client monolith, keep client) |

| WizardStep DC chrome | ❌ inline stepper |
| Tokens / hex debt | ❌ `#FBF8F5` on page |

## File inventory (verified 2026-07-05)

**May edit (restyle only):**

* `app/src/app/(operator)/app/shoots/new/page.tsx` — extract inline styles → CSS module, keep all handlers; stays a client component (do **not** refactor to RSC)
* `app/src/components/shoot/shoot-wizard.module.css` — **CREATE**
* `app/src/components/shoot/hitl/*ApprovalCard.tsx` (×3) — restyle only

**Must NOT edit:** `mastra/workflows/shoot-wizard.ts` · `api/shoots/commit|suggest-brief|[shootId]/route.ts` · `shoot-wizard-context.tsx` · shell/nav/intel/dock · migrations · `types/supabase.ts`

## Acceptance criteria

* Center workspace matches DC `WizardStep` chrome across all 6 steps at 1440 / 1024 / 390
* Zeely tokens only — zero `#FBF8F5` / legacy hex in changed files
* Existing behavior preserved (see regression checklist below)
* §9 parity scorecard filled 🟢 (Layout · Typography · Tokens · States · Responsive · A11y) with side-by-side screenshots

## Regression checklist (must still pass after reskin)

- [ ] Back / Continue navigation between all 6 steps
- [ ] 3 HITL gates still block progression until approved
- [ ] Edit / approve draft in each approval card
- [ ] Brief autogeneration (`suggest-brief`) unchanged
- [ ] Commit + redirect to `/app/shoots/[id]`

- [ ] Only existing endpoints called — no new routes

## Scope

* Extract **WizardStep** · apply Zeely tokens · polish **OperatorChatDock** step context
* Preserve 6-step Mastra HITL — **no workflow rewrite**
* 10-step DC expansion → [IPI-252](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-252) · booking flow → IPI-340/341/342

Description:
## Context

[IPI-243](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-243/intel-001-build-intelligencepanel-context-dna-assets-suggestions-chat) Phase A **✅ Done** (PR #149). This issue adds the write path: approve, edit, and reject actions directly from the panel, using the existing `POST /api/workflows/brand-intelligence/approve` route.

**Audit 2026-06-30:** IPI-243 is **no longer a blocker** — Phase B thumbnails optional.

---

## Existing write path (verified on disk)

`app/src/app/api/workflows/brand-intelligence/approve/route.ts` already exists:

* `POST { runId: string, approved: boolean }` — resumes the Mastra HITL workflow
* Updates `brand_intake_drafts.status` via RLS-gated Supabase
* Returns success/error

The panel calls this route on approve/reject. Edit action navigates to the workspace draft.

---

### Blocked by / Related (Linear)

| Type | Issues |
| -- | -- |
| **Blocked by** | none — IPI-243 Phase A ✅ |
| **Soft** | IPI-172 EvidenceBlock payload on card `[why]` |
| **Related** | IPI-246 component ✅ · DESIGN-072 |

**SSOT:** MASTER-DEPENDENCIES · DESIGN-072

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A · ApprovalQueue replaces `PendingDraftsPreview` in IntelligencePanel
- [ ] B · Each card shows before/after diff, confidence %, evidence source
- [ ] C · Approve/Edit/Reject functional via existing approve route
- [ ] D · Optimistic green transition; fade-out on success; amber revert on error
- [ ] E · Toast error on API failure
- [ ] F · Pending count badge updates after approve/reject
- [ ] G · All tokens via CSS variables
- [ ] H · `npm run typecheck` + `npm test` green
- [ ] I · No direct Supabase writes from UI components

---

# Implementation Prompt Pack (2026-06-30)

**Worktree:** `ipi/244-approval-queue` · `../wt-ipi-244-approval-queue`
**Skills to run:** copilotkit (v2 useInterrupt) · mastra · frontend-design · shadcn · gen-test · mermaid-diagrams
**Design file (READ FIRST):** `tasks/design-docs/design/AI-EXPLAINABILITY.md` · `Universal design prompt/components/` ApprovalCard + EvidenceBlock (IPI-246) · handoff DESIGN-040 ApprovalCard parity
**MCP:** CopilotKit MCP (HITL interrupt-flow docs) · Mastra docs server

## User stories

* As a **brand guardian**, I review AI draft changes (before/after + confidence + evidence) and approve/edit/reject inline — without leaving the panel.
* As a **brand guardian**, every AI write is reversible behind a gate — nothing persists until I approve.
* As an **operator**, the pending-count badge updates immediately so I always see the queue depth.
* As a **developer**, all writes go through the existing approve route — no direct Supabase writes from UI.

## Wireframe (ApprovalQueue in panel)

```
+-----------------------------+
| Intelligence  . Pending (3) |  <- badge
| +-------------------------+ |
| | Tone: "bold" -> "refined"| |  <- before/after diff
| | confidence 82%  [why]    | |  <- EvidenceBlock (IPI-246)
| | [Approve] [Edit] [Reject]| |  <- HITL actions
| +-------------------------+ |
| | Palette draft ...        | |
| +-------------------------+ |
+-----------------------------+
```

## Card state flow

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Pending
    Pending --> Approving: click Approve
    Approving --> Approved: route 200 (green, fade-out)
    Approving --> Pending: route error (amber revert + toast)
    Pending --> Rejecting: click Reject
    Rejecting --> Removed: route 200
    Pending --> Editing: click Edit
    Editing --> Workspace: navigate to draft
```

## HITL approve sequence

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    actor BG as Brand Guardian
    participant Q as ApprovalQueue
    participant API as POST /api/workflows/brand-intelligence/approve
    participant M as Mastra workflow (suspended)
    participant DB as Supabase (brand_intake_drafts)
    BG->>Q: click Approve
    Q->>Q: optimistic green
    Q->>API: { runId, approved: true } (withOperatorAuth)
    API->>M: resume HITL
    M->>DB: update status (RLS)
    API-->>Q: 200
    Q-->>BG: fade-out + badge decrement
    Note over Q: on error -> amber revert + toast
```

## Frontend <-> backend wiring

| Route | Status | Auth | Body / Returns |
| -- | -- | -- | -- |
| `POST /api/workflows/brand-intelligence/approve` | exists | withOperatorAuth | `{ runId, approved }` -> success/error |

State: optimistic local update + SWR mutate on the drafts key. No direct Supabase from UI.

## Implementation steps (Test block each)

| Step | Prompt | Test / proof |
| -- | -- | -- |
| **A** | Build `ApprovalQueue` replacing `PendingDraftsPreview`; map drafts -> cards | renders pending list |
| **B** | Card: before/after diff + confidence % + EvidenceBlock `[why]` (IPI-246) | diff + evidence show |
| **C** | Wire Approve/Reject to approve route; Edit -> navigate to draft | route called, edit nav works |
| **D** | Optimistic green->fade on 200; amber revert + toast on error; badge updates | forced-error path reverts + toasts |
| **E** | Vitest: approve success, approve error revert, badge decrement | `npm test` green |

## Out of scope

IntelligencePanel shell (IPI-243 ✅) · EvidenceBlock component itself (IPI-246) · new agent tools. Write-path UI only.

## Verification

```bash
cd app && npm run lint && npx tsc --noEmit && npm test && npm run build
```

Route to test: brand route with pending drafts — approve, reject, edit-nav, forced API error.

## Evidence required (Done gate)

- [ ] Screenshots: pending / approving / success / error-revert
- [ ] Vitest output · task-verifier
- [ ] PR link · Linear → Done

Title: FIX · social-discovery edge deploy or retire
Description:
## FIX · Phase 2.3 — social-discovery edge deploy or retire

**Status:** 🟡 **In Review** — [PR #139](<https://github.com/amo-tech-ai/lumina-studio/pull/139>) · branch `ipi/229-social-discovery-refactor`  
**Parent:** [IPI-222](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-222) · **Audit:** Phase 2.3 fix plan  
**Decision (locked):** **Option B — Path B** (same pattern as IPI-228 shoot commit). Mastra tool writes via shared server lib; **do not deploy** edge fn.

---

## User stories

### Operator (brand intake)

| ID | Story | Acceptance |
| -- | -- | -- |
| US-1 | **As an** operator running brand onboarding, **I want** official social channels discovered automatically from my brand URL and crawl data, **so that** I don't have to hunt down Instagram/TikTok handles manually. | Brand intelligence workflow completes social step; verified channels appear on brand profile |
| US-2 | **As an** operator, **I want** only verified official accounts saved (not fan pages or resellers), **so that** downstream shoot and channel planning uses trustworthy links. | Tool filters `verified=true` + https URLs; unverified platforms omitted |
| US-3 | **As an** operator, **I want** social discovery to survive a Gemini hiccup without losing audit history, **so that** I can see what failed and retry. | Failed runs log `brand_agent_results` with `status=failed`; workflow continues |

### Operator (downstream UX — enabled by this fix)

| ID | Story | Acceptance |
| -- | -- | -- |
| US-4 | **As an** operator on Brand Hub, **I want** discovered channels to persist in the database after intake, **so that** channel preview and shoot wizard can reference them later. | Rows in `brand_social_channels` after workflow (V-005 / IPI-233) |
| US-5 | **As an** operator re-running intake on the same brand, **I want** social channels updated not duplicated, **so that** I don't get conflicting platform rows. | Upsert on `(brand_id, platform)` — idempotent |

### Platform / engineering

| ID | Story | Acceptance |
| -- | -- | -- |
| US-6 | **As a** platform engineer, **I want** Mastra social writes to succeed without an undeployed edge fn, **so that** cert-path V-005 chains are not silently broken. | No `callEdgeFunction("social-discovery")`; `persistSocialDiscovery` used |
| US-7 | **As a** platform engineer, **I want** social write logic in one shared lib (like shoot commit), **so that** route/tool/edge retirement stays maintainable. | `app/src/lib/brand/persist-social-discovery.ts` SSOT |
| US-8 | **As a** security reviewer, **I want** social channel writes gated to server-side service role only, **so that** no browser client can upsert another brand's channels. | Admin client in Mastra runtime only; no `NEXT_PUBLIC_*` service key |

### User journey (operator happy path)

```mermaid
journey
    title Social discovery during brand intake
    section Submit brand
      Enter brand URL: 5: Operator
      Start intelligence workflow: 5: Operator
    section AI discovery
      Crawl completes: 4: System
      Gemini finds official socials: 4: System
      Channels saved to DB: 5: System
    section Review
      See channels on Brand Hub: 5: Operator
      Plan shoot per channel: 5: Operator
```

---

## Problem

`social-discovery` edge fn exists in repo but was **never deployed** on remote. Mastra tool called `callEdgeFunction("social-discovery")` → writes silently failed in brand-intelligence workflow.

| Probe | Before PR #139 | After PR #139 |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Edge fn in repo | ✅ `supabase/functions/social-discovery/` | ⏭️ corpse → **IPI-231** delete |
| Edge fn on remote | ❌ not deployed | N/A (Path B) |
| Mastra tool write path | 🔴 `callEdgeFunction` | ✅ `persistSocialDiscovery` |
| Unit tests | 4 (mocked edge) | **7/7** (lib + tool) |

---

## Architecture — Path B (shipped in PR #139)

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant WF as brand-intelligence workflow
    participant Tool as discoverSocialChannelsTool
    participant G as Gemini (generateObject)
    participant Lib as persistSocialDiscovery
    participant DB as Supabase (service_role)

    WF->>Tool: execute({ brandId })
    Tool->>DB: READ brands + brand_crawls
    Tool->>G: discover channels (structured output)
    G-->>Tool: channels[]
    Tool->>Lib: persistSocialDiscovery(admin, payload)
    Lib->>DB: UPSERT brand_social_channels
    Lib->>DB: INSERT brand_agent_results
    Lib-->>Tool: { ok, count, status }
    Tool-->>WF: { channelsFound, status }
```

### Before vs after (edge fn retirement)

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    subgraph Before["Before — broken"]
        T1[Mastra tool] -->|callEdgeFunction| E1[social-discovery edge fn]
        E1 -.->|not deployed| X1[404 / silent fail]
    end

    subgraph After["After — Path B"]
        T2[Mastra tool] --> L2[persistSocialDiscovery lib]
        L2 -->|service_role| DB2[(brand_social_channels)]
        L2 --> LOG2[(brand_agent_results)]
    end
```

### Data model (writes)

```mermaid
erDiagram
    BRANDS ||--o{ BRAND_SOCIAL_CHANNELS : has
    BRANDS ||--o{ BRAND_AGENT_RESULTS : logs
    BRAND_SOCIAL_CHANNELS {
        uuid brand_id PK
        text platform PK
        text url
        text handle
        boolean verified
        text bio
        jsonb content_themes
        text posting_frequency
        timestamptz discovered_at
    }
    BRAND_AGENT_RESULTS {
        uuid brand_id
        text agent_name
        text status
        jsonb output
        timestamptz started_at
        timestamptz completed_at
    }
```

### Issue lifecycle

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Todo
    Todo --> InProgress: worktree + Path B impl
    InProgress --> InReview: PR #139 open
    InReview --> Done: merge + tests green
    Done --> FollowUp: IPI-231 edge corpse delete
```

---

## Edge function best practices (`ipix-supabase`)

Per [edge-functions.md](<../../.claude/skills/ipix-supabase/references/edge-functions/edge-functions.md>) and project rules:

| Rule | IPI-229 application |
| -- | -- |
| **When to use edge** | Public webhooks, JWT user-scoped writes, Gemini at edge without Next.js |
| **When NOT to use edge** | Server-only Mastra tool writes with service_role — use shared lib or `/api/*` (IPI-228 precedent) |
| `_shared/` **only** | No cross-fn imports; CORS via `_shared/cors.ts`, auth via `_shared/auth.ts` |
| **Service-role gate** | Old edge fn: service-role only (correct — no user-JWT write to arbitrary `brandId`) |
| **Inventory sync** | Repo fn not deployed → **retire** from repo + update inventory (IPI-231), not deploy orphan |
| **Verify after change** | `npm run supabase:verify-edge` — must pass with fn **removed** from expected deploy list |
| **Secrets** | `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` server-only in Next.js/Mastra — never client |

**Path B rationale:** Edge fn duplicated logic already runnable in Next.js runtime; undeployed corpse caused production gap. Aligns with shoot commit Path B ([IPI-228](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-228)).

---

## Implementation (PR #139)

| File | Role |
| -- | -- |
| `app/src/lib/brand/persist-social-discovery.ts` | Upsert channels + agent log (ported from edge fn) |
| `app/src/lib/brand/persist-social-discovery.test.ts` | 3 unit tests |
| `app/src/mastra/tools/social-discovery.ts` | READ Supabase + Gemini; WRITE via lib |
| `app/src/mastra/tools/social-discovery.test.ts` | 4 tool tests (mock lib) |

**Follow-up (IPI-231 — edge-only PR):**

* Delete `supabase/functions/social-discovery/`
* Remove from `supabase/config.toml` if listed
* Update `references/edge-functions-inventory.md`

## IPI-229 Audit Report

**Verdict: mostly correct — 88/100** (disk-verified 2026-06-28, PR #139 @ `f6ef098`)

The plan is sound: **do not deploy the unused Edge Function**; move Mastra writes to a shared server-side lib. Aligns with Supabase guidance — Edge Functions for HTTP/webhook/public edge workloads; internal service-role writes do not need an extra Edge hop. ([Edge Functions](<https://supabase.com/docs/guides/functions>) · [RLS](<https://supabase.com/docs/guides/database/postgres/row-level-security>))

### What is correct (verified on disk)

| Area | Verdict | Probe |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Path B decision | ✅ | Issue + PR #139 scope |
| Shared lib `persistSocialDiscovery` | ✅ | `app/src/lib/brand/persist-social-discovery.ts` |
| Mastra tool off `callEdgeFunction` | ✅ | `rg callEdgeFunction social-discovery.ts` → 0 matches |
| Edge fn retirement deferred to IPI-231 | ✅ | `supabase/functions/social-discovery/` still in repo |
| Tests 7/7 (lib + tool) | ✅ | vitest run 2026-06-28 |
| Runtime V-005 deferred to IPI-233 | ✅ | No live workflow smoke yet |
| CI green | ✅ | app-build + supabase-web015 SUCCESS |
| Unresolved PR threads | ✅ | 0 on PR #139 |
| Idempotency `(brand_id, platform)` | ✅ | upsert `onConflict` + DB `unique (brand_id, platform)` |
| `verified=true` before persist | ✅ | tool filter L121–127 before calling lib |
| Failed runs log `brand_agent_results` | ✅ | lib always inserts; upstream `status=failed` path tested |
| `persistSocialDiscovery` import scope | ✅ | only `mastra/tools/social-discovery.ts` (+ tests) |

### Red flags (confirmed — follow-up required)

| Risk | Severity | Status | Fix |
| -- | -- | -- | -- |
| Edge corpse still in repo | 🟡 Medium | Open | IPI-231 delete fn + inventory |
| Runtime not proven | 🟡 Medium | Open | IPI-233 V-005 chain |
| Service-role write path | 🟡 Medium | **Mitigated** | Admin client server-only; no client import of lib |
| Idempotency | 🟡 Medium | **Verified in code** | upsert + unique constraint |
| Gemini failure handling | 🟡 Medium | **Verified in tests** | persist called with `status=failed`, `channels=[]` |
| Official account verification | 🟡 Medium | **Verified in tool** | `verified && (url\|\|handle) && https` filter |
| `brand_agent_results` insert fail | 🟢 Low | Pre-existing | lib warns on logErr only (same as edge fn) |

### Blocker (certification only — not merge)

```text
Brand intelligence workflow → social-discovery tool → brand_social_channels rows
```

PR #139 is **mergeable** with green CI + 7/7 tests. **Certification cannot pass** until IPI-233 proves this chain live.

### Pre-merge checklist (all green 2026-06-28)

- [X] PR #139 — 0 unresolved review threads
- [X] CI — app-build + supabase-web015 SUCCESS
- [X] `cd app && npm run lint && npm run typecheck`

```bash

rg 'callEdgeFunction\s*\(\s*"social-discovery"' app/src   # expect: 0
rg "persistSocialDiscovery" app/src                         # tool + lib + tests only
rg "SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY" app/src --glob '!app/src/app/api/**'  # mastra/server only (pre-existing)
```

### Official-doc alignment
