Title: MI-03w · Show required image sizes while planning a shoot
Description:
## MI-03w · Show required image sizes while planning a shoot

**Plain English:** When an operator picks channels in the shoot wizard (e.g. "IG Story"), immediately show the exact image dimensions, aspect ratio, and format requirements inline — so they know what they're committing to shoot *before* they plan deliverables and shot lists.

**Blocker resolved:** MI-02 `lookupChannelSpecs` tool shipped in PR #111 ✅

---

## The problem this solves

Right now, step 1 of the shoot wizard shows a row of channel buttons (IG Feed, IG Story, TikTok, Amazon…). The operator clicks them, then moves on to plan shots — without ever seeing what specs those channels actually require.

This creates mistakes downstream:

* They plan a square shot for "IG Story" → gets cropped (9:16, not 1:1)
* They commit to "Amazon" with no idea it needs a specific white-background format
* The AI generates deliverables based on channels the operator didn't fully understand

**Fix:** Surface specs the moment a channel is selected. No extra clicks, no leaving the page.

---

## User story

> As an operator planning a shoot, when I tick "IG Story" in step 1,
> I immediately see "1080 × 1920 px · 9:16 · JPG or MP4 · max 30 MB"
> so I can tell the photographer exactly what to capture before we plan shots.

---

## Wireframe — Step 1 (Brief & Channels)

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  ● ○ ○ ○ ○ ○   New Shoot — Step 1 of 6                     │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                              │
│  Shoot name   [Spring Collection 2026              ]         │
│  Brand        [Maison Cleo                         ▾]        │
│  Brief        [Soft naturals, outdoor light…       ]         │
│                                                              │
│  Channels  (pick all that apply)                             │
│                                                              │
│  [✓ IG Feed]  [✓ IG Story]  [ TikTok ]  [✓ Amazon ]        │
│  [ Facebook]  [ Pinterest]  [ YouTube]  [ Website ]         │
│                                                              │
│  ── Specs for your selected channels ──────────────────────  │
│                                                              │
│  ┌─────────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────┐           │
│  │ 📸  Instagram Feed  │  │ 📱  Instagram Story  │          │
│  │  1080 × 1350 px    │  │  1080 × 1920 px     │          │
│  │  Ratio  4:5        │  │  Ratio  9:16        │          │
│  │  JPG · PNG         │  │  JPG · MP4          │          │
│  │  Max 30 MB         │  │  Max 30 MB          │          │
│  └─────────────────────┘  └─────────────────────┘           │
│                                                              │
│  ⚠  Amazon — no spec on file yet (safe zone data coming)    │
│                                                              │
│  [  Plan deliverables with AI  →  ]                          │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**States the spec section must handle:**

| State | What to show |
| -- | -- |
| No channels selected | Nothing (section hidden) |
| Channel selected, fetching | Skeleton shimmer card |
| Spec loaded (known channel) | Spec card: dimensions, ratio, formats, max size |
| Unknown channel (not in DB) | Amber warning: "No spec on file yet" |
| Fetch error | Red inline error with retry |

---

## Flow diagram

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Op as Operator
    participant UI as Wizard Step 1
    participant API as /api/media/specs
    participant DB as image_specs (Supabase)

    Op->>UI: Ticks a channel (e.g. IG Story)
    UI->>UI: Add to state.channels[]
    UI->>API: GET /api/media/specs?channels=instagram_story
    API->>DB: getChannelSpec("instagram_story")
    DB-->>API: { widthPx: 1080, heightPx: 1920, ... }
    API-->>UI: SpecResult[]
    UI->>UI: Render spec card below channel buttons
    UI->>Op: Shows "1080 × 1920 · 9:16 · JPG/MP4 · 30 MB"

    Op->>UI: Ticks "Amazon" (not seeded)
    UI->>API: GET /api/media/specs?channels=amazon
    API->>DB: getChannelSpec("amazon")
    DB-->>API: null
    API-->>UI: { spec: null, warning: "Unknown channel" }
    UI->>Op: Shows amber "No spec on file yet" badge
```

---

## Acceptance criteria

* **A — Spec cards appear inline:** Selecting any seeded channel (instagram_feed, instagram_story, instagram_reel, tiktok, facebook) shows a spec card with: dimensions (W × H px), aspect ratio label, accepted formats, max file size.
* **B — Unknown channels show a warning:** Any channel not in the DB (amazon, shopify, pinterest, youtube, website, instagram_reel) shows an amber "No spec on file yet" badge — no error, no crash, no fabricated data.
* **C — States are handled:** Loading shows skeleton shimmer; fetch error shows inline red message with retry; no channels selected hides the section entirely.
* **D — Specs update live:** Unticking a channel removes its spec card immediately; ticking re-fetches.
* **E — Existing wizard behaviour unchanged:** Step progression, HITL gates, deliverable planning, shot list, and budget gates all work as before. Adding this section must not break any existing step.

---

## Technical notes

**Where to add the fetch:**

* The wizard is a `"use client"` component (`app/src/app/(operator)/app/shoots/new/page.tsx`)
* Server-side `getChannelSpec()` already exists in `app/src/lib/media/channel-specs.server.ts`
* Add a thin API route: `app/src/app/api/media/specs/route.ts` — accepts `?channels=a,b,c`, calls `getChannelSpec()` for each (the same function the preview studio already uses), returns `{ results: SpecResult[] }`
* In the wizard: `useEffect` on `state.channels` → debounced fetch to `/api/media/specs`

**Do NOT call the Mastra** `lookupChannelSpecs` **tool directly** — that tool is for AI agents, not UI. Use the existing `getChannelSpec()` server function via the API route instead (same data source, no agent overhead).

**Seeded channels (have specs in DB):** `instagram_feed`, `instagram_story`, `tiktok`, `facebook`

**Unseeded channels (wizard shows these too):** `instagram_reel`, `pinterest`, `amazon`, `shopify`, `youtube`, `website` → all get the "no spec" warning

**Files to touch:**

* `app/src/app/api/media/specs/route.ts` — new thin route (≈20 lines)
* `app/src/app/(operator)/app/shoots/new/page.tsx` — add spec fetch + render below channel buttons
* No DB migrations, no new components required

---

## Out of scope

* Editing specs from within the wizard (read-only display only)
* Safe-zone visualisation (that's MI-04+)
* Seeding the missing channels (amazon, shopify etc.) — separate data task
* Changing which channels appear in the wizard selector

Title: SHOOT-AI-002 · Engine that runs the shoot-planning steps
Description:
## SHOOT-AI-002 — Shoot Wizard Workflow (`shoot-wizard`)

**Phase:** 5 · **Estimate:** 5 · **Source:** `docs/prd/shoot-prd.md` §8

**In plain terms:** The `shoot-wizard` Mastra workflow implements the 6-step creation flow with HITL suspend/resume at each approval gate. Operator edits survive workflow suspension (drafts in `shoot_intake_drafts`).

**Blocked by:** [IPI-134](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-134/aior-018-workflow-snapshots-recovery) (workflow snapshots) · [IPI-129](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-129/aior-013-mastra-durable-storage-postgres) (PG storage) · [IPI-148](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-148/shoot-ai-001-shoot-planner-agent) (tools)

**Supersedes:** [IPI-115](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-115/aior-005-mastra-workflow-shoot-planner-workflow-hitl-deterministic) (canceled — legacy `:4111` pattern)

---

### Workflow: `shoot-wizard` (PRD §8.1 happy path)

```
/app/shoots/new
  → L1 context: brand, products, channels, campaign goal (useAgentContext IPI-50)
  → Step 1-2: Basics + Brief
  → planDeliverables → editable deliverables artifact → HITL gate 1: approve deliverables
  → generateShotListDraft (from approved deliverables only) → HITL gate 2: approve shot list
  → estimateShootBudget → HITL gate 3: approve budget
  → saveApprovedShootDraft + approveShotList → navigate to /app/shoots/:id
```

**Deliverables-first rule (§8.3):** `generateShotListDraft` cannot run until deliverables are approved. Every shot maps to ≥1 deliverable. Un-covered deliverables are flagged before the operator approves.

### File path (non-negotiable)

```
app/src/mastra/workflows/shoot-wizard.ts   ← NOT shoot-production.ts
```

### Draft/commit pattern (mirrors brand intake)

* Agent writes to `shoot_intake_drafts` (jsonb: draft_shoot + draft_shot_list + draft_deliverables)
* HITL commit promotes approved drafts into `shoots` + `shot_list` + `shoot_deliverables`
* Rejected drafts retained; workflow resumes from last checkpoint ([IPI-134](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-134/aior-018-workflow-snapshots-recovery) snapshots)

### Mermaid Flow

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Wizard start] --> B[planDeliverables]
    B --> C{Gate 1 suspend\ndeliverables_approval}
    C -->|Reject| B
    C -->|Approve| D[shoot_intake_drafts\ndraft_deliverables saved]
    D --> E[generateShotListDraft\napproved_deliverables REQUIRED]
    E --> F{Gate 2 suspend\nshot_list_approval}
    F -->|Reject| E
    F -->|Approve| G[draft_shot_list saved]
    G --> H[estimateShootBudget]
    H --> I{Gate 3 suspend\nbudget_approval}
    I -->|Reject| H
    I -->|Approve| J[saveApprovedShootDraft]
    J --> K[commit-approved-shoot edge fn]
    K --> L[shoots + shot_list + shoot_deliverables]
    L --> M[Navigate /app/shoots/:id]
    style C fill:#f3b93c,color:#000
    style F fill:#f3b93c,color:#000
    style I fill:#f3b93c,color:#000
    style K fill:#e87c4d,color:#fff
```

### Acceptance

- [ ] `app/src/mastra/workflows/shoot-``wizard``.ts` registered on Mastra instance
- [ ] Three explicit suspend/resume HITL gates (deliverables · shot list · budget)
- [ ] `shoot_intake_drafts` table writable by agent; commit edge fn gated by approval
- [ ] Workflow file path: `app/src/mastra/workflows/shoot-wizard.ts` (NOT `shoot-production.ts`)
- [ ] Snapshot recovery: suspended workflow resumes from last checkpoint after server restart ([IPI-134](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-134/aior-018-workflow-snapshots-recovery))
- [ ] `/api/workflows/resume` custom route (or CopilotKit interrupt bridge)
- [ ] `npm run build` green in `app/`

### Testing Checklist

- [ ] Wizard completes end-to-end: all 3 gates approved → `shoots` + `shot_list` + `shoot_deliverables` rows created, `shoot_intake_drafts.status = approved`
- [ ] Navigate to Shot List step without Gate 1 approval → workflow throws, step navigation blocked
- [ ] Abandon wizard at Step 4 → `shoot_intake_drafts` row persists with `status: pending`
- [ ] Resume wizard via `?draft=<id>` → wizard pre-filled, starts at last incomplete step
- [ ] Server restart mid-wizard after Gate 1 suspend → workflow resumes from checkpoint (requires [IPI-134](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-134/aior-018-workflow-snapshots-recovery))
- [ ] Reject at Gate 2 → workflow stays suspended at `shot_list_approval`, draft retained, re-edit and re-approve works
- [ ] No `shoots` or `shot_list` rows exist in DB until after Gate 3 `commit-approved-shoot` fires
- [ ] `shoot_intake_drafts.agent_run_id` matches the workflow run that created it
- [ ] `npm run build` green in `app/`

Title: AIOR-014 · brand-intelligence Mastra agent
Description:
## AIOR-014 — brand-intelligence Mastra agent

**Plain terms:** When operator is on `/app/brand/*` the AI panel loads the `brand-intelligence` agent — page-context-aware, knows the current brand's scores + profile, can trigger re-analysis and guide the HITL approval flow.

[**Status:** In Progress — IPI-51 ✅ IPI-46 ✅ IPI-129 ✅ — all blockers cleared.](<https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-48>)

---

### Architecture

```
/app/brand/[id]
  → resolveAgentId() → "brand-intelligence"
  → CopilotChatConfigurationProvider agentId="brand-intelligence"
  → brand-intelligence Agent (Mastra)
      ├── getBrandProfile   READ  brands table (no edge needed)
      ├── getBrandScores    READ  brand_scores table (no edge needed)
      └── startBrandAnalysis  WRITE → callEdgeFunction("start-brand-crawl")
```

### Operator panel flow (wireframe)

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  🤖 Brand Intelligence                  │
│  ─────────────────────────────────────  │
│  📍 Viewing: Everlane                   │
│  Last analyzed: 3 days ago              │
│  Overall score: 72/100                  │
│                                         │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────┐    │
│  │ What would you like to do?      │    │
│  │                                 │    │
│  │ [Re-analyze brand]              │    │
│  │ [Explain my scores]             │    │
│  │ [What needs improvement?]       │    │
│  └─────────────────────────────────┘    │
│                                         │
│  > Explain my scores                    │
│  ─────────────────────────────────────  │
│  Your Visual Identity score is 68/100.  │
│  Main gap: inconsistent color palette   │
│  across social channels. Top fix:       │
│  standardize to 2 primary + 1 accent.   │
│                                         │
│  [Draft approval pending ⚠️]            │
│  Review draft → approve or reject       │
└─────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

### Completion steps

- [ ] **A1** `app/src/mastra/tools/brand-intelligence-tools.ts` — `getBrandProfile`, `getBrandScores`, `startBrandAnalysis`
- [ ] **A2** `app/src/mastra/agents/brand-intelligence-agent.ts` — Agent with id `brand-intelligence`, page-aware system prompt, 3 tools
- [ ] **A3** Register in `app/src/mastra/index.ts` — add to agents map
- [ ] **A4** Update `app/src/lib/route-agent-map.ts` — `/app/brand` → `brand-intelligence`
- [ ] **A5** Tests — unit tests for the 3 tools + agent registration smoke test

---

### Key rules for the agent

* **Opens with context** — always calls `getBrandProfile` on thread start, surfaces name + intake_status + scores summary
* **Read tools** query Supabase direct with service-role (server-only Mastra runtime)
* **Write tool** uses `callEdgeFunction` — never writes DB directly
* **HITL aware** — when `intake_status = draft_ready`, agent surfaces "Draft pending review" and links operator to the approval card
* **Proactive** — opens with next best action, not "How can I help?"

---

### Gantt

```mermaid
gantt
    title IPI-130 · AIOR-014
    dateFormat YYYY-MM-DD
    section Agent
    Tools (read + write)   :g1, 2026-06-28, 1d

    Register + route map   :g3, after g2, 1d
```

Title: PLT-012 · Vendor Next.js operator app (app/) into monorepo + CI split
Description:
## PLT-012 — Vendor the Next.js operator app into the monorepo + CI split

**Why:** the Next.js operator app at `/home/sk/ipix/app` is currently a **nested git repo with no remote**, untracked by `lumina-studio`. Until it's vendored, **no Next.js operator PR can land** (brand intake, shoot UI, CopilotKit work all blocked). Decision (2026-06-22): vendor `app/` into the monorepo.

**Scope:**

* Remove `app/.git`; track `app/` as regular files (respect `app/.gitignore` → exclude `node_modules`, `.next`, `.env*`, `.mastra`).
* CI split: extend `.github/workflows/ci.yml` to a matrix / second job — **Vite build** (root) + **Next build** (`app/`), each with its own install. Don't let Vite env errors block the Next job (or vice-versa); document the split.
* Confirm `app/` builds in CI (`cd app && npm ci && npm run build`).

**Out of scope:** PLT-010 squash, the 94 untracked Supabase migrations, Graphify.

**Acceptance:**

- [ ] `app/` is tracked in `lumina-studio` (no nested `.git`); `node_modules`/`.next`/`.env` not committed.
- [ ] CI builds both Vite and Next; both green.
- [ ] A Next.js operator PR (e.g. brand intake) can be opened against `main`.

Source: `docs/linear/june-22-audit-linear.md` §5.
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: IPI-BI-010 — Brand Intelligence: MVP Integration Tests
Description:
# [IPI-33](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-33/ipi-bi-010-brand-intelligence-mvp-integration-tests) · IPI-BI-010 — Brand Intelligence: MVP Integration Tests

**Goal:** Prove the full Brand Intelligence MVP works end-to-end: **URL → crawl → Gemini profile → scores → Brand Hub → AI report context**.

In plain terms: this task is the safety net. It makes sure a real operator can onboard a brand, the system creates one clean brand record, AI analyzes it, scores are saved without duplicates, and the Brand Hub/report shows the right information.

**Track:** BRAND · **Epic:** [IPI-20](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-20/epic-1-brand-intelligence-mvp) · Epic 1 — Brand Intelligence MVP
**Branch:** `ipi/ipi-33-integration-tests`
**Estimate:** 5
**Priority:** High
**Blocked by:** [IPI-25](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-25/ipi-bi-002-brand-intelligence-gemini-prompt-v2), [IPI-29](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-29/ipi-bi-006-brand-intelligence-brand-scoring-v2), [IPI-30](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-30/ipi-bi-007-brand-hub-v2-full-profile-ui), [IPI-46](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-46/ipi-bi-p0-fix-brand-onboarding-orchestration-and-profile-persistence)
**Related:** [IPI-24](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-24/ipi-bi-001-brand-intelligence-firecrawl-integration), [IPI-26](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-26/ipi-bi-003-brand-intelligence-supabase-schema-v2), [IPI-123](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-123/dash-003-d1-brand-intelligence-report-view), [IPI-52](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-52/dash-006-d1-brand-approval-cards-timeline)

---

## Why this matters

Without integration tests, each part can pass alone while the real workflow still breaks.

Example failure this task must catch:

* onboarding creates two brands
* crawl runs but never connects to the brand
* Gemini writes nested `ai_profile.profile` instead of flat fields
* scores duplicate on re-analysis
* Brand Hub shows `undefined`
* AI panel does not know the visible brand context

This task prevents those regressions.

---

## MVP user journey

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  A[Operator opens onboarding] --> B[Creates org + brand shell]
  B --> C[Start Firecrawl crawl]
  C --> D[Store crawl data in Supabase]
  D --> E[Run Gemini Brand Intelligence]
  E --> F[Write flat ai_profile]
  F --> G[Upsert brand_scores]
  G --> H[Open Brand Hub]
  H --> I[Verify tabs, scores, gaps, citations]
  I --> J[Ask AI panel a brand question]
  J --> K[AI answers using visible brand context]
```

---

## Core system flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant O as Operator
  participant UI as Next.js Operator App
  participant SB as Supabase
  participant FC as Firecrawl
  participant GM as Gemini
  participant HUB as Brand Hub
  participant AI as CopilotKit Panel

  O->>UI: Submit brand URL
  UI->>SB: Create org + brand shell
  UI->>FC: Start crawl with brandId
  FC->>SB: Webhook stores crawl pages/raw_data
  UI->>GM: Run brand-intelligence with brandId
  GM->>SB: Merge flat ai_profile + upsert scores
  O->>HUB: Open /app/brand/[id]
  HUB->>SB: Load brand, scores, crawl, evidence
  HUB->>AI: Inject visible brand context
  AI-->>O: Explain score / gaps / next actions
```

---

## User stories

| Role | Story | Test expectation |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Operator | As an operator, I want to onboard a brand and land on a populated Brand Hub. | One brand row, profile fields visible, scores visible. |
| Brand manager | As a brand manager, I want scores to be explainable. | Evidence/citations render, no unsafe links. |
| AI reviewer | As an AI reviewer, I want repeated analysis to update scores without duplicates. | Second run still has one row per score type. |
| Engineer | As an engineer, I want failed dependencies to be visible and testable. | Crawl/Gemini/RLS failures produce deterministic errors. |
| Executive | As an executive, I want the AI panel to explain the report. | AI references brand name, scores, gaps, and recommendations. |

---

## Test groups

### A. Onboarding orchestration regression

Purpose: protect [IPI-46](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-46/ipi-bi-p0-fix-brand-onboarding-orchestration-and-profile-persistence).

- [ ] `createOrgAndBrand` runs before `brand-intelligence`
- [ ] Edge function receives `brandId`
- [ ] Only one brand row exists after onboarding
- [ ] Brand has `org_id`
- [ ] No client-side `dna_readiness` insert
- [ ] Analysis failure shows error and does not redirect as complete

### B. Firecrawl integration regression

Purpose: protect [IPI-24](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-24/ipi-bi-001-brand-intelligence-firecrawl-integration).

- [ ] `start-brand-crawl` creates a `brand_crawls` job for an existing `brand_id`
- [ ] `firecrawl-webhook` stores page data / raw data
- [ ] Client bundle has no `FIRECRAWL_*` secrets or Firecrawl SDK imports
- [ ] If crawl fails, onboarding continues to Gemini fallback path
- [ ] Stuck crawl jobs are documented for watchdog follow-up

### C. Gemini profile regression

Purpose: protect [IPI-25](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-25/ipi-bi-002-brand-intelligence-gemini-prompt-v2).

- [ ] `brand-intelligence` requires `brandId`
- [ ] Loads latest crawl data when available
- [ ] Writes flat `ai_profile` fields
- [ ] Preserves shell fields like industry, goal, instagram_handle
- [ ] Logs to `ai_agent_logs`
- [ ] Sets lifecycle/status to `scores_complete` on success

### D. Scoring regression

Purpose: protect [IPI-29](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-29/ipi-bi-006-brand-intelligence-brand-scoring-v2).

- [ ] Upserts scores using `(brand_id, score_type)`
- [ ] Re-analysis does not duplicate score rows
- [ ] DNA score uses only base four: visual, audience, consistency, commerce_readiness
- [ ] Score rows support `score_version`, `source`, and `details`
- [ ] Confidence/evidence fields render safely when present

### E. Brand Hub / Report UI regression

Purpose: protect [IPI-30](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-30/ipi-bi-007-brand-hub-v2-full-profile-ui) and [IPI-123](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-123/dash-003-d1-brand-intelligence-report-view).

- [ ] `/app/brand/[id]` renders for an analyzed brand
- [ ] Overview, Profile, Scores, Activity tabs work
- [ ] Gaps section renders when gaps exist and hides when not needed
- [ ] Citations/evidence render safely
- [ ] Recommended actions render
- [ ] No `undefined`, broken fields, unsafe URLs, or 404 CTAs
- [ ] Mobile layout remains usable

### F. CopilotKit context regression

Purpose: protect [IPI-123](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-123/dash-003-d1-brand-intelligence-report-view) AC6 and AIOR-016.

- [ ] AI panel receives brand context
- [ ] AI can answer: “Why is this score high/low?”
- [ ] AI mentions brand-specific facts from visible context
- [ ] No internal tool calls appear in chat
- [ ] No `navigateTo`, `updateWorkingMemory`, JSON payloads, or tool cards appear

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Integration test covers the full MVP path: onboarding → crawl → Gemini → scores → Brand Hub
- [ ] Regression test proves exactly one brand row after onboarding
- [ ] Re-analysis keeps score rows idempotent: no duplicates
- [ ] Brand Hub renders profile, scores, gaps, citations, and recommended actions
- [ ] AI panel can explain the visible brand report using injected context
- [ ] Internal tool calls stay hidden from users
- [ ] RLS verification passes for owner/editor/viewer/non-member paths
- [ ] CI documents both root and app jobs: root build/test + app build/test
- [ ] Browser smoke evidence is attached before Done
- [ ] Test fails on the old broken flow where possible

---

## CI correction

`.github/workflows/ci.yml` already has an **app-build** job that runs inside `app``/`. Keep the test plan aligned with both surfaces:

| Surface | What to run |
| -- | -- |
| Root / Vite legacy safety | root build/test where still present |
| Next.js operator app | `cd app && npm test && npm run build` |
| Supabase | `npm run supabase:verify`, `npm run supabase:verify-rls`, `npm run supabase:verify-brand-intelligence`, `npm run supabase:verify-edge` |
| Browser | Chrome MCP / Playwright smoke on `/app/brand/[id]` |

---

## Browser smoke checklist

Use one analyzed brand, for example Ola Azul or a verified test brand.

```text
1. Open /app/brand/[id]
2. Verify Overview tab loads
3. Verify DNA + quick scores
4. Open Scores tab
5. Expand citations/evidence
6. Ask AI panel: “Why is the visual score high?”
7. Confirm answer references brand-specific context
8. Confirm no internal tool cards appear
9. Check mobile width
10. Check console/network for errors
```

---

## Recommended file targets

| Area | Likely files |
| -- | -- |
| App tests | `app/src/test/brand-hub.test.ts`, `app/src/test/onboarding-orchestration.test.ts` |
| Score tests | `app/src/test/brand-scores.test.ts` |
| Verify scripts | `scripts/verify-brand-intelligence.mjs`, `scripts/verify-rls.mjs` |
| Browser evidence | `docs/plan/audit/evidence/ipi-33-YYYY-MM-DD/` |
| Audit note | `docs/plan/audit/ipi-33-integration-tests.md` |

---

## Mermaid test matrix

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  T1[Onboarding test] --> T2[Firecrawl verify]
  T2 --> T3[Gemini verify]
  T3 --> T4[Score idempotency]
  T4 --> T5[Brand Hub browser smoke]
  T5 --> T6[AI context smoke]
  T6 --> DONE[IPI-33 Done]
```

---

## Out of scope

* New scoring dimensions beyond [IPI-29](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-29/ipi-bi-006-brand-intelligence-brand-scoring-v2)
* New GraphRAG or graph extraction work
* New Brand Hub features beyond existing [IPI-30](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-30/ipi-bi-007-brand-hub-v2-full-profile-ui) / [IPI-123](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-123/dash-003-d1-brand-intelligence-report-view) acceptance criteria
* Full HITL implementation unless PR D / [IPI-52](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-52/dash-006-d1-brand-approval-cards-timeline) has landed
* Neo4j or external graph database work

---

## Definition of Done

[IPI-33](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-33/ipi-bi-010-brand-intelligence-mvp-integration-tests) is Done when the MVP path is covered by automated tests + browser proof:

```text
Brand URL
→ brand shell
→ crawl data
→ Gemini profile
→ idempotent scores
→ Brand Hub report
→ AI context answer
```

The result should be simple: if someone breaks the Brand Intelligence MVP, CI or the smoke test should catch it before merge.
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: IPI-BI-009 — Brand Intelligence: Mastra Workflow
Description:
# [IPI-32](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-32/ipi-bi-009-brand-intelligence-mastra-workflow) · IPI-BI-009 — Brand Intelligence: Mastra Workflow

**Goal:** Replace scattered manual pipeline calls with one durable Mastra workflow for Brand Intelligence: crawl → profile → scores → social/visual agents → draft → approval/commit.

In plain terms: this is the conductor. It does not create brands. It takes an existing `brand_id` from onboarding, runs the right AI steps in the right order, handles retries, waits for webhooks, and records what happened.

**Branch:** `ipi/ipi-32-mastra-workflow`
**Estimate:** 8
**Priority:** High
**Blocked by:** [IPI-24](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-24/ipi-bi-001-brand-intelligence-firecrawl-integration), [IPI-25](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-25/ipi-bi-002-brand-intelligence-gemini-prompt-v2), [IPI-27](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-27/ipi-bi-004-brand-intelligence-social-discovery-agent), [IPI-28](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-28/ipi-bi-005-brand-intelligence-visual-identity-agent), [IPI-46](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-46/ipi-bi-p0-fix-brand-onboarding-orchestration-and-profile-persistence)
**Related:** [IPI-29](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-29/ipi-bi-006-brand-intelligence-brand-scoring-v2), [IPI-31](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-31/ipi-bi-008-brand-intelligence-analysis-progress-ux), [IPI-52](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-52/dash-006-d1-brand-approval-cards-timeline), [IPI-123](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-123/dash-003-d1-brand-intelligence-report-view), [IPI-33](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-33/ipi-bi-010-brand-intelligence-mvp-integration-tests)

---

## Non-negotiable corrections

* This workflow **consumes** an existing brand from [IPI-46](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-46/ipi-bi-p0-fix-brand-onboarding-orchestration-and-profile-persistence). It must not create orgs or brands.
* Do not use `LibSQLStore(:memory:)` for production workflow state. Vercel cold starts must not lose workflow progress.
* Firecrawl webhook must be able to resume the suspended workflow.
* Human approval hooks must align with [IPI-52](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-52/dash-006-d1-brand-approval-cards-timeline) · DASH-006 — D1 Brand Approval Cards + Timeline.

---

## User story

As an operator, I want to start brand intelligence once and trust the system to crawl, analyze, score, enrich, and request approval without me manually triggering every step.

---

## Workflow overview

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  A[Existing brand_id from onboarding] --> B[Start brand-intake workflow]
  B --> C[Start Firecrawl crawl]
  C --> D[Suspend until webhook]
  D --> E[Resume with crawl result]
  E --> F[Run Gemini profile extraction]
  F --> G[Run scoring v2]
  G --> H[Fan out optional agents]
  H --> I[Social discovery]
  H --> J[Visual identity]
  I --> K[Build draft summary]
  J --> K
  K --> L[Human approval / HITL]
  L --> M{Approved?}
  M -->|Yes| N[Commit profile + scores + enrichments]
  M -->|No| O[Keep draft / mark rejected]
  N --> P[Brand Hub ready]
```

---

## Mastra execution sequence

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant UI as Brand Hub / Onboarding
  participant WF as Mastra Workflow
  participant FC as Firecrawl
  participant WH as firecrawl-webhook
  participant GM as Gemini
  participant SC as score-brand-v2
  participant SD as social-discovery
  participant DB as Supabase
  participant HITL as Approval UI

  UI->>WF: start({ brandId })
  WF->>DB: verify brand + org access
  WF->>FC: start crawl
  WF->>DB: save workflow_run_id + crawl job id
  WF-->>UI: status crawl_running
  FC->>WH: crawl.completed
  WH->>DB: store crawl result
  WH->>WF: resume(workflow_run_id)
  WF->>GM: brand-intelligence profile extraction
  WF->>SC: scoring v2
  WF->>SD: social discovery
  WF->>DB: save draft results
  WF->>HITL: request approval
  HITL-->>WF: approve / reject
  WF->>DB: commit or reject draft
```

---

## Workflow states

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
  [*] --> queued
  queued --> crawl_running
  crawl_running --> suspended_waiting_for_webhook
  suspended_waiting_for_webhook --> crawl_complete
  crawl_complete --> analysis_running
  analysis_running --> scoring_running
  scoring_running --> enrichment_running
  enrichment_running --> draft_pending_approval
  draft_pending_approval --> committed: approved
  draft_pending_approval --> rejected: rejected
  committed --> ready
  crawl_running --> failed
  analysis_running --> failed
  scoring_running --> failed
  failed --> queued: retry
```

---

## Required workflow steps

| Step | Name | Owner | Output |
| -- | -- | -- | -- |
| 1 | Validate brand | Workflow | Existing `brand_id`, org access verified |
| 2 | Start crawl | [IPI-24](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-24/ipi-bi-001-brand-intelligence-firecrawl-integration) function | `brand_crawls` row + Firecrawl job id |
| 3 | Suspend | Mastra workflow | Durable wait state |
| 4 | Resume from webhook | `firecrawl-webhook` | workflow resumes after crawl complete |
| 5 | Gemini profile | [IPI-25](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-25/ipi-bi-002-brand-intelligence-gemini-prompt-v2) function | flat `ai_profile` draft |
| 6 | Scoring v2 | [IPI-29](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-29/ipi-bi-006-brand-intelligence-brand-scoring-v2) | 10 score rows / draft scores |
| 7 | Social/visual fan-out | [IPI-27](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-27/ipi-bi-004-brand-intelligence-social-discovery-agent) / [IPI-28](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-28/ipi-bi-005-brand-intelligence-visual-identity-agent) | enrichment rows + evidence |
| 8 | Approval draft | [IPI-52](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-52/dash-006-d1-brand-approval-cards-timeline) | approval card / diff |
| 9 | Commit | Workflow | writes approved data + status ready |
| 10 | Log | Workflow | `ai_agent_logs` / `brand_agent_results` |

---

## Data requirements

| Data | Storage |
| -- | -- |
| `workflow_run_id` | workflow durable store + `ai_agent_logs` |
| `brand_id` | `brands.id` |
| `crawl_id` | `brand_crawls.id` |
| `firecrawl_job_id` | `brand_crawls.firecrawl_job_id` |
| Draft profile | `brand_intake_drafts.draft_profile` or equivalent |
| Draft scores | `brand_intake_drafts.draft_scores` or equivalent |
| Approval status | `brand_intake_drafts.status` |
| Final profile | `brands.ai_profile` |
| Final scores | `brand_scores` |

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Workflow starts from existing `brandId`; no org/brand creation
- [ ] Workflow state is durable outside `:memory:`
- [ ] Firecrawl webhook can resume suspended workflow
- [ ] Workflow records `workflow_run_id` in logs
- [ ] Failed crawl/analysis/scoring steps produce retryable failed state
- [ ] Profile/scoring writes are idempotent
- [ ] Social discovery and visual identity can run as optional fan-out steps
- [ ] HITL draft approval integrates with [IPI-52](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-52/dash-006-d1-brand-approval-cards-timeline)
- [ ] Rejected draft does not overwrite approved profile
- [ ] Browser smoke proves user sees progress and approval state

---

## Test checklist

```bash
cd app && npm test -- mastra brand-intake workflow
cd app && npm run build
infisical run -- npm run supabase:verify-rls
```

Workflow smoke:

```text
1. Start workflow with existing brandId
2. Confirm crawl starts
3. Confirm workflow suspends
4. Simulate / receive Firecrawl webhook
5. Confirm workflow resumes
6. Confirm profile + scores draft created
7. Approve draft
8. Confirm final brand profile/scores update
9. Retry same workflow and confirm no duplicates
```

---

## Out of scope

* Creating orgs or brand shells
* New graph database / GraphRAG
* Full campaign generation
* Full product graph
* Non-brand workflows

---

## Definition of Done

[IPI-32](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-32/ipi-bi-009-brand-intelligence-mastra-workflow) is Done when an existing brand can run through a durable Mastra workflow that survives webhook waits/retries, produces draft intelligence, requires human approval before commit, and updates Brand Hub only after approval.

Title: IPI-BI-008 — Brand Intelligence: Analysis Progress UX
Description:
# [IPI-31](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-31/ipi-bi-008-brand-intelligence-analysis-progress-ux) · IPI-BI-008 — Brand Intelligence: Analysis Progress UX

**Goal:** Show operators a clear, real-time progress experience while Brand Intelligence runs.

In plain terms: when a brand is being crawled, analyzed, scored, or failing, the user should see what is happening instead of staring at a blank page or guessing if the system is stuck.

**Branch:** `ipi/ipi-31-progress-ux`
**Estimate:** 3
**Priority:** High
**Blocked by:** [IPI-26](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-26/ipi-bi-003-brand-intelligence-supabase-schema-v2) · IPI-BI-003 — Brand Intelligence: Supabase Schema v2, [IPI-24](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-24/ipi-bi-001-brand-intelligence-firecrawl-integration) · IPI-BI-001 — Brand Intelligence: Firecrawl Integration
**Coordinates with:** [IPI-32](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-32/ipi-bi-009-brand-intelligence-mastra-workflow) · IPI-BI-009 — Brand Intelligence: Mastra Workflow

---

## Important rule

This task is **UX only**.

It must subscribe to existing state and events. It must **not** duplicate orchestration.

Do not call `brand-intelligence` directly from the progress component once [IPI-32](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-32/ipi-bi-009-brand-intelligence-mastra-workflow) workflow is active. The workflow owns pipeline execution; this task owns visibility.

---

## User story

As an operator, I want to see crawl, analysis, scoring, and failure progress so I know whether the brand intelligence pipeline is working and what step needs attention.

---

## What this adds

| Feature | Simple explanation |
| -- | -- |
| Progress stepper | Shows current stage: crawl, analyze, score, ready |
| Realtime updates | Uses Supabase Realtime / status polling where needed |
| Error card | Shows useful retry guidance instead of silent failure |
| Brand Hub banner | Shows state on `/app/brand/[id]` |
| Onboarding progress | Shows state after brand shell is created |

---

## User journey

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  A[Operator submits brand URL] --> B[Brand shell created]
  B --> C[Progress UI appears]
  C --> D[Crawl running]
  D --> E[Analysis running]
  E --> F[Scores complete]
  F --> G[Hub ready]
  D --> X[Crawl failed / stuck]
  E --> Y[Gemini failed]
  X --> R[Show retry / continue fallback]
  Y --> R
```

---

## State machine

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
  [*] --> brand_created
  brand_created --> crawl_running
  crawl_running --> crawl_complete
  crawl_complete --> analysis_running
  analysis_running --> scores_complete
  scores_complete --> ready
  crawl_running --> failed
  analysis_running --> failed
  failed --> crawl_running: retry crawl
  failed --> analysis_running: retry analysis
```

---

## Realtime sequence

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant UI as Progress UI
  participant DB as Supabase
  participant FC as Firecrawl Webhook
  participant BI as brand-intelligence
  participant WF as Mastra Workflow Future

  UI->>DB: Subscribe to brand + brand_crawls + brand_crawl_results
  FC->>DB: Update crawl status/pages
  DB-->>UI: Realtime event
  BI->>DB: Update intake_status + scores
  DB-->>UI: Realtime event
  WF->>DB: Future workflow status update
  DB-->>UI: Realtime event
```

---

## UI states

| State | What user sees |
| -- | -- |
| `brand_created` | “Brand created. Preparing analysis…” |
| `crawl_running` | “Crawling website…” + pages found/crawled if available |
| `crawl_complete` | “Crawl complete. Preparing AI analysis…” |
| `analysis_running` | “Gemini is analyzing brand profile…” |
| `scores_complete` | “Scores generated. Opening Brand Hub…” |
| `ready` | No blocking banner; show normal Hub |
| `failed` | Error card with reason + retry action |

---

## Data sources

| Source | Purpose |
| -- | -- |
| `brands.intake_status` | Main lifecycle state |
| `brands.ai_profile._lifecycle` | Compatibility fallback |
| `brand_crawls.status` | Crawl job state |
| `brand_crawls.pages_crawled/pages_found` | Crawl progress numbers |
| `brand_crawl_results` | Page-level progress/evidence |
| `ai_agent_logs` | Analysis start/end/failure logs |
| Future `workflow_run_id` | [IPI-32](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-32/ipi-bi-009-brand-intelligence-mastra-workflow) workflow state |

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Progress component reads status from Supabase; it does not own orchestration
- [ ] Brand Hub displays useful banners for `brand_created`, `crawl_running`, `analysis_running`, `scores_complete`, `ready`, and `failed`
- [ ] Crawl progress shows page counts when available
- [ ] Failed state shows retry guidance and useful error text
- [ ] No duplicate edge function calls are introduced
- [ ] Works before and after [IPI-32](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-32/ipi-bi-009-brand-intelligence-mastra-workflow) workflow is enabled
- [ ] Mobile-friendly progress view
- [ ] Browser smoke captures each major state where possible

---

## Test checklist

```bash
cd app && npm test -- progress
cd app && npm run build
infisical run -- npm run supabase:verify-rls
```

Browser smoke:

```text
1. Start onboarding for a brand
2. Confirm progress UI appears after shell creation
3. Confirm crawl_running state appears
4. Confirm analysis/scores states appear or fallback path is shown
5. Confirm failed state can be rendered with fixture/mock
6. Confirm final Hub view is normal when ready
```

---

## Out of scope

* Starting crawl jobs
* Calling `brand-intelligence`
* Mastra workflow execution
* Realtime approval cards
* HITL draft review
* Full progress percentage accuracy before workflow emits richer events

---

## Definition of Done

[IPI-31](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-31/ipi-bi-008-brand-intelligence-analysis-progress-ux) is Done when operators can clearly see the Brand Intelligence pipeline status from onboarding through Brand Hub, without this UI duplicating pipeline execution logic.
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: IPI-BI-007 — Brand Hub v2: Full Profile UI
Description:
# IPI-30 · IPI-BI-007 — Brand Hub v2: Full Profile UI

**Linear:** [IPI-30](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-30/ipi-bi-007-brand-hub-v2-full-profile-ui)  
**Track:** BRAND · **Epic:** [IPI-20](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-20)  
**Milestone:** BI-M3: Hub + Workflow + Tests  
**Blocked by:** [IPI-25](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-25) ✅ · [IPI-29](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-29) (10 scores + evidence — can ship tabs with 4 scores first)  
**Unblocks:** [IPI-33](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-33) · proof **#6** UX completion  
**Skills:** `ipix-task-lifecycle` · `ipix-wireframe` · `frontend-design` · `mermaid-diagrams`  
**MVP proof:** #6 — operator sees full brand intelligence after onboarding  
**Branch:** `ipi/ipi-30-brand-hub-v2` · **Estimate:** 5 points

---

## In plain terms

After Gemini analyzes a brand, the operator opens **Brand Hub** to review identity, scores, and next actions. Today `/app/brand/[id]` is a **single scroll page** with ~11 profile fields and a score grid (IPI-46 partial fix).

**IPI-30** turns it into a **tabbed command center**: clear sections, loading/error states tied to `intake_status`, a **Re-analyze** action, and CTAs that land on real placeholder routes (not 404). No new AI logic — read-only UI over `brands`, `brand_scores`, and optional crawl metadata.

**Lifecycle:** step **9** → transition `scores_complete` → `ready` when Hub gating passes ([`docs/plan/19-brand-lifecycle.md`](<../../plan/19-brand-lifecycle.md>)).

---

## Skills (load in order)

| # | Skill | When |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 1 | `ipix-task-lifecycle` | Branch, phases A–E, verify gates, Linear sync |
| 2 | `ipix-wireframe` | Lo-fi tabs + states before coding |
| 3 | `frontend-design` | Typography, tokens, mobile layout |
| 4 | `mermaid-diagrams` | Flows in PR + Linear |

**Wireframe files:** `tasks/wireframes-ipix/brand-hub-v2-tabs.wire` (v2 tabs) · `brand-hub-profile.wire` (v1 reference)

---

## User journey

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  OB[Onboarding / Intake] --> BI[brand-intelligence]
  BI --> HUB[Brand Hub v2]
  HUB --> SH[Plan Shoot /app/shoots]
  HUB --> AS[Analyze Assets /app/assets]
  HUB --> RA[Re-analyze]
  RA --> BI
```

---

## Information architecture (Epic 1 tabs)

| Tab | Purpose | Data source | MVP |
| -- | -- | -- | -- |
| **Overview** | DNA badge, headline, status chip, quick CTAs | `brands`, `brand_scores` (base 4) | ✅ |
| **Profile** | Full `ai_profile` (20 fields from IPI-25) | `brands.ai_profile` | ✅ |
| **Scores** | All dimensions + confidence/evidence | `brand_scores` (+ `details` from IPI-29) | ✅ |
| **Activity** | Timeline: created, crawl, analyzed, failed | `intake_status`, `ai_profile.analyzedAt`, `ai_agent_logs` | ✅ lite |
| Products | Mercur catalog bridge | defer COM | 🔲 placeholder |
| Competitors | Epic 2+ | defer | 🔲 “Coming soon” |
| Assets | DNA pipeline | defer IPI-19 | 🔲 link → `/app/assets` |

---

## Wireframe — Overview tab (ASCII)

```text
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  ← Dashboard          Maison Lumière                    [Re-analyze]      │
│  org-name · plan          everlane.com                  intake: ready ●   │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ [Overview] [Profile] [Scores] [Activity]  | Products* | Competitors*    │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  ┌──────────┐   Tagline in serif italic                                 │
│  │ DNA  78  │   Sustainable basics for modern life                       │
│  │  badge   │   [Apparel]  [B2C]                                         │
│  └──────────┘                                                             │
│                                                                           │
│  ┌─ Quick scores (base 4) ─────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ Visual 72   Audience 81   Consistency 68   Commerce 74            │   │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                           │
│  [Plan Shoot]  [Create Campaign]  [Analyze Assets]                        │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
* Products / Competitors = disabled tab or “Soon” — not 404
```

---

## Wireframe — States

| State | `intake_status` | UI |
| -- | -- | -- |
| **Empty** | `brand_created` | “Analysis not started” + CTA → intake or Re-analyze |
| **Crawling** | `crawl_running` | Progress banner (static % or “Crawl in progress” until IPI-31) |
| **Analyzing** | `analysis_running` | Skeleton + “Analyzing brand…” |
| **Partial** | `scores_complete` | Tabs work; optional banner “Extended scores pending” |
| **Ready** | `ready` | Full tabs, no blocking banner |
| **Failed** | `failed` | Error card + `ai_profile._error` or `failure_reason` + Retry |

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
  [*] --> Overview
  Overview --> Profile: click tab
  Overview --> Scores: click tab
  Overview --> Activity: click tab
  Overview --> ReAnalyze: button
  ReAnalyze --> Analyzing: invoke edge fn
  Analyzing --> Overview: success refresh
  Analyzing --> Failed: error
  Failed --> ReAnalyze: retry
```

---

## Data flow (read path)

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant Op as Operator
  participant Page as Brand Hub RSC
  participant DB as Supabase
  participant EF as brand-intelligence

  Op->>Page: GET /app/brand/[id]
  Page->>DB: brands + org + brand_scores
  Page-->>Op: Render tabs (SSR)

  Op->>Page: Click Re-analyze
  Page->>EF: POST { brandId, url } (client action)
  EF->>DB: merge profile + upsert scores
  Page->>Page: router.refresh()
```

**DNA rule (locked):** badge = `AVG(visual, audience, consistency, commerce_readiness)` via `computeDnaScore()` — never `dna_readiness` row.

---

## Component tree (target)

```text
app/(operator)/app/brand/[id]/page.tsx
├── BrandHubHeader (name, org, url, DNA badge, status chip)
├── BrandHubTabs (client)
│   ├── OverviewTab
│   ├── ProfileTab      ← all ai_profile fields
│   ├── ScoresTab       ← grid + evidence tooltips (IPI-29)
│   └── ActivityTab     ← status timeline
├── BrandHubCTAs
└── ReAnalyzeButton     ← server action or client invoke
```

Extract to `app/src/components/brand-hub/*` when >1 file grows.

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [X] **AC1** Tabbed layout: Overview · Profile · Scores · Activity — keyboard + mobile friendly
- [X] **AC2** Renders all **11 core** + **9 v2 extension** `ai_profile` fields when present (empty fields hidden, not “undefined”)
- [X] **AC3** Scores tab shows **all** `brand_scores` rows; DNA badge uses **base 4 only**
- [X] **AC4** `intake_status` drives banner/skeleton/error (6 states in wireframe table)
- [X] **AC5** **Re-analyze** calls `brand-intelligence` with `brandId` + `brand_url`; refreshes page on success
- [X] **AC6** On successful re-analyze with scores: set `intake_status` → `ready` (server action `markBrandFailed` on error)
- [X] **AC7** CTAs: `/app/shoots`, `/app/campaigns`, `/app/assets`, `/app` — **no 404** (placeholders OK)
- [X] **AC8** Typography: Cormorant headings + Outfit body ([`ui-design.mdc`](<../../../.cursor/rules/ui-design.mdc>))
- [X] **AC9** `cd app && npm test` — extend `brand-hub.test.ts` for tabs + DNA + empty state
- [X] **AC10** Browser smoke: open Hub after onboarding → tabs populated · Re-analyze affordance verified (not clicked in smoke)

### Post-merge verification (2026-06-25)

**PR:** [#78](<https://github.com/amo-tech-ai/lumina-studio/pull/78>) · **merge:** `ee64ed273cfd67aaf0d6e2af1282030013261119`  
**Audit:** [`docs/plan/audit/pr-78-tests-brandui.md`](<../../plan/audit/pr-78-tests-brandui.md>)

---

## Wiring plan

| -- | -- | -- |
| Modify | `app/src/app/(operator)/app/brand/[id]/page.tsx` | Split into tab shell + server data fetch |
| Create | `app/src/components/brand-hub/BrandHubTabs.tsx` | Client component for tab state |

**Order:** lib helpers → components → page refactor → Re-analyze action → tests.

---

## Completion steps

Title: IPI-BI-005 — Brand Intelligence: Visual Identity Agent
Description:
# IPI-BI-005 — Visual Identity Agent

**Goal:** Extract visual identity from **Firecrawl screenshot + Gemini vision**, upload to Cloudinary, merge into `ai_profile.visualIdentity`.

## Skills

* `@gemini` — **generateContent()** with image inlineData parts (NOT generateImages)
* `@cloudinary` — upload `brands/{brandId}/screenshots/homepage`
* `@mastra` — agent + tool pattern
* `@firecrawl` — screenshot capture
* `@task-verifier`

## Blocked by

* **IPI-24** (Firecrawl screenshots)
* Cloudinary secrets in Infisical `/cloudinary`

Title: IPI-BI-004 — Brand Intelligence: Social Discovery Agent
Description:
# [IPI-27](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-27/ipi-bi-004-brand-intelligence-social-discovery-agent) · IPI-BI-004 — Brand Intelligence: Social Discovery Agent

**Goal:** Discover and verify a brand's public social channels, then save clean rows to `brand_social_channels` and useful evidence to the Brand Intelligence report.

In plain terms: after a brand is crawled/analyzed, this agent finds the official Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, Pinterest, LinkedIn, Facebook, and X accounts. It should avoid fake/duplicate accounts and explain why each channel is trusted.

**Branch:** `ipi/ipi-27-social-discovery`
**Estimate:** 3
**Priority:** High
**Blocked by:** [IPI-26](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-26/ipi-bi-003-brand-intelligence-supabase-schema-v2) · IPI-BI-003 — Brand Intelligence: Supabase Schema v2, [IPI-24](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-24/ipi-bi-001-brand-intelligence-firecrawl-integration) · IPI-BI-001 — Brand Intelligence: Firecrawl Integration
**Blocks:** [IPI-32](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-32/ipi-bi-009-brand-intelligence-mastra-workflow) · IPI-BI-009 — Brand Intelligence: Mastra Workflow
**Parallel with:** [IPI-28](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-28/ipi-bi-005-brand-intelligence-visual-identity-agent) · IPI-BI-005 — Brand Intelligence: Visual Identity Agent

---

## User story

As an operator, I want the system to automatically find a brand's official social accounts so I do not manually search Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, Pinterest, LinkedIn, Facebook, and X before planning shoots, campaigns, or outreach.

---

## What this adds

| Feature | Simple explanation |
| -- | -- |
| Social discovery agent | Finds official social profiles from crawl data and search grounding |
| Verification scoring | Decides if an account is likely official |
| Channel persistence | Writes one row per platform into `brand_social_channels` |
| Evidence | Stores source URLs and confidence so operators can trust results |
| Mastra-ready output | Produces structured results for [IPI-32](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-32/ipi-bi-009-brand-intelligence-mastra-workflow) workflow fan-out |

---

## User journey

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  A[Brand has website + crawl data] --> B[Run social-discovery agent]
  B --> C[Read footer links, metadata, crawl pages]
  C --> D[Use Gemini/Search to verify official accounts]
  D --> E[Upsert brand_social_channels]
  E --> F[Show social channels in Brand Hub / Report]
  F --> G[Use channels for campaigns, content, and outreach]
```

---

## Agent decision flow

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  A[Candidate social URL found] --> B{Platform supported?}
  B -->|No| X[Ignore]
  B -->|Yes| C{Brand name/handle match?}
  C -->|Weak| D[Search verify]
  C -->|Strong| E[Mark likely official]
  D --> F{Evidence confirms?}
  F -->|Yes| E
  F -->|No| Y[Store as low-confidence or ignore]
  E --> Z[Upsert channel with confidence + source]
```

---

## System sequence

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant WF as Mastra / Manual Trigger
  participant AG as social-discovery agent
  participant CR as Crawl Data
  participant GM as Gemini Search Grounding
  participant DB as Supabase

  WF->>AG: discoverSocialChannels(brandId)
  AG->>DB: Load brand + brand_crawl_results
  AG->>CR: Extract candidate URLs/handles
  AG->>GM: Verify official profiles
  GM-->>AG: Structured channel list + evidence
  AG->>DB: Upsert brand_social_channels
  AG->>DB: Append brand_agent_results log
```

---

## Data contract

Each discovered channel should include:

| Field | Example |
| -- | -- |
| `platform` | `instagram`, `tiktok`, `youtube`, `pinterest`, `linkedin`, `facebook`, `x` |
| `url` | `https://instagram.com/brand` |
| `handle` | `@brand` |
| `confidence` | `0.92` |
| `verification_reason` | `Linked from official website footer and handle matches brand name` |
| `source_url` | Website page or search result URL |
| `content_themes` | `swimwear`, `beach`, `lifestyle` |
| `posting_frequency` | `weekly`, `monthly`, `unknown` |

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Agent `social-discovery` is registered in Mastra with matching agent id
- [ ] Tool `discoverSocialChannels(brandId)` exists and requires `brandId`
- [ ] Reads existing brand and crawl data; does not create brands
- [ ] Discovers supported platforms when present
- [ ] Verifies official accounts using crawl evidence and optional search grounding
- [ ] Upserts `brand_social_channels` with `UNIQUE(brand_id, platform)` behavior
- [ ] Stores confidence and source evidence
- [ ] Logs run to `brand_agent_results`
- [ ] No Instagram Graph API / paid social API keys required
- [ ] Unit tests use fixtures; no live network in tests

---

## Test checklist

```bash
cd app && npm test -- social-discovery
rg "social-discovery" app/src/mastra/index.ts app/src/mastra
infisical run -- npm run supabase:verify-rls
```

Browser smoke:

```text
1. Open an analyzed brand
2. Trigger social discovery
3. Confirm channels are saved
4. Confirm duplicate rerun does not create duplicate platform rows
5. Confirm low-confidence/fake accounts are not shown as verified
```

---

## Out of scope

* Posting to social media
* Paid social API integrations
* Follower analytics requiring private API tokens
* Campaign generation
* Full social content calendar

---

## Definition of Done

[IPI-27](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-27/ipi-bi-004-brand-intelligence-social-discovery-agent) is Done when a brand can run social discovery, save verified social channels with evidence, and safely rerun without duplicates. The results should be ready for [IPI-32](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-32/ipi-bi-009-brand-intelligence-mastra-workflow) · IPI-BI-009 — Brand Intelligence: Mastra Workflow.

```mermaid
  C --> D[Step 1: Brand URL + name]

  G --> H[invokeStartBrandCrawl]
  H --> I[invokeBrandIntelligence with brandId]
  I --> J[Redirect to /app/brand/brandId — Hub populated]
  I -->|failure| K[Error state — no Hub redirect]
```

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant Op as Operator
  participant UI as /app/onboarding
  participant DB as Supabase
  participant SBC as start-brand-crawl
  participant BI as brand-intelligence

  Op->>UI: Submit step 3 (URL + category + goal)
  UI->>DB: createOrgAndBrand -> orgs + brands row (brand_id)
  UI->>SBC: POST { brandId, url, idempotencyKey }
  SBC->>DB: INSERT brand_crawls job
  UI->>BI: POST { brandId, url, brand_name, crawlResultId? }
  BI->>DB: UPDATE brands.ai_profile (flat merge)
  BI->>DB: UPSERT brand_scores x4 (brand_id, score_type UNIQUE)

```

## intake_status state machine (onboarding path)

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
  [*] --> brand_created : createOrgAndBrand
  brand_created --> crawl_running : start-brand-crawl
  crawl_running --> crawl_complete : firecrawl-webhook crawl.completed
  crawl_complete --> analysis_running : brand-intelligence invoked
  analysis_running --> scores_complete : profile + 4 scores written
  scores_complete --> ready : Hub gating passes
  analysis_running --> failed : edge fn error
  crawl_running --> failed : Firecrawl timeout or error
  failed --> analysis_running : Re-analyze
```

---

## Brand Lifecycle (SSOT)

Canonical flow, state machine, idempotency, and ownership: `docs/brand-intelligence/19-brand-lifecycle.md`

Every Epic 1 PR must cite lifecycle steps + `intake_status` transitions touched.

Title: next-cloudinary — Consolidate Hand-Rolled URL Builders
Description:
Install `next-cloudinary` ([https://github.com/cloudinary-community/next-cloudinary](<https://github.com/cloudinary-community/next-cloudinary>)) and consolidate 4 separate hand-rolled Cloudinary URL builders into one shared helper.

**Real-world example:** today 4 different files each hand-write a Cloudinary delivery URL from memory — like writing the same mailing address from scratch every time instead of using a saved contact card. One copy already has the wrong city (`dev-panel-fixture.ts` hardcodes cloud `dza2bjwwp`, not the project's real `dzqy2ixl0`); another forgot the postal code (`assets/route.ts` is missing `q_auto,f_auto` that the other builders include). `next-cloudinary`'s `getCldImageUrl`/`CldImage` is the one saved template all four should use instead.

## The problem this solves

Four independent Cloudinary URL builders exist in `app/`, each slightly different:

1. `app/src/lib/command-center/sample-images.ts:34-43` — `cloudinaryImageUrl()`, has `q_auto,f_auto`, correct cloud name (`dzqy2ixl0` via env, fallback matches)
2. `app/src/app/api/brands/[id]/assets/route.ts:11-12` — inline template string, **missing** `q_auto,f_auto` (inconsistent optimization vs. #1)
3. `app/src/lib/intelligence/dev-panel-fixture.ts:9-18` — duplicate `cloudinaryImageUrl()`, **hardcoded to the wrong cloud** (`dza2bjwwp` instead of `dzqy2ixl0`)
4. `app/src/components/media/channel-preview-studio.tsx:12` — hardcoded literal pointing at Cloudinary's public `demo` account, not this project's own

No `next-cloudinary` package is installed today — every one of these is a hand-rolled string instead of using the SDK's URL builder.

## Tasks

- [ ] Install `next-cloudinary` into `app/package.json`
- [ ] Add one shared helper (e.g. `app/src/lib/cloudinary/url.ts`) wrapping `getCldImageUrl` that all four call sites import instead of building URLs inline
- [ ] Replace `cloudinaryImageUrl()` in `sample-images.ts:34-43` with the shared helper
- [ ] Replace the inline template string in `assets/route.ts:11-12` with the shared helper (fixes missing `q_auto,f_auto`)
- [ ] Delete the duplicate, wrong-cloud `cloudinaryImageUrl()` in `dev-panel-fixture.ts:9-18` once its callers use the shared helper
- [ ] Replace the hardcoded `demo`-account fallback in `channel-preview-studio.tsx:12` with the shared helper + this project's real cloud name

## Out of scope

`CldUploadWidget`, `CldVideoPlayer`, signed-upload flows, and Assets-panel UI — those are IPI-257's own sub-deliverables (074a/074c/074f). This ticket only consolidates *read-side* delivery-URL construction into the SDK.

## Depends on

Blocked by IPI-349 (Cloudinary Config Cleanup) — the corrupted `CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME` and wrong hardcoded cloud in `dev-panel-fixture.ts` must be fixed first (or fixed as part of this consolidation if IPI-349 lands the env fix but not the fixture file).

## Completion proof

- [ ] `grep -rn "res.cloudinary.com" app/src` shows only the shared helper file (no more inline builders)
- [ ] `next-cloudinary` present in `app/package.json` dependencies
- [ ] `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` green in `app/`
- [ ] Manual: BrandCard, Assets thumbnails, dev panel, and Channel Preview all render via the shared helper against the correct cloud (`dzqy2ixl0`)
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: Cloudinary Node SDK — Replace Hand-Rolled Upload/Signature Code
Description:
Swap the Mastra agent's hand-rolled Cloudinary signature/upload code for the official `cloudinary` npm SDK.

**Real-world example:** `visual-identity.ts` computes its own upload signature by hand and does a raw `fetch()` to Cloudinary's API — like mixing your own paint from raw pigments instead of buying the can that's already the exact color. It works today, but the recipe ("fields signed in alphabetical order, excluding file and api_key") is exactly what the SDK already does, tested, for every edge case — a hand-rolled copy is one typo away from silently minting bad signatures.

## The problem this solves

* `app/src/mastra/agents/visual-identity.ts` (`uploadToCloudinary()`, ~lines 60-95) reads `CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME`/`CLOUDINARY_API_KEY`/`CLOUDINARY_API_SECRET` from `process.env` directly, manually computes the upload signature, and does a raw `fetch()` to `https://api.cloudinary.com/v1_1/${cloudName}/image/upload` — duplicating logic the official SDK's `uploader.upload()` already implements and maintains.
* No `cloudinary` npm package is installed in `app/package.json` today — this hand-rolled code is the *only* thing standing in for the SDK.

## Tasks

- [ ] Install `cloudinary` (official server-side SDK) into `app/package.json`
- [ ] Add one `cloudinary.config({ cloud_name, api_key, api_secret })` call from server env, reused across server code (not re-read per call)
- [ ] Replace the manual signature computation + raw `fetch()` in `uploadToCloudinary()` (`app/src/mastra/agents/visual-identity.ts`) with `cloudinary.uploader.upload()`
- [ ] Confirm no client-side/`NEXT_PUBLIC_*` exposure is introduced (secret-guard grep clean — SDK stays server-only, per this repo's Cloudinary skill note)

## Out of scope

Signed-upload API route (IPI-257 / 074a), webhook handler (074c), and Assets-panel UI — those are IPI-257's own sub-deliverables. This ticket only swaps the Mastra agent's *internal* upload call for the SDK equivalent; it does not add new upload surfaces.

## Depends on

Blocked by IPI-349 (Cloudinary Config Cleanup) — the corrupted `CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME` must be fixed first so SDK calls resolve against the correct cloud.

## Completion proof

- [ ] `grep -rn "api.cloudinary.com" app/src/mastra` returns nothing (raw fetch removed)
- [ ] `cloudinary` present in `app/package.json` dependencies
- [ ] `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` green in `app/`
- [ ] Manual: trigger `visual-identity` agent upload path, confirm asset lands in Cloudinary via SDK call (not raw fetch)

Title: Intelligence Panel: Route-Aware Context Sections
Description:
# IPI-286: Intelligence Panel — Route-Aware Context Sections

**Linear:** [IPI-286](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-286)  
**Design Ref:** Route-aware intelligence (adaptive panel content)  
**Parent:** IPI-255 (DESIGN-071 deferred items)  
**Priority:** Medium (P3)

---

## Context

IPI-255 shipped DNA scores and pending approvals in the Intelligence Panel. Route-aware sections were deferred as part of the original DESIGN-071 scope.

---

## Problem

The Intelligence Panel shows the same content regardless of the user's current context. When a user is planning a shoot or viewing a campaign, the panel could surface context-specific intelligence instead of generic brand data.

---

## User Stories

### Story 1: Creative Director planning shoot

**As a** Creative Director  
**I want** shoot-specific intelligence when in shoot wizard  
**So that** I see DNA criteria and asset recommendations relevant to the current shoot

**Acceptance:** Panel shows "Shoot DNA Criteria" section on `/app/shoots/new`

### Story 2: Brand Manager on brand detail page

**As a** Brand Manager  
**I want** brand-level intelligence on brand detail page  
**So that** I see DNA scores and assets specific to this brand

**Acceptance:** Panel shows "DNA Scores" + "Assets" sections on `/app/brand/[id]`

### Story 3: Campaign Manager viewing campaign

**As a** Campaign Manager  
**I want** campaign-level intelligence on campaign page  
**So that** I see multi-brand alignment and channel recommendations

**Acceptance:** Panel shows "Campaign Performance" section on `/app/campaigns/[id]`

---

## User Journey

```mermaid
journey
    title Creative Director plans Spring Campaign shoot
    section Brand Context
      Start at /app/brand/acme: 5: Director
      See DNA scores + assets in panel: 5: Director
    section Navigate to Shoot Wizard
      Click "Plan new shoot": 5: Director
      Navigate to /app/shoots/new: 5: Director
    section Shoot Context
      Panel transitions to shoot context: 5: Director
      See "Shoot DNA Criteria": Visual 80+ required: 4: Director
      See "Asset Recommendations": 12 product shots needed: 5: Director
      See "Budget Warnings": $8.5k vs $12k avg: 4: Director
    section Take Action
      Adjust deliverables based on budget warning: 5: Director
      Submit shoot plan: 5: Director
```

---

## Proposal

Make Intelligence Panel content adaptive based on the current route:

**Brand Detail (**`/app/brand/[id]`**):**

* DNA scores (current)
* Pending approvals (current)
* AI suggestions (IPI-285)
* Recent assets (IPI-284)

**Shoot Planning (**`/app/shoots/new`**,** `/app/shoots/[id]`**):**

* Shoot-specific DNA criteria
* Asset recommendations for deliverables
* Budget vs. brand standards warnings
* Similar shoot references

**Campaign View (**`/app/campaigns/[id]`**):**

* Campaign performance insights
* Multi-brand alignment scores
* Channel-specific recommendations
* Asset reuse opportunities

---

## Route Detection Logic

### ⚠️ Merge with Existing `resolveRouteBriefing`

**Existing file:** `app/src/components/intelligence-panel/route-briefing.ts`

```typescript
export function resolveRouteBriefing(pathname: string): RouteBriefing {
  // Already maps pathname → { section, headline, nextActions }
}
```

**Conflict:** Creating a separate `resolvePanelSections` will duplicate route detection logic.

**Resolution:** Extend `resolveRouteBriefing` to return section config:

```typescript
// app/src/components/intelligence-panel/route-briefing.ts (MODIFIED)
export type RouteBriefing = {
  section: string;
  headline: string;
  nextActions: string[];
  panelSections?: SectionConfig[];  // ← ADD THIS
};

export function resolveRouteBriefing(pathname: string): RouteBriefing {
  if (pathname.startsWith('/app/brand/')) {
    return {
      section: 'brand-detail',
      headline: 'Brand Intelligence',
      nextActions: ['View assets', 'Plan shoot'],
      panelSections: [  // ← ADD THIS
        { type: 'dna-scores', priority: 1 },
        { type: 'suggestions', priority: 2 },
        { type: 'assets', priority: 3 },
        { type: 'approvals', priority: 4 },
      ],
    };
  }
  // ... other routes
}
  if (pathname.startsWith('/app/brand/')) {
    return [
      { type: 'dna-scores', priority: 1 },
      { type: 'suggestions', priority: 2 },
      { type: 'assets', priority: 3 },
      { type: 'approvals', priority: 4 },
    ];
  }
  
  if (pathname.match(/\/app\/shoots\/(new|\d+)/)) {
    return [
      { type: 'shoot-dna', priority: 1 },
      { type: 'asset-recommendations', priority: 2 },
      { type: 'budget-warnings', priority: 3 },
    ];
  }
  
  if (pathname.startsWith('/app/campaigns/')) {
    return [
      { type: 'campaign-performance', priority: 1 },
      { type: 'multi-brand-alignment', priority: 2 },
      { type: 'channel-recommendations', priority: 3 },
    ];
  }
  
  return []; // Default: no sections
}
```

---

## Sequence Diagram

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant IP as IntelligencePanel
    participant R as resolvePanelSections
    participant H as useIntelligencePanel

    U->>IP: navigate to /app/brand/[id]
    IP->>R: resolvePanelSections(pathname)
    R-->>IP: [dna-scores, suggestions, assets, approvals]
    IP->>H: fetch panel data for brand context
    H-->>IP: { scores, suggestions, assets, approvals }
    IP->>IP: render sections in priority order
    
    U->>IP: navigate to /app/shoots/new
    IP->>R: resolvePanelSections(pathname)
    R-->>IP: [shoot-dna, asset-recommendations, budget-warnings]
    IP->>H: fetch panel data for shoot context
    H-->>IP: { shootCriteria, assetRecs, budgetWarnings }
    IP->>IP: render shoot-specific sections
```

---

## State Diagram

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> BrandContext
    [*] --> ShootContext
    [*] --> CampaignContext
    
    BrandContext --> LoadingBrand
    LoadingBrand --> BrandSections: data loaded
    
    ShootContext --> LoadingShoot
    LoadingShoot --> ShootSections: data loaded
    
    CampaignContext --> LoadingCampaign
    LoadingCampaign --> CampaignSections: data loaded
    
    BrandSections --> ShootContext: route change
    ShootSections --> CampaignContext: route change
    CampaignSections --> BrandContext: route change
```

---

## Component Tree

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    IP[IntelligencePanel] --> RPS[resolvePanelSections pathname]
    
    RPS --> BC[Brand Context]
    RPS --> SC[Shoot Context]
    RPS --> CC[Campaign Context]
    
    BC --> DNS[DnaScoresSection]
    BC --> SR[SuggestionRail]
    BC --> AG[AssetsGrid]
    BC --> AS[ApprovalsSection]
    
    SC --> SDN[ShootDnaSection]
    SC --> AR[AssetRecommendations]
    SC --> BW[BudgetWarnings]
    
    CC --> CP[CampaignPerformance]
    CC --> MBA[MultiBrandAlignment]
    CC --> CR[ChannelRecommendations]
```

---

## Wireframe — Brand Context

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Intelligence Panel  (Brand: Acme)               │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ DNA Scores                    ← priority 1      │
│ ┌─────┬─────┬─────┬─────┐                      │
│ │ 75  │ V:80│ A:70│ C:90│                      │
│ └─────┴─────┴─────┴─────┘                      │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Suggestions (3)               ← priority 2      │
│ 💡 Visual inconsistency detected               │
│ ⚠️  Commerce readiness low                     │
│ ✨ High consistency score                      │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Assets (6)                    ← priority 3      │
│ ┌───┬───┬───┬───┬───┬───┐                      │
│ │img│img│img│img│img│img│                      │
│ └───┴───┴───┴───┴───┴───┘                      │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Pending Approvals (2)         ← priority 4      │
│ • Beta Brand (draft_ready)                      │
│ • Gamma Inc (draft_ready)                       │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Wireframe — Shoot Context

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Intelligence Panel  (Shoot: Spring Campaign)    │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Shoot DNA Criteria            ← priority 1      │
│ ✅ Visual: 80+ required (brand standard)       │
│ ⚠️  Commerce: 70+ required (deliverables)      │
│ ✅ Consistency: maintain 90+                   │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Asset Recommendations         ← priority 2      │
│ 📸 Product shots: 12 missing                   │
│ 📸 Lifestyle: 6 recommended                    │
│ 📸 Close-ups: 8 for detail pages               │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Budget Warnings               ← priority 3      │
│ ⚠️  $8,500 budget vs $12,000 avg for similar   │
│ 💡 Consider: reduce deliverables or extend     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Files to Create/Modify

### New Files

* `app/src/lib/intelligence/resolve-panel-sections.ts` — Section resolver by route
* `app/src/lib/intelligence/resolve-panel-sections.test.ts` — Resolver tests
* `app/src/components/intelligence-panel/shoot-dna-section.tsx` — Shoot DNA
* `app/src/components/intelligence-panel/asset-recommendations.tsx` — Asset recs
* `app/src/components/intelligence-panel/budget-warnings.tsx` — Budget warnings
* `app/src/components/intelligence-panel/campaign-performance.tsx` — Campaign perf

### Modified Files

* `app/src/components/intelligence-panel/intelligence-panel.tsx` — Route detection + conditional rendering
* `app/src/lib/intelligence/panel-contract.ts` — Add shoot/campaign context types

---

## Acceptance Criteria

### A. Section Resolver Logic

- [ ] `resolvePanelSections(pathname)` returns correct sections per route
- [ ] Brand routes return `[dna-scores, suggestions, assets, approvals]`
- [ ] Shoot routes return `[shoot-dna, asset-recommendations, budget-warnings]`
- [ ] Campaign routes return `[campaign-performance, multi-brand-alignment, channel-recommendations]`
- [ ] Unknown routes return empty array (no sections)
- [ ] Test: `npm test -- resolve-panel-sections` → 5 tests passing

### B. Component — IntelligencePanel

- [ ] Panel calls `resolvePanelSections(pathname)` on mount
- [ ] Panel re-resolves sections when pathname changes
- [ ] Sections render in priority order (1, 2, 3, 4)
- [ ] Smooth transition when navigating between routes
- [ ] No flash of wrong content during route change
- [ ] Test: `npm test -- intelligence-panel` → existing + new tests passing

### C. Shoot Context Sections

- [ ] ShootDnaSection shows DNA criteria for current shoot
- [ ] AssetRecommendations shows missing/recommended assets
- [ ] BudgetWarnings compares budget to brand standards
- [ ] Test: `npm test -- shoot-context` → 3 tests passing

### D. Campaign Context Sections (placeholder)

- [ ] CampaignPerformance shows placeholder "Coming soon"
- [ ] MultiBrandAlignment shows placeholder "Coming soon"
- [ ] ChannelRecommendations shows placeholder "Coming soon"
- [ ] Test: `npm test -- campaign-context` → 3 tests passing

### E. Manual Verification

- [ ] Navigate `/app/brand/[id]` → see DNA scores + suggestions + assets + approvals
- [ ] Navigate `/app/shoots/new` → see shoot DNA + asset recs + budget warnings
- [ ] Navigate `/app/campaigns/[id]` → see campaign placeholders
- [ ] Route transitions smooth, no layout shift

---

## Verification

```bash
cd app

# Section resolver tests
npm test -- resolve-panel-sections

# Component tests
npm test -- intelligence-panel

# Context-specific section tests
npm test -- shoot-context
npm test -- campaign-context

# Full suite
npm test

# Typecheck
npx tsc --noEmit

# Manual test
npm run dev
# Navigate between /app/brand/[id], /app/shoots/new, /app/campaigns/[id]
# Verify panel sections change per route
```

---

## Out of Scope

* Real-time data updates during route changes (handled by existing polling)
* Custom sections for marketing/assets routes
* User preferences for section visibility
* Section drag-and-drop reordering
* Full implementation of campaign context sections (placeholders only)

---

## Dependencies

* IPI-255 ✅ (base Intelligence Panel)
* IPI-247 ✅ (route-agent map for context detection)
* IPI-284 (asset thumbnails) — optional, enhances brand context
* IPI-285 (suggestions) — optional, enhances brand context

---

## Skills Required

| Skill | Purpose |
| -- | -- |
| `/ipix-wireframe` | Context-aware wireframes for each route type |
| `/mermaid-diagrams` | State transitions between route contexts |
| `/frontend-design` | Extend existing resolveRouteBriefing pattern |
| `/shadcn` | Conditional section rendering |
| `/gen-test` | Route resolver tests + integration tests |

**Load order:** Read `route-briefing.ts` → extend (don't duplicate) → `mermaid-diagrams` → implement

---

## Design Principle

**Route-aware intelligence** — panel adapts to user's current task without explicit configuration. Brand routes show brand intelligence, shoot routes show shoot intelligence, campaign routes show campaign intelligence.

**UX Principle:** "Keep users in context" — no need to navigate away to see relevant data.

**Design files:**

* `Universal design prompt/Brand Detail.v2.image-first.dc.html` → Brand context
* `Universal design prompt/Shoot Wizard.v2.image-first.dc.html` → Shoot context reference
* `Universal design prompt/Campaigns.v2.image-first.dc.html` → Campaign context reference

Title: WEB-015.5 · Homepage chat widget UI (CopilotKit v2, (marketing))
Description:
## WEB-015.5 — Homepage Chat Widget (CopilotKit v2)

**Implemented** 2026-06-24

### Files

| Action | File |
| -- | -- |
| CREATE | `app/src/components/marketing/marketing-chat.tsx` |
| CREATE | `app/src/components/marketing/marketing-chat.test.tsx` |
| MODIFY | `app/src/app/(marketing)/page.tsx` |
| MODIFY | `app/src/app/(marketing)/marketing.css` |

### Architecture

```
MarketingChat (client island, feature-flag gated, SSR-safe)
└── ChatErrorBoundary (class component — graceful fallback)
    └── CopilotKit runtimeUrl="/api/marketing-chat"
        ├── useConfigureSuggestions (4 quick-prompt chips)
        ├── useFrontendTool "capture_lead" → POST /api/marketing-lead
        └── CopilotPopup (floating, streaming, brand-labelled)
```

### Key decisions

* **CopilotKit v2 native components only** — no custom UI library; `CopilotPopup` + `useConfigureSuggestions` + `useFrontendTool` cover all requirements
* Feature flag: `NEXT_PUBLIC_MARKETING_CHAT_ENABLED === "true"` — off by default
* SSR guard: `useState(false)` + `useEffect` prevents hydration errors
* Quick chips via `useConfigureSuggestions({ available: "before-first-message", suggestions: [...] })` — native CopilotKit pattern
* Lead capture: agent calls `capture_lead` tool, handler POSTs to `/api/marketing-lead`, draftId shown in chat via `render` component
* Gemini high-demand errors: caught in `onError`, logged only (CopilotKit shows its own retry UI)
* Never imports from operator-panel, /api/copilotkit, or operator auth

### Tests

22 passing source-analysis tests covering:

* No operator imports contract
* `runtimeUrl` = `/api/marketing-chat` exactly
* Feature flag guard
* Quick prompt content
* Lead capture tool registration
* Error boundary + Gemini error handling
* SSR guard

### Validation

* `npm run lint` ✅ (ESLint v2 guard passes)
* `npx tsc --noEmit` ✅ (no new type errors)
* `npx vitest run` ✅ 186/186 passed

Title: WEB-015.4 · Public runtime /api/marketing-chat (ungated)
Description:
**Purpose:** Create the public chatbot API endpoint used by the homepage.

## ✅ Completed 2026-06-24

### Architecture

Two endpoints, cleanly separated:

* `/api/marketing-chat/[[...slug]]` — public CopilotKit runtime, isolated `publicMastra` with only `public-marketing`, no auth gate, `InMemoryAgentRunner`
* `/api/marketing-lead` — lead submission proxy: Zod validates payload, maps `website→brand_url`, POSTs to `capture-lead` edge fn, returns draft ID

### Files created

* `app/src/app/api/marketing-chat/[[...slug]]/route.ts` — public CopilotKit runtime
* `app/src/app/api/marketing-chat/[[...slug]]/route.test.ts` — 9 tests (security + isolation)
* `app/src/app/api/marketing-lead/route.ts` — lead submission proxy
* `app/src/app/api/marketing-lead/route.test.ts` — 16 tests (validation, happy path, error cases)

### Files modified

* `app/src/mastra/types/marketing-lead.ts` — fixed `clothing-manufacturing` → `clothing` (matches edge fn)

### Test results

* 164/164 passing (20 test files, 0 regressions)

### Security

* No `withOperatorAuth` / `resolveOperatorUser` on marketing-chat
* `publicMastra` isolated — operator agents (production-planner, creative-director) unreachable
* No service-role key in Next.js — capture-lead called with anon key only
* Zod rejects unknown service slugs, bad emails, missing required fields

### Risk: capture-lead origin check

If `ALLOWED_ORIGINS` is set in edge fn env, server-side calls (no Origin header) will be rejected. Dev works (unset). Prod may need app URL added to that list.
