Title: AIOR-002b · Replace demo-user identity in CopilotKit runtime (real auth)
Description:
**AIOR-002b — Real Auth in CopilotKit Runtime**

Replaces the demo-user identity with a real Supabase session gate on the operator runtime. Fails closed in production; dev-only fallback explicitly guarded by `NODE_ENV !== "production"`.

---

## Implementation (completed 2026-06-24)

### Auth flow

```
Browser → /login (signInWithPassword) → Supabase session cookie
                                              ↓
/app/* proxy (middleware) → cookie shape check (3-part JWT) → redirect anon → /login
                                              ↓
/api/copilotkit → withOperatorAuth → resolveOperatorUser (supabase.auth.getUser) → 401 if invalid
                                              ↓
CopilotRuntime agents factory → AsyncLocalStorage.getStore() → resourceId = user.id, requestContext = { userId, email }
```

### Security audit fixes (PR #62 — 2026-06-24)

| Finding | Fix | PR |
| -- | -- | -- |
| C1 — name field data loss in lead capture | `SubmitLeadSchema` + `buildCaptureLeadPayload` forward `name` | #62 |
| C2 — localStorage crash in Safari private mode | `getAnonId()` try/catch + `_memoryAnonId` module-level fallback | #62 |
| C3 — triple auth per request (WeakMap) | `AsyncLocalStorage` propagates user through full call-stack; `withOperatorAuth` called once at HTTP boundary only | #62 |
| C3v2 — WeakMap miss when CopilotKit wraps Request | Replaced WeakMap with `AsyncLocalStorage` — no Request-object dependency | #62 |
| C5 — `publicMarketingAgent` in shared operator registry | Removed from shared `agents` map; stays in `publicMastra` only | #62 |

### Files

| File | Change |
| -- | -- |
| `src/lib/auth.ts` | `resolveOperatorUser` — real `supabase.auth.getUser`, dev-only fallback, fails closed in prod |
| `src/lib/operator-gate.ts` | `withOperatorAuth` — wraps `resolveOperatorUser`, `OperatorAuthError` on failure |
| `src/proxy.ts` | Middleware — fast cookie shape check, redirect anon `/app/*` → `/login` |
| `src/middleware.ts` | Next.js entry — re-exports `proxy` + `config` (IPI2-127 gate wired) |
| `src/app/api/copilotkit/[[...slug]]/route.ts` | AsyncLocalStorage replaces WeakMap; boundary handler sets user once; factory + identifyUser read `getStore()` |
| `src/app/api/marketing-lead/route.ts` | C1 fix: `name` field added to schema + payload builder |
| `src/components/marketing/marketing-chat.tsx` | C2 fix: `getAnonId()` try/catch with `_memoryAnonId` fallback |
| `src/components/marketing/marketing-chat-lead.tsx` | Extracted lead helpers with C2 fix applied |
| `src/mastra/index.ts` | C5 fix: `publicMarketingAgent` removed from shared operator registry |
| `src/components/marketing/login-form.tsx` | Real Supabase `signInWithPassword`/`signUp`, email normalize, submit lock |
| `src/lib/supabase/client.ts` | `createSupabaseBrowserClient` via `@supabase/ssr` |

### Tests

| Coverage | File |
| -- | -- |
| Login real Supabase session | `login-form.test.tsx` |
| `/app/*` anon → `/login` redirect | `proxy.test.ts` |
| Auth users pass `/app/*` | `proxy.test.ts` |
| `/api/copilotkit` → 401 when `OPERATOR_AUTH_ENABLED=true` + anon | `route.runtime.test.ts` |
| `resourceId = validated user.id` | `route.test.ts`, `route.runtime.test.ts` |
| Two-user isolation via AsyncLocalStorage | `route.runtime.test.ts` |
| No demo-user in production | `auth.test.ts` |
| Dev fallback explicitly guarded | `auth.test.ts` |
| C1 regression: name in capture-lead payload | `route.test.ts` (marketing-lead) |
| C2 regression: getAnonId localStorage fallback | `marketing-chat.behavior.test.tsx` |
| Middleware wiring contract | `middleware.test.ts`, `operator-middleware-contract.test.ts` |
| Lead ↔ API field contract | `lead-pipeline-contract.test.ts` |

**237/237 tests,** `tsc --noEmit` **clean**

---

## Gate

`OPERATOR_AUTH_ENABLED=false` in `.env.local` — keep until Vercel env is provisioned.

Title: DATA-AUDIT-001 · Investigate public.shoots vs shoot.shoots duplication (no orphan-drop bug — verified)
Description:
## DATA-AUDIT-001 — `public.shoots` vs `shoot.shoots` duplication (investigation only)

> **⚠️ Rescoped 2026-07-06 after verification (task-verifier + ipix-supabase).** The original "2 orphan shoots dropped by the view" premise was a **measurement error** — a cross-table miscount. There is **no orphan-drop bug**. This is now a read-only *investigation* of a schema duplication, **not** a data fix. **No data mutation in this issue.**

**Role:** iPix engineer (backend/data). Investigation-first — read-only until findings are reviewed.

**Plain English:** The DB has two `shoots` tables — `public.shoots` (12 rows) and `shoot.shoots` (10 rows). The Shoots List reads `shoot.shoots` (via `shoot_portfolio_view`) and shows all 10 correctly. Understand why both exist and whether `public.shoots` is stale/legacy.

**Blocked by:** none · **Skills:** `@ipix-supabase` · `@task-verifier`

---

## Verified facts (Supabase MCP, 2026-07-06)

* `shoot.shoots` = **10 rows** · `brand_id is null` = **0** · orphans (`LEFT JOIN brands` where `b.id is null`) = **0**.
* FK `shoots_brand_id_fkey → brands ON DELETE CASCADE` exists → an orphan `brand_id` in `shoot.shoots` is **impossible**.
* `information_schema.tables` shows a `shoots` table in **both** `public` and `shoot` schemas.
* The earlier "12 vs 10 = 2 orphans" compared `public.shoots` **(12)** to `shoot.shoots` **(10)** — different tables, not a drop.
* `shoot_portfolio_view` reads `shoot.shoots` and returns all 10 to the owner. **No user-facing data loss.**

---

## The actual question

* Why do `public.shoots` **and** `shoot.shoots` both exist? Which is canonical?
* Is `public.shoots` a legacy / pre-migration table that should be deprecated, or is it still written to?
* Does anything in `app/` still read `public.shoots`?

**Do NOT** mutate or drop either table in this issue — investigation only. Any cleanup is a separate, reviewed migration.

---

### Completion steps (investigation only)

#### A. Inventory (read-only)

- [ ] **A1** Diff `public.shoots` vs `shoot.shoots` (columns, row counts, latest `updated_at`) — proof: SQL result
- [ ] **A2** grep `app/` + `supabase/` for `public.shoots` / `.from("shoots")` readers — proof: grep output
- [ ] **A3** Identify the migration that created `shoot.shoots` + `shoot_portfolio_view` — proof: migration filename

#### B. Decide (write findings — do not execute)

- [ ] **B1** Recommend keep-both / deprecate `public.shoots` / consolidate — proof: 1-paragraph decision in an issue comment

#### C. Optional follow-up

- [ ] **C1** If cleanup is chosen, spin a **separate** reviewed migration issue (not this one)

---

## Out of scope

* Any `DROP` / data move (own migration issue after B1).
* Cover images → [IPI-426](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-426/shoot-cover-001-wire-real-cover-url-into-shoots-list-grid).

---

## Evidence (2026-07-06)

`select count(*) from shoot.shoots` = 10; `... where brand_id is null` = 0; orphans = 0. `pg_constraint`: `shoots_brand_id_fkey -> brands del=c`. `information_schema.tables where table_name='shoots'` → schemas `public, shoot`.
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: SHOOT-COVER-001 · Wire real cover_url into Shoots List grid
Description:
## SHOOT-COVER-001 — Wire real `cover_url` into Shoots List grid

**Role:** iPix engineer. One concern per PR — cover wiring only, no layout/token changes.

**Plain English:** The Shoots List shows placeholder cover photos today; after this, each shoot card shows its **own** moodboard cover, falling back to a decorative image only when a shoot genuinely has none.

**Blocked by:** none (schema ready) · **Unblocks:** true image-first Shoots List parity ([IPI-372](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-372/design-055b-shoots-list-react-parity-workspace-restart-v2-image-first) follow-up) · **Skills:** `@design-to-production` · `@cloudinary` · `@ipix-supabase` · `@nextjs-developer` · `@task-verifier`

---

## The problem this solves

* `shoot_portfolio_view` now exposes `cover_url` (derived from `mood_board_urls[1]`) — verified live 2026-07-06 — but the app **ignores it** and renders placeholder art.
* `app/src/lib/command-center/sample-images.ts:62` literally says `TODO: replace with real shoot cover when schema adds cover_url` — that schema condition is now **true**.
* Result: every shoot card shows a generic sample photo, not the operator's actual moodboard cover → the "image-first" design intent is faked.

**Fix:** select `cover_url` from the view and render it; keep the `sample-images` helper only as the decorative fallback when `cover_url` is null.

---

## User story

> As an **Operator**, when I open Shoots List,
> I see each shoot's real moodboard cover,
> so the grid reflects my actual work instead of stock placeholders.

---

## Wireframe

```
┌───────────────────────────────┐
│  Shoots · /app/shoots         │
├───────────────────────────────┤
│  ┌─────────┐ ┌─────────┐      │
│  │[cover_ur│ │[cover_ur│      │  ← real moodboard[0], not sample
│  │ l img]  │ │ l img]  │      │
│  │ Name    │ │ Name    │      │
│  │ status  │ │ status  │      │
│  └─────────┘ └─────────┘      │
└───────────────────────────────┘
```

**States this UI must handle:**

| State | What to show |
| -- | -- |
| Cover present (`cover_url` not null) | render `cover_url` via `next/image` |
| Cover null (no moodboard) | decorative `shootListCoverForShoot()` fallback — clearly a placeholder, never presented as a real asset |
| Loading | existing skeleton (unchanged) |
| Error | existing error banner (unchanged) |

---

## Acceptance criteria

* **A — real cover:** cards with a moodboard render `cover_url`, not a sample image.
* **B — graceful fallback:** `cover_url = null` cards show the decorative fallback, not a broken image or a fake "uploaded asset".
* **C — no query break:** the view select uses `cover_url`; **zero** references to `cover_image` (that column does not exist → 42703).
* **D — states:** loading/error/empty unchanged from [IPI-372](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-372/design-055b-shoots-list-react-parity-workspace-restart-v2-image-first).
* **E — regression guard:** filter/search/sort + the 5 existing states still work.

---

## Technical notes

**Files to touch:**

* `app/src/app/(operator)/app/shoots/page.tsx` — add `cover_url` to the `shoot_portfolio_view` select; pass to card.
* `app/src/components/shoot/ShootCard.tsx` — use `cover_url` when present, else fallback.
* `app/src/lib/command-center/sample-images.ts` — demote `shootListCoverForShoot()` to fallback-only; drop the TODO.

**Do NOT:** select `cover_image` (column doesn't exist) · present the sample fallback as a real uploaded asset · change grid layout/tokens ([IPI-372](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-372/design-055b-shoots-list-react-parity-workspace-restart-v2-image-first) scope, already Done).

**Seeded/known data:** view column is `cover_url` (text). 8 of 10 owned shoots have it today; 2 are null → exercise the fallback path.

---

## Out of scope

* Uploading/replacing shoot covers (Assets/Cloudinary upload = [IPI-248](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-248/design-057-asset-library-react-parity-workspace) / [IPI-257](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-257/design-074-cloudinary-media-pipeline-upload-to-delivery)).
* The 2 orphaned-`brand_id` shoots that never reach the view → **DATA-FIX-001**.
* Any grid/layout/token change ([IPI-372](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-372/design-055b-shoots-list-react-parity-workspace-restart-v2-image-first), Done).

---

### Completion steps (check in order)

#### A. Wire data

- [ ] **A1** Add `cover_url` to the view select in `page.tsx` — proof: `grep cover_url app/src/app/\(operator\)/app/shoots/page.tsx`

#### B. Render

- [ ] **B1** ShootCard renders `cover_url` when set, fallback when null — proof: browser — a shoot with a moodboard shows the real cover

#### C. States

- [ ] **C1** null-cover card shows decorative fallback (not broken img) — proof: screenshot

#### D. Tests

- [ ] **D1** `npm run typecheck` passes
- [ ] **D2** `npm test` — ShootCard / shoots test passes

#### E. Verify + ship

- [ ] **E1** `CI=true npm run build` passes
- [ ] **E2** Browser smoke `qa@ipix.test` · `:3002` — real covers on `/app/shoots`
- [ ] **E3** Linear → Done

---

## Evidence (live verification, 2026-07-06 · Supabase MCP)

* `shoot_portfolio_view` columns include `cover_url` (text) + `start_date` (date) — `information_schema.columns`.
* View def derives cover: `CASE WHEN s.mood_board_urls IS NOT NULL AND cardinality(...) > 0 THEN s.mood_board_urls[1] ELSE NULL END AS cover_url`.
* App code probe: `grep -rc cover_image src/app/(operator)/app/shoots` → **0** (no broken select today, but no real cover either).
* Base data: 12 shoots → 10 owned; **8/10 have a moodboard cover**, 2 null.

Title: MODEL-P5 — Booking Detail, Approval Gate, Crew Attach, Reschedule, Cancel
Description:
**Bundles implementation tasks:** `MODEL-021`–`MODEL-023`, `MODEL-023a` from `06-model-booking-implementation-plan.md` Phase 5. **This is the highest-risk phase in the feature** — it implements the transactional/idempotent/double-booking-safe confirm path.

## Context

Only one route may ever confirm a booking: `/api/bookings/[id]/approve`. It calls the `confirm_booking` RPC (`MODEL-P1`), which is transactional and idempotent, and relies on the `EXCLUDE` constraint (also `MODEL-P1`) as the actual double-booking safety net — this phase's own availability checks are UX feedback, not the guarantee.

## Scope

* Booking Detail screen (one component, two placements: inline on Shoot Detail's crew list + standalone `/app/bookings/:id`)
* `BookingApprovalCard` — booking-local, **not** an import of a shared `ApprovalCard` (verified: 6 divergent forks exist in this codebase, none canonical) — shaped to match the target canonical prop contract for future [IPI-304](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-304/design-081-de-fork-approvalcard-consolidate-4-implementations-into-1) de-fork consolidation
* `/api/bookings/[id]/approve` route → `confirm_booking` RPC → crew-attach on `shoots.crew`
* Reschedule (reuses the request flow, not a new state) and Cancel (requires a reason, `cancellation_reason`)
* Double-booking conflict surfaced as a clear error, not a silent overwrite
* Timeline rendering from `booking_status_history`

## Out of scope

Any UI path that bypasses the approve route to set `confirmed`. Payment/refund/dispute states.

## Implementation steps

1. `BookingApprovalCard` (booking-local).
2. Booking Detail (inline + standalone, same component).
3. `/approve` route calling the RPC.
4. Reschedule/Cancel actions.

## Files likely touched

`app/src/components/booking/{booking-approval-card,booking-detail}.tsx`, `app/src/app/api/bookings/[id]/approve/route.ts`, `app/src/components/shoot/shoot-detail-client.tsx` (crew row addition).

## Required skills

`ipix-supabase`, `copilotkit`, `mermaid-diagrams`.

## Dependencies

Blocked by `MODEL-P4` (booking request flow must exist first).

## User journey impact

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant Brand
  participant UI
  participant API
  participant DB
  Brand->>UI: Approve booking
  UI->>API: POST /api/bookings/:id/approve
  API->>DB: confirm_booking RPC
  DB-->>API: confirmed + crew attached
  API-->>UI: booking confirmed
```

## Wireframe reference

`04-model-booking-wireframes.wire` (`booking-detail` block). Design prompt: `tasks/models/design/11c-model-booking-wizard-detail.md`.

## Tests

```bash
infisical run -- npm run supabase:verify-rls
cd app && npm run typecheck
cd app && npm test
```

Mandatory cases: (a) valid approve confirms + creates exactly one crew row, (b) calling approve twice on the same booking is a no-op on the second call (idempotent, no duplicate, no error), (c) approving two different bookings for the same talent with overlapping dates — the second surfaces the `EXCLUDE` constraint's conflict as a clean API error, never a silent 500 or a silent double-confirm.

## Success criteria

* Only the approve route can confirm a booking.
* Repeated approve calls do not duplicate crew rows.
* Overlapping confirmed bookings are blocked.
* Cancellation requires a reason, recorded and shown in the timeline.

## Risk level

**High** — the 3 mandatory test cases above are not optional; this phase is the literal implementation of "confirm is transactional, idempotent, and double-booking-checked immediately before commit."

## Related docs

`06-model-booking-implementation-plan.md` Phase 5, Blockers #1/#8/#9.

Title: SEC-001 — Remove exposed VITE_GEMINI_API_KEY from Vercel
Description:
The root Vite deployment at [www.ipix.co](<http://www.ipix.co>) has `VITE_GEMINI_API_KEY` configured for Preview/Production. Because `VITE_*` variables are bundled into client JavaScript, this Gemini key must be treated as **public/compromised**.

### Required actions

1. Remove `VITE_GEMINI_API_KEY` from Vercel Preview and Production.
2. Rotate the Gemini API key in Google AI Studio / Google Cloud.
3. Keep only server-side `GEMINI_API_KEY`.
4. Confirm no frontend code depends on `VITE_GEMINI_API_KEY`.
5. Redeploy root app.
6. Verify built client JS no longer contains the Gemini key.
7. Document completion.

> Do not put the real key in Linear.

---

**Evidence (2026-06-22):** `vercel env ls --scope mdeai` on project `mdeai/lumina-studio` lists `VITE_GEMINI_API_KEY` in **Preview, Production** (created 8d ago). The project's Production alias is [**www.ipix.co**](<http://www.ipix.co>) (Ready). `VITE_`-prefixed vars are inlined into the client bundle at build time → the key is extractable from the live site. A non-`VITE_` `GEMINI_API_KEY` already exists for server/edge use, so removing the `VITE_` one should not break server-side Gemini calls (verify step 4).

Title: MOBILE-004 · Focus-Trap, Esc-to-Close, Streaming aria-live Region
Description:
## Context

`Universal design prompt/MOBILE-PLAN.md` §16.2/§16.3/§17: toasts already carry `role="status" aria-live="polite"` across 7 surfaces (done, design-side confirmed). Remaining, explicitly Claude-Code-owned: focus-trap on all modals/sheets (EvidenceBlock modal, BottomSheet, brand-switcher sheet, intelligence sheet), Escape-to-close, and a streaming `aria-live` region on the AI "thinking"/streaming text so screen readers hear it as it arrives (not just at the end). No existing Linear issue tracks this (confirmed via `docs/linear/issues/` search).

## Problem

Keyboard and screen-reader users can't reliably navigate modals/sheets (focus can escape to background content) and don't get live feedback while the AI response streams in.

## Proposal

1. Add a shared focus-trap behavior (trap Tab/Shift+Tab within the open modal/sheet, restore focus to the trigger element on close) to every modal/sheet component: EvidenceBlock modal, BottomSheet, brand-switcher sheet, intelligence sheet.
2. Wire Escape key to close the topmost open modal/sheet.
3. Add an `aria-live="polite"` (or `"assertive"` where appropriate) region around the AI chat-dock's streaming text so partial content is announced as it streams, not just the final message.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Tab/Shift+Tab cannot move focus outside an open modal/sheet
- [ ] Focus returns to the triggering element when a modal/sheet closes
- [ ] Escape closes the topmost open modal/sheet
- [ ] Streaming AI response text is wrapped in an `aria-live` region; verified with a screen reader (VoiceOver/NVDA) or axe
- [ ] No regression to existing toast `aria-live` behavior (already done, do not duplicate/break)

## Skills

`accessibility` (focus-trap + live-region patterns) · `vercel-react-best-practices` — `rerender-use-ref-transient-values` (streaming text updates should not force full-tree re-renders — use a ref/imperative update for the visible text, only touch React state for the finalized message), `client-event-listeners` (dedupe the global Escape-key listener rather than attaching one per open modal)

## Dependencies

* Design spec: `MOBILE-PLAN.md` §10, §16.3, §17
* Toasts' existing `aria-live="polite"` implementation (already shipped) — reuse the same pattern for the streaming region

## Out of scope

Full WCAG audit beyond these 3 items (touch targets tracked in MOBILE-002, long-press in MOBILE-003)
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: MOBILE-002 · Touch Target Enforcement (≥44px hit areas)
Description:
## Context

Design-side spec is done and verified: `Universal design prompt/MOBILE-PLAN.md` §16.2/§17 (mobile audit, 2026-06-30, re-confirmed 2026-07-01) found selection checkboxes render at 20–24px on Matching/Campaigns/Assets — below the 44×44px minimum touch target. The design source explicitly assigns this as **Claude Code owned**: "pad the hit area on mobile (React: `min-h-11 min-w-11`)." Confirmed via `docs/linear/issues/` search: **no existing Linear issue tracks this** despite being a named, specified gate item on the design side.

## Problem

Selection checkboxes and other small interactive controls (icon buttons, chips) may render below the accessible touch-target minimum on mobile breakpoints, especially in Matching/Campaigns/Assets selection UI.

## Proposal

Audit all interactive controls in `app/src/components/**` rendered at mobile breakpoints (<768px) and ensure a ≥44×44px hit area — the *visual* control can stay its current size; pad the tappable/clickable region (e.g. Tailwind `min-h-11 min-w-11` + centered visual glyph, or an invisible padding wrapper).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Identify every selection checkbox / icon-only control rendered on Matching, Campaigns, and Assets screens
- [ ] Each has a ≥44×44px tap target on mobile (verify via computed box size, not just CSS declaration)
- [ ] axe-core / Playwright a11y check added asserting minimum target size where feasible
- [ ] No visual regression on desktop (checkbox/icon visual size unchanged — only the tappable padding grows)
- [ ] Verified at 390px and 430px viewport widths

## Skills

`accessibility` (WCAG target-size guidance) · `vercel-react-best-practices` (keep the padding change CSS/Tailwind-only, no new JS-driven layout — avoid unnecessary re-renders per `rerender-*` rules)

## Dependencies

* Design spec: `Universal design prompt/MOBILE-PLAN.md` §16.2, §17 (source of truth for the requirement)
* Related, done: [IPI-264](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-264/design-mobile-verification-passfail-matrix-and-breakpoints) (DESIGN · Mobile Verification — Pass/Fail Matrix & Breakpoints)

## Out of scope

Long-press-to-select (tracked separately, see MOBILE-003) · tablet layout (see MOBILE-005)

# Implementation Prompt Pack (2026-06-30)

**Worktree:** `ipi/275-persistent-chat-dock` · `../wt-ipi-275-persistent-chat-dock`
**Skills:** copilotkit (v2) · mastra · frontend-design · shadcn · mermaid-diagrams · ipix-wireframe
**MCP:** CopilotKit MCP (docs) · Mastra docs server
**Design ref:** `Universal design prompt/components/` dock prototype · handoff §03 component map

## User stories

* As an **operator**, the dock is always visible at the workspace base with streaming responses — never a blank chat area.
* As an **operator on mobile**, the dock pins to the bottom and expands as a sheet without covering the active form.
* As a **developer**, there is exactly **one** dock component; routes pass context via `useAgentContext`, never fork the UI.

## Desktop wireframe

```
+-----------------------------------------------+
| workspace content (scrolls)                   |
+-----------------------------------------------+
| [*] production-planner . Spring Campaign      |  <- context strip (from IPI-197 hook)
| [Generate deliverables] [Plan shoot] [Explain]|  <- chips (from IPI-197 hook)
| +-------------------------------------------+ |
| | Ask anything...                      [up] | |  <- input + send
| +-------------------------------------------+ |
+-----------------------------------------------+
```

## Mobile wireframe (<=1024px)

```
+----------------+   collapsed:
| content        |   | chat . Spring  [up] |  <- pinned bar
|                |   tap up -> expands to sheet (70vh)
+----------------+
| chat . Spring  |  <- dock pinned above BottomNav (IPI-251)
+----------------+
| [o][o][o][o]   |  <- BottomNavigation
+----------------+
```

## State diagram

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Collapsed
    Collapsed --> Expanded: tap / focus
    Expanded --> Streaming: send message
    Streaming --> Expanded: response complete
    Streaming --> Error: stream fail
    Error --> Expanded: retry
    Expanded --> Collapsed: dismiss / route blur
```

## Message sequence (route-aware)

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    actor Op as Operator
    participant Dock as PersistentChatDock
    participant CK as CopilotKit runtime
    participant M as Mastra agent
    Op->>Dock: type / click chip
    Dock->>CK: send + useAgentContext(brandId, shootId, selection)
    CK->>M: resolve agentId via route-agent-map (IPI-247)
    M-->>CK: stream tokens
    CK-->>Dock: render streaming
    Dock-->>Op: answer + follow-up chips
```

## Implementation steps (Test block each)

| Step | Prompt | Test / proof |
| -- | -- | -- |
| **A** | Create `app/src/components/persistent-chat-dock/` — single client component; mount in operator layout below workspace slot | renders on `/app/shoots` |
| **B** | Wire CopilotKit v2 (`/v2` imports) + `useAgent({ agentId })` resolved from route-agent-map (IPI-247) | agent connects, no "not found" |
| **C** | Integrate IPI-197 greeting/chip hooks (`useRouteWelcome` / `useConfigureSuggestions`) — **no duplicate strings here** | greeting names active brand/shoot |
| **D** | Streaming render + error/retry banner (states above) | stream + forced-error retry work |
| **E** | Mobile pin + expand sheet (<=1024px), above BottomNav; touch targets >=44px | Playwright mobile viewport pass |

## Out of scope

HITL approve UI (IPI-244) · per-agent tools (IPI-259/260/261) · thread persistence (license). Dock shell + context + streaming only. **Welcome/chip copy owned by IPI-197.**

## Verification

```bash
cd app && npm run lint && npx tsc --noEmit && npm run build && npm run test:e2e
```

Routes to test: `/app` · `/app/shoots` · `/app/shoots/new` · `/app/brand/[id]` (greeting changes per route).

- [ ] Desktop + mobile screenshots (collapsed/expanded/streaming/error)

Title: SHOOT-DETAIL-EDIT-SHOTS — Add, edit, and remove shot cards inline
Description:
## Purpose

Add, edit, and remove shot cards inline on `/app/shoots/[shootId]`. Separate from EDIT-BASIC (IPI-210) and EDIT-DELIVERABLES (IPI-216) per one-concern rule.

## User story

As an operator I can add, edit, and remove shots inline so that the shot list reflects the current plan.

## Schema facts (verified)

* `shoot.shot_list.id uuid primary key` — stable IDs exist
* `shoot.shot_list.status shoot.shot_status` = `pending | captured | approved` — already present
* No new status column needed for this task

## API routes

```
POST   /api/shoots/[shootId]/shots            → 201 { shot_id }
PATCH  /api/shoots/[shootId]/shots/[id]       → 200 { shot_id }
DELETE /api/shoots/[shootId]/shots/[id]       → 204
Auth: withOperatorAuth → 401
```

## Supabase

* `upsert_shoot_shot(p_shoot_id uuid, p_id uuid default null, p_description text, p_style_notes text default null, p_order integer default null)` — SECURITY DEFINER + SET search_path = '' — schema-qualified: `shoot.shot_list`
* `delete_shoot_shot(p_shoot_id uuid, p_shot_id uuid)` — verify ownership before delete
* Audit: `agent_name: "operator-edit"` (non-blocking)

## Files

| File | Change |
| -- | -- |
| `app/src/components/shoots/shoot-shot-cards.tsx` | New — editable shot cards |
| `app/src/app/api/shoots/[shootId]/shots/route.ts` | New — POST |
| `app/src/app/api/shoots/[shootId]/shots/[id]/route.ts` | New — PATCH + DELETE |
| `supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDD_shoot_shot_rpcs.sql` | upsert + delete RPCs |

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A. Add shot → new row in DB, appears in list
- [ ] B. Edit description/style_notes → DB updated
- [ ] C. Remove shot → row deleted
- [ ] D. 403 if not owner; 404 if shoot or shot missing
- [ ] E. Audit log per action (non-blocking)
- [ ] F. Shot order preserved after edit
- [ ] G. Button disabled while POST in flight

## Implementation prompt

```
Implement SHOOT-DETAIL-EDIT-SHOTS for the iPix operator app.

Schema: shoot.shot_list.id is uuid primary key (stable IDs exist).
shoot.shot_list.status is shoot.shot_status enum (pending|captured|approved) — do not modify status in this task.

1. Create supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDD_shoot_shot_rpcs.sql
   upsert_shoot_shot(p_shoot_id uuid, p_id uuid default null, p_description text, p_style_notes text default null, p_order integer default null)
   - SECURITY DEFINER; SET search_path = ''
   - IF p_id IS NULL: INSERT INTO shoot.shot_list(shoot_id, description, style_notes, "order")
   - ELSE: UPDATE shoot.shot_list SET description=p_description, style_notes=p_style_notes, "order"=COALESCE(p_order,"order") WHERE id=p_id AND shoot_id=p_shoot_id
   - RETURN shot_id

   delete_shoot_shot(p_shoot_id uuid, p_shot_id uuid)
   - SECURITY DEFINER; SET search_path = ''
   - DELETE FROM shoot.shot_list WHERE id=p_shot_id AND shoot_id=p_shoot_id

2. Create route.ts files for POST, PATCH, DELETE
   Auth: withOperatorAuth → brand check → svc.rpc(...)
   Audit (non-blocking)

3. Create shoot-shot-cards.tsx
   Editable cards: add card button, inline edit form, delete button
   Disable buttons while request in flight

Branch: ipi/217-shoot-detail-edit-shots
```

**Spec:** `docs/plan/tasks/03-shoot-detail-actions.md`
**Depends on:** IPI-210 merged
**Blocks:** IPI-213

Title: SHOOT-DETAIL-EDIT-BASIC — Edit shoot name, brief, channels, and budget inline
Description:
## Purpose

Edit name, brief, target channels, estimated budget, and budget breakdown on `/app/shoots/[shootId]` — one PATCH route, one RPC. Deliverables and shots are separate issues (IPI-216, IPI-217).

## User story

As an operator I can edit the shoot name, brief, channels, and budget inline on the Shoot Detail page so that I keep the plan accurate without re-running the wizard.

## What changes

* Edit toggle (pencil icon) on each field group; on save POST to API route
* Optimistic UI update; revert + toast on error

## API route

```
PATCH /api/shoots/[shootId]
Auth: withOperatorAuth → 401
Body: Partial<{ name, brief, target_channels, estimated_budget, budget_breakdown }>
Response: 200 { shoot_id } | 400 | 401 | 403 | 404 | 500
```

## Supabase

* RPC `update_shoot_draft(p_shoot_id uuid, p_name text default null, p_brief text default null, p_target_channels text[] default null, p_estimated_budget numeric default null, p_budget_breakdown jsonb default null)` — SECURITY DEFINER + SET search_path = '' — schema-qualified: `shoot.shoots`
* Audit: `ai_agent_logs` row `agent_name: "operator-edit"` — non-blocking (catch + log, never throw)

## Files

| File | Change |
| -- | -- |
| `app/src/app/api/shoots/[shootId]/route.ts` | New — PATCH handler |
| `app/src/components/shoots/shoot-edit-basic.tsx` | New — name/brief/channels/budget form |
| `supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDD_update_shoot_draft_rpc.sql` | New |

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A. PATCH updates name/brief/channels/budget in DB
- [ ] B. Optimistic UI update on save; revert on error
- [ ] C. 400 on validation failure; 403 if access denied; 404 if shoot missing
- [ ] D. Audit log inserted (non-blocking)
- [ ] E. RPC uses SECURITY DEFINER + SET search_path = ''
- [ ] F. `npm run typecheck` + `npm test` clean

## Implementation prompt

```
Implement SHOOT-DETAIL-EDIT-BASIC for the iPix operator app.

Stack: Next.js 15 (app/), TypeScript strict, Supabase Postgres, Mastra agents.
Auth pattern: withOperatorAuth(req) → OperatorAuthError → 401
Brand check: createSupabaseServerClient() RLS — see app/src/app/api/shoots/commit/route.ts
Service role writes: createClient(url, SERVICE_ROLE_KEY) + svc.rpc(...)
Page pattern: app/src/app/(operator)/app/brand/[id]/page.tsx

Task:
1. Create supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDD_update_shoot_draft_rpc.sql
   - Function: update_shoot_draft(p_shoot_id uuid, p_name text default null, ...)
   - SECURITY DEFINER; SET search_path = ''
   - Schema-qualified: UPDATE shoot.shoots SET ...
   - Only update non-null params

2. Create app/src/app/api/shoots/[shootId]/route.ts (PATCH handler)
   - withOperatorAuth → 401
   - createSupabaseServerClient().from("brands").select("id")... → 403
   - validate body (zod or manual)
   - svc.rpc("update_shoot_draft", { p_shoot_id: shootId, ...fields })
   - try { await svc.from("ai_agent_logs").insert({...}) } catch {} // non-blocking

3. Create app/src/components/shoots/shoot-edit-basic.tsx
   - Client component; Edit toggle shows form
   - useMutation pattern: optimistic update → PATCH → revert on error

Run: npm run typecheck && npm test before committing.
Branch: ipi/210-shoot-detail-edit-basic
```

- [ ] Agent greeting personalized:

Title: MOBILE-003 · Long-Press Select + Mobile Action Sheet
Description:
## Context

`Universal design prompt/MOBILE-PLAN.md` §16.2/§17: on mobile, selection currently only works via a desktop-style "Select" toggle — there is no long-press-to-enter-selection gesture, which MOBILE-PLAN §1.7 specifies as the mobile pattern. Separately, bulk actions (Save to shortlist / Duplicate / Archive / Invite) currently render as a sticky top bar; the design source's suggested improvement (§16.5) is to present them as a bottom **action sheet** on mobile instead, reusing the `BottomSheet` primitive. Drag-to-target (Assets/Campaigns drop dock) is confirmed **desktop-only** — HTML5 drag events don't fire on touch — and the bulk-bar/action-sheet is the documented working fallback, not a bug to fix.

No existing Linear issue tracks this (confirmed via `docs/linear/issues/` search).

## Problem

Mobile users have no gesture-based entry into multi-select mode, and bulk actions in a sticky top bar are a weaker mobile pattern than a bottom sheet.

## Proposal

1. Add a long-press pointer handler (long-press ~500ms) on selectable cards (Matching, Campaigns, Assets) that enters selection mode — same end state as tapping "Select".
2. On mobile viewports, render the bulk-action bar as a bottom action sheet (reuse the `BottomSheet` component) instead of the sticky top bar.
3. Explicitly document (code comment + `PATTERNS.md#selection`) that drag-to-target stays desktop-only; the bulk-bar/action-sheet is the mobile equivalent, per design.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Long-press (~500ms, cancels on scroll/move) enters selection mode on Matching/Campaigns/Assets cards
- [ ] Bulk-action bar renders as a bottom sheet on mobile (<768px), sticky bar unchanged on desktop
- [ ] Drag-to-target remains desktop-only; no touch drag implementation attempted (out of scope per design)
- [ ] Existing desktop "Select" toggle behavior unchanged

## Skills

`vercel-react-best-practices` — `client-passive-event-listeners` (long-press touch handlers should be passive where they don't need to call `preventDefault`), `rerender-move-effect-to-event` (long-press logic belongs in the pointer event handler, not an effect), `advanced-event-handler-refs` (stable handler refs to avoid re-attaching listeners per render)

## Dependencies

* Design spec: `MOBILE-PLAN.md` §1.7, §16.2, §16.5, §17
* `BottomSheet` component (confirm production equivalent exists or needs building — check `app/src/components/**` for an existing sheet primitive before building a new one)

## Out of scope

Touch drag-and-drop (explicitly desktop-only per design) · tablet layout (MOBILE-005)
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: MOBILE-005 · Tablet Breakpoint Verification (768/1024)
Description:
## Context

`Universal design prompt/MOBILE-PLAN.md` §2, §16.4, §17: tablet (768–1024px, the `md` breakpoint) is currently treated identically to mobile — nav rail hidden, intelligence panel as a sheet. The design source flags this as **unspecified**: "Tablet 2-pane (768–1024) layout — currently 'rail returns at 1024'; iPad-landscape split view unspecified" (M3 in the 2026-07-01 audit). No existing Linear issue tracks this (confirmed via `docs/linear/issues/` search).

## Problem

There is no verified, intentional tablet layout — it's an unreviewed side-effect of the mobile/desktop breakpoint logic, and iPad-landscape (a real, common device class for this product's users — creative directors reviewing on iPad) has no dedicated design.

## Proposal

This is primarily a **verification + decision** task, not new UI work: run the existing shell at 768px and 1024px and confirm the current "rail hidden below 1024" behavior is acceptable, OR flag it back to Claude Design for a proper 2-pane tablet spec (nav rail + workspace, intelligence still a sheet, per MOBILE-PLAN §2's own breakpoint table) before building anything net-new.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Every operator screen manually verified at exactly 768px and 1024px viewport widths (Playwright viewport test or manual DevTools)
- [ ] Document findings: does "rail hidden until 1024" feel acceptable, or does it waste horizontal space on iPad?
- [ ] If a 2-pane tablet layout is wanted: file a design request back to Claude Design (do not build ahead of a spec, per the Design/Development ownership rule in `MOBILE-PLAN.md` §17)
- [ ] If current behavior is accepted as-is: close this out with a one-line rationale, no code change needed

## Skills

`vercel-react-best-practices` — if a tablet-specific layout is greenlit, prefer `bundle-dynamic-imports` for any tablet-only heavier component (e.g. a 2-pane shell variant) so it doesn't bloat the mobile bundle

## Dependencies

* Design spec: `MOBILE-PLAN.md` §2 (breakpoint table), §16.4 (M3 gap), §17 (ownership)

## Out of scope

Building a full 2-pane tablet layout without a design spec — verify + decide first

Title: DESIGN-045 · Mobile Operator Shell — Bottom Nav & Sheet
Description:
## Overview

Ship **mobile operator shell** — BottomNavigation + BottomSheet for IntelligencePanel at ≤1024px per Claude Design.

**Tracker:** DESIGN-045 · Components: BottomNavigation.dc.html · BottomSheet.dc.html

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] ≤1024px: bottom nav visible, sidebar hidden
- [ ] Intel panel opens as BottomSheet (after IPI-243 shell)
- [ ] Touch targets ≥44px
- [ ] No horizontal scroll on 375px
- [ ] Playwright mobile viewport

---

## Execution

**Depends on:** IPI-243 IntelligencePanel shell

**Blocks:** IPI-264 mobile verification (app surfaces)

**SSOT:** MASTER-DEPENDENCIES

---

# Implementation Prompt Pack (2026-06-30)

**Worktree:** `ipi/251-mobile-shell` · `../wt-ipi-251-mobile-shell`
**Skills to run:** frontend-design · shadcn · accessibility · ipix-wireframe · mermaid-diagrams · task-verifier
**Design file (READ FIRST):** components: `BottomNavigation.dc.html` · `BottomSheet.dc.html` · `OperatorShell.dc.html` · `IntelligencePanel.dc.html` · `tasks/design-docs/design/MOBILE-VERIFICATION.md` (breakpoints) · handoff §03 component map

## User stories

* As an **operator on mobile**, I navigate via a thumb-reachable bottom bar instead of a hidden desktop sidebar.
* As an **operator on mobile**, I open the IntelligencePanel as a bottom sheet that slides up over content, then dismiss it back to my work.
* As an **operator**, every tap target is ≥44px and nothing scrolls sideways at 375px.
* As a **developer**, the shell switches layouts purely on breakpoint — one OperatorShell, responsive, no separate mobile routes.

## Breakpoint layout

```
DESKTOP (>1024px)              MOBILE (<=1024px)
+------+----------+------+      +------------------+
| nav  | content  |intel |      | content          |
| side |          |panel |      |                  |
| bar  |          |      |      +------------------+
+------+----------+------+      | [up] intel sheet |  <- BottomSheet (drag/tap)
                               +------------------+
                               | [o][o][o][o][o]  |  <- BottomNavigation (>=44px)
                               +------------------+
```

## BottomSheet states

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Hidden
    Hidden --> Peek: tap intel trigger
    Peek --> Expanded: drag up / tap
    Expanded --> Peek: drag down
    Peek --> Hidden: dismiss / backdrop tap
    Expanded --> Hidden: dismiss
```

## Responsive decision

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    VW{viewport <= 1024px?} -->|no| DESK[Sidebar + inline IntelligencePanel]
    VW -->|yes| MOB[Hide sidebar -> BottomNavigation]
    MOB --> SHEET[IntelligencePanel renders in BottomSheet]
    SHEET --> A11Y[focus trap + ESC + aria-expanded + >=44px targets]
```

## Implementation steps (Test block each)

| Step | Prompt | Test / proof |
| -- | -- | -- |
| **A** | Add breakpoint logic to OperatorShell: hide NavSidebar, show BottomNavigation at <=1024px | sidebar hidden at 1024 |
| **B** | Build `BottomNavigation` per DC (icons + labels, active state, >=44px targets) | nav renders, routes work |
| **C** | Build `BottomSheet`; mount IntelligencePanel (IPI-243) inside; peek/expand/dismiss | sheet opens/drags/dismisses |
| **D** | A11y: focus trap, ESC closes, `aria-expanded`, reduced-motion; no horizontal scroll at 375 | axe pass; no x-scroll |
| **E** | Playwright mobile viewport (390 + 768) across CC/Shoots/Brand | mobile specs green |

## Out of scope

IntelligencePanel content (IPI-243 ✅) · PersistentChatDock mobile pin (IPI-275) · per-screen verification matrix (IPI-264). Shell responsive chrome only.

## Verification

```bash
cd app && npm run lint && npx tsc --noEmit && npm run build && npm run test:e2e
```

Viewports to test: 375 · 390 · 768 — nav visible, sheet opens, targets ≥44px, no horizontal scroll.

## Evidence required (Done gate)

- [ ] Screenshots: 375 + 768 with nav + sheet (peek + expanded)
- [ ] axe/a11y output · Playwright mobile report · task-verifier
- [ ] PR link · Linear → Done

Title: SHOOT-DETAIL-APPROVALS — Approve shots and deliverables from detail page
Description:
## Purpose

Approve or reject individual shots and deliverables from the detail page.

## Schema facts (verified)

* `shoot.shot_list.status` is `shoot.shot_status` enum: `pending | captured | approved` — **no migration needed for shots**
* `shoot.shoot_deliverables.status` = `planned | covered | delivered` — no `approved` value. **Migration required** to add `approved` to the check constraint (or expand enum).
* `shoot.shoots.status` enum has `active` (not `approved`) — advance shoot to `active` when all items approved.

## API route

```
POST /api/shoots/[shootId]/approve
Auth: withOperatorAuth → 401
Body: { item_type: "shot" | "deliverable", item_id: string, approved: boolean }
Response: 200 { item_id, item_status, shoot_status } | 400 | 401 | 403 | 404 | 500
```

## Supabase

* Migration: expand `shoot.shoot_deliverables.status` check to include `approved` (deliverables only)
* RPC `approve_shoot_item(p_shoot_id, p_item_type, p_item_id, p_approved, p_user_id)` — SECURITY DEFINER + SET search_path = ''
  * Shots: approved=true → status='approved'; approved=false → status='pending'
  * Deliverables: approved=true → status='approved'; approved=false → status='planned'
  * All items approved → UPDATE shoot.shoots SET status='active'
  * Returns { item_status, shoot_status }
* Audit: `agent_name: "operator-approve"` (non-blocking)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A. Approve shot → `shot_list.status = 'approved'`
- [ ] B. Reset shot → `shot_list.status = 'pending'`
- [ ] C. Approve deliverable → `shoot_deliverables.status = 'approved'`
- [ ] D. All items approved → shoot advances to `active`
- [ ] E. Hero status badge updates without page reload
- [ ] F. 403 if not owner; 400 if item_id missing
- [ ] G. Audit log per action (non-blocking)
- [ ] H. Double-click → idempotent

## Implementation prompt

```
Implement SHOOT-DETAIL-APPROVALS for the iPix operator app.

Schema facts — do not contradict these:
- shoot.shot_list.status is shoot.shot_status enum: pending | captured | approved
- shoot.shoot_deliverables.status is text check: planned | covered | delivered
  → needs migration to add 'approved' to the check constraint
- shoot.shoots.status enum: planning | active | post_production | complete | archived
  → use 'active' (not 'approved') when all items confirmed

1. Create supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDD_shoot_deliverable_approved_status.sql
   ALTER TABLE shoot.shoot_deliverables DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS shoot_deliverables_status_check;
   ALTER TABLE shoot.shoot_deliverables ADD CONSTRAINT shoot_deliverables_status_check
     CHECK (status IN ('planned','covered','delivered','approved'));

2. Create supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDD_approve_shoot_item_rpc.sql
   approve_shoot_item(p_shoot_id uuid, p_item_type text, p_item_id uuid, p_approved boolean, p_user_id uuid)
   SECURITY DEFINER; SET search_path = ''
   - If shot: UPDATE shoot.shot_list SET status = CASE WHEN p_approved THEN 'approved' ELSE 'pending' END
   - If deliverable: UPDATE shoot.shoot_deliverables SET status = CASE WHEN p_approved THEN 'approved' ELSE 'planned' END
   - Check if all shots approved AND all deliverables approved → UPDATE shoot.shoots SET status = 'active'
   - Return (item_status, shoot_status)

3. Create app/src/app/api/shoots/[shootId]/approve/route.ts
   - withOperatorAuth → 401; brand check → 403
   - svc.rpc("approve_shoot_item", {...})
   - Audit log (non-blocking)

4. Update shot cards and deliverables table with Approve/Reset buttons

Branch: ipi/213-shoot-detail-approvals
```

**Spec:** `docs/plan/tasks/03-shoot-detail-actions.md`
**Depends on:** IPI-216 + IPI-217 merged

---

## Skills

| # | Skill | When |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 1 | `/worktrees` | Create `ipi/213-shoot-detail-approvals` worktree |
| 2 | `/ipix-task-lifecycle` | Load ACs, confirm schema facts |
| 3 | `/ipix-supabase` | Before writing migration (deliverable constraint) + RPC |
| 4 | `/frontend-design` | Approve/Reset buttons on shot cards + deliverables table |
| 5 | `/verify` | Approve all items, confirm shoot advances to `active` |
| 6 | `/ponytail` | One RPC handles both shot + deliverable approval — no branching routes |

Title: SHOOT-DETAIL-DUPLICATE — Clone shoot into new planning draft
Description:
## Purpose

Clone a committed shoot as a new draft so operators can remix an existing plan without re-running the wizard.

## User story

As an operator I can duplicate an existing shoot so that I get a new draft pre-populated with the same brief, deliverables, shots, and budget that I can modify before committing.

## Idempotency note

MVP behaviour: double-POST creates two clones. This is acceptable — document it and add client-side button disable while pending to prevent accidental doubles.

## What changes

* "Duplicate" button in hero `⋯` overflow menu
* POST → `new_shoot_id` → redirect to new detail page

## API route

```
POST /api/shoots/[shootId]/duplicate
Auth: withOperatorAuth → 401
Body: {} | { name_override?: string }
Response: 201 { new_shoot_id } | 401 | 403 | 404 | 500
```

## Supabase

* RPC `duplicate_shoot(p_shoot_id uuid, p_created_by uuid, p_name_override text default null)` — SECURITY DEFINER + SET search_path = ''
  * Inserts new row in shoot.shoots with `status = 'planning'`, name = `"<original> (copy)"`
  * Copies all shoot.shoot_deliverables rows
  * Copies all shoot.shot_list rows (reset status to `pending`)
  * Returns `{ new_shoot_id }`
* Audit: `agent_name: "operator-duplicate"` (non-blocking)

## Files

| File | Change |
| -- | -- |
| `app/src/components/shoots/shoot-hero.tsx` | Add Duplicate to overflow menu |
| `app/src/app/api/shoots/[shootId]/duplicate/route.ts` | New |
| `supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDD_duplicate_shoot_rpc.sql` | New |

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A. New shoot inserted with `status = 'planning'`
- [ ] B. All deliverables and shots copied; shot status reset to `pending`
- [ ] C. Name = `"<original> (copy)"`
- [ ] D. Redirect to `/app/shoots/<new_shoot_id>`
- [ ] E. 403 if not owner; 404 if source missing
- [ ] F. Audit log (non-blocking)
- [ ] G. Button disabled while POST in flight (prevent accidental doubles)

## Implementation prompt

```
Implement SHOOT-DETAIL-DUPLICATE for the iPix operator app.

1. Create supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDD_duplicate_shoot_rpc.sql
   duplicate_shoot(p_shoot_id uuid, p_created_by uuid, p_name_override text default null)
   SECURITY DEFINER; SET search_path = ''
   Schema-qualified: shoot.shoots, shoot.shoot_deliverables, shoot.shot_list
   Steps:
     a. SELECT shoot row → verify exists
     b. INSERT INTO shoot.shoots (..., status='planning', name=COALESCE(p_name_override, original_name || ' (copy)'))
     c. INSERT INTO shoot.shoot_deliverables (shoot_id=new_id, channel, format, quantity) SELECT ...
     d. INSERT INTO shoot.shot_list (shoot_id=new_id, description, style_notes, "order", status='pending') SELECT ...
     e. RETURN new shoot_id

2. Create app/src/app/api/shoots/[shootId]/duplicate/route.ts
   - withOperatorAuth → 401
   - brand ownership check → 403
   - svc.rpc("duplicate_shoot", { p_shoot_id, p_created_by: operator.id, p_name_override: body.name_override ?? null })
   - Return 201 { new_shoot_id }
   - Audit log (non-blocking)

3. Update app/src/components/shoots/shoot-hero.tsx
   - Add Duplicate to ⋯ overflow
   - Disable button while POST in flight

Branch: ipi/212-shoot-detail-duplicate
```

**Depends on:** IPI-209 merged

Title: SHOOT-DETAIL-ARCHIVE — Archive / unarchive shoot with confirmation modal
Description:
## Purpose

Soft-archive a shoot using the existing `shoot.shoot_status` enum — no new column needed.

## User story

As an operator I can archive a shoot I no longer need so that it disappears from the active list but can be recovered.

## Schema correction

`shoot.shoots.status` is already `shoot.shoot_status` enum which includes `'archived'`. **No migration needed.** Archive = set `status = 'archived'`. Unarchive = set `status = 'planning'`.

## What changes

* "Archive" button in hero `⋯` overflow → confirmation modal → POST
* Redirect to `/app/shoots` on success
* Show "Unarchive" if `shoot.status === 'archived'`

## API route

```
POST /api/shoots/[shootId]/archive
Auth: withOperatorAuth → 401
Body: { archive: boolean }
Response: 200 { shoot_id, status } | 401 | 403 | 404 | 500
```

## Supabase

No new column. RPC only:

* `set_shoot_archived(p_shoot_id uuid, p_archived boolean, p_user_id uuid)` — SECURITY DEFINER + SET search_path = ''
  * `p_archived = true` → `UPDATE shoot.shoots SET status = 'archived' WHERE id = p_shoot_id`
  * `p_archived = false` → `UPDATE shoot.shoots SET status = 'planning' WHERE id = p_shoot_id`
* Shoots list: filter `status <> 'archived'`
* Audit: `agent_name: "operator-archive"` (non-blocking)

## Files

| File | Change |
| -- | -- |
| `app/src/components/shoots/shoot-hero.tsx` | Archive/Unarchive button + modal |
| `app/src/app/api/shoots/[shootId]/archive/route.ts` | New |
| `supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDD_set_shoot_archived_rpc.sql` | RPC only |

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A. Archive requires confirmation modal — never single-click
- [ ] B. POST sets `shoot.status = 'archived'`
- [ ] C. Unarchive sets `shoot.status = 'planning'`
- [ ] D. Redirect to `/app/shoots` after archive
- [ ] E. Archived shoots hidden from list (`status <> 'archived'`)
- [ ] F. 403 if not owner
- [ ] G. Audit log (non-blocking)
- [ ] H. Double-POST → idempotent (already archived, no error)

## Implementation prompt

```
Implement SHOOT-DETAIL-ARCHIVE for the iPix operator app.

Key fact: shoot.shoots.status is a shoot.shoot_status enum that already includes
'archived' (planning | active | post_production | complete | archived).
NO new column or data migration is needed.

1. Create supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDD_set_shoot_archived_rpc.sql
   - set_shoot_archived(p_shoot_id uuid, p_archived boolean, p_user_id uuid)
   - SECURITY DEFINER; SET search_path = ''
   - UPDATE shoot.shoots SET status = CASE WHEN p_archived THEN 'archived'::shoot.shoot_status ELSE 'planning'::shoot.shoot_status END WHERE id = p_shoot_id

2. Create app/src/app/api/shoots/[shootId]/archive/route.ts
   - withOperatorAuth → 401
   - brand ownership check → 403
   - svc.rpc("set_shoot_archived", { p_shoot_id, p_archived, p_user_id })
   - Audit log (non-blocking)

3. Update app/src/components/shoots/shoot-hero.tsx
   - Show Archive button when status !== 'archived'
   - Show Unarchive button when status === 'archived'
   - Confirmation modal before POST
   - On success: router.push('/app/shoots')

4. Update shoots list query to add: .neq('status', 'archived')

Branch: ipi/211-shoot-detail-archive
```

Title: UX P2: Smart notifications — alert when drafts, approvals, and analyses complete
Description:
## Problem

When a brand analysis completes (which takes ~2 minutes), the user has likely navigated away. There is no notification to bring them back. They only find out the draft is ready if they happen to return to the brand page.

## Expected behaviour

**In-app notifications (bell icon in nav):**

* "Ola Azul Swimwear — analysis complete. Draft ready for review." → direct link to brand page
* "Draft approved — Ola Azul profile is now live." → with undo link
* "Analysis failed for Ola Azul — click to retry." → direct retry action

**Browser push notifications (opt-in):**

* Same events, delivered even when the tab is in the background
* One-click action from the notification: "Review draft →" opens directly to the brand page

**Notification centre:**

* Bell icon in the operator nav shows unread count badge
* Dropdown lists last 10 notifications with read/unread state
* Mark all as read

## Implementation

* `brands` Realtime subscription on `intake_status` column — trigger in-app notification when status transitions to `draft_ready` or `failed`
* `brand_intake_drafts` Realtime subscription — trigger on `approved_at` / `rejected_at` updates
* Notification store: `useState` in a top-level provider or lightweight Zustand slice
* Push notifications: `Notification` Web API + `ServiceWorker` registration (opt-in prompt after first analysis completes)
* Notification centre: dropdown component in operator nav, unread count badge

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] In-app notification fires when `intake_status` transitions to `draft_ready`
- [ ] In-app notification fires when analysis fails
- [ ] In-app notification fires when a draft is approved or rejected
- [ ] Notification centre shows last 10 events with read/unread state
- [ ] Bell icon shows unread count badge
- [ ] Browser push opt-in prompt offered after first analysis
- [ ] Push notification includes direct link to the relevant brand page
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: Refactor: extract 6 inline Shoot Wizard steps into step components
Description:
# Extract Shoot Wizard steps into components

**Role:** You are implementing this as an iPix engineer. Stack: Next.js `app/`, Supabase remote-only, one concern per PR.

**Plain English:** Break the 824-line `shoots/new/page.tsx` client monolith into one presentational component per wizard step — zero behavior change.

**Context — what's broken today:**

* `app/src/app/(operator)/app/shoots/new/page.tsx` is an ~824-line `"use client"` monolith: all 6 step bodies live inline as `{step === n && (...)}` blocks alongside every handler, `useState`, and Mastra/API call.
* One giant file makes each step hard to unit-test in isolation and slow to reason about; a change to one step risks the others.
* **Deferred from** [IPI-274](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-274) — that issue was a *visual reskin only* and explicitly froze structure (`§0.3a: "Reskin in place; architecture change is out of scope"`). The reskin is now shipped (PR #226), so the extraction can happen safely as its own concern.
* SSOT plan: `tasks/design-docs/shoot/PLAN/shoot-wizard-dc-conversion.md` §3 (component map).

**Related:** IPI-274 (reskin, Done) · **Skills:** `@ipix-task-lifecycle` `@gen-test`

---

## User story

> As an **Engineer**, when I change or test one wizard step, I want that step in its own file with typed props, so I can unit-test it and edit it without touching the other five.

---

## Acceptance criteria (testable outcomes only)

* **A — Structure:** each of the 6 step bodies (Basics, Brief, Deliverables, Shot list, Budget, Review) lives in its own file under `app/src/components/shoot/steps/` (e.g. `BasicsStep.tsx`), imported by `page.tsx`. (proof: `ls app/src/components/shoot/steps/` shows 6 files)
* **B — State stays put:** `page.tsx` keeps all `useState`, handlers, and Mastra/API calls; steps receive state + callbacks as **props only** (no data fetching moved into step components). (proof: `grep -c "createBrowserClient\|fetch(" app/src/components/shoot/steps/*.tsx` returns 0)
* **C — No behavior change:** existing e2e passes unchanged. (proof: `npx playwright test e2e/shoot-wizard.spec.ts` green)
* **D — Still a client wizard:** no RSC conversion; each step file is `"use client"`-safe (presentational). (proof: no `async function` server component in step files)
* **E — Regression guard:** each new step component has a focused Vitest (render + one key interaction, e.g. Brief Continue disabled until non-empty). (proof: `npm test` — 6 new step tests pass; typecheck + lint + build green)

---

## Technical notes

**Do NOT:**

* Do NOT change wizard behavior, the 3 HITL gates, navigation, or any Mastra/API call.
* Do NOT restyle — IPI-274 already reskinned; this is pure structural extraction (diff should be move + prop-wiring, not CSS).
* Do NOT convert `page.tsx` or any step to an RSC / move data fetching.
* Do NOT mix any other change into this PR (one concern).

**Out of scope:** visual changes · the 4 deferred DC steps (→ IPI-252) · booking flow (→ IPI-340/341/342) · any workflow/route/schema change.

---

## Completion steps A–E

- [ ] **A** Create `components/shoot/steps/` + move Basics/Review bodies out first (simplest) — proof: `npx tsc --noEmit` exit 0
- [ ] **B** Move Brief + the 3 HITL-gate step bodies, wiring props/callbacks — proof: e2e green
- [ ] **C** Thin `page.tsx` to state + handlers + `<XStep .../>` switch — proof: `page.tsx` LOC materially reduced
- [ ] **D** Add one Vitest per step component — proof: `npm test` 6 new tests pass
- [ ] **E** Full gate + PR — proof: `npm run lint && npm test && npx tsc --noEmit && npm run build` all green

Title: SHOOT-DETAIL-EXPORT — Export shot list and budget as PDF / CSV
Description:
## Purpose

Download shot list and budget as CSV (PDF only if a PDF lib is already in package.json).

## User story

As an operator I can download a CSV of the shot list and budget to share with crew or clients without giving app access.

## Ponytail rule

Ship **CSV-only** MVP. Check `package.json` before touching PDF — do not install `jsPDF` or `@react-pdf/renderer` if absent.

## API route

```
GET /api/shoots/[shootId]/export?format=csv
Auth: withOperatorAuth → 401
Response: 200 text/csv | 400 | 401 | 403 | 404 | 500
Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="shoot-<name>-<YYYY-MM-DD>.csv"
```

## Supabase

Read-only via `get_shoot_detail` RPC (IPI-209). No writes.

## Files

| File | Change |
| -- | -- |
| `app/src/components/shoots/shoot-hero.tsx` | Add Export → CSV button |
| `app/src/app/api/shoots/[shootId]/export/route.ts` | New — GET handler |

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A. CSV includes all shots (number, description, style_notes) + budget line items
- [ ] B. Filename: `shoot-<name>-<YYYY-MM-DD>.csv`
- [ ] C. No new npm dependency added
- [ ] D. 403 if not owner; 404 if missing; 400 if format invalid
- [ ] E. Download triggers in Chrome + Firefox

## Implementation prompt

```
Implement SHOOT-DETAIL-EXPORT for the iPix operator app. CSV only — no PDF dep.

1. Create app/src/app/api/shoots/[shootId]/export/route.ts

export const dynamic = "force-dynamic"
export async function GET(req, { params }) {
  const { shootId } = await params
  await withOperatorAuth(req) // → 401
  // brand ownership check → 403
  const svc = serviceClient()
  const { data } = await svc.rpc("get_shoot_detail", { p_shoot_id: shootId, p_user_id: operator.id })
  if (!data) return NextResponse.json({ error: "Not found" }, { status: 404 })

  const format = new URL(req.url).searchParams.get("format")
  if (format !== "csv") return NextResponse.json({ error: "format must be csv" }, { status: 400 })

  // Build CSV with plain string — no library
  const rows = [
    ["Shot #", "Description", "Style Notes"],
    ...data.shots.map(s => [s.shot_number, s.description, s.style_notes ?? ""]),
    [],
    ["Budget Category", "Amount"],
    ...Object.entries(data.shoot.budget_breakdown ?? {}).map(([k, v]) => [k, v]),
    ["Total", data.shoot.estimated_budget],
  ]
  const csv = rows.map(r => r.map(c => `"${String(c).replace(/"/g, '""')}"`).join(",")).join("\n")
  const date = new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10)
  const filename = `shoot-${data.shoot.name.replace(/[^a-z0-9]/gi, "-")}-${date}.csv`

  return new Response(csv, {
    headers: {
      "Content-Type": "text/csv",
      "Content-Disposition": `attachment; filename="${filename}"`,
    },
  })
}

2. Add Export button to shoot-hero.tsx overflow menu

Branch: ipi/214-shoot-detail-export
```

**Spec:** `docs/plan/tasks/03-shoot-detail-actions.md`
**Depends on:** IPI-209 merged

---

## Skills

| # | Skill | When |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 1 | `/worktrees` | Create `ipi/214-shoot-detail-export` worktree |
| 2 | `/ipix-task-lifecycle` | Load ACs, confirm no PDF dep needed |
| 3 | `/ipix-supabase` | Confirm `get_shoot_detail` RPC exists (read-only) |
| 4 | `/frontend-design` | Export button in overflow menu |
| 5 | `/verify` | Click export, confirm CSV downloads with correct filename + data |
| 6 | `/ponytail` | Plain string CSV — no library, no abstraction |

Title: GEMINI-001 · Interactions API migration (Mastra operator)
Description:
## ⛔ DEFER — no Interactions API through MVP

**Label:** `defer:no-interactions` · **Priority:** Low · Do not start before MVP 8/8 proofs.

---

## GEMINI-001 — Interactions API migration (Mastra operator)

**In plain terms:** Migrate `app/` Mastra agents from legacy `generateContent` to GA **Interactions API** with `previous_interaction_id` for multi-turn state.

**MVP priority:** **Deferred** — CopilotKit + Mastra already manage thread state; Interactions adds interaction IDs, thread mapping, and migration cost without solving a current problem.

### Why defer

Current path works:

```text
Next.js → Mastra → CopilotKit → Supabase → Gemini generateContent
```

Adopt Interactions only when a concrete gap appears (e.g. managed Deep Research agent, cross-service interaction continuity). Until then: **generateContent** + streaming + thought signatures.

### Acceptance criteria (when un-deferred)

- [ ] Spike `client.interactions.create` on `brand-intelligence` agent

Title: SCR-29 · CRM Contact Detail — React Parity
Description:
## SCR-29 — CRM Contact Detail React Parity

**Priority:** P1 · **Dependencies:** [IPI-391](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-391/rf-04a-crm-company-detail-page) (RF-04a Company Detail) — builds same pattern; [IPI-392](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-392/rf-04b-contact-detail-profile360-extract) (RF-04b Profile360) — will consume shared template

**SSOT:** `Universal-design-prompt-new/tasks/screens/SCR-29-crm-contact-detail.md`

**Route:** `/app/crm/contacts/[id]`

**Design source:** `Pages/SCR-29-CRM-Contact-Detail.dc.html`

**Current state:** 30-line stub with id/org validation + CrmScreenGate

---

## Overview

Build Contact Detail screen sharing the same layout architecture as Company Detail. After extraction ([IPI-392](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-392/rf-04b-contact-detail-profile360-extract)), both will consume the **Profile360** template with per-entity configs.

## Purpose

Operator opens a contact from the list and sees: header with avatar, name, type chip, role, org link; multi-row email/phone display; 3 tabs (Overview, Deals, Activity).

## User journey

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  A[Contacts list] -->|click row| B[Contact detail /app/crm/contacts/[id]]
  B --> C[Header: avatar, name, type chip, org link, platform user link]
  B --> D[3 tabs: Overview, Deals, Activity]
  D --> E[Overview: multi-row emails + phones + summary]
  D --> F[Deals: EntityList → links to pipeline]
  D --> G[Activity: unified timeline]
  B --> H[IntelligencePanel: relationship health, activity]
  B --> I[Chat dock: crm-assistant with contact context]
```

## Component reuse

| Component | Usage | Reuse from |
| -- | -- | -- |
| StatusChip | Contact type chip (contact/model/photographer/crew) | RF-01 |
| EmptyState | Empty tab sections | RF-A7b |
| ErrorState | Error with retry | RF-A7b |
| Profile360 | **After** [IPI-392](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-392/rf-04b-contact-detail-profile360-extract) — shares config with Company Detail | RF-04b |
| N/A | NO duplicate email/phone layout | Profile360 shared slot |

## Data source table

| Section | Source | Status |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Header (name, avatar, type chip, role, org link) | `crm_contacts` + join `crm_companies` | 🟢 live |
| Email(s) | `contacts.emails` (jsonb array) | 🟢 verify shape |
| Phone(s) | `contacts.phones` (jsonb array) | 🟢 verify shape |
| Type chip | `contacts.contact_type` (contact/model/photographer/crew) | 🟢 verify column |
| Platform user link | `contacts.user_id` → platform auth users | 🟢 verify FK |
| Summary | Static or crm-assistant | 🟡 verify |
| Deals tab | `crm_deals` linked to contact | 🟢 live (if FK exists) |
| Activity tab | `crm_activities` linked to contact | 🟢 live |
| Intel panel | crm-assistant route context | 🟢 wired |

## DC grid spec — Contact Detail specific differences from Company Detail

| Property | DC value |
| -- | -- |
| Avatar | 46px circle, initials in muted bg |
| Type chip | Pill with colored border per role (contact/text-2, model/prospect, photographer/active, crew/approval) |
| Breadcrumb | Contacts > {name} |
| Email rows | Array display: mail icon, value, type label badge, "Primary" tag |
| Phone rows | Array display: phone icon, value, type label badge, "Primary" tag |
| Tabs | 3: Overview, Deals, Activity |

## Acceptance criteria

| # | Criterion | Proof |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 1 | Contact loads by id — header: avatar initials, name, type chip, role, org link | browser |
| 2 | Breadcrumb: Contacts > {name} | click breadcrumb → list |
| 3 | Email list: multi-row with type labels + "Primary" badge | visual match to DC array layout |
| 4 | Phone list: multi-row with type labels + "Primary" badge | visual match to DC |
| 5 | 3 tabs: Overview, Deals, Activity — switching works | click each tab |
| 6 | Overview: emails + phones + summary card | data from DB |
| 7 | Deals tab: list with stage chip + value + close date | rows link to pipeline detail |
| 8 | Activity tab: unified timeline (call/email/note/meeting/ai) | icon + title + body + time |
| 9 | Org link → `/app/crm/companies/[id]` | click navigates |
| 10 | Loading: skeleton | visible during fetch |
| 11 | Empty: no deals → EmptyState | truncate data |
| 12 | Error: ErrorState with retry | mock failure |
| 13 | Not found: invalid id → 404 | notFound() |
| 14 | tokens.css only — no legacy hex | grep check |
| 15 | Profile360 compatible: layout matches config contract | review vs PROFILE-360-template.md |

## Mobile & a11y

| # | Requirement |
| -- | -- |
| 1 | Email/phone rows selectable (tap to copy) |
| 2 | Tabs keyboard-accessible |
| 3 | Avatar decorative (alt="") or initials as text |
| 4 | Touch targets >=44px |
| 5 | 390px: stacked layout, no overflow |

## AI boundaries

| Area | Status |
| -- | -- |
| crm-assistant | 🟢 receives contact context |
| IntelligencePanel | 🟢 health, recent activity, NBA |
| Chat dock | 🟡 placeholder |
| AI writes | 🔴 none |
| Summary | 🟡 AI or static — accept honest empty |

## Test plan

```bash
cd app && npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm test && CI=true npm run build
```

Browser: contact renders · 3 tabs · email/phone arrays render · nav works · 390px pass

## Out of scope

* Profile360 extraction ([IPI-392](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-392/rf-04b-contact-detail-profile360-extract) follow-up)
* AI draft/log — separate issue
* Photographer/crew 360° (Phase 2)

Title: CRM-UX-004 · Deal detail screen (read + ungated stage moves)
Description:
**In plain terms:** The single deal record — overview, ungated stage control, and activity timeline. The `won`/`lost` gate itself is a separate, safety-critical follow-up issue.

**Blocked by:** CRM-DATA-001 (IPI-362) only — IPI-365 loosened to a soft dependency · **Unblocks:** CRM-AI-001, CRM-AI-002

## User story

As a **brand operator**, I want to open one deal and see its full context — company, contact, value, and history — in one place, so I can decide what to do next without hunting across screens.

## User journey

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  A[Operator opens a deal from Pipeline or Company Detail] --> B[Overview: company/contact/value/dates]
  B --> C{Move stage?}
  C -->|lead/qualified/proposal/negotiation| D[Writes immediately via IPI-365's PATCH route]
  C -->|won/lost| E["Inert in this issue — real gate is IPI-367"]
  B --> F[Activity timeline + Log activity]
```

## Wireframe (ASCII — superseded by the real Claude Design prototype)

Real DC HTML now exists: `Universal design prompt/crm/SCR-31-CRM-Deal-Detail.dc.html`. Use it as the visual source of truth, not this ASCII sketch.

```
┌────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────┐
│ NavSidebar │ Pipeline / Acme Co. — Spring Order    │ IntelligencePanel│
│  · CRM     │ Value: $12,000   Stage: [Negotiation▾]│  deal health     │
│            │  (won/lost options shown, inert here) │  EvidenceBlock   │
│            │ Linked shoot: — · Linked campaign: —   │  next-best-action│
│            │ ── Activity ──────────────────────────│                  │
│            │  · note "sent proposal" — 2d ago       │                  │
│            │  [+ Log activity]                      │                  │
│            │  ── PersistentChatDock ────────────▼   │                  │
└────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────┘
```

## Phase 0 — production state (verified 2026-07-05 against `origin/main`)

| Area | Exists today? | This issue changes? |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Route `/app/crm/pipeline/[id]` | ✅ merged, renders `<CrmScreenGate screen="Pipeline" />` | Replace gate with real workspace |
| `getDeal(id)` | 🔴 does not exist | **Build this issue** |
| `deriveTaskState` | ✅ merged as `app/src/lib/crm/activity-state.ts` (PR #216) — **not** `derive-task-state.ts` as an earlier version of this issue named it; same function, real file path differs | Reuse as-is |
| `ActivityTimeline` component | 🔴 does not exist yet — was briefly built into IPI-363's PR by mistake and pulled back out; **this issue is its correct, original owner** | **Build this issue** — Company Detail (IPI-363) and Contact Detail (IPI-364) import it once it lands, they don't rebuild it |
| `PATCH /api/crm/deals/[id]` | Built by IPI-365 (shared route) | Reuse — do not duplicate |

## AI Integration Matrix (per `tasks/design-docs/copilotkit-mastra.md` §12)

```text
CopilotKit
- [x] Headless UI hooks used: CrmRecordContext (shared, already wired — injects dealId here)
- [ ] Frontend tools / Display components: none page-specific

Mastra
- [x] Agent: crm-assistant (existing) — moveDealStage/logActivity tools already exist server-side
- [ ] Workflow / new Tools: none — won/lost write path is explicitly out of scope (IPI-367)
```

## Implementation Steps

**Full plan:** `docs/plan/tasks/2026-07-04-crm-deal-detail.md` · `tasks/crm/tasks/02-crm-design-to-react-conversion-plan.md` (conversion plan, current).

### Task 0 — `getDeal(id)`

* Add to `app/src/lib/crm/queries.ts` — joins company → contact → shoot; `shoot_id`/`campaign_id` render as `null` when unset, never a fabricated placeholder
* Test: `app/src/lib/crm/queries.test.ts` — mocked Supabase client

### Task 1 — Deal Detail overview block, replacing `<CrmScreenGate>`

* Create `app/src/app/(operator)/app/crm/pipeline/[id]/page.tsx` matching `SCR-31-CRM-Deal-Detail.dc.html`

### Task 2 — Shared `ActivityTimeline` component ⭐ (also used by IPI-363, IPI-364)

* `deriveTaskState` already merged (`app/src/lib/crm/activity-state.ts`)
* Create `app/src/components/crm/activity-timeline.tsx` — props: `anchor: {companyId?, contactId?, dealId?}`, one query on `crm_activities` filtered by whichever anchor is set, type icon per row
* **Build this once, here — Company Detail and Contact Detail import it, they don't rebuild it**

### Task 3 — Stage control (ungated) + inert won/lost

* Create `app/src/components/crm/deal-stage-control.tsx`
* Test: selecting `qualified` calls IPI-365's `PATCH /api/crm/deals/[id]` — do not build a second write path; selecting `won`/`lost` never calls any write function (assert mock not called)

## Description

`/app/crm/pipeline/:id`. **Route already exists on** `main`**, gated behind** `<CrmScreenGate>` — this issue replaces the gate. Design source of truth: `Universal design prompt/crm/SCR-31-CRM-Deal-Detail.dc.html` (the old `tasks/crm/design/02f` prompt doc is superseded). Overview block (company/contact/value/dates/linked shoot-campaign), stage control (`Select`/`ToggleGroup`), and the unified `crm_activities` timeline scoped to `deal_id`. This issue builds everything **except** the `won`/`lost` transition itself — selecting `won`/`lost` in this issue's scope should be visibly disabled/inert (a follow-up, CRM-AI-001, wires the real gate) so the two pieces of work don't race.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Overview block renders company/contact/value/dates, linked shoot/campaign only when populated
- [ ] Stage control writes immediately for `lead`/`qualified`/`proposal`/`negotiation` via IPI-365's PATCH route
- [ ] `won`/`lost` selection is present but inert in this issue (no write path yet)
- [ ] Activity timeline + `[Log activity]` inline form work
- [ ] Empty/loading/error states match `SCR-31` DC file

## Implementation notes

* Non-goals: any send integration on activity logging (draft/log only, per the module-wide non-goal)
